{{Cite source|date=June 28, 2018}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name=Tsuruoka Hachimangū | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image=[[File:GrfKanagawakenChigasakishi-Turuminehachimangu-01.jpg|270px|Tsuruoka Hachimangū Haiden, Chigasaki City]]
Haiden
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=270|coord={{coord|35|20|18.3|N|139|23|28.3|E}}}} | location=〒 253-0086
{{ill|Kanagawa Prefecture|ja|神奈川県}}{{ill|Chigasaki|ja|茅ヶ崎市}} Hamano-go 462 | ISO=JP-14 | Latitude degrees = 35|Latitude minutes = 20|Latitude seconds = 18.38 | Longitude degrees=139|Longitude minutes=23|Longitude seconds=28.30 | deity={{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|応神天皇}}
{{ill|Emperor Nintoku|ja|仁徳天皇}}
{{ill|Sazuka Daimyōjin|ja|佐塚大神}}
{{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|菅原道真}} | Shrine rank=Former {{ill|Village shrine|ja|村社}}, Former {{ill|District shrine|ja|郷社}} | Foundation={{ill|Chōgen 3|ja|長元3年}} ({{ill|1030|ja|1030年}}) | Main Hall=Nagare-zukuri | Alternate name=Tsuruoka Hachiman Shrine | festival={{ill|September 15|ja|9月15日}} | Rituals={{ill|Hamaori Festival|ja|浜降祭}} ({{ill|Marine Day|ja|海の日}}) | Map=Japan Kanagawa }} '''Tsuruoka Hachimangū''' (鶴嶺八幡宮) is a {{ill|shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Chigasaki|ja|茅ヶ崎市}}, {{ill|Kanagawa Prefecture|ja|神奈川県}}. It is also known as Tsuruoka Hachiman Shrine. Renowned as the center of Hachiman worship in {{ill|Sagami Province|ja|相模国}}, the shrine has been revered since ancient times as a significant site of {{ill|Hachiman faith|ja|八幡信仰}}.Cited by the Kanagawa Prefecture Shrine Office According to the shrine, it was the first {{ill|clan shrine|ja|氏社}} established by the {{ill|Genji|ja|源氏}} when they expanded into the Kanto region. == History == The shrine was founded in {{ill|Chōgen 3 (1030)|ja|長元3年（1030年）}}, during the {{ill|Subjugation of Shimōsa|ja|下総の乱}} by {{ill|Minamoto no Yoriyoshi|ja|源頼義}}, who hailed from {{ill|Kawachi Province|ja|河内国}} (modern-day {{ill|Habikino|ja|羽曳野市}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}). Yoriyoshi enshrined {{ill|Iwashimizu Hachimangū|ja|石清水八幡宮}} at the site under the name "Kaijima Hachimangū" (another theory suggests it was {{ill|Usa Hachimangū|ja|宇佐八幡宮}}). Later, the Genji moved it to its current location. In {{ill|Tenki 3 (1055)|ja|天喜3年（1055年）}}, during the {{ill|Earlier Nine Years' War|ja|前九年の役}}, Yoriyoshi prayed at the shrine and achieved victory. To honor this, he established the {{ill|Tsurugaoka Hachimangū|lt=Original Hachiman Shrine|ja|元八幡}} in {{ill|Yuigahama|ja|由比ヶ浜}}, Kamakura, in {{ill|Kōhei 6 (1063)|ja|康平6年（1063年）}}. In {{ill|Ōtoku 2 (1085)|ja|応徳2年（1085年）}}, Minamoto no Yoshiie, Yoriyoshi's heir, dedicated the shrine in Hamano-go as "Tsuruoka Hachimangū." Subsequently, Tsuruoka Hachimangū gained prominence, receiving land and monetary support from {{ill|Minamoto no Yoritomo|ja|源頼朝}} and others. Over time, it became the center for the famous {{ill|Hamaori Festival|ja|浜降祭}} and absorbed nearby shrines such as Sazuka Daimyōjin. == Deities == * {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|応神天皇}} * {{ill|Emperor Nintoku|ja|仁徳天皇}} * Sazuka Daimyōjin (associated with {{ill|Ōyamazumi Shrine|ja|大山祇神社}}) * {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|菅原道真}} (from Tsurumine Tenmangū) == Annual Events == * {{ill|Hamaori Festival|ja|浜降祭}} (Marine Day) * {{ill|Misorogi Ritual|ja|禊の神事}} (June 29, formerly held on July 15) * {{ill|Reitaisai|ja|例大祭}} (September 15) == Grounds == ; '''Great Ginkgo Tree''' : A 950-year-old tree said to have been planted by Minamoto no Yoshiie to pray for victory. ; '''Miyagane (Shrine Bell)''' : The shrine bell, originally installed in {{ill|Enpō 8 (1680)|ja|延宝8年（1680年）}}, was reconstructed after the war in 1965 as a "peace bell." === Sub-Shrines === * '''Shōnan Awashima Shrine''' — Dedicated to {{ill|Sukunahikona|ja|少彦名命}} and {{ill|Awashima-no-Kami|ja|淡島神}}. * '''Tsurumine Inari Shrine''' — Dedicated to {{ill|Ukanomitama|ja|宇迦之御魂}}. == Access == Take the JR Tōkaidō Main Line to {{ill|Chigasaki Station|ja|茅ケ崎駅}}. From the north exit, take a bus and alight at "Tsurumine Elementary School," then walk 1 minute. == References == {{Cite source help}} {{Reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|Minamoto no Yoriyoshi|ja|源頼義}} {{Cite source|date=June 28, 2018}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name=Tsuruoka Hachimangū | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image=[[File:GrfKanagawakenChigasakishi-Turuminehachimangu-01.jpg|270px|Tsuruoka Hachimangū Haiden, Chigasaki City]]
Haiden
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=270|coord={{coord|35|20|18.3|N|139|23|28.3|E}}}} | location=〒 253-0086
{{ill|Kanagawa Prefecture|ja|神奈川県}}{{ill|Chigasaki|ja|茅ヶ崎市}} Hamano-go 462 | ISO=JP-14 | Latitude degrees = 35|Latitude minutes = 20|Latitude seconds = 18.38 | Longitude degrees=139|Longitude minutes=23|Longitude seconds=28.30 | deity={{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|応神天皇}}
{{ill|Emperor Nintoku|ja|仁徳天皇}}
{{ill|Sazuka Daimyōjin|ja|佐塚大神}}
{{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|菅原道真}} | Shrine rank=Former {{ill|Village shrine|ja|村社}}, Former {{ill|District shrine|ja|郷社}} | Foundation={{ill|Chōgen 3|ja|長元3年}} ({{ill|1030|ja|1030年}}) | Main Hall=Nagare-zukuri | Alternate name=Tsuruoka Hachiman Shrine | festival={{ill|September 15|ja|9月15日}} | Rituals={{ill|Hamaori Festival|ja|浜降祭}} ({{ill|Marine Day|ja|海の日}}) | Map=Japan Kanagawa }} '''Tsuruoka Hachimangū''' (鶴嶺八幡宮) is a {{ill|shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Chigasaki|ja|茅ヶ崎市}}, {{ill|Kanagawa Prefecture|ja|神奈川県}}. It is also known as Tsuruoka Hachiman Shrine. Renowned as the center of Hachiman worship in {{ill|Sagami Province|ja|相模国}}, the shrine has been revered since ancient times as a significant site of {{ill|Hachiman faith|ja|八幡信仰}}.Cited by the Kanagawa Prefecture Shrine Office According to the shrine, it was the first {{ill|clan shrine|ja|氏社}} established by the {{ill|Genji|ja|源氏}} when they expanded into the Kanto region. == History == The shrine was founded in {{ill|Chōgen 3 (1030)|ja|長元3年（1030年）}}, during the {{ill|Subjugation of Shimōsa|ja|下総の乱}} by {{ill|Minamoto no Yoriyoshi|ja|源頼義}}, who hailed from {{ill|Kawachi Province|ja|河内国}} (modern-day {{ill|Habikino|ja|羽曳野市}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}). Yoriyoshi enshrined {{ill|Iwashimizu Hachimangū|ja|石清水八幡宮}} at the site under the name "Kaijima Hachimangū" (another theory suggests it was {{ill|Usa Hachimangū|ja|宇佐八幡宮}}). Later, the Genji moved it to its current location. In {{ill|Tenki 3 (1055)|ja|天喜3年（1055年）}}, during the {{ill|Earlier Nine Years' War|ja|前九年の役}}, Yoriyoshi prayed at the shrine and achieved victory. To honor this, he established the {{ill|Tsurugaoka Hachimangū|lt=Original Hachiman Shrine|ja|元八幡}} in {{ill|Yuigahama|ja|由比ヶ浜}}, Kamakura, in {{ill|Kōhei 6 (1063)|ja|康平6年（1063年）}}. In {{ill|Ōtoku 2 (1085)|ja|応徳2年（1085年）}}, Minamoto no Yoshiie, Yoriyoshi's heir, dedicated the shrine in Hamano-go as "Tsuruoka Hachimangū." Subsequently, Tsuruoka Hachimangū gained prominence, receiving land and monetary support from {{ill|Minamoto no Yoritomo|ja|源頼朝}} and others. Over time, it became the center for the famous {{ill|Hamaori Festival|ja|浜降祭}} and absorbed nearby shrines such as Sazuka Daimyōjin. == Deities == * {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|応神天皇}} * {{ill|Emperor Nintoku|ja|仁徳天皇}} * Sazuka Daimyōjin (associated with {{ill|Ōyamazumi Shrine|ja|大山祇神社}}) * {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|菅原道真}} (from Tsurumine Tenmangū) == Annual Events == * {{ill|Hamaori Festival|ja|浜降祭}} (Marine Day) * {{ill|Misorogi Ritual|ja|禊の神事}} (June 29, formerly held on July 15) * {{ill|Reitaisai|ja|例大祭}} (September 15) == Grounds == ; '''Great Ginkgo Tree''' : A 950-year-old tree said to have been planted by Minamoto no Yoshiie to pray for victory. ; '''Miyagane (Shrine Bell)''' : The shrine bell, originally installed in {{ill|Enpō 8 (1680)|ja|延宝8年（1680年）}}, was reconstructed after the war in 1965 as a "peace bell." === Sub-Shrines === * '''Shōnan Awashima Shrine''' — Dedicated to {{ill|Sukunahikona|ja|少彦名命}} and {{ill|Awashima-no-Kami|ja|淡島神}}. * '''Tsurumine Inari Shrine''' — Dedicated to {{ill|Ukanomitama|ja|宇迦之御魂}}. == Access == Take the JR Tōkaidō Main Line to {{ill|Chigasaki Station|ja|茅ケ崎駅}}. From the north exit, take a bus and alight at "Tsurumine Elementary School," then walk 1 minute. == References == {{Cite source help}} {{Reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|Minamoto no Yoriyoshi|ja|源頼義}} {{Cite source|date=June 28, 2018}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name=Tsuruoka Hachimangū | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image=[[File:GrfKanagawakenChigasakishi-Turuminehachimangu-01.jpg|270px|Tsuruoka Hachimangū Haiden, Chigasaki City]]
Haiden
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=270|coord={{coord|35|20|18.3|N|139|23|28.3|E}}}} | location=〒 253-0086
{{ill|Kanagawa Prefecture|ja|神奈川県}}{{ill|Chigasaki|ja|茅ヶ崎市}} Hamano-go 462 | ISO=JP-14 | Latitude degrees = 35|Latitude minutes = 20|Latitude seconds = 18.38 | Longitude degrees=139|Longitude minutes=23|Longitude seconds=28.30 | deity={{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|応神天皇}}
{{ill|Emperor Nintoku|ja|仁徳天皇}}
{{ill|Sazuka Daimyōjin|ja|佐塚大神}}
{{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|菅原道真}} | Shrine rank=Former {{ill|Village shrine|ja|村社}}, Former {{ill|District shrine|ja|郷社}} | Foundation={{ill|Chōgen 3|ja|長元3年}} ({{ill|1030|ja|1030年}}) | Main Hall=Nagare-zukuri | Alternate name=Tsuruoka Hachiman Shrine | festival={{ill|September 15|ja|9月15日}} | Rituals={{ill|Hamaori Festival|ja|浜降祭}} ({{ill|Marine Day|ja|海の日}}) | Map=Japan Kanagawa }} '''Tsuruoka Hachimangū''' (鶴嶺八幡宮) is a {{ill|shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Chigasaki|ja|茅ヶ崎市}}, {{ill|Kanagawa Prefecture|ja|神奈川県}}. It is also known as Tsuruoka Hachiman Shrine. Renowned as the center of Hachiman worship in {{ill|Sagami Province|ja|相模国}}, the shrine has been revered since ancient times as a significant site of {{ill|Hachiman faith|ja|八幡信仰}}.Cited by the Kanagawa Prefecture Shrine Office According to the shrine, it was the first {{ill|clan shrine|ja|氏社}} established by the {{ill|Genji|ja|源氏}} when they expanded into the Kanto region. == History == The shrine was founded in {{ill|Chōgen 3 (1030)|ja|長元3年（1030年）}}, during the {{ill|Subjugation of Shimōsa|ja|下総の乱}} by {{ill|Minamoto no Yoriyoshi|ja|源頼義}}, who hailed from {{ill|Kawachi Province|ja|河内国}} (modern-day {{ill|Habikino|ja|羽曳野市}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}). Yoriyoshi enshrined {{ill|Iwashimizu Hachimangū|ja|石清水八幡宮}} at the site under the name "Kaijima Hachimangū" (another theory suggests it was {{ill|Usa Hachimangū|ja|宇佐八幡宮}}). Later, the Genji moved it to its current location. In {{ill|Tenki 3 (1055)|ja|天喜3年（1055年）}}, during the {{ill|Earlier Nine Years' War|ja|前九年の役}}, Yoriyoshi prayed at the shrine and achieved victory. To honor this, he established the {{ill|Tsurugaoka Hachimangū|lt=Original Hachiman Shrine|ja|元八幡}} in {{ill|Yuigahama|ja|由比ヶ浜}}, Kamakura, in {{ill|Kōhei 6 (1063)|ja|康平6年（1063年）}}. In {{ill|Ōtoku 2 (1085)|ja|応徳2年（1085年）}}, Minamoto no Yoshiie, Yoriyoshi's heir, dedicated the shrine in Hamano-go as "Tsuruoka Hachimangū." Subsequently, Tsuruoka Hachimangū gained prominence, receiving land and monetary support from {{ill|Minamoto no Yoritomo|ja|源頼朝}} and others. Over time, it became the center for the famous {{ill|Hamaori Festival|ja|浜降祭}} and absorbed nearby shrines such as Sazuka Daimyōjin. == Deities == * {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|応神天皇}} * {{ill|Emperor Nintoku|ja|仁徳天皇}} * Sazuka Daimyōjin (associated with {{ill|Ōyamazumi Shrine|ja|大山祇神社}}) * {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|菅原道真}} (from Tsurumine Tenmangū) == Annual Events == * {{ill|Hamaori Festival|ja|浜降祭}} (Marine Day) * {{ill|Misorogi Ritual|ja|禊の神事}} (June 29, formerly held on July 15) * {{ill|Reitaisai|ja|例大祭}} (September 15) == Grounds == ; '''Great Ginkgo Tree''' : A 950-year-old tree said to have been planted by Minamoto no Yoshiie to pray for victory. ; '''Miyagane (Shrine Bell)''' : The shrine bell, originally installed in {{ill|Enpō 8 (1680)|ja|延宝8年（1680年）}}, was reconstructed after the war in 1965 as a "peace bell." === Sub-Shrines === * '''Shōnan Awashima Shrine''' — Dedicated to {{ill|Sukunahikona|ja|少彦名命}} and {{ill|Awashima-no-Kami|ja|淡島神}}. * '''Tsurumine Inari Shrine''' — Dedicated to {{ill|Ukanomitama|ja|宇迦之御魂}}. == Access == Take the JR Tōkaidō Main Line to {{ill|Chigasaki Station|ja|茅ケ崎駅}}. From the north exit, take a bus and alight at "Tsurumine Elementary School," then walk 1 minute. == References == {{Cite source help}} {{Reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|Minamoto no Yoriyoshi|ja|源頼義}} {{Cite source|date=June 28, 2018}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name=Tsuruoka Hachimangū | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image=[[File:GrfKanagawakenChigasakishi-Turuminehachimangu-01.jpg|270px|Tsuruoka Hachimangū Haiden, Chigasaki City]]
Haiden
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=270|coord={{coord|35|20|18.3|N|139|23|28.3|E}}}} | location=〒 253-0086
{{ill|Kanagawa Prefecture|ja|神奈川県}}{{ill|Chigasaki|ja|茅ヶ崎市}} Hamano-go 462 | ISO=JP-14 | Latitude degrees = 35|Latitude minutes = 20|Latitude seconds = 18.38 | Longitude degrees=139|Longitude minutes=23|Longitude seconds=28.30 | deity={{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|応神天皇}}
{{ill|Emperor Nintoku|ja|仁徳天皇}}
{{ill|Sazuka Daimyōjin|ja|佐塚大神}}
{{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|菅原道真}} | Shrine rank=Former {{ill|Village shrine|ja|村社}}, Former {{ill|District shrine|ja|郷社}} | Foundation={{ill|Chōgen 3|ja|長元3年}} ({{ill|1030|ja|1030年}}) | Main Hall=Nagare-zukuri | Alternate name=Tsuruoka Hachiman Shrine | festival={{ill|September 15|ja|9月15日}} | Rituals={{ill|Hamaori Festival|ja|浜降祭}} ({{ill|Marine Day|ja|海の日}}) | Map=Japan Kanagawa }} '''Tsuruoka Hachimangū''' (鶴嶺八幡宮) is a {{ill|shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Chigasaki|ja|茅ヶ崎市}}, {{ill|Kanagawa Prefecture|ja|神奈川県}}. It is also known as Tsuruoka Hachiman Shrine. Renowned as the center of Hachiman worship in {{ill|Sagami Province|ja|相模国}}, the shrine has been revered since ancient times as a significant site of {{ill|Hachiman faith|ja|八幡信仰}}.Cited by the Kanagawa Prefecture Shrine Office According to the shrine, it was the first {{ill|clan shrine|ja|氏社}} established by the {{ill|Genji|ja|源氏}} when they expanded into the Kanto region. == History == The shrine was founded in {{ill|Chōgen 3 (1030)|ja|長元3年（1030年）}}, during the {{ill|Subjugation of Shimōsa|ja|下総の乱}} by {{ill|Minamoto no Yoriyoshi|ja|源頼義}}, who hailed from {{ill|Kawachi Province|ja|河内国}} (modern-day {{ill|Habikino|ja|羽曳野市}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}). Yoriyoshi enshrined {{ill|Iwashimizu Hachimangū|ja|石清水八幡宮}} at the site under the name "Kaijima Hachimangū" (another theory suggests it was {{ill|Usa Hachimangū|ja|宇佐八幡宮}}). Later, the Genji moved it to its current location. In {{ill|Tenki 3 (1055)|ja|天喜3年（1055年）}}, during the {{ill|Earlier Nine Years' War|ja|前九年の役}}, Yoriyoshi prayed at the shrine and achieved victory. To honor this, he established the {{ill|Tsurugaoka Hachimangū|lt=Original Hachiman Shrine|ja|元八幡}} in {{ill|Yuigahama|ja|由比ヶ浜}}, Kamakura, in {{ill|Kōhei 6 (1063)|ja|康平6年（1063年）}}. In {{ill|Ōtoku 2 (1085)|ja|応徳2年（1085年）}}, Minamoto no Yoshiie, Yoriyoshi's heir, dedicated the shrine in Hamano-go as "Tsuruoka Hachimangū." Subsequently, Tsuruoka Hachimangū gained prominence, receiving land and monetary support from {{ill|Minamoto no Yoritomo|ja|源頼朝}} and others. Over time, it became the center for the famous {{ill|Hamaori Festival|ja|浜降祭}} and absorbed nearby shrines such as Sazuka Daimyōjin. == Deities == * {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|応神天皇}} * {{ill|Emperor Nintoku|ja|仁徳天皇}} * Sazuka Daimyōjin (associated with {{ill|Ōyamazumi Shrine|ja|大山祇神社}}) * {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|菅原道真}} (from Tsurumine Tenmangū) == Annual Events == * {{ill|Hamaori Festival|ja|浜降祭}} (Marine Day) * {{ill|Misorogi Ritual|ja|禊の神事}} (June 29, formerly held on July 15) * {{ill|Reitaisai|ja|例大祭}} (September 15) == Grounds == ; '''Great Ginkgo Tree''' : A 950-year-old tree said to have been planted by Minamoto no Yoshiie to pray for victory. ; '''Miyagane (Shrine Bell)''' : The shrine bell, originally installed in {{ill|Enpō 8 (1680)|ja|延宝8年（1680年）}}, was reconstructed after the war in 1965 as a "peace bell." === Sub-Shrines === * '''Shōnan Awashima Shrine''' — Dedicated to {{ill|Sukunahikona|ja|少彦名命}} and {{ill|Awashima-no-Kami|ja|淡島神}}. * '''Tsurumine Inari Shrine''' — Dedicated to {{ill|Ukanomitama|ja|宇迦之御魂}}. == Access == Take the JR Tōkaidō Main Line to {{ill|Chigasaki Station|ja|茅ケ崎駅}}. From the north exit, take a bus and alight at "Tsurumine Elementary School," then walk 1 minute. == References == {{Cite source help}} {{Reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|Minamoto no Yoriyoshi|ja|源頼義}} {{Cite source|date=June 28, 2018}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name=Tsuruoka Hachimangū | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image=[[File:GrfKanagawakenChigasakishi-Turuminehachimangu-01.jpg|270px|Tsuruoka Hachimangū Haiden, Chigasaki City]]
Haiden
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=270|coord={{coord|35|20|18.3|N|139|23|28.3|E}}}} | location=〒 253-0086
{{ill|Kanagawa Prefecture|ja|神奈川県}}{{ill|Chigasaki|ja|茅ヶ崎市}} Hamano-go 462 | ISO=JP-14 | Latitude degrees = 35|Latitude minutes = 20|Latitude seconds = 18.38 | Longitude degrees=139|Longitude minutes=23|Longitude seconds=28.30 | deity={{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|応神天皇}}
{{ill|Emperor Nintoku|ja|仁徳天皇}}
{{ill|Sazuka Daimyōjin|ja|佐塚大神}}
{{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|菅原道真}} | Shrine rank=Former {{ill|Village shrine|ja|村社}}, Former {{ill|District shrine|ja|郷社}} | Foundation={{ill|Chōgen 3|ja|長元3年}} ({{ill|1030|ja|1030年}}) | Main Hall=Nagare-zukuri | Alternate name=Tsuruoka Hachiman Shrine | festival={{ill|September 15|ja|9月15日}} | Rituals={{ill|Hamaori Festival|ja|浜降祭}} ({{ill|Marine Day|ja|海の日}}) | Map=Japan Kanagawa }} '''Tsuruoka Hachimangū''' (鶴嶺八幡宮) is a {{ill|shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Chigasaki|ja|茅ヶ崎市}}, {{ill|Kanagawa Prefecture|ja|神奈川県}}. It is also known as Tsuruoka Hachiman Shrine. Renowned as the center of Hachiman worship in {{ill|Sagami Province|ja|相模国}}, the shrine has been revered since ancient times as a significant site of {{ill|Hachiman faith|ja|八幡信仰}}.Cited by the Kanagawa Prefecture Shrine Office According to the shrine, it was the first {{ill|clan shrine|ja|氏社}} established by the {{ill|Genji|ja|源氏}} when they expanded into the Kanto region. == History == The shrine was founded in {{ill|Chōgen 3 (1030)|ja|長元3年（1030年）}}, during the {{ill|Subjugation of Shimōsa|ja|下総の乱}} by {{ill|Minamoto no Yoriyoshi|ja|源頼義}}, who hailed from {{ill|Kawachi Province|ja|河内国}} (modern-day {{ill|Habikino|ja|羽曳野市}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}). Yoriyoshi enshrined {{ill|Iwashimizu Hachimangū|ja|石清水八幡宮}} at the site under the name "Kaijima Hachimangū" (another theory suggests it was {{ill|Usa Hachimangū|ja|宇佐八幡宮}}). Later, the Genji moved it to its current location. In {{ill|Tenki 3 (1055)|ja|天喜3年（1055年）}}, during the {{ill|Earlier Nine Years' War|ja|前九年の役}}, Yoriyoshi prayed at the shrine and achieved victory. To honor this, he established the {{ill|Tsurugaoka Hachimangū|lt=Original Hachiman Shrine|ja|元八幡}} in {{ill|Yuigahama|ja|由比ヶ浜}}, Kamakura, in {{ill|Kōhei 6 (1063)|ja|康平6年（1063年）}}. In {{ill|Ōtoku 2 (1085)|ja|応徳2年（1085年）}}, Minamoto no Yoshiie, Yoriyoshi's heir, dedicated the shrine in Hamano-go as "Tsuruoka Hachimangū." Subsequently, Tsuruoka Hachimangū gained prominence, receiving land and monetary support from {{ill|Minamoto no Yoritomo|ja|源頼朝}} and others. Over time, it became the center for the famous {{ill|Hamaori Festival|ja|浜降祭}} and absorbed nearby shrines such as Sazuka Daimyōjin. == Deities == * {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|応神天皇}} * {{ill|Emperor Nintoku|ja|仁徳天皇}} * Sazuka Daimyōjin (associated with {{ill|Ōyamazumi Shrine|ja|大山祇神社}}) * {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|菅原道真}} (from Tsurumine Tenmangū) == Annual Events == * {{ill|Hamaori Festival|ja|浜降祭}} (Marine Day) * {{ill|Misorogi Ritual|ja|禊の神事}} (June 29, formerly held on July 15) * {{ill|Reitaisai|ja|例大祭}} (September 15) == Grounds == ; '''Great Ginkgo Tree''' : A 950-year-old tree said to have been planted by Minamoto no Yoshiie to pray for victory. ; '''Miyagane (Shrine Bell)''' : The shrine bell, originally installed in {{ill|Enpō 8 (1680)|ja|延宝8年（1680年）}}, was reconstructed after the war in 1965 as a "peace bell." === Sub-Shrines === * '''Shōnan Awashima Shrine''' — Dedicated to {{ill|Sukunahikona|ja|少彦名命}} and {{ill|Awashima-no-Kami|ja|淡島神}}. * '''Tsurumine Inari Shrine''' — Dedicated to {{ill|Ukanomitama|ja|宇迦之御魂}}. == Access == Take the JR Tōkaidō Main Line to {{ill|Chigasaki Station|ja|茅ケ崎駅}}. From the north exit, take a bus and alight at "Tsurumine Elementary School," then walk 1 minute. == References == {{Cite source help}} {{Reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|Minamoto no Yoriyoshi|ja|源頼義}} {{Cite source|date=June 28, 2018}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name=Tsuruoka Hachimangū | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image=[[File:GrfKanagawakenChigasakishi-Turuminehachimangu-01.jpg|270px|Tsuruoka Hachimangū Haiden, Chigasaki City]]
Haiden
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=270|coord={{coord|35|20|18.3|N|139|23|28.3|E}}}} | location=〒 253-0086
{{ill|Kanagawa Prefecture|ja|神奈川県}}{{ill|Chigasaki|ja|茅ヶ崎市}} Hamano-go 462 | ISO=JP-14 | Latitude degrees = 35|Latitude minutes = 20|Latitude seconds = 18.38 | Longitude degrees=139|Longitude minutes=23|Longitude seconds=28.30 | deity={{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|応神天皇}}
{{ill|Emperor Nintoku|ja|仁徳天皇}}
{{ill|Sazuka Daimyōjin|ja|佐塚大神}}
{{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|菅原道真}} | Shrine rank=Former {{ill|Village shrine|ja|村社}}, Former {{ill|District shrine|ja|郷社}} | Foundation={{ill|Chōgen 3|ja|長元3年}} ({{ill|1030|ja|1030年}}) | Main Hall=Nagare-zukuri | Alternate name=Tsuruoka Hachiman Shrine | festival={{ill|September 15|ja|9月15日}} | Rituals={{ill|Hamaori Festival|ja|浜降祭}} ({{ill|Marine Day|ja|海の日}}) | Map=Japan Kanagawa }} '''Tsuruoka Hachimangū''' (鶴嶺八幡宮) is a {{ill|shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Chigasaki|ja|茅ヶ崎市}}, {{ill|Kanagawa Prefecture|ja|神奈川県}}. It is also known as Tsuruoka Hachiman Shrine. Renowned as the center of Hachiman worship in {{ill|Sagami Province|ja|相模国}}, the shrine has been revered since ancient times as a significant site of {{ill|Hachiman faith|ja|八幡信仰}}.Cited by the Kanagawa Prefecture Shrine Office According to the shrine, it was the first {{ill|clan shrine|ja|氏社}} established by the {{ill|Genji|ja|源氏}} when they expanded into the Kanto region. == History == The shrine was founded in {{ill|Chōgen 3 (1030)|ja|長元3年（1030年）}}, during the {{ill|Subjugation of Shimōsa|ja|下総の乱}} by {{ill|Minamoto no Yoriyoshi|ja|源頼義}}, who hailed from {{ill|Kawachi Province|ja|河内国}} (modern-day {{ill|Habikino|ja|羽曳野市}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}). Yoriyoshi enshrined {{ill|Iwashimizu Hachimangū|ja|石清水八幡宮}} at the site under the name "Kaijima Hachimangū" (another theory suggests it was {{ill|Usa Hachimangū|ja|宇佐八幡宮}}). Later, the Genji moved it to its current location. In {{ill|Tenki 3 (1055)|ja|天喜3年（1055年）}}, during the {{ill|Earlier Nine Years' War|ja|前九年の役}}, Yoriyoshi prayed at the shrine and achieved victory. To honor this, he established the {{ill|Tsurugaoka Hachimangū|lt=Original Hachiman Shrine|ja|元八幡}} in {{ill|Yuigahama|ja|由比ヶ浜}}, Kamakura, in {{ill|Kōhei 6 (1063)|ja|康平6年（1063年）}}. In {{ill|Ōtoku 2 (1085)|ja|応徳2年（1085年）}}, Minamoto no Yoshiie, Yoriyoshi's heir, dedicated the shrine in Hamano-go as "Tsuruoka Hachimangū." Subsequently, Tsuruoka Hachimangū gained prominence, receiving land and monetary support from {{ill|Minamoto no Yoritomo|ja|源頼朝}} and others. Over time, it became the center for the famous {{ill|Hamaori Festival|ja|浜降祭}} and absorbed nearby shrines such as Sazuka Daimyōjin. == Deities == * {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|応神天皇}} * {{ill|Emperor Nintoku|ja|仁徳天皇}} * Sazuka Daimyōjin (associated with {{ill|Ōyamazumi Shrine|ja|大山祇神社}}) * {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|菅原道真}} (from Tsurumine Tenmangū) == Annual Events == * {{ill|Hamaori Festival|ja|浜降祭}} (Marine Day) * {{ill|Misorogi Ritual|ja|禊の神事}} (June 29, formerly held on July 15) * {{ill|Reitaisai|ja|例大祭}} (September 15) == Grounds == ; '''Great Ginkgo Tree''' : A 950-year-old tree said to have been planted by Minamoto no Yoshiie to pray for victory. ; '''Miyagane (Shrine Bell)''' : The shrine bell, originally installed in {{ill|Enpō 8 (1680)|ja|延宝8年（1680年）}}, was reconstructed after the war in 1965 as a "peace bell." === Sub-Shrines === * '''Shōnan Awashima Shrine''' — Dedicated to {{ill|Sukunahikona|ja|少彦名命}} and {{ill|Awashima-no-Kami|ja|淡島神}}. * '''Tsurumine Inari Shrine''' — Dedicated to {{ill|Ukanomitama|ja|宇迦之御魂}}. == Access == Take the JR Tōkaidō Main Line to {{ill|Chigasaki Station|ja|茅ケ崎駅}}. From the north exit, take a bus and alight at "Tsurumine Elementary School," then walk 1 minute. == References == {{Cite source help}} {{Reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|Minamoto no Yoriyoshi|ja|源頼義}} {{Cite source|date=June 28, 2018}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name=Tsuruoka Hachimangū | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image=[[File:GrfKanagawakenChigasakishi-Turuminehachimangu-01.jpg|270px|Tsuruoka Hachimangū Haiden, Chigasaki City]]
Haiden
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=270|coord={{coord|35|20|18.3|N|139|23|28.3|E}}}} | location=〒 253-0086
{{ill|Kanagawa Prefecture|ja|神奈川県}}{{ill|Chigasaki|ja|茅ヶ崎市}} Hamano-go 462 | ISO=JP-14 | Latitude degrees = 35|Latitude minutes = 20|Latitude seconds = 18.38 | Longitude degrees=139|Longitude minutes=23|Longitude seconds=28.30 | deity={{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|応神天皇}}
{{ill|Emperor Nintoku|ja|仁徳天皇}}
{{ill|Sazuka Daimyōjin|ja|佐塚大神}}
{{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|菅原道真}} | Shrine rank=Former {{ill|Village shrine|ja|村社}}, Former {{ill|District shrine|ja|郷社}} | Foundation={{ill|Chōgen 3|ja|長元3年}} ({{ill|1030|ja|1030年}}) | Main Hall=Nagare-zukuri | Alternate name=Tsuruoka Hachiman Shrine | festival={{ill|September 15|ja|9月15日}} | Rituals={{ill|Hamaori Festival|ja|浜降祭}} ({{ill|Marine Day|ja|海の日}}) | Map=Japan Kanagawa }} '''Tsuruoka Hachimangū''' (鶴嶺八幡宮) is a {{ill|shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Chigasaki|ja|茅ヶ崎市}}, {{ill|Kanagawa Prefecture|ja|神奈川県}}. It is also known as Tsuruoka Hachiman Shrine. Renowned as the center of Hachiman worship in {{ill|Sagami Province|ja|相模国}}, the shrine has been revered since ancient times as a significant site of {{ill|Hachiman faith|ja|八幡信仰}}.Cited by the Kanagawa Prefecture Shrine Office According to the shrine, it was the first {{ill|clan shrine|ja|氏社}} established by the {{ill|Genji|ja|源氏}} when they expanded into the Kanto region. == History == The shrine was founded in {{ill|Chōgen 3 (1030)|ja|長元3年（1030年）}}, during the {{ill|Subjugation of Shimōsa|ja|下総の乱}} by {{ill|Minamoto no Yoriyoshi|ja|源頼義}}, who hailed from {{ill|Kawachi Province|ja|河内国}} (modern-day {{ill|Habikino|ja|羽曳野市}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}). Yoriyoshi enshrined {{ill|Iwashimizu Hachimangū|ja|石清水八幡宮}} at the site under the name "Kaijima Hachimangū" (another theory suggests it was {{ill|Usa Hachimangū|ja|宇佐八幡宮}}). Later, the Genji moved it to its current location. In {{ill|Tenki 3 (1055)|ja|天喜3年（1055年）}}, during the {{ill|Earlier Nine Years' War|ja|前九年の役}}, Yoriyoshi prayed at the shrine and achieved victory. To honor this, he established the {{ill|Tsurugaoka Hachimangū|lt=Original Hachiman Shrine|ja|元八幡}} in {{ill|Yuigahama|ja|由比ヶ浜}}, Kamakura, in {{ill|Kōhei 6 (1063)|ja|康平6年（1063年）}}. In {{ill|Ōtoku 2 (1085)|ja|応徳2年（1085年）}}, Minamoto no Yoshiie, Yoriyoshi's heir, dedicated the shrine in Hamano-go as "Tsuruoka Hachimangū." Subsequently, Tsuruoka Hachimangū gained prominence, receiving land and monetary support from {{ill|Minamoto no Yoritomo|ja|源頼朝}} and others. Over time, it became the center for the famous {{ill|Hamaori Festival|ja|浜降祭}} and absorbed nearby shrines such as Sazuka Daimyōjin. == Deities == * {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|応神天皇}} * {{ill|Emperor Nintoku|ja|仁徳天皇}} * Sazuka Daimyōjin (associated with {{ill|Ōyamazumi Shrine|ja|大山祇神社}}) * {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|菅原道真}} (from Tsurumine Tenmangū) == Annual Events == * {{ill|Hamaori Festival|ja|浜降祭}} (Marine Day) * {{ill|Misorogi Ritual|ja|禊の神事}} (June 29, formerly held on July 15) * {{ill|Reitaisai|ja|例大祭}} (September 15) == Grounds == ; '''Great Ginkgo Tree''' : A 950-year-old tree said to have been planted by Minamoto no Yoshiie to pray for victory. ; '''Miyagane (Shrine Bell)''' : The shrine bell, originally installed in {{ill|Enpō 8 (1680)|ja|延宝8年（1680年）}}, was reconstructed after the war in 1965 as a "peace bell." === Sub-Shrines === * '''Shōnan Awashima Shrine''' — Dedicated to {{ill|Sukunahikona|ja|少彦名命}} and {{ill|Awashima-no-Kami|ja|淡島神}}. * '''Tsurumine Inari Shrine''' — Dedicated to {{ill|Ukanomitama|ja|宇迦之御魂}}. == Access == Take the JR Tōkaidō Main Line to {{ill|Chigasaki Station|ja|茅ケ崎駅}}. From the north exit, take a bus and alight at "Tsurumine Elementary School," then walk 1 minute. == References == {{Cite source help}} {{Reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|Minamoto no Yoriyoshi|ja|源頼義}} {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|菅原道真}} | Shrine rank=Former {{ill|Village shrine|ja|村社}}, Former {{ill|District shrine|ja|郷社}} | Foundation={{ill|Chōgen 3|ja|長元3年}} ({{ill|1030|ja|1030年}}) | Main Hall=Nagare-zukuri | Alternate name=Tsuruoka Hachiman Shrine | festival={{ill|September 15|ja|9月15日}} | Rituals={{ill|Hamaori Festival|ja|浜降祭}} ({{ill|Marine Day|ja|海の日}}) | Map=Japan Kanagawa }} '''Tsuruoka Hachimangū''' (鶴嶺八幡宮) is a {{ill|shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Chigasaki|ja|茅ヶ崎市}}, {{ill|Kanagawa Prefecture|ja|神奈川県}}. It is also known as Tsuruoka Hachiman Shrine. Renowned as the center of Hachiman worship in {{ill|Sagami Province|ja|相模国}}, the shrine has been revered since ancient times as a significant site of {{ill|Hachiman faith|ja|八幡信仰}}.Cited by the Kanagawa Prefecture Shrine Office According to the shrine, it was the first {{ill|clan shrine|ja|氏社}} established by the {{ill|Genji|ja|源氏}} when they expanded into the Kanto region. == History == The shrine was founded in {{ill|Chōgen 3 (1030)|ja|長元3年（1030年）}}, during the {{ill|Subjugation of Shimōsa|ja|下総の乱}} by {{ill|Minamoto no Yoriyoshi|ja|源頼義}}, who hailed from {{ill|Kawachi Province|ja|河内国}} (modern-day {{ill|Habikino|ja|羽曳野市}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}). Yoriyoshi enshrined {{ill|Iwashimizu Hachimangū|ja|石清水八幡宮}} at the site under the name "Kaijima Hachimangū" (another theory suggests it was {{ill|Usa Hachimangū|ja|宇佐八幡宮}}). Later, the Genji moved it to its current location. In {{ill|Tenki 3 (1055)|ja|天喜3年（1055年）}}, during the {{ill|Earlier Nine Years' War|ja|前九年の役}}, Yoriyoshi prayed at the shrine and achieved victory. To honor this, he established the {{ill|Tsurugaoka Hachimangū|lt=Original Hachiman Shrine|ja|元八幡}} in {{ill|Yuigahama|ja|由比ヶ浜}}, Kamakura, in {{ill|Kōhei 6 (1063)|ja|康平6年（1063年）}}. In {{ill|Ōtoku 2 (1085)|ja|応徳2年（1085年）}}, Minamoto no Yoshiie, Yoriyoshi's heir, dedicated the shrine in Hamano-go as "Tsuruoka Hachimangū." Subsequently, Tsuruoka Hachimangū gained prominence, receiving land and monetary support from {{ill|Minamoto no Yoritomo|ja|源頼朝}} and others. Over time, it became the center for the famous {{ill|Hamaori Festival|ja|浜降祭}} and absorbed nearby shrines such as Sazuka Daimyōjin. == Deities == * {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|応神天皇}} * {{ill|Emperor Nintoku|ja|仁徳天皇}} * Sazuka Daimyōjin (associated with {{ill|Ōyamazumi Shrine|ja|大山祇神社}}) * {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|菅原道真}} (from Tsurumine Tenmangū) == Annual Events == * {{ill|Hamaori Festival|ja|浜降祭}} (Marine Day) * {{ill|Misorogi Ritual|ja|禊の神事}} (June 29, formerly held on July 15) * {{ill|Reitaisai|ja|例大祭}} (September 15) == Grounds == ; '''Great Ginkgo Tree''' : A 950-year-old tree said to have been planted by Minamoto no Yoshiie to pray for victory. ; '''Miyagane (Shrine Bell)''' : The shrine bell, originally installed in {{ill|Enpō 8 (1680)|ja|延宝8年（1680年）}}, was reconstructed after the war in 1965 as a "peace bell." === Sub-Shrines === * '''Shōnan Awashima Shrine''' — Dedicated to {{ill|Sukunahikona|ja|少彦名命}} and {{ill|Awashima-no-Kami|ja|淡島神}}. * '''Tsurumine Inari Shrine''' — Dedicated to {{ill|Ukanomitama|ja|宇迦之御魂}}. == Access == Take the JR Tōkaidō Main Line to {{ill|Chigasaki Station|ja|茅ケ崎駅}}. From the north exit, take a bus and alight at "Tsurumine Elementary School," then walk 1 minute. == References == {{Cite source help}} {{Reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|Minamoto no Yoriyoshi|ja|源頼義}} {{Cite source|date=June 28, 2018}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name=Tsuruoka Hachimangū | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image=[[File:GrfKanagawakenChigasakishi-Turuminehachimangu-01.jpg|270px|Tsuruoka Hachimangū Haiden, Chigasaki City]]
Haiden
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=270|coord={{coord|35|20|18.3|N|139|23|28.3|E}}}} | location=〒 253-0086
{{ill|Kanagawa Prefecture|ja|神奈川県}}{{ill|Chigasaki|ja|茅ヶ崎市}} Hamano-go 462 | ISO=JP-14 | Latitude degrees = 35|Latitude minutes = 20|Latitude seconds = 18.38 | Longitude degrees=139|Longitude minutes=23|Longitude seconds=28.30 | deity={{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|応神天皇}}
{{ill|Emperor Nintoku|ja|仁徳天皇}}
{{ill|Sazuka Daimyōjin|ja|佐塚大神}}
{{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|菅原道真}} | Shrine rank=Former {{ill|Village shrine|ja|村社}}, Former {{ill|District shrine|ja|郷社}} | Foundation={{ill|Chōgen 3|ja|長元3年}} ({{ill|1030|ja|1030年}}) | Main Hall=Nagare-zukuri | Alternate name=Tsuruoka Hachiman Shrine | festival={{ill|September 15|ja|9月15日}} | Rituals={{ill|Hamaori Festival|ja|浜降祭}} ({{ill|Marine Day|ja|海の日}}) | Map=Japan Kanagawa }} '''Tsuruoka Hachimangū''' (鶴嶺八幡宮) is a {{ill|shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Chigasaki|ja|茅ヶ崎市}}, {{ill|Kanagawa Prefecture|ja|神奈川県}}. It is also known as Tsuruoka Hachiman Shrine. Renowned as the center of Hachiman worship in {{ill|Sagami Province|ja|相模国}}, the shrine has been revered since ancient times as a significant site of {{ill|Hachiman faith|ja|八幡信仰}}.Cited by the Kanagawa Prefecture Shrine Office According to the shrine, it was the first {{ill|clan shrine|ja|氏社}} established by the {{ill|Genji|ja|源氏}} when they expanded into the Kanto region. == History == The shrine was founded in {{ill|Chōgen 3 (1030)|ja|長元3年（1030年）}}, during the {{ill|Subjugation of Shimōsa|ja|下総の乱}} by {{ill|Minamoto no Yoriyoshi|ja|源頼義}}, who hailed from {{ill|Kawachi Province|ja|河内国}} (modern-day {{ill|Habikino|ja|羽曳野市}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}). Yoriyoshi enshrined {{ill|Iwashimizu Hachimangū|ja|石清水八幡宮}} at the site under the name "Kaijima Hachimangū" (another theory suggests it was {{ill|Usa Hachimangū|ja|宇佐八幡宮}}). Later, the Genji moved it to its current location. In {{ill|Tenki 3 (1055)|ja|天喜3年（1055年）}}, during the {{ill|Earlier Nine Years' War|ja|前九年の役}}, Yoriyoshi prayed at the shrine and achieved victory. To honor this, he established the {{ill|Tsurugaoka Hachimangū|lt=Original Hachiman Shrine|ja|元八幡}} in {{ill|Yuigahama|ja|由比ヶ浜}}, Kamakura, in {{ill|Kōhei 6 (1063)|ja|康平6年（1063年）}}. In {{ill|Ōtoku 2 (1085)|ja|応徳2年（1085年）}}, Minamoto no Yoshiie, Yoriyoshi's heir, dedicated the shrine in Hamano-go as "Tsuruoka Hachimangū." Subsequently, Tsuruoka Hachimangū gained prominence, receiving land and monetary support from {{ill|Minamoto no Yoritomo|ja|源頼朝}} and others. Over time, it became the center for the famous {{ill|Hamaori Festival|ja|浜降祭}} and absorbed nearby shrines such as Sazuka Daimyōjin. == Deities == * {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|応神天皇}} * {{ill|Emperor Nintoku|ja|仁徳天皇}} * Sazuka Daimyōjin (associated with {{ill|Ōyamazumi Shrine|ja|大山祇神社}}) * {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|菅原道真}} (from Tsurumine Tenmangū) == Annual Events == * {{ill|Hamaori Festival|ja|浜降祭}} (Marine Day) * {{ill|Misorogi Ritual|ja|禊の神事}} (June 29, formerly held on July 15) * {{ill|Reitaisai|ja|例大祭}} (September 15) == Grounds == ; '''Great Ginkgo Tree''' : A 950-year-old tree said to have been planted by Minamoto no Yoshiie to pray for victory. ; '''Miyagane (Shrine Bell)''' : The shrine bell, originally installed in {{ill|Enpō 8 (1680)|ja|延宝8年（1680年）}}, was reconstructed after the war in 1965 as a "peace bell." === Sub-Shrines === * '''Shōnan Awashima Shrine''' — Dedicated to {{ill|Sukunahikona|ja|少彦名命}} and {{ill|Awashima-no-Kami|ja|淡島神}}. * '''Tsurumine Inari Shrine''' — Dedicated to {{ill|Ukanomitama|ja|宇迦之御魂}}. == Access == Take the JR Tōkaidō Main Line to {{ill|Chigasaki Station|ja|茅ケ崎駅}}. From the north exit, take a bus and alight at "Tsurumine Elementary School," then walk 1 minute. == References == {{Cite source help}} {{Reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|Minamoto no Yoriyoshi|ja|源頼義}} revision.comment: Text replacement - "菅原道真" to "Sugawara no Michizane"
revision.text:
{{Cite source|date=June 28, 2018}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name=Tsuruoka Hachimangū | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image=[[File:GrfKanagawakenChigasakishi-Turuminehachimangu-01.jpg|270px|Tsuruoka Hachimangū Haiden, Chigasaki City]]
Haiden
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=270|coord={{coord|35|20|18.3|N|139|23|28.3|E}}}} | location=〒 253-0086
{{ill|Kanagawa Prefecture|ja|神奈川県}}{{ill|Chigasaki|ja|茅ヶ崎市}} Hamano-go 462 | ISO=JP-14 | Latitude degrees = 35|Latitude minutes = 20|Latitude seconds = 18.38 | Longitude degrees=139|Longitude minutes=23|Longitude seconds=28.30 | deity={{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|応神天皇}}
{{ill|Emperor Nintoku|ja|仁徳天皇}}
{{ill|Sazuka Daimyōjin|ja|佐塚大神}}
{{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|Sugawara no Michizane}} | Shrine rank=Former {{ill|Village shrine|ja|村社}}, Former {{ill|District shrine|ja|郷社}} | Foundation={{ill|Chōgen 3|ja|長元3年}} ({{ill|1030|ja|1030年}}) | Main Hall=Nagare-zukuri | Alternate name=Tsuruoka Hachiman Shrine | festival={{ill|September 15|ja|9月15日}} | Rituals={{ill|Hamaori Festival|ja|浜降祭}} ({{ill|Marine Day|ja|海の日}}) | Map=Japan Kanagawa }} '''Tsuruoka Hachimangū''' (鶴嶺八幡宮) is a {{ill|shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Chigasaki|ja|茅ヶ崎市}}, {{ill|Kanagawa Prefecture|ja|神奈川県}}. It is also known as Tsuruoka Hachiman Shrine. Renowned as the center of Hachiman worship in {{ill|Sagami Province|ja|相模国}}, the shrine has been revered since ancient times as a significant site of {{ill|Hachiman faith|ja|八幡信仰}}.Cited by the Kanagawa Prefecture Shrine Office According to the shrine, it was the first {{ill|clan shrine|ja|氏社}} established by the {{ill|Genji|ja|源氏}} when they expanded into the Kanto region. == History == The shrine was founded in {{ill|Chōgen 3 (1030)|ja|長元3年（1030年）}}, during the {{ill|Subjugation of Shimōsa|ja|下総の乱}} by {{ill|Minamoto no Yoriyoshi|ja|源頼義}}, who hailed from {{ill|Kawachi Province|ja|河内国}} (modern-day {{ill|Habikino|ja|羽曳野市}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}). Yoriyoshi enshrined {{ill|Iwashimizu Hachimangū|ja|石清水八幡宮}} at the site under the name "Kaijima Hachimangū" (another theory suggests it was {{ill|Usa Hachimangū|ja|宇佐八幡宮}}). Later, the Genji moved it to its current location. In {{ill|Tenki 3 (1055)|ja|天喜3年（1055年）}}, during the {{ill|Earlier Nine Years' War|ja|前九年の役}}, Yoriyoshi prayed at the shrine and achieved victory. To honor this, he established the {{ill|Tsurugaoka Hachimangū|lt=Original Hachiman Shrine|ja|元八幡}} in {{ill|Yuigahama|ja|由比ヶ浜}}, Kamakura, in {{ill|Kōhei 6 (1063)|ja|康平6年（1063年）}}. In {{ill|Ōtoku 2 (1085)|ja|応徳2年（1085年）}}, Minamoto no Yoshiie, Yoriyoshi's heir, dedicated the shrine in Hamano-go as "Tsuruoka Hachimangū." Subsequently, Tsuruoka Hachimangū gained prominence, receiving land and monetary support from {{ill|Minamoto no Yoritomo|ja|源頼朝}} and others. Over time, it became the center for the famous {{ill|Hamaori Festival|ja|浜降祭}} and absorbed nearby shrines such as Sazuka Daimyōjin. == Deities == * {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|応神天皇}} * {{ill|Emperor Nintoku|ja|仁徳天皇}} * Sazuka Daimyōjin (associated with {{ill|Ōyamazumi Shrine|ja|大山祇神社}}) * {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|Sugawara no Michizane}} (from Tsurumine Tenmangū) == Annual Events == * {{ill|Hamaori Festival|ja|浜降祭}} (Marine Day) * {{ill|Misorogi Ritual|ja|禊の神事}} (June 29, formerly held on July 15) * {{ill|Reitaisai|ja|例大祭}} (September 15) == Grounds == ; '''Great Ginkgo Tree''' : A 950-year-old tree said to have been planted by Minamoto no Yoshiie to pray for victory. ; '''Miyagane (Shrine Bell)''' : The shrine bell, originally installed in {{ill|Enpō 8 (1680)|ja|延宝8年（1680年）}}, was reconstructed after the war in 1965 as a "peace bell." === Sub-Shrines === * '''Shōnan Awashima Shrine''' — Dedicated to {{ill|Sukunahikona|ja|少彦名命}} and {{ill|Awashima-no-Kami|ja|淡島神}}. * '''Tsurumine Inari Shrine''' — Dedicated to {{ill|Ukanomitama|ja|宇迦之御魂}}. == Access == Take the JR Tōkaidō Main Line to {{ill|Chigasaki Station|ja|茅ケ崎駅}}. From the north exit, take a bus and alight at "Tsurumine Elementary School," then walk 1 minute. == References == {{Cite source help}} {{Reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|Minamoto no Yoriyoshi|ja|源頼義}} * {{ill|Iwashimizu Hachimangū|ja|石清水八幡宮}} * {{ill|Hamaori Festival|ja|浜降祭}} [[Category:completion tier 2]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Tsurumine Hachiman Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Tsurumine revision.text:
{{Cite source|date=June 28, 2018}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name=Tsuruoka Hachimangū | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image=[[File:GrfKanagawakenChigasakishi-Turuminehachimangu-01.jpg|270px|Tsuruoka Hachimangū Haiden, Chigasaki City]]
Haiden
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=270|coord={{coord|35|20|18.3|N|139|23|28.3|E}}}} | location=〒 253-0086
{{ill|Kanagawa Prefecture|ja|神奈川県}}{{ill|Chigasaki|ja|茅ヶ崎市}} Hamano-go 462 | ISO=JP-14 | Latitude degrees = 35|Latitude minutes = 20|Latitude seconds = 18.38 | Longitude degrees=139|Longitude minutes=23|Longitude seconds=28.30 | deity={{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|応神天皇}}
{{ill|Emperor Nintoku|ja|仁徳天皇}}
{{ill|Sazuka Daimyōjin|ja|佐塚大神}}
{{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|Sugawara no Michizane}} | Shrine rank=Former {{ill|Village shrine|ja|村社}}, Former {{ill|District shrine|ja|郷社}} | Foundation={{ill|Chōgen 3|ja|長元3年}} ({{ill|1030|ja|1030年}}) | Main Hall=Nagare-zukuri | Alternate name=Tsuruoka Hachiman Shrine | festival={{ill|September 15|ja|9月15日}} | Rituals={{ill|Hamaori Festival|ja|浜降祭}} ({{ill|Marine Day|ja|海の日}}) | Map=Japan Kanagawa }} '''Tsuruoka Hachimangū''' (鶴嶺八幡宮) is a {{ill|shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Chigasaki|ja|茅ヶ崎市}}, {{ill|Kanagawa Prefecture|ja|神奈川県}}. It is also known as Tsuruoka Hachiman Shrine. Renowned as the center of Hachiman worship in {{ill|Sagami Province|ja|相模国}}, the shrine has been revered since ancient times as a significant site of {{ill|Hachiman faith|ja|八幡信仰}}.Cited by the Kanagawa Prefecture Shrine Office According to the shrine, it was the first {{ill|clan shrine|ja|氏社}} established by the {{ill|Genji|ja|源氏}} when they expanded into the Kanto region. == History == The shrine was founded in {{ill|Chōgen 3 (1030)|ja|長元3年（1030年）}}, during the {{ill|Subjugation of Shimōsa|ja|下総の乱}} by {{ill|Minamoto no Yoriyoshi|ja|源頼義}}, who hailed from {{ill|Kawachi Province|ja|河内国}} (modern-day {{ill|Habikino|ja|羽曳野市}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}). Yoriyoshi enshrined {{ill|Iwashimizu Hachimangū|ja|石清水八幡宮}} at the site under the name "Kaijima Hachimangū" (another theory suggests it was {{ill|Usa Hachimangū|ja|宇佐八幡宮}}). Later, the Genji moved it to its current location. In {{ill|Tenki 3 (1055)|ja|天喜3年（1055年）}}, during the {{ill|Earlier Nine Years' War|ja|前九年の役}}, Yoriyoshi prayed at the shrine and achieved victory. To honor this, he established the {{ill|Tsurugaoka Hachimangū|lt=Original Hachiman Shrine|ja|元八幡}} in {{ill|Yuigahama|ja|由比ヶ浜}}, Kamakura, in {{ill|Kōhei 6 (1063)|ja|康平6年（1063年）}}. In {{ill|Ōtoku 2 (1085)|ja|応徳2年（1085年）}}, Minamoto no Yoshiie, Yoriyoshi's heir, dedicated the shrine in Hamano-go as "Tsuruoka Hachimangū." Subsequently, Tsuruoka Hachimangū gained prominence, receiving land and monetary support from {{ill|Minamoto no Yoritomo|ja|源頼朝}} and others. Over time, it became the center for the famous {{ill|Hamaori Festival|ja|浜降祭}} and absorbed nearby shrines such as Sazuka Daimyōjin. == Deities == * {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|応神天皇}} * {{ill|Emperor Nintoku|ja|仁徳天皇}} * Sazuka Daimyōjin (associated with {{ill|Ōyamazumi Shrine|ja|大山祇神社}}) * {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|Sugawara no Michizane}} (from Tsurumine Tenmangū) == Annual Events == * {{ill|Hamaori Festival|ja|浜降祭}} (Marine Day) * {{ill|Misorogi Ritual|ja|禊の神事}} (June 29, formerly held on July 15) * {{ill|Reitaisai|ja|例大祭}} (September 15) == Grounds == ; '''Great Ginkgo Tree''' : A 950-year-old tree said to have been planted by Minamoto no Yoshiie to pray for victory. ; '''Miyagane (Shrine Bell)''' : The shrine bell, originally installed in {{ill|Enpō 8 (1680)|ja|延宝8年（1680年）}}, was reconstructed after the war in 1965 as a "peace bell." === Sub-Shrines === * '''Shōnan Awashima Shrine''' — Dedicated to {{ill|Sukunahikona|ja|少彦名命}} and {{ill|Awashima-no-Kami|ja|淡島神}}. * '''Tsurumine Inari Shrine''' — Dedicated to {{ill|Ukanomitama|ja|宇迦之御魂}}. == Access == Take the JR Tōkaidō Main Line to {{ill|Chigasaki Station|ja|茅ケ崎駅}}. From the north exit, take a bus and alight at "Tsurumine Elementary School," then walk 1 minute. == References == {{Cite source help}} {{Reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|Minamoto no Yoriyoshi|ja|源頼義}} * {{ill|Iwashimizu Hachimangū|ja|石清水八幡宮}} * {{ill|Hamaori Festival|ja|浜降祭}} [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Tsurumine Hachiman Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Tsurumine revision.text:
{{Cite source|date=June 28, 2018}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name=Tsuruoka Hachimangū | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image=[[File:GrfKanagawakenChigasakishi-Turuminehachimangu-01.jpg|270px|Tsuruoka Hachimangū Haiden, Chigasaki City]]
Haiden
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=270|coord={{coord|35|20|18.3|N|139|23|28.3|E}}}} | location=〒 253-0086
{{ill|Kanagawa Prefecture|ja|神奈川県}}{{ill|Chigasaki|ja|茅ヶ崎市}} Hamano-go 462 | ISO=JP-14 | Latitude degrees = 35|Latitude minutes = 20|Latitude seconds = 18.38 | Longitude degrees=139|Longitude minutes=23|Longitude seconds=28.30 | deity={{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|応神天皇}}
{{ill|Emperor Nintoku|ja|仁徳天皇}}
{{ill|Sazuka Daimyōjin|ja|佐塚大神}}
{{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|Sugawara no Michizane}} | Shrine rank=Former {{ill|Village shrine|ja|村社}}, Former {{ill|District shrine|ja|郷社}} | Foundation={{ill|Chōgen 3|ja|長元3年}} ({{ill|1030|ja|1030年}}) | Main Hall=Nagare-zukuri | Alternate name=Tsuruoka Hachiman Shrine | festival={{ill|September 15|ja|9月15日}} | Rituals={{ill|Hamaori Festival|ja|浜降祭}} ({{ill|Marine Day|ja|海の日}}) | Map=Japan Kanagawa }} '''Tsuruoka Hachimangū''' (鶴嶺八幡宮) is a {{ill|shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Chigasaki|ja|茅ヶ崎市}}, {{ill|Kanagawa Prefecture|ja|神奈川県}}. It is also known as Tsuruoka Hachiman Shrine. Renowned as the center of Hachiman worship in {{ill|Sagami Province|ja|相模国}}, the shrine has been revered since ancient times as a significant site of {{ill|Hachiman faith|ja|八幡信仰}}.Cited by the Kanagawa Prefecture Shrine Office According to the shrine, it was the first {{ill|clan shrine|ja|氏社}} established by the {{ill|Genji|ja|源氏}} when they expanded into the Kanto region. == History == The shrine was founded in {{ill|Chōgen 3 (1030)|ja|長元3年（1030年）}}, during the {{ill|Subjugation of Shimōsa|ja|下総の乱}} by {{ill|Minamoto no Yoriyoshi|ja|源頼義}}, who hailed from {{ill|Kawachi Province|ja|河内国}} (modern-day {{ill|Habikino|ja|羽曳野市}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}). Yoriyoshi enshrined {{ill|Iwashimizu Hachimangū|ja|石清水八幡宮}} at the site under the name "Kaijima Hachimangū" (another theory suggests it was {{ill|Usa Hachimangū|ja|宇佐八幡宮}}). Later, the Genji moved it to its current location. In {{ill|Tenki 3 (1055)|ja|天喜3年（1055年）}}, during the {{ill|Earlier Nine Years' War|ja|前九年の役}}, Yoriyoshi prayed at the shrine and achieved victory. To honor this, he established the {{ill|Tsurugaoka Hachimangū|lt=Original Hachiman Shrine|ja|元八幡}} in {{ill|Yuigahama|ja|由比ヶ浜}}, Kamakura, in {{ill|Kōhei 6 (1063)|ja|康平6年（1063年）}}. In {{ill|Ōtoku 2 (1085)|ja|応徳2年（1085年）}}, Minamoto no Yoshiie, Yoriyoshi's heir, dedicated the shrine in Hamano-go as "Tsuruoka Hachimangū." Subsequently, Tsuruoka Hachimangū gained prominence, receiving land and monetary support from {{ill|Minamoto no Yoritomo|ja|源頼朝}} and others. Over time, it became the center for the famous {{ill|Hamaori Festival|ja|浜降祭}} and absorbed nearby shrines such as Sazuka Daimyōjin. == Deities == * {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|応神天皇}} * {{ill|Emperor Nintoku|ja|仁徳天皇}} * Sazuka Daimyōjin (associated with {{ill|Ōyamazumi Shrine|ja|大山祇神社}}) * {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|Sugawara no Michizane}} (from Tsurumine Tenmangū) == Annual Events == * {{ill|Hamaori Festival|ja|浜降祭}} (Marine Day) * {{ill|Misorogi Ritual|ja|禊の神事}} (June 29, formerly held on July 15) * {{ill|Reitaisai|ja|例大祭}} (September 15) == Grounds == ; '''Great Ginkgo Tree''' : A 950-year-old tree said to have been planted by Minamoto no Yoshiie to pray for victory. ; '''Miyagane (Shrine Bell)''' : The shrine bell, originally installed in {{ill|Enpō 8 (1680)|ja|延宝8年（1680年）}}, was reconstructed after the war in 1965 as a "peace bell." === Sub-Shrines === * '''Shōnan Awashima Shrine''' — Dedicated to {{ill|Sukunahikona|ja|少彦名命}} and {{ill|Awashima-no-Kami|ja|淡島神}}. * '''Tsurumine Inari Shrine''' — Dedicated to {{ill|Ukanomitama|ja|宇迦之御魂}}. == Access == Take the JR Tōkaidō Main Line to {{ill|Chigasaki Station|ja|茅ケ崎駅}}. From the north exit, take a bus and alight at "Tsurumine Elementary School," then walk 1 minute. == References == {{Cite source help}} {{Reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|Minamoto no Yoriyoshi|ja|源頼義}} * {{ill|Iwashimizu Hachimangū|ja|石清水八幡宮}} * {{ill|Hamaori Festival|ja|浜降祭}} [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Tsurumine Hachiman Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Tsurumine {{Cite source|date=June 28, 2018}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name=Tsuruoka Hachimangū | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image=[[File:GrfKanagawakenChigasakishi-Turuminehachimangu-01.jpg|270px|Tsuruoka Hachimangū Haiden, Chigasaki City]]
Haiden
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=270|coord={{coord|35|20|18.3|N|139|23|28.3|E}}}} | location=〒 253-0086
{{ill|Kanagawa Prefecture|ja|神奈川県}}{{ill|Chigasaki|ja|茅ヶ崎市}} Hamano-go 462 | ISO=JP-14 | Latitude degrees = 35|Latitude minutes = 20|Latitude seconds = 18.38 | Longitude degrees=139|Longitude minutes=23|Longitude seconds=28.30 | deity={{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|応神天皇}}
{{ill|Emperor Nintoku|ja|仁徳天皇}}
{{ill|Sazuka Daimyōjin|ja|佐塚大神}}
{{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|Sugawara no Michizane}} | Shrine rank=Former {{ill|Village shrine|ja|村社}}, Former {{ill|District shrine|ja|郷社}} | Foundation={{ill|Chōgen 3|ja|長元3年}} ({{ill|1030|ja|1030年}}) | Main Hall=Nagare-zukuri | Alternate name=Tsuruoka Hachiman Shrine | festival={{ill|September 15|ja|9月15日}} | Rituals={{ill|Hamaori Festival|ja|浜降祭}} ({{ill|Marine Day|ja|海の日}}) | Map=Japan Kanagawa }} '''Tsuruoka Hachimangū''' (鶴嶺八幡宮) is a {{ill|shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Chigasaki|ja|茅ヶ崎市}}, {{ill|Kanagawa Prefecture|ja|神奈川県}}. It is also known as Tsuruoka Hachiman Shrine. Renowned as the center of Hachiman worship in {{ill|Sagami Province|ja|相模国}}, the shrine has been revered since ancient times as a significant site of {{ill|Hachiman faith|ja|八幡信仰}}.Cited by the Kanagawa Prefecture Shrine Office According to the shrine, it was the first {{ill|clan shrine|ja|氏社}} established by the {{ill|Genji|ja|源氏}} when they expanded into the Kanto region. == History == The shrine was founded in {{ill|Chōgen 3 (1030)|ja|長元3年（1030年）}}, during the {{ill|Subjugation of Shimōsa|ja|下総の乱}} by {{ill|Minamoto no Yoriyoshi|ja|源頼義}}, who hailed from {{ill|Kawachi Province|ja|河内国}} (modern-day {{ill|Habikino|ja|羽曳野市}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}). Yoriyoshi enshrined {{ill|Iwashimizu Hachimangū|ja|石清水八幡宮}} at the site under the name "Kaijima Hachimangū" (another theory suggests it was {{ill|Usa Hachimangū|ja|宇佐八幡宮}}). Later, the Genji moved it to its current location. In {{ill|Tenki 3 (1055)|ja|天喜3年（1055年）}}, during the {{ill|Earlier Nine Years' War|ja|前九年の役}}, Yoriyoshi prayed at the shrine and achieved victory. To honor this, he established the {{ill|Tsurugaoka Hachimangū|lt=Original Hachiman Shrine|ja|元八幡}} in {{ill|Yuigahama|ja|由比ヶ浜}}, Kamakura, in {{ill|Kōhei 6 (1063)|ja|康平6年（1063年）}}. In {{ill|Ōtoku 2 (1085)|ja|応徳2年（1085年）}}, Minamoto no Yoshiie, Yoriyoshi's heir, dedicated the shrine in Hamano-go as "Tsuruoka Hachimangū." Subsequently, Tsuruoka Hachimangū gained prominence, receiving land and monetary support from {{ill|Minamoto no Yoritomo|ja|源頼朝}} and others. Over time, it became the center for the famous {{ill|Hamaori Festival|ja|浜降祭}} and absorbed nearby shrines such as Sazuka Daimyōjin. == Deities == * {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|応神天皇}} * {{ill|Emperor Nintoku|ja|仁徳天皇}} * Sazuka Daimyōjin (associated with {{ill|Ōyamazumi Shrine|ja|大山祇神社}}) * {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|Sugawara no Michizane}} (from Tsurumine Tenmangū) == Annual Events == * {{ill|Hamaori Festival|ja|浜降祭}} (Marine Day) * {{ill|Misorogi Ritual|ja|禊の神事}} (June 29, formerly held on July 15) * {{ill|Reitaisai|ja|例大祭}} (September 15) == Grounds == ; '''Great Ginkgo Tree''' : A 950-year-old tree said to have been planted by Minamoto no Yoshiie to pray for victory. ; '''Miyagane (Shrine Bell)''' : The shrine bell, originally installed in {{ill|Enpō 8 (1680)|ja|延宝8年（1680年）}}, was reconstructed after the war in 1965 as a "peace bell." === Sub-Shrines === * '''Shōnan Awashima Shrine''' — Dedicated to {{ill|Sukunahikona|ja|少彦名命}} and {{ill|Awashima-no-Kami|ja|淡島神}}. * '''Tsurumine Inari Shrine''' — Dedicated to {{ill|Ukanomitama|ja|宇迦之御魂}}. == Access == Take the JR Tōkaidō Main Line to {{ill|Chigasaki Station|ja|茅ケ崎駅}}. From the north exit, take a bus and alight at "Tsurumine Elementary School," then walk 1 minute. == References == {{Cite source help}} {{Reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|Minamoto no Yoriyoshi|ja|源頼義}} {{Cite source|date=June 28, 2018}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name=Tsuruoka Hachimangū | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image=[[File:GrfKanagawakenChigasakishi-Turuminehachimangu-01.jpg|270px|Tsuruoka Hachimangū Haiden, Chigasaki City]]
Haiden
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=270|coord={{coord|35|20|18.3|N|139|23|28.3|E}}}} | location=〒 253-0086
{{ill|Kanagawa Prefecture|ja|神奈川県}}{{ill|Chigasaki|ja|茅ヶ崎市}} Hamano-go 462 | ISO=JP-14 | Latitude degrees = 35|Latitude minutes = 20|Latitude seconds = 18.38 | Longitude degrees=139|Longitude minutes=23|Longitude seconds=28.30 | deity={{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|応神天皇}}
{{ill|Emperor Nintoku|ja|仁徳天皇}}
{{ill|Sazuka Daimyōjin|ja|佐塚大神}}
{{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|Sugawara no Michizane}} | Shrine rank=Former {{ill|Village shrine|ja|村社}}, Former {{ill|District shrine|ja|郷社}} | Foundation={{ill|Chōgen 3|ja|長元3年}} ({{ill|1030|ja|1030年}}) | Main Hall=Nagare-zukuri | Alternate name=Tsuruoka Hachiman Shrine | festival={{ill|September 15|ja|9月15日}} | Rituals={{ill|Hamaori Festival|ja|浜降祭}} ({{ill|Marine Day|ja|海の日}}) | Map=Japan Kanagawa }} '''Tsuruoka Hachimangū''' (鶴嶺八幡宮) is a {{ill|shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Chigasaki|ja|茅ヶ崎市}}, {{ill|Kanagawa Prefecture|ja|神奈川県}}. It is also known as Tsuruoka Hachiman Shrine. Renowned as the center of Hachiman worship in {{ill|Sagami Province|ja|相模国}}, the shrine has been revered since ancient times as a significant site of {{ill|Hachiman faith|ja|八幡信仰}}.Cited by the Kanagawa Prefecture Shrine Office According to the shrine, it was the first {{ill|clan shrine|ja|氏社}} established by the {{ill|Genji|ja|源氏}} when they expanded into the Kanto region. == History == The shrine was founded in {{ill|Chōgen 3 (1030)|ja|長元3年（1030年）}}, during the {{ill|Subjugation of Shimōsa|ja|下総の乱}} by {{ill|Minamoto no Yoriyoshi|ja|源頼義}}, who hailed from {{ill|Kawachi Province|ja|河内国}} (modern-day {{ill|Habikino|ja|羽曳野市}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}). Yoriyoshi enshrined {{ill|Iwashimizu Hachimangū|ja|石清水八幡宮}} at the site under the name "Kaijima Hachimangū" (another theory suggests it was {{ill|Usa Hachimangū|ja|宇佐八幡宮}}). Later, the Genji moved it to its current location. In {{ill|Tenki 3 (1055)|ja|天喜3年（1055年）}}, during the {{ill|Earlier Nine Years' War|ja|前九年の役}}, Yoriyoshi prayed at the shrine and achieved victory. To honor this, he established the {{ill|Tsurugaoka Hachimangū|lt=Original Hachiman Shrine|ja|元八幡}} in {{ill|Yuigahama|ja|由比ヶ浜}}, Kamakura, in {{ill|Kōhei 6 (1063)|ja|康平6年（1063年）}}. In {{ill|Ōtoku 2 (1085)|ja|応徳2年（1085年）}}, Minamoto no Yoshiie, Yoriyoshi's heir, dedicated the shrine in Hamano-go as "Tsuruoka Hachimangū." Subsequently, Tsuruoka Hachimangū gained prominence, receiving land and monetary support from {{ill|Minamoto no Yoritomo|ja|源頼朝}} and others. Over time, it became the center for the famous {{ill|Hamaori Festival|ja|浜降祭}} and absorbed nearby shrines such as Sazuka Daimyōjin. == Deities == * {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|応神天皇}} * {{ill|Emperor Nintoku|ja|仁徳天皇}} * Sazuka Daimyōjin (associated with {{ill|Ōyamazumi Shrine|ja|大山祇神社}}) * {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|Sugawara no Michizane}} (from Tsurumine Tenmangū) == Annual Events == * {{ill|Hamaori Festival|ja|浜降祭}} (Marine Day) * {{ill|Misorogi Ritual|ja|禊の神事}} (June 29, formerly held on July 15) * {{ill|Reitaisai|ja|例大祭}} (September 15) == Grounds == ; '''Great Ginkgo Tree''' : A 950-year-old tree said to have been planted by Minamoto no Yoshiie to pray for victory. ; '''Miyagane (Shrine Bell)''' : The shrine bell, originally installed in {{ill|Enpō 8 (1680)|ja|延宝8年（1680年）}}, was reconstructed after the war in 1965 as a "peace bell." === Sub-Shrines === * '''Shōnan Awashima Shrine''' — Dedicated to {{ill|Sukunahikona|ja|少彦名命}} and {{ill|Awashima-no-Kami|ja|淡島神}}. * '''Tsurumine Inari Shrine''' — Dedicated to {{ill|Ukanomitama|ja|宇迦之御魂}}. == Access == Take the JR Tōkaidō Main Line to {{ill|Chigasaki Station|ja|茅ケ崎駅}}. From the north exit, take a bus and alight at "Tsurumine Elementary School," then walk 1 minute. == References == {{Cite source help}} {{Reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|Minamoto no Yoriyoshi|ja|源頼義}} revision.text:
{{Cite source|date=June 28, 2018}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name=Tsuruoka Hachimangū | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image=[[File:GrfKanagawakenChigasakishi-Turuminehachimangu-01.jpg|270px|Tsuruoka Hachimangū Haiden, Chigasaki City]]
Haiden
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=270|coord={{coord|35|20|18.3|N|139|23|28.3|E}}}} | location=〒 253-0086
{{ill|Kanagawa Prefecture|ja|神奈川県}}{{ill|Chigasaki|ja|茅ヶ崎市}} Hamano-go 462 | ISO=JP-14 | Latitude degrees = 35|Latitude minutes = 20|Latitude seconds = 18.38 | Longitude degrees=139|Longitude minutes=23|Longitude seconds=28.30 | deity={{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|応神天皇}}
{{ill|Emperor Nintoku|ja|仁徳天皇}}
{{ill|Sazuka Daimyōjin|ja|佐塚大神}}
{{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|Sugawara no Michizane}} | Shrine rank=Former {{ill|Village shrine|ja|村社}}, Former {{ill|District shrine|ja|郷社}} | Foundation={{ill|Chōgen 3|ja|長元3年}} ({{ill|1030|ja|1030年}}) | Main Hall=Nagare-zukuri | Alternate name=Tsuruoka Hachiman Shrine | festival={{ill|September 15|ja|9月15日}} | Rituals={{ill|Hamaori Festival|ja|浜降祭}} ({{ill|Marine Day|ja|海の日}}) | Map=Japan Kanagawa }} '''Tsuruoka Hachimangū''' (鶴嶺八幡宮) is a {{ill|shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Chigasaki|ja|茅ヶ崎市}}, {{ill|Kanagawa Prefecture|ja|神奈川県}}. It is also known as Tsuruoka Hachiman Shrine. Renowned as the center of Hachiman worship in {{ill|Sagami Province|ja|相模国}}, the shrine has been revered since ancient times as a significant site of {{ill|Hachiman faith|ja|八幡信仰}}.Cited by the Kanagawa Prefecture Shrine Office According to the shrine, it was the first {{ill|clan shrine|ja|氏社}} established by the {{ill|Genji|ja|源氏}} when they expanded into the Kanto region. == History == The shrine was founded in {{ill|Chōgen 3 (1030)|ja|長元3年（1030年）}}, during the {{ill|Subjugation of Shimōsa|ja|下総の乱}} by {{ill|Minamoto no Yoriyoshi|ja|源頼義}}, who hailed from {{ill|Kawachi Province|ja|河内国}} (modern-day {{ill|Habikino|ja|羽曳野市}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}). Yoriyoshi enshrined {{ill|Iwashimizu Hachimangū|ja|石清水八幡宮}} at the site under the name "Kaijima Hachimangū" (another theory suggests it was {{ill|Usa Hachimangū|ja|宇佐八幡宮}}). Later, the Genji moved it to its current location. In {{ill|Tenki 3 (1055)|ja|天喜3年（1055年）}}, during the {{ill|Earlier Nine Years' War|ja|前九年の役}}, Yoriyoshi prayed at the shrine and achieved victory. To honor this, he established the {{ill|Tsurugaoka Hachimangū|lt=Original Hachiman Shrine|ja|元八幡}} in {{ill|Yuigahama|ja|由比ヶ浜}}, Kamakura, in {{ill|Kōhei 6 (1063)|ja|康平6年（1063年）}}. In {{ill|Ōtoku 2 (1085)|ja|応徳2年（1085年）}}, Minamoto no Yoshiie, Yoriyoshi's heir, dedicated the shrine in Hamano-go as "Tsuruoka Hachimangū." Subsequently, Tsuruoka Hachimangū gained prominence, receiving land and monetary support from {{ill|Minamoto no Yoritomo|ja|源頼朝}} and others. Over time, it became the center for the famous {{ill|Hamaori Festival|ja|浜降祭}} and absorbed nearby shrines such as Sazuka Daimyōjin. == Deities == * {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|応神天皇}} * {{ill|Emperor Nintoku|ja|仁徳天皇}} * Sazuka Daimyōjin (associated with {{ill|Ōyamazumi Shrine|ja|大山祇神社}}) * {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|Sugawara no Michizane}} (from Tsurumine Tenmangū) == Annual Events == * {{ill|Hamaori Festival|ja|浜降祭}} (Marine Day) * {{ill|Misorogi Ritual|ja|禊の神事}} (June 29, formerly held on July 15) * {{ill|Reitaisai|ja|例大祭}} (September 15) == Grounds == ; '''Great Ginkgo Tree''' : A 950-year-old tree said to have been planted by Minamoto no Yoshiie to pray for victory. ; '''Miyagane (Shrine Bell)''' : The shrine bell, originally installed in {{ill|Enpō 8 (1680)|ja|延宝8年（1680年）}}, was reconstructed after the war in 1965 as a "peace bell." === Sub-Shrines === * '''Shōnan Awashima Shrine''' — Dedicated to {{ill|Sukunahikona|ja|少彦名命}} and {{ill|Awashima-no-Kami|ja|淡島神}}. * '''Tsurumine Inari Shrine''' — Dedicated to {{ill|Ukanomitama|ja|宇迦之御魂}}. == Access == Take the JR Tōkaidō Main Line to {{ill|Chigasaki Station|ja|茅ケ崎駅}}. From the north exit, take a bus and alight at "Tsurumine Elementary School," then walk 1 minute. == References == {{Cite source help}} {{Reflist}}== See Also == * {{ill|Minamoto no Yoriyoshi|ja|源頼義}} * {{ill|Iwashimizu Hachimangū|ja|石清水八幡宮}} * {{ill|Hamaori Festival|ja|浜降祭}} [[Category:Completion tier 2]][[Category: tier 2]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Tsurumine Hachiman Shrine]][[Category:qq]] {{Cite source|date=June 28, 2018}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name=Tsuruoka Hachimangū | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image=[[File:GrfKanagawakenChigasakishi-Turuminehachimangu-01.jpg|270px|Tsuruoka Hachimangū Haiden, Chigasaki City]]
Haiden
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=270|coord={{coord|35|20|18.3|N|139|23|28.3|E}}}} | location=〒 253-0086
{{ill|Kanagawa Prefecture|ja|神奈川県}}{{ill|Chigasaki|ja|茅ヶ崎市}} Hamano-go 462 | ISO=JP-14 | Latitude degrees = 35|Latitude minutes = 20|Latitude seconds = 18.38 | Longitude degrees=139|Longitude minutes=23|Longitude seconds=28.30 | deity={{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|応神天皇}}
{{ill|Emperor Nintoku|ja|仁徳天皇}}
{{ill|Sazuka Daimyōjin|ja|佐塚大神}}
{{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|Sugawara no Michizane}} | Shrine rank=Former {{ill|Village shrine|ja|村社}}, Former {{ill|District shrine|ja|郷社}} | Foundation={{ill|Chōgen 3|ja|長元3年}} ({{ill|1030|ja|1030年}}) | Main Hall=Nagare-zukuri | Alternate name=Tsuruoka Hachiman Shrine | festival={{ill|September 15|ja|9月15日}} | Rituals={{ill|Hamaori Festival|ja|浜降祭}} ({{ill|Marine Day|ja|海の日}}) | Map=Japan Kanagawa }} '''Tsuruoka Hachimangū''' (鶴嶺八幡宮) is a {{ill|shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Chigasaki|ja|茅ヶ崎市}}, {{ill|Kanagawa Prefecture|ja|神奈川県}}. It is also known as Tsuruoka Hachiman Shrine. Renowned as the center of Hachiman worship in {{ill|Sagami Province|ja|相模国}}, the shrine has been revered since ancient times as a significant site of {{ill|Hachiman faith|ja|八幡信仰}}.Cited by the Kanagawa Prefecture Shrine Office According to the shrine, it was the first {{ill|clan shrine|ja|氏社}} established by the {{ill|Genji|ja|源氏}} when they expanded into the Kanto region. == History == The shrine was founded in {{ill|Chōgen 3 (1030)|ja|長元3年（1030年）}}, during the {{ill|Subjugation of Shimōsa|ja|下総の乱}} by {{ill|Minamoto no Yoriyoshi|ja|源頼義}}, who hailed from {{ill|Kawachi Province|ja|河内国}} (modern-day {{ill|Habikino|ja|羽曳野市}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}). Yoriyoshi enshrined {{ill|Iwashimizu Hachimangū|ja|石清水八幡宮}} at the site under the name "Kaijima Hachimangū" (another theory suggests it was {{ill|Usa Hachimangū|ja|宇佐八幡宮}}). Later, the Genji moved it to its current location. In {{ill|Tenki 3 (1055)|ja|天喜3年（1055年）}}, during the {{ill|Earlier Nine Years' War|ja|前九年の役}}, Yoriyoshi prayed at the shrine and achieved victory. To honor this, he established the {{ill|Tsurugaoka Hachimangū|lt=Original Hachiman Shrine|ja|元八幡}} in {{ill|Yuigahama|ja|由比ヶ浜}}, Kamakura, in {{ill|Kōhei 6 (1063)|ja|康平6年（1063年）}}. In {{ill|Ōtoku 2 (1085)|ja|応徳2年（1085年）}}, Minamoto no Yoshiie, Yoriyoshi's heir, dedicated the shrine in Hamano-go as "Tsuruoka Hachimangū." Subsequently, Tsuruoka Hachimangū gained prominence, receiving land and monetary support from {{ill|Minamoto no Yoritomo|ja|源頼朝}} and others. Over time, it became the center for the famous {{ill|Hamaori Festival|ja|浜降祭}} and absorbed nearby shrines such as Sazuka Daimyōjin. == Deities == * {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|応神天皇}} * {{ill|Emperor Nintoku|ja|仁徳天皇}} * Sazuka Daimyōjin (associated with {{ill|Ōyamazumi Shrine|ja|大山祇神社}}) * {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|Sugawara no Michizane}} (from Tsurumine Tenmangū) == Annual Events == * {{ill|Hamaori Festival|ja|浜降祭}} (Marine Day) * {{ill|Misorogi Ritual|ja|禊の神事}} (June 29, formerly held on July 15) * {{ill|Reitaisai|ja|例大祭}} (September 15) == Grounds == ; '''Great Ginkgo Tree''' : A 950-year-old tree said to have been planted by Minamoto no Yoshiie to pray for victory. ; '''Miyagane (Shrine Bell)''' : The shrine bell, originally installed in {{ill|Enpō 8 (1680)|ja|延宝8年（1680年）}}, was reconstructed after the war in 1965 as a "peace bell." === Sub-Shrines === * '''Shōnan Awashima Shrine''' — Dedicated to {{ill|Sukunahikona|ja|少彦名命}} and {{ill|Awashima-no-Kami|ja|淡島神}}. * '''Tsurumine Inari Shrine''' — Dedicated to {{ill|Ukanomitama|ja|宇迦之御魂}}. == Access == Take the JR Tōkaidō Main Line to {{ill|Chigasaki Station|ja|茅ケ崎駅}}. From the north exit, take a bus and alight at "Tsurumine Elementary School," then walk 1 minute. == References == {{Cite source help}} {{Reflist}}== See Also == * {{ill|Minamoto no Yoriyoshi|ja|源頼義}} revision.text:
{{Cite source|date=June 28, 2018}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name=Tsuruoka Hachimangū | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image=[[File:GrfKanagawakenChigasakishi-Turuminehachimangu-01.jpg|270px|Tsuruoka Hachimangū Haiden, Chigasaki City]]
Haiden
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=270|coord={{coord|35|20|18.3|N|139|23|28.3|E}}}} | location=〒 253-0086
{{ill|Kanagawa Prefecture|ja|神奈川県}}{{ill|Chigasaki|ja|茅ヶ崎市}} Hamano-go 462 | ISO=JP-14 | Latitude degrees = 35|Latitude minutes = 20|Latitude seconds = 18.38 | Longitude degrees=139|Longitude minutes=23|Longitude seconds=28.30 | deity={{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|応神天皇}}
{{ill|Emperor Nintoku|ja|仁徳天皇}}
{{ill|Sazuka Daimyōjin|ja|佐塚大神}}
{{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|Sugawara no Michizane}} | Shrine rank=Former {{ill|Village shrine|ja|村社}}, Former {{ill|District shrine|ja|郷社}} | Foundation={{ill|Chōgen 3|ja|長元3年}} ({{ill|1030|ja|1030年}}) | Main Hall=Nagare-zukuri | Alternate name=Tsuruoka Hachiman Shrine | festival={{ill|September 15|ja|9月15日}} | Rituals={{ill|Hamaori Festival|ja|浜降祭}} ({{ill|Marine Day|ja|海の日}}) | Map=Japan Kanagawa }} '''Tsuruoka Hachimangū''' (鶴嶺八幡宮) is a {{ill|shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Chigasaki|ja|茅ヶ崎市}}, {{ill|Kanagawa Prefecture|ja|神奈川県}}. It is also known as Tsuruoka Hachiman Shrine. Renowned as the center of Hachiman worship in {{ill|Sagami Province|ja|相模国}}, the shrine has been revered since ancient times as a significant site of {{ill|Hachiman faith|ja|八幡信仰}}.Cited by the Kanagawa Prefecture Shrine Office According to the shrine, it was the first {{ill|clan shrine|ja|氏社}} established by the {{ill|Genji|ja|源氏}} when they expanded into the Kanto region. == History == The shrine was founded in {{ill|Chōgen 3 (1030)|ja|長元3年（1030年）}}, during the {{ill|Subjugation of Shimōsa|ja|下総の乱}} by {{ill|Minamoto no Yoriyoshi|ja|源頼義}}, who hailed from {{ill|Kawachi Province|ja|河内国}} (modern-day {{ill|Habikino|ja|羽曳野市}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}). Yoriyoshi enshrined {{ill|Iwashimizu Hachimangū|ja|石清水八幡宮}} at the site under the name "Kaijima Hachimangū" (another theory suggests it was {{ill|Usa Hachimangū|ja|宇佐八幡宮}}). Later, the Genji moved it to its current location. In {{ill|Tenki 3 (1055)|ja|天喜3年（1055年）}}, during the {{ill|Earlier Nine Years' War|ja|前九年の役}}, Yoriyoshi prayed at the shrine and achieved victory. To honor this, he established the {{ill|Tsurugaoka Hachimangū|lt=Original Hachiman Shrine|ja|元八幡}} in {{ill|Yuigahama|ja|由比ヶ浜}}, Kamakura, in {{ill|Kōhei 6 (1063)|ja|康平6年（1063年）}}. In {{ill|Ōtoku 2 (1085)|ja|応徳2年（1085年）}}, Minamoto no Yoshiie, Yoriyoshi's heir, dedicated the shrine in Hamano-go as "Tsuruoka Hachimangū." Subsequently, Tsuruoka Hachimangū gained prominence, receiving land and monetary support from {{ill|Minamoto no Yoritomo|ja|源頼朝}} and others. Over time, it became the center for the famous {{ill|Hamaori Festival|ja|浜降祭}} and absorbed nearby shrines such as Sazuka Daimyōjin. == Deities == * {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|応神天皇}} * {{ill|Emperor Nintoku|ja|仁徳天皇}} * Sazuka Daimyōjin (associated with {{ill|Ōyamazumi Shrine|ja|大山祇神社}}) * {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|Sugawara no Michizane}} (from Tsurumine Tenmangū) == Annual Events == * {{ill|Hamaori Festival|ja|浜降祭}} (Marine Day) * {{ill|Misorogi Ritual|ja|禊の神事}} (June 29, formerly held on July 15) * {{ill|Reitaisai|ja|例大祭}} (September 15) == Grounds == ; '''Great Ginkgo Tree''' : A 950-year-old tree said to have been planted by Minamoto no Yoshiie to pray for victory. ; '''Miyagane (Shrine Bell)''' : The shrine bell, originally installed in {{ill|Enpō 8 (1680)|ja|延宝8年（1680年）}}, was reconstructed after the war in 1965 as a "peace bell." === Sub-Shrines === * '''Shōnan Awashima Shrine''' — Dedicated to {{ill|Sukunahikona|ja|少彦名命}} and {{ill|Awashima-no-Kami|ja|淡島神}}. * '''Tsurumine Inari Shrine''' — Dedicated to {{ill|Ukanomitama|ja|宇迦之御魂}}. == Access == Take the JR Tōkaidō Main Line to {{ill|Chigasaki Station|ja|茅ケ崎駅}}. From the north exit, take a bus and alight at "Tsurumine Elementary School," then walk 1 minute. == References == {{Cite source help}} {{Reflist}} == See Also! == * {{ill|Minamoto no Yoriyoshi|ja|源頼義}} * {{ill|Iwashimizu Hachimangū|ja|石清水八幡宮}} * {{ill|Hamaori Festival|ja|浜降祭}} [[Category:Completion tier 2]][[Category: tier 2]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Tsurumine Hachiman Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Tsurumine Hachiman Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Tsurumine Hachiman Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Tsurumine Hachiman Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Tsurumine Hachiman Shrine]][[Category:11]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Tsurumine Hachiman Shrine}}[[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Tsurumine Hachiman {{ill|Emperor Nintoku|ja|仁徳天皇}}
{{ill|Sazuka Daimyōjin|ja|佐塚大神}}
{{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|Sugawara no Michizane}} | Shrine rank=Former {{ill|Village shrine|ja|村社}}, Former {{ill|District shrine|ja|郷社}} | Foundation={{ill|Chōgen 3|ja|長元3年}} ({{ill|1030|ja|1030年}}) | Main Hall=Nagare-zukuri | Alternate name=Tsuruoka Hachiman Shrine | festival={{ill|September 15|ja|9月15日}} | Rituals={{ill|Hamaori Festival|ja|浜降祭}} ({{ill|Marine Day|ja|海の日}}) | Map=Japan Kanagawa }} '''Tsuruoka Hachimangū''' (鶴嶺八幡宮) is a {{ill|shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Chigasaki|ja|茅ヶ崎市}}, {{ill|Kanagawa Prefecture|ja|神奈川県}}. It is also known as Tsuruoka Hachiman Shrine. Renowned as the center of Hachiman worship in {{ill|Sagami Province|ja|相模国}}, the shrine has been revered since ancient times as a significant site of {{ill|Hachiman faith|ja|八幡信仰}}.Cited by the Kanagawa Prefecture Shrine Office According to the shrine, it was the first {{ill|clan shrine|ja|氏社}} established by the {{ill|Genji|ja|源氏}} when they expanded into the Kanto region. == History == The shrine was founded in {{ill|Chōgen 3 (1030)|ja|長元3年（1030年）}}, during the {{ill|Subjugation of Shimōsa|ja|下総の乱}} by {{ill|Minamoto no Yoriyoshi|ja|源頼義}}, who hailed from {{ill|Kawachi Province|ja|河内国}} (modern-day {{ill|Habikino|ja|羽曳野市}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}). Yoriyoshi enshrined {{ill|Iwashimizu Hachimangū|ja|石清水八幡宮}} at the site under the name "Kaijima Hachimangū" (another theory suggests it was {{ill|Usa Hachimangū|ja|宇佐八幡宮}}). Later, the Genji moved it to its current location. In {{ill|Tenki 3 (1055)|ja|天喜3年（1055年）}}, during the {{ill|Earlier Nine Years' War|ja|前九年の役}}, Yoriyoshi prayed at the shrine and achieved victory. To honor this, he established the {{ill|Tsurugaoka Hachimangū|lt=Original Hachiman Shrine|ja|元八幡}} in {{ill|Yuigahama|ja|由比ヶ浜}}, Kamakura, in {{ill|Kōhei 6 (1063)|ja|康平6年（1063年）}}. In {{ill|Ōtoku 2 (1085)|ja|応徳2年（1085年）}}, Minamoto no Yoshiie, Yoriyoshi's heir, dedicated the shrine in Hamano-go as "Tsuruoka Hachimangū." Subsequently, Tsuruoka Hachimangū gained prominence, receiving land and monetary support from {{ill|Minamoto no Yoritomo|ja|源頼朝}} and others. Over time, it became the center for the famous {{ill|Hamaori Festival|ja|浜降祭}} and absorbed nearby shrines such as Sazuka Daimyōjin. == Deities == * {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|応神天皇}} * {{ill|Emperor Nintoku|ja|仁徳天皇}} * Sazuka Daimyōjin (associated with {{ill|Ōyamazumi Shrine|ja|大山祇神社}}) * {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|Sugawara no Michizane}} (from Tsurumine Tenmangū) == Annual Events == * {{ill|Hamaori Festival|ja|浜降祭}} (Marine Day) * {{ill|Misorogi Ritual|ja|禊の神事}} (June 29, formerly held on July 15) * {{ill|Reitaisai|ja|例大祭}} (September 15) == Grounds == ; '''Great Ginkgo Tree''' : A 950-year-old tree said to have been planted by Minamoto no Yoshiie to pray for victory. ; '''Miyagane (Shrine Bell)''' : The shrine bell, originally installed in {{ill|Enpō 8 (1680)|ja|延宝8年（1680年）}}, was reconstructed after the war in 1965 as a "peace bell." === Sub-Shrines === * '''Shōnan Awashima Shrine''' — Dedicated to {{ill|Sukunahikona|ja|少彦名命}} and {{ill|Awashima-no-Kami|ja|淡島神}}. * '''Tsurumine Inari Shrine''' — Dedicated to {{ill|Ukanomitama|ja|宇迦之御魂}}. == Access == Take the JR Tōkaidō Main Line to {{ill|Chigasaki Station|ja|茅ケ崎駅}}. From the north exit, take a bus and alight at "Tsurumine Elementary School," then walk 1 minute. == References == {{Cite source help}} {{Reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|Minamoto no Yoriyoshi|ja|源頼義}} revision.comment: Text replacement - "|大山祇神社}}" to "|大山祇神社|1=Ōyamazumi Shrine}}"
revision.text:
{{Cite source|date=June 28, 2018}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name=Tsuruoka Hachimangū | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image=[[File:GrfKanagawakenChigasakishi-Turuminehachimangu-01.jpg|270px|Tsuruoka Hachimangū Haiden, Chigasaki City]]
Haiden
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=270|coord={{coord|35|20|18.3|N|139|23|28.3|E}}}} | location=〒 253-0086
{{ill|Kanagawa Prefecture|ja|神奈川県}}{{ill|Chigasaki|ja|茅ヶ崎市}} Hamano-go 462 | ISO=JP-14 | Latitude degrees = 35|Latitude minutes = 20|Latitude seconds = 18.38 | Longitude degrees=139|Longitude minutes=23|Longitude seconds=28.30 | deity={{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|応神天皇}}
{{ill|Emperor Nintoku|ja|仁徳天皇}}
{{ill|Sazuka Daimyōjin|ja|佐塚大神}}
{{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|Sugawara no Michizane}} | Shrine rank=Former {{ill|Village shrine|ja|村社}}, Former {{ill|District shrine|ja|郷社}} | Foundation={{ill|Chōgen 3|ja|長元3年}} ({{ill|1030|ja|1030年}}) | Main Hall=Nagare-zukuri | Alternate name=Tsuruoka Hachiman Shrine | festival={{ill|September 15|ja|9月15日}} | Rituals={{ill|Hamaori Festival|ja|浜降祭}} ({{ill|Marine Day|ja|海の日}}) | Map=Japan Kanagawa }} '''Tsuruoka Hachimangū''' (鶴嶺八幡宮) is a {{ill|shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Chigasaki|ja|茅ヶ崎市}}, {{ill|Kanagawa Prefecture|ja|神奈川県}}. It is also known as Tsuruoka Hachiman Shrine. Renowned as the center of Hachiman worship in {{ill|Sagami Province|ja|相模国}}, the shrine has been revered since ancient times as a significant site of {{ill|Hachiman faith|ja|八幡信仰}}.Cited by the Kanagawa Prefecture Shrine Office According to the shrine, it was the first {{ill|clan shrine|ja|氏社}} established by the {{ill|Genji|ja|源氏}} when they expanded into the Kanto region. == History == The shrine was founded in {{ill|Chōgen 3 (1030)|ja|長元3年（1030年）}}, during the {{ill|Subjugation of Shimōsa|ja|下総の乱}} by {{ill|Minamoto no Yoriyoshi|ja|源頼義}}, who hailed from {{ill|Kawachi Province|ja|河内国}} (modern-day {{ill|Habikino|ja|羽曳野市}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}). Yoriyoshi enshrined {{ill|Iwashimizu Hachimangū|ja|石清水八幡宮}} at the site under the name "Kaijima Hachimangū" (another theory suggests it was {{ill|Usa Hachimangū|ja|宇佐八幡宮}}). Later, the Genji moved it to its current location. In {{ill|Tenki 3 (1055)|ja|天喜3年（1055年）}}, during the {{ill|Earlier Nine Years' War|ja|前九年の役}}, Yoriyoshi prayed at the shrine and achieved victory. To honor this, he established the {{ill|Tsurugaoka Hachimangū|lt=Original Hachiman Shrine|ja|元八幡}} in {{ill|Yuigahama|ja|由比ヶ浜}}, Kamakura, in {{ill|Kōhei 6 (1063)|ja|康平6年（1063年）}}. In {{ill|Ōtoku 2 (1085)|ja|応徳2年（1085年）}}, Minamoto no Yoshiie, Yoriyoshi's heir, dedicated the shrine in Hamano-go as "Tsuruoka Hachimangū." Subsequently, Tsuruoka Hachimangū gained prominence, receiving land and monetary support from {{ill|Minamoto no Yoritomo|ja|源頼朝}} and others. Over time, it became the center for the famous {{ill|Hamaori Festival|ja|浜降祭}} and absorbed nearby shrines such as Sazuka Daimyōjin. == Deities == * {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|応神天皇}} * {{ill|Emperor Nintoku|ja|仁徳天皇}} * Sazuka Daimyōjin (associated with {{ill|Ōyamazumi Shrine|ja|大山祇神社|1=Ōyamazumi Shrine}}) * {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|Sugawara no Michizane}} (from Tsurumine Tenmangū) == Annual Events == * {{ill|Hamaori Festival|ja|浜降祭}} (Marine Day) * {{ill|Misorogi Ritual|ja|禊の神事}} (June 29, formerly held on July 15) * {{ill|Reitaisai|ja|例大祭}} (September 15) == Grounds == ; '''Great Ginkgo Tree''' : A 950-year-old tree said to have been planted by Minamoto no Yoshiie to pray for victory. ; '''Miyagane (Shrine Bell)''' : The shrine bell, originally installed in {{ill|Enpō 8 (1680)|ja|延宝8年（1680年）}}, was reconstructed after the war in 1965 as a "peace bell." === Sub-Shrines === * '''Shōnan Awashima Shrine''' — Dedicated to {{ill|Sukunahikona|ja|少彦名命}} and {{ill|Awashima-no-Kami|ja|淡島神}}. * '''Tsurumine Inari Shrine''' — Dedicated to {{ill|Ukanomitama|ja|宇迦之御魂}}. == Access == Take the JR Tōkaidō Main Line to {{ill|Chigasaki Station|ja|茅ケ崎駅}}. From the north exit, take a bus and alight at "Tsurumine Elementary School," then walk 1 minute. == References == {{Cite source help}} {{Reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|Minamoto no Yoriyoshi|ja|源頼義}} * {{ill|Iwashimizu Hachimangū|ja|石清水八幡宮}} * {{ill|Hamaori Festival|ja|浜降祭}} [[Category:Completion tier 2]][[Category: tier 2]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Tsurumine Hachiman Shrine]][[Category:qq]]
