{{Cite source|date=June 28, 2018}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name=Tsuruoka Hachimangū | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image=[[File:GrfKanagawakenChigasakishi-Turuminehachimangu-01.jpg|270px|Tsuruoka Hachimangū Haiden, Chigasaki City]]
Haiden
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=270|coord={{coord|35|20|18.3|N|139|23|28.3|E}}}} | location=〒 253-0086
{{ill|Kanagawa Prefecture|ja|神奈川県}}{{ill|Chigasaki|ja|茅ヶ崎市}} Hamano-go 462 | ISO=JP-14 | Latitude degrees = 35|Latitude minutes = 20|Latitude seconds = 18.38 | Longitude degrees=139|Longitude minutes=23|Longitude seconds=28.30 | deity={{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|応神天皇}}
{{ill|Emperor Nintoku|ja|仁徳天皇}}
{{ill|Sazuka Daimyōjin|ja|佐塚大神}}
{{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|Sugawara no Michizane}} | Shrine rank=Former {{ill|Village shrine|ja|村社}}, Former {{ill|District shrine|ja|郷社}} | Foundation={{ill|Chōgen 3|ja|長元3年}} ({{ill|1030|ja|1030年}}) | Main Hall=Nagare-zukuri | Alternate name=Tsuruoka Hachiman Shrine | festival={{ill|September 15|ja|9月15日}} | Rituals={{ill|Hamaori Festival|ja|浜降祭}} ({{ill|Marine Day|ja|海の日}}) | Map=Japan Kanagawa }} '''Tsuruoka Hachimangū''' (鶴嶺八幡宮) is a {{ill|shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Chigasaki|ja|茅ヶ崎市}}, {{ill|Kanagawa Prefecture|ja|神奈川県}}. It is also known as Tsuruoka Hachiman Shrine. Renowned as the center of Hachiman worship in {{ill|Sagami Province|ja|相模国}}, the shrine has been revered since ancient times as a significant site of {{ill|Hachiman faith|ja|八幡信仰}}.Cited by the Kanagawa Prefecture Shrine Office According to the shrine, it was the first {{ill|clan shrine|ja|氏社}} established by the {{ill|Genji|ja|源氏}} when they expanded into the Kanto region. == History == The shrine was founded in {{ill|Chōgen 3 (1030)|ja|長元3年（1030年）}}, during the {{ill|Subjugation of Shimōsa|ja|下総の乱}} by {{ill|Minamoto no Yoriyoshi|ja|源頼義}}, who hailed from {{ill|Kawachi Province|ja|河内国}} (modern-day {{ill|Habikino|ja|羽曳野市}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}). Yoriyoshi enshrined {{ill|Iwashimizu Hachimangū|ja|石清水八幡宮}} at the site under the name "Kaijima Hachimangū" (another theory suggests it was {{ill|Usa Hachimangū|ja|宇佐八幡宮}}). Later, the Genji moved it to its current location. In {{ill|Tenki 3 (1055)|ja|天喜3年（1055年）}}, during the {{ill|Earlier Nine Years' War|ja|前九年の役}}, Yoriyoshi prayed at the shrine and achieved victory. To honor this, he established the {{ill|Tsurugaoka Hachimangū|lt=Original Hachiman Shrine|ja|元八幡}} in {{ill|Yuigahama|ja|由比ヶ浜}}, Kamakura, in {{ill|Kōhei 6 (1063)|ja|康平6年（1063年）}}. In {{ill|Ōtoku 2 (1085)|ja|応徳2nd year （1085年）}}, Minamoto no Yoshiie, Yoriyoshi's heir, dedicated the shrine in Hamano-go as "Tsuruoka Hachimangū." Subsequently, Tsuruoka Hachimangū gained prominence, receiving land and monetary support from {{ill|Minamoto no Yoritomo|ja|源頼朝}} and others. Over time, it became the center for the famous {{ill|Hamaori Festival|ja|浜降祭}} and absorbed nearby shrines such as Sazuka Daimyōjin. == Deities == * {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|応神天皇}} * {{ill|Emperor Nintoku|ja|仁徳天皇}} * Sazuka Daimyōjin (associated with {{ill|Ōyamazumi Shrine|ja|大山祇神社|1=Ōyamazumi Shrine}}) * {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|Sugawara no Michizane}} (from Tsurumine Tenmangū) == Annual Events == * {{ill|Hamaori Festival|ja|浜降祭}} (Marine Day) * {{ill|Misorogi Ritual|ja|禊の神事}} (June 29, formerly held on July 15) * {{ill|Reitaisai|ja|例大祭}} (September 15) == Grounds == ; '''Great Ginkgo Tree''' : A 950-year-old tree said to have been planted by Minamoto no Yoshiie to pray for victory. ; '''Miyagane (Shrine Bell)''' : The shrine bell, originally installed in {{ill|Enpō 8 (1680)|ja|延宝8年（1680年）}}, was reconstructed after the war in 1965 as a "peace bell." === Sub-Shrines === * '''Shōnan Awashima Shrine''' — Dedicated to {{ill|Sukunahikona|ja|少彦名命}} and {{ill|Awashima-no-Kami|ja|淡島神}}. * '''Tsurumine Inari Shrine''' — Dedicated to {{ill|Ukanomitama|ja|宇迦之御魂}}. == Access == Take the JR Tōkaidō Main Line to {{ill|Chigasaki Station|ja|茅ケ崎駅}}. From the north exit, take a bus and alight at "Tsurumine Elementary School," then walk 1 minute. == References == {{Cite source help}} {{Reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|Minamoto no Yoriyoshi|ja|源頼義}} {{Cite source|date=June 28, 2018}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name=Tsuruoka Hachimangū | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image=[[File:GrfKanagawakenChigasakishi-Turuminehachimangu-01.jpg|270px|Tsuruoka Hachimangū Haiden, Chigasaki City]]
Haiden
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=270|coord={{coord|35|20|18.3|N|139|23|28.3|E}}}} | location=〒 253-0086
{{ill|Kanagawa Prefecture|ja|神奈川県}}{{ill|Chigasaki|ja|茅ヶ崎市}} Hamano-go 462 | ISO=JP-14 | Latitude degrees = 35|Latitude minutes = 20|Latitude seconds = 18.38 | Longitude degrees=139|Longitude minutes=23|Longitude seconds=28.30 | deity={{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|応神天皇}}
{{ill|Emperor Nintoku|ja|仁徳天皇}}
{{ill|Sazuka Daimyōjin|ja|佐塚大神}}
{{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|Sugawara no Michizane}} | Shrine rank=Former {{ill|Village shrine|ja|村社}}, Former {{ill|District shrine|ja|郷社}} | Foundation={{ill|Chōgen 3|ja|長元3rd year }} ({{ill|1030|ja|1030年}}) | Main Hall=Nagare-zukuri | Alternate name=Tsuruoka Hachiman Shrine | festival={{ill|September 15|ja|9月15日}} | Rituals={{ill|Hamaori Festival|ja|浜降祭}} ({{ill|Marine Day|ja|海の日}}) | Map=Japan Kanagawa }} '''Tsuruoka Hachimangū''' (鶴嶺八幡宮) is a {{ill|shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Chigasaki|ja|茅ヶ崎市}}, {{ill|Kanagawa Prefecture|ja|神奈川県}}. It is also known as Tsuruoka Hachiman Shrine. Renowned as the center of Hachiman worship in {{ill|Sagami Province|ja|相模国}}, the shrine has been revered since ancient times as a significant site of {{ill|Hachiman faith|ja|八幡信仰}}.Cited by the Kanagawa Prefecture Shrine Office According to the shrine, it was the first {{ill|clan shrine|ja|氏社}} established by the {{ill|Genji|ja|源氏}} when they expanded into the Kanto region. == History == The shrine was founded in {{ill|Chōgen 3 (1030)|ja|長元3rd year （1030年）}}, during the {{ill|Subjugation of Shimōsa|ja|下総の乱}} by {{ill|Minamoto no Yoriyoshi|ja|源頼義}}, who hailed from {{ill|Kawachi Province|ja|河内国}} (modern-day {{ill|Habikino|ja|羽曳野市}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}). Yoriyoshi enshrined {{ill|Iwashimizu Hachimangū|ja|石清水八幡宮}} at the site under the name "Kaijima Hachimangū" (another theory suggests it was {{ill|Usa Hachimangū|ja|宇佐八幡宮}}). Later, the Genji moved it to its current location. In {{ill|Tenki 3 (1055)|ja|天喜3rd year （1055年）}}, during the {{ill|Earlier Nine Years' War|ja|前九年の役}}, Yoriyoshi prayed at the shrine and achieved victory. To honor this, he established the {{ill|Tsurugaoka Hachimangū|lt=Original Hachiman Shrine|ja|元八幡}} in {{ill|Yuigahama|ja|由比ヶ浜}}, Kamakura, in {{ill|Kōhei 6 (1063)|ja|康平6年（1063rd year ）}}. In {{ill|Ōtoku 2 (1085)|ja|応徳2nd year （1085年）}}, Minamoto no Yoshiie, Yoriyoshi's heir, dedicated the shrine in Hamano-go as "Tsuruoka Hachimangū." Subsequently, Tsuruoka Hachimangū gained prominence, receiving land and monetary support from {{ill|Minamoto no Yoritomo|ja|源頼朝}} and others. Over time, it became the center for the famous {{ill|Hamaori Festival|ja|浜降祭}} and absorbed nearby shrines such as Sazuka Daimyōjin. == Deities == * {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|応神天皇}} * {{ill|Emperor Nintoku|ja|仁徳天皇}} * Sazuka Daimyōjin (associated with {{ill|Ōyamazumi Shrine|ja|大山祇神社|1=Ōyamazumi Shrine}}) * {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|Sugawara no Michizane}} (from Tsurumine Tenmangū) == Annual Events == * {{ill|Hamaori Festival|ja|浜降祭}} (Marine Day) * {{ill|Misorogi Ritual|ja|禊の神事}} (June 29, formerly held on July 15) * {{ill|Reitaisai|ja|例大祭}} (September 15) == Grounds == ; '''Great Ginkgo Tree''' : A 950-year-old tree said to have been planted by Minamoto no Yoshiie to pray for victory. ; '''Miyagane (Shrine Bell)''' : The shrine bell, originally installed in {{ill|Enpō 8 (1680)|ja|延宝8年（1680年）}}, was reconstructed after the war in 1965 as a "peace bell." === Sub-Shrines === * '''Shōnan Awashima Shrine''' — Dedicated to {{ill|Sukunahikona|ja|少彦名命}} and {{ill|Awashima-no-Kami|ja|淡島神}}. * '''Tsurumine Inari Shrine''' — Dedicated to {{ill|Ukanomitama|ja|宇迦之御魂}}. == Access == Take the JR Tōkaidō Main Line to {{ill|Chigasaki Station|ja|茅ケ崎駅}}. From the north exit, take a bus and alight at "Tsurumine Elementary School," then walk 1 minute. == References == {{Cite source help}} {{Reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|Minamoto no Yoriyoshi|ja|源頼義}} * {{ill|Iwashimizu Hachimangū|ja|石清水八幡宮}} * {{ill|Hamaori Festival|ja|浜降祭}} [[Category:Completion tier 2]][[Category: tier 2]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Tsurumine Hachiman Shrine]][[Category:qq]] {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|Sugawara no Michizane}} | Shrine rank=Former {{ill|Village shrine|ja|村社}}, Former {{ill|District shrine|ja|郷社}} | Foundation={{ill|Chōgen 3|ja|長元3rd year }} ({{ill|1030|ja|1030年}}) | Main Hall=Nagare-zukuri | Alternate name=Tsuruoka Hachiman Shrine | festival={{ill|September 15|ja|9月15日}} | Rituals={{ill|Hamaori Festival|ja|浜降祭}} ({{ill|Marine Day|ja|海の日}}) | Map=Japan Kanagawa }} '''Tsuruoka Hachimangū''' (鶴嶺八幡宮) is a {{ill|shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Chigasaki|ja|茅ヶ崎市}}, {{ill|Kanagawa Prefecture|ja|神奈川県}}. It is also known as Tsuruoka Hachiman Shrine. Renowned as the center of Hachiman worship in {{ill|Sagami Province|ja|相模国}}, the shrine has been revered since ancient times as a significant site of {{ill|Hachiman faith|ja|八幡信仰}}.Cited by the Kanagawa Prefecture Shrine Office According to the shrine, it was the first {{ill|clan shrine|ja|氏社}} established by the {{ill|Genji|ja|源氏}} when they expanded into the Kanto region. == History == The shrine was founded in {{ill|Chōgen 3 (1030)|ja|長元3rd year （1030年）}}, during the {{ill|Subjugation of Shimōsa|ja|下総の乱}} by {{ill|Minamoto no Yoriyoshi|ja|源頼義}}, who hailed from {{ill|Kawachi Province|ja|河内国}} (modern-day {{ill|Habikino|ja|羽曳野市}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}). Yoriyoshi enshrined {{ill|Iwashimizu Hachimangū|ja|石清水八幡宮}} at the site under the name "Kaijima Hachimangū" (another theory suggests it was {{ill|Usa Hachimangū|ja|宇佐八幡宮}}). Later, the Genji moved it to its current location. In {{ill|Tenki 3 (1055)|ja|天喜3rd year （1055th year ）}}, during the {{ill|Earlier Nine Years' War|ja|前九年の役}}, Yoriyoshi prayed at the shrine and achieved victory. To honor this, he established the {{ill|Tsurugaoka Hachimangū|lt=Original Hachiman Shrine|ja|元八幡}} in {{ill|Yuigahama|ja|由比ヶ浜}}, Kamakura, in {{ill|Kōhei 6 (1063)|ja|康平6年（1063rd year ）}}. In {{ill|Ōtoku 2 (1085)|ja|応徳2nd year （1085th year ）}}, Minamoto no Yoshiie, Yoriyoshi's heir, dedicated the shrine in Hamano-go as "Tsuruoka Hachimangū." Subsequently, Tsuruoka Hachimangū gained prominence, receiving land and monetary support from {{ill|Minamoto no Yoritomo|ja|源頼朝}} and others. Over time, it became the center for the famous {{ill|Hamaori Festival|ja|浜降祭}} and absorbed nearby shrines such as Sazuka Daimyōjin. == Deities == * {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|応神天皇}} * {{ill|Emperor Nintoku|ja|仁徳天皇}} * Sazuka Daimyōjin (associated with {{ill|Ōyamazumi Shrine|ja|大山祇神社|1=Ōyamazumi Shrine}}) * {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|Sugawara no Michizane}} (from Tsurumine Tenmangū) == Annual Events == * {{ill|Hamaori Festival|ja|浜降祭}} (Marine Day) * {{ill|Misorogi Ritual|ja|禊の神事}} (June 29, formerly held on July 15) * {{ill|Reitaisai|ja|例大祭}} (September 15) == Grounds == ; '''Great Ginkgo Tree''' : A 950-year-old tree said to have been planted by Minamoto no Yoshiie to pray for victory. ; '''Miyagane (Shrine Bell)''' : The shrine bell, originally installed in {{ill|Enpō 8 (1680)|ja|延宝8年（1680年）}}, was reconstructed after the war in 1965 as a "peace bell." === Sub-Shrines === * '''Shōnan Awashima Shrine''' — Dedicated to {{ill|Sukunahikona|ja|少彦名命}} and {{ill|Awashima-no-Kami|ja|淡島神}}. * '''Tsurumine Inari Shrine''' — Dedicated to {{ill|Ukanomitama|ja|宇迦之御魂}}. == Access == Take the JR Tōkaidō Main Line to {{ill|Chigasaki Station|ja|茅ケ崎駅}}. From the north exit, take a bus and alight at "Tsurumine Elementary School," then walk 1 minute. == References == {{Cite source help}} {{Reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|Minamoto no Yoriyoshi|ja|源頼義}} {{Cite source|date=June 28, 2018}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name=Tsuruoka Hachimangū | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image=[[File:GrfKanagawakenChigasakishi-Turuminehachimangu-01.jpg|270px|Tsuruoka Hachimangū Haiden, Chigasaki City]]
Haiden
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=270|coord={{coord|35|20|18.3|N|139|23|28.3|E}}}} | location=〒 253-0086
{{ill|Kanagawa Prefecture|ja|神奈川県}}{{ill|Chigasaki|ja|茅ヶ崎市}} Hamano-go 462 | ISO=JP-14 | Latitude degrees = 35|Latitude minutes = 20|Latitude seconds = 18.38 | Longitude degrees=139|Longitude minutes=23|Longitude seconds=28.30 | deity={{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|応神天皇}}
{{ill|Emperor Nintoku|ja|仁徳天皇}}
{{ill|Sazuka Daimyōjin|ja|佐塚大神}}
{{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|Sugawara no Michizane}} | Shrine rank=Former {{ill|Village shrine|ja|村社}}, Former {{ill|District shrine|ja|郷社}} | Foundation={{ill|Chōgen 3|ja|長元3rd year }} ({{ill|1030|ja|1030年}}) | Main Hall=Nagare-zukuri | Alternate name=Tsuruoka Hachiman Shrine | festival={{ill|September 15|ja|9月15日}} | Rituals={{ill|Hamaori Festival|ja|浜降祭}} ({{ill|Marine Day|ja|海の日}}) | Map=Japan Kanagawa }} '''Tsuruoka Hachimangū''' (鶴嶺八幡宮) is a {{ill|shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Chigasaki|ja|茅ヶ崎市}}, {{ill|Kanagawa Prefecture|ja|神奈川県}}. It is also known as Tsuruoka Hachiman Shrine. Renowned as the center of Hachiman worship in {{ill|Sagami Province|ja|相模国}}, the shrine has been revered since ancient times as a significant site of {{ill|Hachiman faith|ja|八幡信仰}}.Cited by the Kanagawa Prefecture Shrine Office According to the shrine, it was the first {{ill|clan shrine|ja|氏社}} established by the {{ill|Genji|ja|源氏}} when they expanded into the Kanto region. == History == The shrine was founded in {{ill|Chōgen 3 (1030)|ja|長元3rd year （1030年）}}, during the {{ill|Subjugation of Shimōsa|ja|下総の乱}} by {{ill|Minamoto no Yoriyoshi|ja|源頼義}}, who hailed from {{ill|Kawachi Province|ja|河内国}} (modern-day {{ill|Habikino|ja|羽曳野市}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}). Yoriyoshi enshrined {{ill|Iwashimizu Hachimangū|ja|石清水八幡宮}} at the site under the name "Kaijima Hachimangū" (another theory suggests it was {{ill|Usa Hachimangū|ja|宇佐八幡宮}}). Later, the Genji moved it to its current location. In {{ill|Tenki 3 (1055)|ja|天喜3rd year （1055th year ）}}, during the {{ill|Earlier Nine Years' War|ja|前九年の役}}, Yoriyoshi prayed at the shrine and achieved victory. To honor this, he established the {{ill|Tsurugaoka Hachimangū|lt=Original Hachiman Shrine|ja|元八幡}} in {{ill|Yuigahama|ja|由比ヶ浜}}, Kamakura, in {{ill|Kōhei 6 (1063)|ja|康平6年（1063rd year ）}}. In {{ill|Ōtoku 2 (1085)|ja|応徳2nd year （1085th year ）}}, Minamoto no Yoshiie, Yoriyoshi's heir, dedicated the shrine in Hamano-go as "Tsuruoka Hachimangū." Subsequently, Tsuruoka Hachimangū gained prominence, receiving land and monetary support from {{ill|Minamoto no Yoritomo|ja|源頼朝}} and others. Over time, it became the center for the famous {{ill|Hamaori Festival|ja|浜降祭}} and absorbed nearby shrines such as Sazuka Daimyōjin. == Deities == * {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|応神天皇}} * {{ill|Emperor Nintoku|ja|仁徳天皇}} * Sazuka Daimyōjin (associated with {{ill|Ōyamazumi Shrine|ja|大山祇神社|1=Ōyamazumi Shrine}}) * {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|Sugawara no Michizane}} (from Tsurumine Tenmangū) == Annual Events == * {{ill|Hamaori Festival|ja|浜降祭}} (Marine Day) * {{ill|Misorogi Ritual|ja|禊の神事}} (June 29, formerly held on July 15) * {{ill|Reitaisai|ja|例大祭}} (September 15) == Grounds == ; '''Great Ginkgo Tree''' : A 950-year-old tree said to have been planted by Minamoto no Yoshiie to pray for victory. ; '''Miyagane (Shrine Bell)''' : The shrine bell, originally installed in {{ill|Enpō 8 (1680)|ja|延宝8th year （1680年）}}, was reconstructed after the war in 1965 as a "peace bell." === Sub-Shrines === * '''Shōnan Awashima Shrine''' — Dedicated to {{ill|Sukunahikona|ja|少彦名命}} and {{ill|Awashima-no-Kami|ja|淡島神}}. * '''Tsurumine Inari Shrine''' — Dedicated to {{ill|Ukanomitama|ja|宇迦之御魂}}. == Access == Take the JR Tōkaidō Main Line to {{ill|Chigasaki Station|ja|茅ケ崎駅}}. From the north exit, take a bus and alight at "Tsurumine Elementary School," then walk 1 minute. == References == {{Cite source help}} {{Reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|Minamoto no Yoriyoshi|ja|源頼義}} {{Cite source|date=June 28, 2018}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name=Tsuruoka Hachimangū | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image=[[File:GrfKanagawakenChigasakishi-Turuminehachimangu-01.jpg|270px|Tsuruoka Hachimangū Haiden, Chigasaki City]]
Haiden
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=270|coord={{coord|35|20|18.3|N|139|23|28.3|E}}}} | location=〒 253-0086
{{ill|Kanagawa Prefecture|ja|神奈川県}}{{ill|Chigasaki|ja|茅ヶ崎市}} Hamano-go 462 | ISO=JP-14 | Latitude degrees = 35|Latitude minutes = 20|Latitude seconds = 18.38 | Longitude degrees=139|Longitude minutes=23|Longitude seconds=28.30 | deity={{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|応神天皇}}
{{ill|Emperor Nintoku|ja|仁徳天皇}}
{{ill|Sazuka Daimyōjin|ja|佐塚大神}}
{{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|Sugawara no Michizane}} | Shrine rank=Former {{ill|Village shrine|ja|村社}}, Former {{ill|District shrine|ja|郷社}} | Foundation={{ill|Chōgen 3|ja|長元3rd year }} ({{ill|1030|ja|1030th year }}) | Main Hall=Nagare-zukuri | Alternate name=Tsuruoka Hachiman Shrine | festival={{ill|September 15|ja|9月15日}} | Rituals={{ill|Hamaori Festival|ja|浜降祭}} ({{ill|Marine Day|ja|海の日}}) | Map=Japan Kanagawa }} '''Tsuruoka Hachimangū''' (鶴嶺八幡宮) is a {{ill|shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Chigasaki|ja|茅ヶ崎市}}, {{ill|Kanagawa Prefecture|ja|神奈川県}}. It is also known as Tsuruoka Hachiman Shrine. Renowned as the center of Hachiman worship in {{ill|Sagami Province|ja|相模国}}, the shrine has been revered since ancient times as a significant site of {{ill|Hachiman faith|ja|八幡信仰}}.Cited by the Kanagawa Prefecture Shrine Office According to the shrine, it was the first {{ill|clan shrine|ja|氏社}} established by the {{ill|Genji|ja|源氏}} when they expanded into the Kanto region. == History == The shrine was founded in {{ill|Chōgen 3 (1030)|ja|長元3rd year （1030th year ）}}, during the {{ill|Subjugation of Shimōsa|ja|下総の乱}} by {{ill|Minamoto no Yoriyoshi|ja|源頼義}}, who hailed from {{ill|Kawachi Province|ja|河内国}} (modern-day {{ill|Habikino|ja|羽曳野市}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}). Yoriyoshi enshrined {{ill|Iwashimizu Hachimangū|ja|石清水八幡宮}} at the site under the name "Kaijima Hachimangū" (another theory suggests it was {{ill|Usa Hachimangū|ja|宇佐八幡宮}}). Later, the Genji moved it to its current location. In {{ill|Tenki 3 (1055)|ja|天喜3rd year （1055th year ）}}, during the {{ill|Earlier Nine Years' War|ja|前九年の役}}, Yoriyoshi prayed at the shrine and achieved victory. To honor this, he established the {{ill|Tsurugaoka Hachimangū|lt=Original Hachiman Shrine|ja|元八幡}} in {{ill|Yuigahama|ja|由比ヶ浜}}, Kamakura, in {{ill|Kōhei 6 (1063)|ja|康平6年（1063rd year ）}}. In {{ill|Ōtoku 2 (1085)|ja|応徳2nd year （1085th year ）}}, Minamoto no Yoshiie, Yoriyoshi's heir, dedicated the shrine in Hamano-go as "Tsuruoka Hachimangū." Subsequently, Tsuruoka Hachimangū gained prominence, receiving land and monetary support from {{ill|Minamoto no Yoritomo|ja|源頼朝}} and others. Over time, it became the center for the famous {{ill|Hamaori Festival|ja|浜降祭}} and absorbed nearby shrines such as Sazuka Daimyōjin. == Deities == * {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|応神天皇}} * {{ill|Emperor Nintoku|ja|仁徳天皇}} * Sazuka Daimyōjin (associated with {{ill|Ōyamazumi Shrine|ja|大山祇神社|1=Ōyamazumi Shrine}}) * {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|Sugawara no Michizane}} (from Tsurumine Tenmangū) == Annual Events == * {{ill|Hamaori Festival|ja|浜降祭}} (Marine Day) * {{ill|Misorogi Ritual|ja|禊の神事}} (June 29, formerly held on July 15) * {{ill|Reitaisai|ja|例大祭}} (September 15) == Grounds == ; '''Great Ginkgo Tree''' : A 950-year-old tree said to have been planted by Minamoto no Yoshiie to pray for victory. ; '''Miyagane (Shrine Bell)''' : The shrine bell, originally installed in {{ill|Enpō 8 (1680)|ja|延宝8th year （1680th year ）}}, was reconstructed after the war in 1965 as a "peace bell." === Sub-Shrines === * '''Shōnan Awashima Shrine''' — Dedicated to {{ill|Sukunahikona|ja|少彦名命}} and {{ill|Awashima-no-Kami|ja|淡島神}}. * '''Tsurumine Inari Shrine''' — Dedicated to {{ill|Ukanomitama|ja|宇迦之御魂}}. == Access == Take the JR Tōkaidō Main Line to {{ill|Chigasaki Station|ja|茅ケ崎駅}}. From the north exit, take a bus and alight at "Tsurumine Elementary School," then walk 1 minute. == References == {{Cite source help}} {{Reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|Minamoto no Yoriyoshi|ja|源頼義}} revision.comment: Text replacement - "|少彦名命}}" to "|少彦名命|1=Sukunabikona}}"
revision.text:
{{Cite source|date=June 28, 2018}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name=Tsuruoka Hachimangū | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image=[[File:GrfKanagawakenChigasakishi-Turuminehachimangu-01.jpg|270px|Tsuruoka Hachimangū Haiden, Chigasaki City]]
Haiden
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=270|coord={{coord|35|20|18.3|N|139|23|28.3|E}}}} | location=〒 253-0086
{{ill|Kanagawa Prefecture|ja|神奈川県}}{{ill|Chigasaki|ja|茅ヶ崎市}} Hamano-go 462 | ISO=JP-14 | Latitude degrees = 35|Latitude minutes = 20|Latitude seconds = 18.38 | Longitude degrees=139|Longitude minutes=23|Longitude seconds=28.30 | deity={{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|応神天皇}}
{{ill|Emperor Nintoku|ja|仁徳天皇}}
{{ill|Sazuka Daimyōjin|ja|佐塚大神}}
{{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|Sugawara no Michizane}} | Shrine rank=Former {{ill|Village shrine|ja|村社}}, Former {{ill|District shrine|ja|郷社}} | Foundation={{ill|Chōgen 3|ja|長元3rd year }} ({{ill|1030|ja|1030th year }}) | Main Hall=Nagare-zukuri | Alternate name=Tsuruoka Hachiman Shrine | festival={{ill|September 15|ja|9月15日}} | Rituals={{ill|Hamaori Festival|ja|浜降祭}} ({{ill|Marine Day|ja|海の日}}) | Map=Japan Kanagawa }} '''Tsuruoka Hachimangū''' (鶴嶺八幡宮) is a {{ill|shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Chigasaki|ja|茅ヶ崎市}}, {{ill|Kanagawa Prefecture|ja|神奈川県}}. It is also known as Tsuruoka Hachiman Shrine. Renowned as the center of Hachiman worship in {{ill|Sagami Province|ja|相模国}}, the shrine has been revered since ancient times as a significant site of {{ill|Hachiman faith|ja|八幡信仰}}.Cited by the Kanagawa Prefecture Shrine Office According to the shrine, it was the first {{ill|clan shrine|ja|氏社}} established by the {{ill|Genji|ja|源氏}} when they expanded into the Kanto region. == History == The shrine was founded in {{ill|Chōgen 3 (1030)|ja|長元3rd year （1030th year ）}}, during the {{ill|Subjugation of Shimōsa|ja|下総の乱}} by {{ill|Minamoto no Yoriyoshi|ja|源頼義}}, who hailed from {{ill|Kawachi Province|ja|河内国}} (modern-day {{ill|Habikino|ja|羽曳野市}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}). Yoriyoshi enshrined {{ill|Iwashimizu Hachimangū|ja|石清水八幡宮}} at the site under the name "Kaijima Hachimangū" (another theory suggests it was {{ill|Usa Hachimangū|ja|宇佐八幡宮}}). Later, the Genji moved it to its current location. In {{ill|Tenki 3 (1055)|ja|天喜3rd year （1055th year ）}}, during the {{ill|Earlier Nine Years' War|ja|前九年の役}}, Yoriyoshi prayed at the shrine and achieved victory. To honor this, he established the {{ill|Tsurugaoka Hachimangū|lt=Original Hachiman Shrine|ja|元八幡}} in {{ill|Yuigahama|ja|由比ヶ浜}}, Kamakura, in {{ill|Kōhei 6 (1063)|ja|康平6年（1063rd year ）}}. In {{ill|Ōtoku 2 (1085)|ja|応徳2nd year （1085th year ）}}, Minamoto no Yoshiie, Yoriyoshi's heir, dedicated the shrine in Hamano-go as "Tsuruoka Hachimangū." Subsequently, Tsuruoka Hachimangū gained prominence, receiving land and monetary support from {{ill|Minamoto no Yoritomo|ja|源頼朝}} and others. Over time, it became the center for the famous {{ill|Hamaori Festival|ja|浜降祭}} and absorbed nearby shrines such as Sazuka Daimyōjin. == Deities == * {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|応神天皇}} * {{ill|Emperor Nintoku|ja|仁徳天皇}} * Sazuka Daimyōjin (associated with {{ill|Ōyamazumi Shrine|ja|大山祇神社|1=Ōyamazumi Shrine}}) * {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|Sugawara no Michizane}} (from Tsurumine Tenmangū) == Annual Events == * {{ill|Hamaori Festival|ja|浜降祭}} (Marine Day) * {{ill|Misorogi Ritual|ja|禊の神事}} (June 29, formerly held on July 15) * {{ill|Reitaisai|ja|例大祭}} (September 15) == Grounds == ; '''Great Ginkgo Tree''' : A 950-year-old tree said to have been planted by Minamoto no Yoshiie to pray for victory. ; '''Miyagane (Shrine Bell)''' : The shrine bell, originally installed in {{ill|Enpō 8 (1680)|ja|延宝8th year （1680th year ）}}, was reconstructed after the war in 1965 as a "peace bell." === Sub-Shrines === * '''Shōnan Awashima Shrine''' — Dedicated to {{ill|Sukunahikona|ja|少彦名命|1=Sukunabikona}} and {{ill|Awashima-no-Kami|ja|淡島神}}. * '''Tsurumine Inari Shrine''' — Dedicated to {{ill|Ukanomitama|ja|宇迦之御魂}}. == Access == Take the JR Tōkaidō Main Line to {{ill|Chigasaki Station|ja|茅ケ崎駅}}. From the north exit, take a bus and alight at "Tsurumine Elementary School," then walk 1 minute. == References == {{Cite source help}} {{Reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|Minamoto no Yoriyoshi|ja|源頼義}} * {{ill|Iwashimizu Hachimangū|ja|石清水八幡宮}} * {{ill|Hamaori Festival|ja|浜降祭}} [[Category:Completion tier 2]][[Category: tier 2]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Tsurumine Hachiman Shrine]][[Category:qq]] {{ill|Emperor Nintoku|ja|仁徳天皇}}
{{ill|Sazuka Daimyōjin|ja|佐塚大神}}
{{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|Sugawara no Michizane}} | Shrine rank=Former {{ill|Village shrine|ja|村社}}, Former {{ill|District shrine|ja|郷社}} | Foundation={{ill|Chōgen 3|ja|長元3rd year }} ({{ill|1030|ja|1030th year }}) | Main Hall=Nagare-zukuri | Alternate name=Tsuruoka Hachiman Shrine | festival={{ill|September 15|ja|9月15日}} | Rituals={{ill|Hamaori Festival|ja|浜降祭}} ({{ill|Marine Day|ja|海の日}}) | Map=Japan Kanagawa }} '''Tsuruoka Hachimangū''' (鶴嶺八幡宮) is a {{ill|shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Chigasaki|ja|茅ヶ崎市}}, {{ill|Kanagawa Prefecture|ja|神奈川県}}. It is also known as Tsuruoka Hachiman Shrine. Renowned as the center of Hachiman worship in {{ill|Sagami Province|ja|相模国}}, the shrine has been revered since ancient times as a significant site of {{ill|Hachiman faith|ja|八幡信仰}}.Cited by the Kanagawa Prefecture Shrine Office According to the shrine, it was the first {{ill|clan shrine|ja|氏社}} established by the {{ill|Genji|ja|源氏}} when they expanded into the Kanto region. == History == The shrine was founded in {{ill|Chōgen 3 (1030)|ja|長元3rd year （1030th year ）}}, during the {{ill|Subjugation of Shimōsa|ja|下総の乱}} by {{ill|Minamoto no Yoriyoshi|ja|源頼義}}, who hailed from {{ill|Kawachi Province|ja|河内国}} (modern-day {{ill|Habikino|ja|羽曳野市}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}). Yoriyoshi enshrined {{ill|Iwashimizu Hachimangū|ja|石清水八幡宮}} at the site under the name "Kaijima Hachimangū" (another theory suggests it was {{ill|Usa Hachimangū|ja|宇佐八幡宮}}). Later, the Genji moved it to its current location. In {{ill|Tenki 3 (1055)|ja|天喜3rd year （1055th year ）}}, during the {{ill|Earlier Nine Years' War|ja|前九年の役}}, Yoriyoshi prayed at the shrine and achieved victory. To honor this, he established the {{ill|Tsurugaoka Hachimangū|lt=Original Hachiman Shrine|ja|元八幡}} in {{ill|Yuigahama|ja|由比ヶ浜}}, Kamakura, in {{ill|Kōhei 6 (1063)|ja|康平6年（1063rd year ）}}. In {{ill|Ōtoku 2 (1085)|ja|応徳2nd year （1085th year ）}}, Minamoto no Yoshiie, Yoriyoshi's heir, dedicated the shrine in Hamano-go as "Tsuruoka Hachimangū." Subsequently, Tsuruoka Hachimangū gained prominence, receiving land and monetary support from {{ill|Minamoto no Yoritomo|ja|源頼朝}} and others. Over time, it became the center for the famous {{ill|Hamaori Festival|ja|浜降祭}} and absorbed nearby shrines such as Sazuka Daimyōjin. == Deities == * {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|応神天皇}} * {{ill|Emperor Nintoku|ja|仁徳天皇}} * Sazuka Daimyōjin (associated with {{ill|Ōyamazumi Shrine|ja|大山祇神社|1=Ōyamazumi Shrine}}) * {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|Sugawara no Michizane}} (from Tsurumine Tenmangū) == Annual Events == * {{ill|Hamaori Festival|ja|浜降祭}} (Marine Day) * {{ill|Misorogi Ritual|ja|禊の神事}} (June 29, formerly held on July 15) * {{ill|Reitaisai|ja|例大祭}} (September 15) == Grounds == ; '''Great Ginkgo Tree''' : A 950-year-old tree said to have been planted by Minamoto no Yoshiie to pray for victory. ; '''Miyagane (Shrine Bell)''' : The shrine bell, originally installed in {{ill|Enpō 8 (1680)|ja|延宝8th year （1680th year ）}}, was reconstructed after the war in 1965 as a "peace bell." === Sub-Shrines === * '''Shōnan Awashima Shrine''' — Dedicated to {{ill|Sukunahikona|ja|少彦名命|1=Sukunabikona}} and {{ill|Awashima-no-Kami|ja|淡島神}}. * '''Tsurumine Inari Shrine''' — Dedicated to {{ill|Ukanomitama|ja|宇迦之御魂}}. == Access == Take the JR Tōkaidō Main Line to {{ill|Chigasaki Station|ja|茅ケ崎駅}}. From the north exit, take a bus and alight at "Tsurumine Elementary School," then walk 1 minute. == References == {{Cite source help}} {{Reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|Minamoto no Yoriyoshi|ja|源頼義}} {{Cite source|date=June 28, 2018}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name=Tsuruoka Hachimangū | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image=[[File:GrfKanagawakenChigasakishi-Turuminehachimangu-01.jpg|270px|Tsuruoka Hachimangū Haiden, Chigasaki City]]
Haiden
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=270|coord={{coord|35|20|18.3|N|139|23|28.3|E}}}} | location=〒 253-0086
{{ill|Kanagawa Prefecture|ja|神奈川県}}{{ill|Chigasaki|ja|茅ヶ崎市}} Hamano-go 462 | ISO=JP-14 | Latitude degrees = 35|Latitude minutes = 20|Latitude seconds = 18.38 | Longitude degrees=139|Longitude minutes=23|Longitude seconds=28.30 | deity={{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|応神天皇}}
{{ill|Emperor Nintoku|ja|仁徳天皇}}
{{ill|Sazuka Daimyōjin|ja|佐塚大神}}
{{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|Sugawara no Michizane}} | Shrine rank=Former {{ill|Village shrine|ja|村社}}, Former {{ill|District shrine|ja|郷社}} | Foundation={{ill|Chōgen 3|ja|長元3rd year }} ({{ill|1030|ja|1030th year }}) | Main Hall=Nagare-zukuri | Alternate name=Tsuruoka Hachiman Shrine | festival={{ill|September 15|ja|9月15日}} | Rituals={{ill|Hamaori Festival|ja|浜降祭}} ({{ill|Marine Day|ja|海の日}}) | Map=Japan Kanagawa }} '''Tsuruoka Hachimangū''' (鶴嶺八幡宮) is a {{ill|shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Chigasaki|ja|茅ヶ崎市}}, {{ill|Kanagawa Prefecture|ja|神奈川県}}. It is also known as Tsuruoka Hachiman Shrine. Renowned as the center of Hachiman worship in {{ill|Sagami Province|ja|相模国}}, the shrine has been revered since ancient times as a significant site of {{ill|Hachiman faith|ja|八幡信仰}}.Cited by the Kanagawa Prefecture Shrine Office According to the shrine, it was the first {{ill|clan shrine|ja|氏社}} established by the {{ill|Genji|ja|源氏}} when they expanded into the Kanto region. == History == The shrine was founded in {{ill|Chōgen 3 (1030)|ja|長元3rd year （1030th year ）}}, during the {{ill|Subjugation of Shimōsa|ja|下総の乱}} by {{ill|Minamoto no Yoriyoshi|ja|源頼義}}, who hailed from {{ill|Kawachi Province|ja|河内国}} (modern-day {{ill|Habikino|ja|羽曳野市}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}). Yoriyoshi enshrined {{ill|Iwashimizu Hachimangū|ja|石清水八幡宮}} at the site under the name "Kaijima Hachimangū" (another theory suggests it was {{ill|Usa Hachimangū|ja|宇佐八幡宮}}). Later, the Genji moved it to its current location. In {{ill|Tenki 3 (1055)|ja|天喜3rd year （1055th year ）}}, during the {{ill|Earlier Nine Years' War|ja|前九年の役}}, Yoriyoshi prayed at the shrine and achieved victory. To honor this, he established the {{ill|Tsurugaoka Hachimangū|lt=Original Hachiman Shrine|ja|元八幡}} in {{ill|Yuigahama|ja|由比ヶ浜}}, Kamakura, in {{ill|Kōhei 6 (1063)|ja|康平6年（1063rd year ）}}. In {{ill|Ōtoku 2 (1085)|ja|応徳2nd year （1085th year ）}}, Minamoto no Yoshiie, Yoriyoshi's heir, dedicated the shrine in Hamano-go as "Tsuruoka Hachimangū." Subsequently, Tsuruoka Hachimangū gained prominence, receiving land and monetary support from {{ill|Minamoto no Yoritomo|ja|源頼朝}} and others. Over time, it became the center for the famous {{ill|Hamaori Festival|ja|浜降祭}} and absorbed nearby shrines such as Sazuka Daimyōjin. == Deities == * {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|応神天皇}} * {{ill|Emperor Nintoku|ja|仁徳天皇}} * Sazuka Daimyōjin (associated with {{ill|Ōyamazumi Shrine|ja|大山祇神社|1=Ōyamazumi Shrine}}) * {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|Sugawara no Michizane}} (from Tsurumine Tenmangū) == Annual Events == * {{ill|Hamaori Festival|ja|浜降祭}} (Marine Day) * {{ill|Misorogi Ritual|ja|禊の神事}} (June 29, formerly held on July 15) * {{ill|Reitaisai|ja|例大祭}} (September 15) == Grounds == ; '''Great Ginkgo Tree''' : A 950-year-old tree said to have been planted by Minamoto no Yoshiie to pray for victory. ; '''Miyagane (Shrine Bell)''' : The shrine bell, originally installed in {{ill|Enpō 8 (1680)|ja|延宝8th year （1680th year ）}}, was reconstructed after the war in 1965 as a "peace bell." === Sub-Shrines === * '''Shōnan Awashima Shrine''' — Dedicated to {{ill|Sukunahikona|ja|少彦名命|1=Sukunabikona}} and {{ill|Awashima-no-Kami|ja|淡島神}}. * '''Tsurumine Inari Shrine''' — Dedicated to {{ill|Ukanomitama|ja|宇迦之御魂}}. == Access == Take the JR Tōkaidō Main Line to {{ill|Chigasaki Station|ja|茅ケ崎駅}}. From the north exit, take a bus and alight at "Tsurumine Elementary School," then walk 1 minute. == References == {{Cite source help}} {{Reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|Minamoto no Yoriyoshi|ja|源頼義}} revision.text:
{{Cite source|date=June 28, 2018}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name=Tsuruoka Hachimangū | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image=[[File:GrfKanagawakenChigasakishi-Turuminehachimangu-01.jpg|270px|Tsuruoka Hachimangū Haiden, Chigasaki City]]
Haiden
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=270|coord={{coord|35|20|18.3|N|139|23|28.3|E}}}} | location=〒 253-0086
{{ill|Kanagawa Prefecture|ja|神奈川県}}{{ill|Chigasaki|ja|茅ヶ崎市}} Hamano-go 462 | ISO=JP-14 | Latitude degrees = 35|Latitude minutes = 20|Latitude seconds = 18.38 | Longitude degrees=139|Longitude minutes=23|Longitude seconds=28.30 | deity={{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|応神天皇}}
{{ill|Emperor Nintoku|ja|仁徳天皇}}
{{ill|Sazuka Daimyōjin|ja|佐塚大神}}
{{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|Sugawara no Michizane}} | Shrine rank=Former {{ill|Village shrine|ja|村社}}, Former {{ill|District shrine|ja|郷社}} | Foundation={{ill|Chōgen 3|ja|長元3rd year }} ({{ill|1030|ja|1030th year }}) | Main Hall=Nagare-zukuri | Alternate name=Tsuruoka Hachiman Shrine | festival={{ill|September 15|ja|9月15日}} | Rituals={{ill|Hamaori Festival|ja|浜降祭}} ({{ill|Marine Day|ja|海の日}}) | Map=Japan Kanagawa }} '''Tsuruoka Hachimangū''' (鶴嶺八幡宮) is a {{ill|shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Chigasaki|ja|茅ヶ崎市}}, {{ill|Kanagawa Prefecture|ja|神奈川県}}. It is also known as Tsuruoka Hachiman Shrine. Renowned as the center of Hachiman worship in {{ill|Sagami Province|ja|相模国}}, the shrine has been revered since ancient times as a significant site of {{ill|Hachiman faith|ja|八幡信仰}}.Cited by the Kanagawa Prefecture Shrine Office According to the shrine, it was the first {{ill|clan shrine|ja|氏社}} established by the {{ill|Genji|ja|源氏}} when they expanded into the Kanto region. == History == The shrine was founded in {{ill|Chōgen 3 (1030)|ja|長元3rd year （1030th year ）}}, during the {{ill|Subjugation of Shimōsa|ja|下総の乱}} by {{ill|Minamoto no Yoriyoshi|ja|源頼義}}, who hailed from {{ill|Kawachi Province|ja|河内国}} (modern-day {{ill|Habikino|ja|羽曳野市}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}). Yoriyoshi enshrined {{ill|Iwashimizu Hachimangū|ja|石清水八幡宮}} at the site under the name "Kaijima Hachimangū" (another theory suggests it was {{ill|Usa Hachimangū|ja|宇佐八幡宮}}). Later, the Genji moved it to its current location. In {{ill|Tenki 3 (1055)|ja|天喜3rd year （1055th year ）}}, during the {{ill|Earlier Nine Years' War|ja|前九年の役}}, Yoriyoshi prayed at the shrine and achieved victory. To honor this, he established the {{ill|Tsurugaoka Hachimangū|lt=Original Hachiman Shrine|ja|元八幡}} in {{ill|Yuigahama|ja|由比ヶ浜}}, Kamakura, in {{ill|Kōhei 6 (1063)|ja|康平6年（1063rd year ）}}. In {{ill|Ōtoku 2 (1085)|ja|応徳2nd year （1085th year ）}}, Minamoto no Yoshiie, Yoriyoshi's heir, dedicated the shrine in Hamano-go as "Tsuruoka Hachimangū." Subsequently, Tsuruoka Hachimangū gained prominence, receiving land and monetary support from {{ill|Minamoto no Yoritomo|ja|源頼朝}} and others. Over time, it became the center for the famous {{ill|Hamaori Festival|ja|浜降祭}} and absorbed nearby shrines such as Sazuka Daimyōjin. == Deities == * {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|応神天皇}} * {{ill|Emperor Nintoku|ja|仁徳天皇}} * Sazuka Daimyōjin (associated with {{ill|Ōyamazumi Shrine|ja|大山祇神社|1=Ōyamazumi Shrine}}) * {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|Sugawara no Michizane}} (from Tsurumine Tenmangū) == Annual Events == * {{ill|Hamaori Festival|ja|浜降祭}} (Marine Day) * {{ill|Misorogi Ritual|ja|禊の神事}} (June 29, formerly held on July 15) * {{ill|Reitaisai|ja|例大祭}} (September 15) == Grounds == ; '''Great Ginkgo Tree''' : A 950-year-old tree said to have been planted by Minamoto no Yoshiie to pray for victory. ; '''Miyagane (Shrine Bell)''' : The shrine bell, originally installed in {{ill|Enpō 8 (1680)|ja|延宝8th year （1680th year ）}}, was reconstructed after the war in 1965 as a "peace bell." === Sub-Shrines === * '''Shōnan Awashima Shrine''' — Dedicated to {{ill|Sukunahikona|ja|少彦名命|1=Sukunabikona}} and {{ill|Awashima-no-Kami|ja|淡島神}}. * '''Tsurumine Inari Shrine''' — Dedicated to {{ill|Ukanomitama|ja|宇迦之御魂}}. == Access == Take the JR Tōkaidō Main Line to {{ill|Chigasaki Station|ja|茅ケ崎駅}}. From the north exit, take a bus and alight at "Tsurumine Elementary School," then walk 1 minute. == References == {{Cite source help}} {{Reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|Minamoto no Yoriyoshi|ja|源頼義}} * {{ill|Iwashimizu Hachimangū|ja|石清水八幡宮}} * {{ill|Hamaori Festival|ja|浜降祭}} [[Category:Completion tier 2]][[Category: tier 2]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Tsurumine Hachiman Shrine]][[Category:qq]] {{Cite source|date=June 28, 2018}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name=Tsuruoka Hachimangū | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image=[[File:GrfKanagawakenChigasakishi-Turuminehachimangu-01.jpg|270px|Tsuruoka Hachimangū Haiden, Chigasaki City]]
Haiden
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=270|coord={{coord|35|20|18.3|N|139|23|28.3|E}}}} | location=〒 253-0086
{{ill|Kanagawa Prefecture|ja|神奈川県}}{{ill|Chigasaki|ja|茅ヶ崎市}} Hamano-go 462 | ISO=JP-14 | Latitude degrees = 35|Latitude minutes = 20|Latitude seconds = 18.38 | Longitude degrees=139|Longitude minutes=23|Longitude seconds=28.30 | deity={{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|応神天皇}}
{{ill|Emperor Nintoku|ja|仁徳天皇}}
{{ill|Sazuka Daimyōjin|ja|佐塚大神}}
{{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|Sugawara no Michizane}} | Shrine rank=Former {{ill|Village shrine|ja|村社}}, Former {{ill|District shrine|ja|郷社}} | Foundation={{ill|Chōgen 3|ja|長元3rd year }} ({{ill|1030|ja|1030th year }}) | Main Hall=Nagare-zukuri | Alternate name=Tsuruoka Hachiman Shrine | festival={{ill|September 15|ja|9月15日}} | Rituals={{ill|Hamaori Festival|ja|浜降祭}} ({{ill|Marine Day|ja|海の日}}) | Map=Japan Kanagawa }} '''Tsuruoka Hachimangū''' (鶴嶺八幡宮) is a {{ill|shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Chigasaki|ja|茅ヶ崎市}}, {{ill|Kanagawa Prefecture|ja|神奈川県}}. It is also known as Tsuruoka Hachiman Shrine. Renowned as the center of Hachiman worship in {{ill|Sagami Province|ja|相模国}}, the shrine has been revered since ancient times as a significant site of {{ill|Hachiman faith|ja|八幡信仰}}.Cited by the Kanagawa Prefecture Shrine Office According to the shrine, it was the first {{ill|clan shrine|ja|氏社}} established by the {{ill|Genji|ja|源氏}} when they expanded into the Kanto region. == History == The shrine was founded in {{ill|Chōgen 3 (1030)|ja|長元3rd year （1030th year ）}}, during the {{ill|Subjugation of Shimōsa|ja|下総の乱}} by {{ill|Minamoto no Yoriyoshi|ja|源頼義}}, who hailed from {{ill|Kawachi Province|ja|河内国}} (modern-day {{ill|Habikino|ja|羽曳野市}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}). Yoriyoshi enshrined {{ill|Iwashimizu Hachimangū|ja|石清水八幡宮}} at the site under the name "Kaijima Hachimangū" (another theory suggests it was {{ill|Usa Hachimangū|ja|宇佐八幡宮}}). Later, the Genji moved it to its current location. In {{ill|Tenki 3 (1055)|ja|天喜3rd year （1055th year ）}}, during the {{ill|Earlier Nine Years' War|ja|前九年の役}}, Yoriyoshi prayed at the shrine and achieved victory. To honor this, he established the {{ill|Tsurugaoka Hachimangū|lt=Original Hachiman Shrine|ja|元八幡}} in {{ill|Yuigahama|ja|由比ヶ浜}}, Kamakura, in {{ill|Kōhei 6 (1063)|ja|康平6年（1063rd year ）}}. In {{ill|Ōtoku 2 (1085)|ja|応徳2nd year （1085th year ）}}, Minamoto no Yoshiie, Yoriyoshi's heir, dedicated the shrine in Hamano-go as "Tsuruoka Hachimangū." Subsequently, Tsuruoka Hachimangū gained prominence, receiving land and monetary support from {{ill|Minamoto no Yoritomo|ja|源頼朝}} and others. Over time, it became the center for the famous {{ill|Hamaori Festival|ja|浜降祭}} and absorbed nearby shrines such as Sazuka Daimyōjin. == Deities == * {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|応神天皇}} * {{ill|Emperor Nintoku|ja|仁徳天皇}} * Sazuka Daimyōjin (associated with {{ill|Ōyamazumi Shrine|ja|大山祇神社|1=Ōyamazumi Shrine}}) * {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|Sugawara no Michizane}} (from Tsurumine Tenmangū) == Annual Events == * {{ill|Hamaori Festival|ja|浜降祭}} (Marine Day) * {{ill|Misorogi Ritual|ja|禊の神事}} (June 29, formerly held on July 15) * {{ill|Reitaisai|ja|例大祭}} (September 15) == Grounds == ; '''Great Ginkgo Tree''' : A 950-year-old tree said to have been planted by Minamoto no Yoshiie to pray for victory. ; '''Miyagane (Shrine Bell)''' : The shrine bell, originally installed in {{ill|Enpō 8 (1680)|ja|延宝8th year （1680th year ）}}, was reconstructed after the war in 1965 as a "peace bell." === Sub-Shrines === * '''Shōnan Awashima Shrine''' — Dedicated to {{ill|Sukunahikona|ja|少彦名命|1=Sukunabikona}} and {{ill|Awashima-no-Kami|ja|淡島神}}. * '''Tsurumine Inari Shrine''' — Dedicated to {{ill|Ukanomitama|ja|宇迦之御魂}}. == Access == Take the JR Tōkaidō Main Line to {{ill|Chigasaki Station|ja|茅ケ崎駅}}. From the north exit, take a bus and alight at "Tsurumine Elementary School," then walk 1 minute. == References == {{Cite source help}} {{Reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|Minamoto no Yoriyoshi|ja|源頼義}} {{Cite source|date=June 28, 2018}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name=Tsuruoka Hachimangū | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image=[[File:GrfKanagawakenChigasakishi-Turuminehachimangu-01.jpg|270px|Tsuruoka Hachimangū Haiden, Chigasaki City]]
Haiden
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=270|coord={{coord|35|20|18.3|N|139|23|28.3|E}}}} | location=〒 253-0086
{{ill|Kanagawa Prefecture|ja|神奈川県}}{{ill|Chigasaki|ja|茅ヶ崎市}} Hamano-go 462 | ISO=JP-14 | Latitude degrees = 35|Latitude minutes = 20|Latitude seconds = 18.38 | Longitude degrees=139|Longitude minutes=23|Longitude seconds=28.30 | deity={{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|応神天皇}}
{{ill|Emperor Nintoku|ja|仁徳天皇}}
{{ill|Sazuka Daimyōjin|ja|佐塚大神}}
{{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|Sugawara no Michizane}} | Shrine rank=Former {{ill|Village shrine|ja|村社}}, Former {{ill|District shrine|ja|郷社}} | Foundation={{ill|Chōgen 3|ja|長元3rd year }} ({{ill|1030|ja|1030th year }}) | Main Hall=Nagare-zukuri | Alternate name=Tsuruoka Hachiman Shrine | festival={{ill|September 15|ja|9月15日}} | Rituals={{ill|Hamaori Festival|ja|浜降祭}} ({{ill|Marine Day|ja|海の日}}) | Map=Japan Kanagawa }} '''Tsuruoka Hachimangū''' (鶴嶺八幡宮) is a {{ill|shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Chigasaki|ja|茅ヶ崎市}}, {{ill|Kanagawa Prefecture|ja|神奈川県}}. It is also known as Tsuruoka Hachiman Shrine. Renowned as the center of Hachiman worship in {{ill|Sagami Province|ja|相模国}}, the shrine has been revered since ancient times as a significant site of {{ill|Hachiman faith|ja|八幡信仰}}.Cited by the Kanagawa Prefecture Shrine Office According to the shrine, it was the first {{ill|clan shrine|ja|氏社}} established by the {{ill|Genji|ja|源氏}} when they expanded into the Kanto region. == History == The shrine was founded in {{ill|Chōgen 3 (1030)|ja|長元3rd year （1030th year ）}}, during the {{ill|Subjugation of Shimōsa|ja|下総の乱}} by {{ill|Minamoto no Yoriyoshi|ja|源頼義}}, who hailed from {{ill|Kawachi Province|ja|河内国}} (modern-day {{ill|Habikino|ja|羽曳野市}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}). Yoriyoshi enshrined {{ill|Iwashimizu Hachimangū|ja|石清水八幡宮}} at the site under the name "Kaijima Hachimangū" (another theory suggests it was {{ill|Usa Hachimangū|ja|宇佐八幡宮}}). Later, the Genji moved it to its current location. In {{ill|Tenki 3 (1055)|ja|天喜3rd year （1055th year ）}}, during the {{ill|Earlier Nine Years' War|ja|前九年の役}}, Yoriyoshi prayed at the shrine and achieved victory. To honor this, he established the {{ill|Tsurugaoka Hachimangū|lt=Original Hachiman Shrine|ja|元八幡}} in {{ill|Yuigahama|ja|由比ヶ浜}}, Kamakura, in {{ill|Kōhei 6 (1063)|ja|康平6年（1063rd year ）}}. In {{ill|Ōtoku 2 (1085)|ja|応徳2nd year （1085th year ）}}, Minamoto no Yoshiie, Yoriyoshi's heir, dedicated the shrine in Hamano-go as "Tsuruoka Hachimangū." Subsequently, Tsuruoka Hachimangū gained prominence, receiving land and monetary support from {{ill|Minamoto no Yoritomo|ja|源頼朝}} and others. Over time, it became the center for the famous {{ill|Hamaori Festival|ja|浜降祭}} and absorbed nearby shrines such as Sazuka Daimyōjin. == Deities == * {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|応神天皇}} * {{ill|Emperor Nintoku|ja|仁徳天皇}} * Sazuka Daimyōjin (associated with {{ill|Ōyamazumi Shrine|ja|大山祇神社|1=Ōyamazumi Shrine}}) * {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|Sugawara no Michizane}} (from Tsurumine Tenmangū) == Annual Events == * {{ill|Hamaori Festival|ja|浜降祭}} (Marine Day) * {{ill|Misorogi Ritual|ja|禊の神事}} (June 29, formerly held on July 15) * {{ill|Reitaisai|ja|例大祭}} (September 15) == Grounds == ; '''Great Ginkgo Tree''' : A 950-year-old tree said to have been planted by Minamoto no Yoshiie to pray for victory. ; '''Miyagane (Shrine Bell)''' : The shrine bell, originally installed in {{ill|Enpō 8 (1680)|ja|延宝8th year （1680th year ）}}, was reconstructed after the war in 1965 as a "peace bell." === Sub-Shrines === * '''Shōnan Awashima Shrine''' — Dedicated to {{ill|Sukunahikona|ja|少彦名命|1=Sukunabikona}} and {{ill|Awashima-no-Kami|ja|淡島神}}. * '''Tsurumine Inari Shrine''' — Dedicated to {{ill|Ukanomitama|ja|宇迦之御魂}}. == Access == Take the JR Tōkaidō Main Line to {{ill|Chigasaki Station|ja|茅ケ崎駅}}. From the north exit, take a bus and alight at "Tsurumine Elementary School," then walk 1 minute. == References == {{Cite source help}} {{Reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|Minamoto no Yoriyoshi|ja|源頼義}} * {{ill|Iwashimizu Hachimangū|ja|石清水八幡宮}} * {{ill|Hamaori Festival|ja|浜降祭}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Tsurumine Hachiman Shrine]] [[Category:Shrines in Kanagawa]] [[Category:Former Village Shrines in Japan]] [[Category:Former District Shrines in Japan]] [[Category:Hachiman Shrines]] [[Category:Chigasaki City History]] [[Category:1 revision imported]] [[Category:refreshing imported pages]] [[Category:refreshing redirects 1-26]] [[Category:refreshing redirects 1-27]] [[Category:second attempt at refreshing on January 27 2024]] [[Category:S11]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:return the see also]] [[Category:pages which might possibly be incomplete but are most likely complete]] [[Category:I think I cleared all the incomplete or blank pages earlier]] }} [[sn:Tsurumine Hachiman Shrine]] [[ja:鶴嶺八幡宮]] [[sn:surumine Hachiman Shrine]] [[en:Tsurumine Hachiman Shrine]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Tsurumine Hachiman Shrine}}

Over time, it became the center for the famous {{ill|Hamaori Festival|ja|浜降祭|12=draft}} and absorbed nearby shrines such as Sazuka Daimyōjin. == Deities == * {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|応神天皇|12=draft}} * {{ill|Emperor Nintoku|ja|仁徳天皇|12=draft}} * Sazuka Daimyōjin (associated with {{ill|Ōyamazumi Shrine|ja|大山祇神社|1=Ōyamazumi Shrine|12=draft}}) * {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|Sugawara no Michizane|12=draft}} (from Tsurumine Tenmangū) == Annual Events == * {{ill|Hamaori Festival|ja|浜降祭|12=draft}} (Marine Day) * {{ill|Misorogi Ritual|ja|禊の神事|12=draft}} (June 29, formerly held on July 15) * {{ill|Reitaisai|ja|例大祭|12=draft}} (September 15) == Grounds == ; '''Great Ginkgo Tree''' : A 950-year-old tree said to have been planted by Minamoto no Yoshiie to pray for victory. ; '''Miyagane (Shrine Bell)''' : The shrine bell, originally installed in {{ill|Enpō 8 (1680)|ja|延宝8th year （1680th year ）|12=draft}}, was reconstructed after the war in 1965 as a "peace bell." === Sub-Shrines === * '''Shōnan Awashima Shrine''' — Dedicated to {{ill|Sukunahikona|ja|少彦名命|1=Sukunabikona|12=draft}} and {{ill|Awashima-no-Kami|ja|淡島神|12=draft}}. * '''Tsurumine Inari Shrine''' — Dedicated to {{ill|Ukanomitama|ja|宇迦之御魂|12=draft}}. == Access == Take the JR Tōkaidō Main Line to {{ill|Chigasaki Station|ja|茅ケ崎駅|12=draft}}. From the north exit, take a bus and alight at "Tsurumine Elementary School," then walk 1 minute. == References == {{Cite source help}} {{Reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|Minamoto no Yoriyoshi|ja|源頼義|12=draft}} * {{ill|Iwashimizu Hachimangū|ja|石清水八幡宮|12=draft}} * {{ill|Hamaori Festival|ja|浜降祭|12=draft}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Tsurumine Hachiman Shrine]] [[Category:Shrines in Kanagawa]] [[Category:Former Village Shrines in Japan]] [[Category:Former District Shrines in Japan]] [[Category:Hachiman Shrines]] [[Category:Chigasaki City History]] [[Category:1 revision imported]] [[Category:refreshing imported pages]] [[Category:refreshing redirects 1-26]] [[Category:refreshing redirects 1-27]] [[Category:second attempt at refreshing on January 27 2024]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:pages which might possibly be incomplete but are most likely complete]] [[Category:I think I cleared all the incomplete or blank pages earlier]] }} [[sn:Tsurumine Hachiman Shrine]] [[ja:鶴嶺八幡宮]] [[sn:surumine Hachiman Shrine]] [[en:Tsurumine Hachiman Shrine]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Tsurumine Hachiman Shrine}}

{{Cite source|date=June 28, 2018}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name=Tsuruoka Hachimangū | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image=[[File:GrfKanagawakenChigasakishi-Turuminehachimangu-01.jpg|270px|Tsuruoka Hachimangū Haiden, Chigasaki City]]
Haiden
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=270|coord={{coord|35|20|18.3|N|139|23|28.3|E}}}} | location=〒 253-0086
{{ill|Kanagawa Prefecture|ja|神奈川県|12=draft}}{{ill|Chigasaki|ja|茅ヶ崎市|12=draft}} Hamano-go 462 | ISO=JP-14 | Latitude degrees = 35|Latitude minutes = 20|Latitude seconds = 18.38 | Longitude degrees=139|Longitude minutes=23|Longitude seconds=28.30 | deity={{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|応神天皇|12=draft}}
{{ill|Emperor Nintoku|ja|仁徳天皇|12=draft}}
{{ill|Sazuka Daimyōjin|ja|佐塚大神|12=draft}}
{{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|Sugawara no Michizane|12=draft}} | Shrine rank=Former {{ill|Village shrine|ja|村社|12=draft}}, Former {{ill|District shrine|ja|郷社|12=draft}} | Foundation={{ill|Chōgen 3|ja|長元3rd year|12=draft}} ({{ill|1030|ja|1030th year|12=draft}}) | Main Hall=Nagare-zukuri | Alternate name=Tsuruoka Hachiman Shrine | festival={{ill|September 15|ja|9月15日|12=draft}} | Rituals={{ill|Hamaori Festival|ja|浜降祭|12=draft}} ({{ill|Marine Day|ja|海の日|12=draft}}) | Map=Japan Kanagawa }} '''Tsuruoka Hachimangū''' (鶴嶺八幡宮) is a {{ill|shrine|ja|神社|12=draft}} located in {{ill|Chigasaki|ja|茅ヶ崎市|12=draft}}, {{ill|Kanagawa Prefecture|ja|神奈川県|12=draft}}. It is also known as Tsuruoka Hachiman Shrine. Renowned as the center of Hachiman worship in {{ill|Sagami Province|ja|相模国|12=draft}}, the shrine has been revered since ancient times as a significant site of {{ill|Hachiman faith|ja|八幡信仰|12=draft}}.Cited by the Kanagawa Prefecture Shrine Office According to the shrine, it was the first {{ill|clan shrine|ja|氏社|12=draft}} established by the {{ill|Genji|ja|源氏|12=draft}} when they expanded into the Kanto region. == History == The shrine was founded in {{ill|Chōgen 3 (1030)|ja|長元3rd year （1030th year ）|12=draft}}, during the {{ill|Subjugation of Shimōsa|ja|下総の乱|12=draft}} by {{ill|Minamoto no Yoriyoshi|ja|源頼義|12=draft}}, who hailed from {{ill|Kawachi Province|ja|河内国|12=draft}} (modern-day {{ill|Habikino|ja|羽曳野市|12=draft}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府|12=draft}}). Yoriyoshi enshrined {{ill|Iwashimizu Hachimangū|ja|石清水八幡宮|12=draft}} at the site under the name "Kaijima Hachimangū" (another theory suggests it was {{ill|Usa Hachimangū|ja|宇佐八幡宮|12=draft}}). Later, the Genji moved it to its current location. In {{ill|Tenki 3 (1055)|ja|天喜3rd year （1055th year ）|12=draft}}, during the {{ill|Earlier Nine Years' War|ja|前九年の役|12=draft}}, Yoriyoshi prayed at the shrine and achieved victory. To honor this, he established the {{ill|Tsurugaoka Hachimangū|lt=Original Hachiman Shrine|ja|元八幡|12=draft}} in {{ill|Yuigahama|ja|由比ヶ浜|12=draft}}, Kamakura, in {{ill|Kōhei 6 (1063)|ja|康平6年（1063rd year ）|12=draft}}. In {{ill|Ōtoku 2 (1085)|ja|応徳2nd year （1085th year ）|12=draft}}, Minamoto no Yoshiie, Yoriyoshi's heir, dedicated the shrine in Hamano-go as "Tsuruoka Hachimangū." Subsequently, Tsuruoka Hachimangū gained prominence, receiving land and monetary support from {{ill|Minamoto no Yoritomo|ja|源頼朝|12=draft}} and others. Over time, it became the center for the famous {{ill|Hamaori Festival|ja|浜降祭|12=draft}} and absorbed nearby shrines such as Sazuka Daimyōjin. == Deities == * {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|応神天皇|12=draft}} * {{ill|Emperor Nintoku|ja|仁徳天皇|12=draft}} * Sazuka Daimyōjin (associated with {{ill|Ōyamazumi Shrine|ja|大山祇神社|1=Ōyamazumi Shrine|12=draft}}) * {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|Sugawara no Michizane|12=draft}} (from Tsurumine Tenmangū) == Annual Events == * {{ill|Hamaori Festival|ja|浜降祭|12=draft}} (Marine Day) * {{ill|Misorogi Ritual|ja|禊の神事|12=draft}} (June 29, formerly held on July 15) * {{ill|Reitaisai|ja|例大祭|12=draft}} (September 15) == Grounds == ; '''Great Ginkgo Tree''' : A 950-year-old tree said to have been planted by Minamoto no Yoshiie to pray for victory. ; '''Miyagane (Shrine Bell)''' : The shrine bell, originally installed in {{ill|Enpō 8 (1680)|ja|延宝8th year （1680th year ）|12=draft}}, was reconstructed after the war in 1965 as a "peace bell." === Sub-Shrines === * '''Shōnan Awashima Shrine''' — Dedicated to {{ill|Sukunahikona|ja|少彦名命|1=Sukunabikona|12=draft}} and {{ill|Awashima-no-Kami|ja|淡島神|12=draft}}. * '''Tsurumine Inari Shrine''' — Dedicated to {{ill|Ukanomitama|ja|宇迦之御魂|12=draft}}. == Access == Take the JR Tōkaidō Main Line to {{ill|Chigasaki Station|ja|茅ケ崎駅|12=draft}}. From the north exit, take a bus and alight at "Tsurumine Elementary School," then walk 1 minute. == References == {{Cite source help}} {{Reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|Minamoto no Yoriyoshi|ja|源頼義|12=draft}} * {{ill|Iwashimizu Hachimangū|ja|石清水八幡宮|12=draft}} * {{ill|Hamaori Festival|ja|浜降祭|12=draft}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Tsurumine Hachiman Shrine]] [[Category:Shrines in Kanagawa]] [[Category:Former Village Shrines in Japan]] [[Category:Former District Shrines in Japan]] [[Category:Hachiman Shrines]] [[Category:Chigasaki City History]] [[Category:1 revision imported]] [[Category:refreshing imported pages]] [[Category:refreshing redirects 1-26]] [[Category:second attempt at refreshing on January 27 2024]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:pages which might possibly be incomplete but are most likely complete]] [[Category:I think I cleared all the incomplete or blank pages earlier]] }} [[sn:Tsurumine Hachiman Shrine]] [[ja:鶴嶺八幡宮]] [[sn:surumine Hachiman Shrine]] [[en:Tsurumine Hachiman Shrine]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Tsurumine Hachiman Shrine}}

{{ill|Emperor Nintoku|ja|仁徳天皇|12=draft}}
{{ill|Sazuka Daimyōjin|ja|佐塚大神|12=draft}}
{{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|Sugawara no Michizane|12=draft}} | Shrine rank=Former {{ill|Village shrine|ja|村社|12=draft}}, Former {{ill|District shrine|ja|郷社|12=draft}} | Foundation={{ill|Chōgen 3|ja|長元3rd year|12=draft}} ({{ill|1030|ja|1030th year|12=draft}}) | Main Hall=Nagare-zukuri | Alternate name=Tsuruoka Hachiman Shrine | festival={{ill|September 15|ja|9月15日|12=draft}} | Rituals={{ill|Hamaori Festival|ja|浜降祭|12=draft}} ({{ill|Marine Day|ja|海の日|12=draft}}) | Map=Japan Kanagawa }} '''Tsuruoka Hachimangū''' (鶴嶺八幡宮) is a {{ill|shrine|ja|神社|12=draft}} located in {{ill|Chigasaki|ja|茅ヶ崎市|12=draft}}, {{ill|Kanagawa Prefecture|ja|神奈川県|12=draft}}. It is also known as Tsuruoka Hachiman Shrine. Renowned as the center of Hachiman worship in {{ill|Sagami Province|ja|相模国|12=draft}}, the shrine has been revered since ancient times as a significant site of {{ill|Hachiman faith|ja|八幡信仰|12=draft}}.Cited by the Kanagawa Prefecture Shrine Office According to the shrine, it was the first {{ill|clan shrine|ja|氏社|12=draft}} established by the {{ill|Genji|ja|源氏|12=draft}} when they expanded into the Kanto region. == History == The shrine was founded in {{ill|Chōgen 3 (1030)|ja|長元3rd year （1030th year ）|12=draft}}, during the {{ill|Subjugation of Shimōsa|ja|下総の乱|12=draft}} by {{ill|Minamoto no Yoriyoshi|ja|源頼義|12=draft}}, who hailed from {{ill|Kawachi Province|ja|河内国|12=draft}} (modern-day {{ill|Habikino|ja|羽曳野市|12=draft}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府|12=draft}}). Yoriyoshi enshrined {{ill|Iwashimizu Hachimangū|ja|石清水八幡宮|12=draft}} at the site under the name "Kaijima Hachimangū" (another theory suggests it was {{ill|Usa Hachimangū|ja|宇佐八幡宮|12=draft}}). Later, the Genji moved it to its current location. In {{ill|Tenki 3 (1055)|ja|天喜3rd year （1055th year ）|12=draft}}, during the {{ill|Earlier Nine Years' War|ja|前九年の役|12=draft}}, Yoriyoshi prayed at the shrine and achieved victory. To honor this, he established the {{ill|Tsurugaoka Hachimangū|lt=Original Hachiman Shrine|ja|元八幡|12=draft}} in {{ill|Yuigahama|ja|由比ヶ浜|12=draft}}, Kamakura, in {{ill|Kōhei 6 (1063)|ja|康平6年（1063rd year ）|12=draft}}. In {{ill|Ōtoku 2 (1085)|ja|応徳2nd year （1085th year ）|12=draft}}, Minamoto no Yoshiie, Yoriyoshi's heir, dedicated the shrine in Hamano-go as "Tsuruoka Hachimangū." Subsequently, Tsuruoka Hachimangū gained prominence, receiving land and monetary support from {{ill|Minamoto no Yoritomo|ja|源頼朝|12=draft}} and others. Over time, it became the center for the famous {{ill|Hamaori Festival|ja|浜降祭|12=draft}} and absorbed nearby shrines such as Sazuka Daimyōjin. == Deities == * {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|応神天皇|12=draft}} * {{ill|Emperor Nintoku|ja|仁徳天皇|12=draft}} * Sazuka Daimyōjin (associated with {{ill|Ōyamazumi Shrine|ja|大山祇神社|1=Ōyamazumi Shrine|12=draft}}) * {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|Sugawara no Michizane|12=draft}} (from Tsurumine Tenmangū) == Annual Events == * {{ill|Hamaori Festival|ja|浜降祭|12=draft}} (Marine Day) * {{ill|Misorogi Ritual|ja|禊の神事|12=draft}} (June 29, formerly held on July 15) * {{ill|Reitaisai|ja|例大祭|12=draft}} (September 15) == Grounds == ; '''Great Ginkgo Tree''' : A 950-year-old tree said to have been planted by Minamoto no Yoshiie to pray for victory. ; '''Miyagane (Shrine Bell)''' : The shrine bell, originally installed in {{ill|Enpō 8 (1680)|ja|延宝8th year （1680th year ）|12=draft}}, was reconstructed after the war in 1965 as a "peace bell." === Sub-Shrines === * '''Shōnan Awashima Shrine''' — Dedicated to {{ill|Sukunahikona|ja|少彦名命|1=Sukunabikona|12=draft}} and {{ill|Awashima-no-Kami|ja|淡島神|12=draft}}. * '''Tsurumine Inari Shrine''' — Dedicated to {{ill|Ukanomitama|ja|宇迦之御魂|12=draft}}. == Access == Take the JR Tōkaidō Main Line to {{ill|Chigasaki Station|ja|茅ケ崎駅|12=draft}}. From the north exit, take a bus and alight at "Tsurumine Elementary School," then walk 1 minute. == References == {{Cite source help}} {{Reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|Minamoto no Yoriyoshi|ja|源頼義|12=draft}} * {{ill|Iwashimizu Hachimangū|ja|石清水八幡宮|12=draft}} * {{ill|Hamaori Festival|ja|浜降祭|12=draft}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Tsurumine Hachiman Shrine]] [[Category:Shrines in Kanagawa]] [[Category:Former Village Shrines in Japan]] [[Category:Former District Shrines in Japan]] [[Category:Hachiman Shrines]] [[Category:Chigasaki City History]] [[Category:refreshing imported pages]] [[Category:refreshing redirects 1-26]] [[Category:second attempt at refreshing on January 27 2024]] [[Category:New]] }} [[sn:Tsurumine Hachiman Shrine]] [[ja:鶴嶺八幡宮]] [[sn:surumine Hachiman Shrine]] [[en:Tsurumine Hachiman Shrine]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Tsurumine Hachiman Shrine}}

Over time, it became the center for the famous {{ill|Hamaori Festival|ja|浜降祭|12=draft|12=draft}} and absorbed nearby shrines such as Sazuka Daimyōjin. == Deities == * {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|応神天皇|12=draft|12=draft}} * {{ill|Emperor Nintoku|ja|仁徳天皇|12=draft|12=draft}} * Sazuka Daimyōjin (associated with {{ill|Ōyamazumi Shrine|ja|大山祇神社|1=Ōyamazumi Shrine|12=draft|12=draft}}) * {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|Sugawara no Michizane|12=draft|12=draft}} (from Tsurumine Tenmangū) == Annual Events == * {{ill|Hamaori Festival|ja|浜降祭|12=draft|12=draft}} (Marine Day) * {{ill|Misorogi Ritual|ja|禊の神事|12=draft|12=draft}} (June 29, formerly held on July 15) * {{ill|Reitaisai|ja|例大祭|12=draft|12=draft}} (September 15) == Grounds == ; '''Great Ginkgo Tree''' : A 950-year-old tree said to have been planted by Minamoto no Yoshiie to pray for victory. ; '''Miyagane (Shrine Bell)''' : The shrine bell, originally installed in {{ill|Enpō 8 (1680)|ja|延宝8th year （1680th year ）|12=draft|12=draft}}, was reconstructed after the war in 1965 as a "peace bell." === Sub-Shrines === * '''Shōnan Awashima Shrine''' — Dedicated to {{ill|Sukunahikona|ja|少彦名命|1=Sukunabikona|12=draft|12=draft}} and {{ill|Awashima-no-Kami|ja|淡島神|12=draft|12=draft}}. * '''Tsurumine Inari Shrine''' — Dedicated to {{ill|Ukanomitama|ja|宇迦之御魂|12=draft|12=draft}}. == Access == Take the JR Tōkaidō Main Line to {{ill|Chigasaki Station|ja|茅ケ崎駅|12=draft|12=draft}}. From the north exit, take a bus and alight at "Tsurumine Elementary School," then walk 1 minute. == References == {{Cite source help}} {{Reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|Minamoto no Yoriyoshi|ja|源頼義|12=draft|12=draft}} * {{ill|Iwashimizu Hachimangū|ja|石清水八幡宮|12=draft|12=draft}} * {{ill|Hamaori Festival|ja|浜降祭|12=draft|12=draft}} {{draft categories| }} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Tsurumine Hachiman Shrine]] [[Category:Shrines in Kanagawa]] [[Category:Former Village Shrines in Japan]] [[Category:Former District Shrines in Japan]] [[Category:Hachiman Shrines]] [[Category:Chigasaki City History]] [[Category:refreshing imported pages]] [[Category:New]] }} [[sn:Tsurumine Hachiman Shrine]] [[ja:鶴嶺八幡宮]] [[sn:surumine Hachiman Shrine]] [[en:Tsurumine Hachiman Shrine]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Tsurumine Hachiman Shrine}}

{{Cite source|date=June 28, 2018}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name=Tsuruoka Hachimangū | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image=[[File:GrfKanagawakenChigasakishi-Turuminehachimangu-01.jpg|270px|Tsuruoka Hachimangū Haiden, Chigasaki City]]
Haiden
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=270|coord={{coord|35|20|18.3|N|139|23|28.3|E}}}} | location=〒 253-0086
{{ill|Kanagawa Prefecture|ja|神奈川県|12=draft|12=draft}}{{ill|Chigasaki|ja|茅ヶ崎市|12=draft|12=draft}} Hamano-go 462 | ISO=JP-14 | Latitude degrees = 35|Latitude minutes = 20|Latitude seconds = 18.38 | Longitude degrees=139|Longitude minutes=23|Longitude seconds=28.30 | deity={{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|応神天皇|12=draft|12=draft}}
{{ill|Emperor Nintoku|ja|仁徳天皇|12=draft|12=draft}}
{{ill|Sazuka Daimyōjin|ja|佐塚大神|12=draft|12=draft}}
{{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|Sugawara no Michizane|12=draft|12=draft}} | Shrine Rank=Former {{ill|Village shrine|ja|村社|12=draft|12=draft}}, Former {{ill|District shrine|ja|郷社|12=draft|12=draft}} | Foundation={{ill|Chōgen 3|ja|長元3rd year|12=draft|12=draft}} ({{ill|1030|ja|1030th year|12=draft|12=draft}}) | Main Hall=Nagare-zukuri | Alternate name=Tsuruoka Hachiman Shrine | festival={{ill|September 15|ja|9月15日|12=draft|12=draft}} | Rituals={{ill|Hamaori Festival|ja|浜降祭|12=draft|12=draft}} ({{ill|Marine Day|ja|海の日|12=draft|12=draft}}) | Map=Japan Kanagawa }} '''Tsuruoka Hachimangū''' (鶴嶺八幡宮) is a {{ill|shrine|ja|神社|12=draft|12=draft}} located in {{ill|Chigasaki|ja|茅ヶ崎市|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Kanagawa Prefecture|ja|神奈川県|12=draft|12=draft}}. It is also known as Tsuruoka Hachiman Shrine. Renowned as the center of Hachiman worship in {{ill|Sagami Province|ja|相模国|12=draft|12=draft}}, the shrine has been revered since ancient times as a significant site of {{ill|Hachiman faith|ja|八幡信仰|12=draft|12=draft}}.Cited by the Kanagawa Prefecture Shrine Office According to the shrine, it was the first {{ill|clan shrine|ja|氏社|12=draft|12=draft}} established by the {{ill|Genji|ja|源氏|12=draft|12=draft}} when they expanded into the Kanto region. == History == The shrine was founded in {{ill|Chōgen 3 (1030)|ja|長元3rd year （1030th year ）|12=draft|12=draft}}, during the {{ill|Subjugation of Shimōsa|ja|下総の乱|12=draft|12=draft}} by {{ill|Minamoto no Yoriyoshi|ja|源頼義|12=draft|12=draft}}, who hailed from {{ill|Kawachi Province|ja|河内国|12=draft|12=draft}} (modern-day {{ill|Habikino|ja|羽曳野市|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府|12=draft|12=draft}}). Yoriyoshi enshrined {{ill|Iwashimizu Hachimangū|ja|石清水八幡宮|12=draft|12=draft}} at the site under the name "Kaijima Hachimangū" (another theory suggests it was {{ill|Usa Hachimangū|ja|宇佐八幡宮|12=draft|12=draft}}). Later, the Genji moved it to its current location. In {{ill|Tenki 3 (1055)|ja|天喜3rd year （1055th year ）|12=draft|12=draft}}, during the {{ill|Earlier Nine Years' War|ja|前九年の役|12=draft|12=draft}}, Yoriyoshi prayed at the shrine and achieved victory. To honor this, he established the {{ill|Tsurugaoka Hachimangū|lt=Original Hachiman Shrine|ja|元八幡|12=draft|12=draft}} in {{ill|Yuigahama|ja|由比ヶ浜|12=draft|12=draft}}, Kamakura, in {{ill|Kōhei 6 (1063)|ja|康平6年（1063rd year ）|12=draft|12=draft}}. In {{ill|Ōtoku 2 (1085)|ja|応徳2nd year （1085th year ）|12=draft|12=draft}}, Minamoto no Yoshiie, Yoriyoshi's heir, dedicated the shrine in Hamano-go as "Tsuruoka Hachimangū." Subsequently, Tsuruoka Hachimangū gained prominence, receiving land and monetary support from {{ill|Minamoto no Yoritomo|ja|源頼朝|12=draft|12=draft}} and others. Over time, it became the center for the famous {{ill|Hamaori Festival|ja|浜降祭|12=draft|12=draft}} and absorbed nearby shrines such as Sazuka Daimyōjin. == Deities == * {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|応神天皇|12=draft|12=draft}} * {{ill|Emperor Nintoku|ja|仁徳天皇|12=draft|12=draft}} * Sazuka Daimyōjin (associated with {{ill|Ōyamazumi Shrine|ja|大山祇神社|1=Ōyamazumi Shrine|12=draft|12=draft}}) * {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|Sugawara no Michizane|12=draft|12=draft}} (from Tsurumine Tenmangū) == Annual Events == * {{ill|Hamaori Festival|ja|浜降祭|12=draft|12=draft}} (Marine Day) * {{ill|Misorogi Ritual|ja|禊の神事|12=draft|12=draft}} (June 29, formerly held on July 15) * {{ill|Reitaisai|ja|例大祭|12=draft|12=draft}} (September 15) == Grounds == ; '''Great Ginkgo Tree''' : A 950-year-old tree said to have been planted by Minamoto no Yoshiie to pray for victory. ; '''Miyagane (Shrine Bell)''' : The shrine bell, originally installed in {{ill|Enpō 8 (1680)|ja|延宝8th year （1680th year ）|12=draft|12=draft}}, was reconstructed after the war in 1965 as a "peace bell." === Sub-Shrines === * '''Shōnan Awashima Shrine''' — Dedicated to {{ill|Sukunahikona|ja|少彦名命|1=Sukunabikona|12=draft|12=draft}} and {{ill|Awashima-no-Kami|ja|淡島神|12=draft|12=draft}}. * '''Tsurumine Inari Shrine''' — Dedicated to {{ill|Ukanomitama|ja|宇迦之御魂|12=draft|12=draft}}. == Access == Take the JR Tōkaidō Main Line to {{ill|Chigasaki Station|ja|茅ケ崎駅|12=draft|12=draft}}. From the north exit, take a bus and alight at "Tsurumine Elementary School," then walk 1 minute. == References == {{Cite source help}} {{Reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|Minamoto no Yoriyoshi|ja|源頼義|12=draft|12=draft}} * {{ill|Iwashimizu Hachimangū|ja|石清水八幡宮|12=draft|12=draft}} * {{ill|Hamaori Festival|ja|浜降祭|12=draft|12=draft}} {{draft categories| }} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Tsurumine Hachiman Shrine]] [[Category:Shrines in Kanagawa]] [[Category:Former Village Shrines in Japan]] [[Category:Former District Shrines in Japan]] [[Category:Hachiman Shrines]] [[Category:Chigasaki City History]] [[Category:refreshing imported pages]] [[Category:New]] }} [[sn:Tsurumine Hachiman Shrine]] [[ja:鶴嶺八幡宮]] [[sn:surumine Hachiman Shrine]] [[en:Tsurumine Hachiman Shrine]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Tsurumine Hachiman Shrine}}

Subsequently, Tsuruoka Hachimangū gained prominence, receiving land and monetary support from {{ill|Minamoto no Yoritomo|ja|源頼朝|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and others. Over time, it became the center for the famous {{ill|Hamaori Festival|ja|浜降祭|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and absorbed nearby shrines such as Sazuka Daimyōjin. == Deities == * {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|応神天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Emperor Nintoku|ja|仁徳天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * Sazuka Daimyōjin (associated with {{ill|Ōyamazumi Shrine|ja|大山祇神社|1=Ōyamazumi Shrine|qq=draft|qq=draft}}) * {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|Sugawara no Michizane|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (from Tsurumine Tenmangū) == Annual Events == * {{ill|Hamaori Festival|ja|浜降祭|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (Marine Day) * {{ill|Misorogi Ritual|ja|禊の神事|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (June 29, formerly held on July 15) * {{ill|Reitaisai|ja|例大祭|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (September 15) == Grounds == ; '''Great Ginkgo Tree''' : A 950-year-old tree said to have been planted by Minamoto no Yoshiie to pray for victory. ; '''Miyagane (Shrine Bell)''' : The shrine bell, originally installed in {{ill|Enpō 8 (1680)|ja|延宝8th year （1680th year ）|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, was reconstructed after the war in 1965 as a "peace bell." === Sub-Shrines === * '''Shōnan Awashima Shrine''' — Dedicated to {{ill|Sukunahikona|ja|少彦名命|1=Sukunabikona|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and {{ill|Awashima-no-Kami|ja|淡島神|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. * '''Tsurumine Inari Shrine''' — Dedicated to {{ill|Ukanomitama|ja|宇迦之御魂|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. == Access == Take the JR Tōkaidō Main Line to {{ill|Chigasaki Station|ja|茅ケ崎駅|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. From the north exit, take a bus and alight at "Tsurumine Elementary School," then walk 1 minute. == References == {{Cite source help}} {{Reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|Minamoto no Yoriyoshi|ja|源頼義|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Iwashimizu Hachimangū|ja|石清水八幡宮|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Hamaori Festival|ja|浜降祭|qq=draft|qq=draft}} {{draft categories| }} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Tsurumine Hachiman Shrine]] [[Category:Shrines in Kanagawa]] [[Category:Former Village Shrines in Japan]] [[Category:Former District Shrines in Japan]] [[Category:Hachiman Shrines]] [[Category:Chigasaki City History]] [[Category:New]] }} [[sn:Tsurumine Hachiman Shrine]] [[ja:鶴嶺八幡宮]] [[sn:surumine Hachiman Shrine]] [[en:Tsurumine Hachiman Shrine]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Tsurumine Hachiman Shrine}}

{{Cite source|date=June 28, 2018}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name=Tsuruoka Hachimangū | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image=[[File:GrfKanagawakenChigasakishi-Turuminehachimangu-01.jpg|270px|Tsuruoka Hachimangū Haiden, Chigasaki City]]
Haiden
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=270|coord={{coord|35|20|18.3|N|139|23|28.3|E}}}} | location=〒 253-0086
{{ill|Kanagawa Prefecture|ja|神奈川県|qq=draft|qq=draft}}{{ill|Chigasaki|ja|茅ヶ崎市|qq=draft|qq=draft}} Hamano-go 462 | ISO=JP-14 | Latitude degrees = 35|Latitude minutes = 20|Latitude seconds = 18.38 | Longitude degrees=139|Longitude minutes=23|Longitude seconds=28.30 | deity={{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|応神天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft}}
{{ill|Emperor Nintoku|ja|仁徳天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft}}
{{ill|Sazuka Daimyōjin|ja|佐塚大神|qq=draft|qq=draft}}
{{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|Sugawara no Michizane|qq=draft|qq=draft}} | Shrine Rank=Former {{ill|Village shrine|ja|村社|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, Former {{ill|District shrine|ja|郷社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} | Foundation={{ill|Chōgen 3|ja|長元3rd year|qq=draft|qq=draft}} ({{ill|1030|ja|1030th year|qq=draft|qq=draft}}) | Main Hall=Nagare-zukuri | Alternate name=Tsuruoka Hachiman Shrine | festival={{ill|September 15|ja|9月15日|qq=draft|qq=draft}} | Rituals={{ill|Hamaori Festival|ja|浜降祭|qq=draft|qq=draft}} ({{ill|Marine Day|ja|海の日|qq=draft|qq=draft}}) | Map=Japan Kanagawa }} '''Tsuruoka Hachimangū''' (鶴嶺八幡宮) is a {{ill|shrine|ja|神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} located in {{ill|Chigasaki|ja|茅ヶ崎市|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Kanagawa Prefecture|ja|神奈川県|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. It is also known as Tsuruoka Hachiman Shrine. Renowned as the center of Hachiman worship in {{ill|Sagami Province|ja|相模国|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, the shrine has been revered since ancient times as a significant site of {{ill|Hachiman faith|ja|八幡信仰|qq=draft|qq=draft}}.Cited by the Kanagawa Prefecture Shrine Office According to the shrine, it was the first {{ill|clan shrine|ja|氏社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} established by the {{ill|Genji|ja|源氏|qq=draft|qq=draft}} when they expanded into the Kanto region. == History == The shrine was founded in {{ill|Chōgen 3 (1030)|ja|長元3rd year （1030th year ）|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, during the {{ill|Subjugation of Shimōsa|ja|下総の乱|qq=draft|qq=draft}} by {{ill|Minamoto no Yoriyoshi|ja|源頼義|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, who hailed from {{ill|Kawachi Province|ja|河内国|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (modern-day {{ill|Habikino|ja|羽曳野市|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府|qq=draft|qq=draft}}). Yoriyoshi enshrined {{ill|Iwashimizu Hachimangū|ja|石清水八幡宮|qq=draft|qq=draft}} at the site under the name "Kaijima Hachimangū" (another theory suggests it was {{ill|Usa Hachimangū|ja|宇佐八幡宮|qq=draft|qq=draft}}). Later, the Genji moved it to its current location. In {{ill|Tenki 3 (1055)|ja|天喜3rd year （1055th year ）|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, during the {{ill|Earlier Nine Years' War|ja|前九年の役|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, Yoriyoshi prayed at the shrine and achieved victory. To honor this, he established the {{ill|Tsurugaoka Hachimangū|lt=Original Hachiman Shrine|ja|元八幡|qq=draft|qq=draft}} in {{ill|Yuigahama|ja|由比ヶ浜|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, Kamakura, in {{ill|Kōhei 6 (1063)|ja|康平6年（1063rd year ）|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. In {{ill|Ōtoku 2 (1085)|ja|応徳2nd year （1085th year ）|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, Minamoto no Yoshiie, Yoriyoshi's heir, dedicated the shrine in Hamano-go as "Tsuruoka Hachimangū." Subsequently, Tsuruoka Hachimangū gained prominence, receiving land and monetary support from {{ill|Minamoto no Yoritomo|ja|源頼朝|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and others. Over time, it became the center for the famous {{ill|Hamaori Festival|ja|浜降祭|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and absorbed nearby shrines such as Sazuka Daimyōjin. == Deities == * {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|応神天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Emperor Nintoku|ja|仁徳天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * Sazuka Daimyōjin (associated with {{ill|Ōyamazumi Shrine|ja|大山祇神社|1=Ōyamazumi Shrine|qq=draft|qq=draft}}) * {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|Sugawara no Michizane|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (from Tsurumine Tenmangū) == Annual Events == * {{ill|Hamaori Festival|ja|浜降祭|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (Marine Day) * {{ill|Misorogi Ritual|ja|禊の神事|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (June 29, formerly held on July 15) * {{ill|Reitaisai|ja|例大祭|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (September 15) == Grounds == ; '''Great Ginkgo Tree''' : A 950-year-old tree said to have been planted by Minamoto no Yoshiie to pray for victory. ; '''Miyagane (Shrine Bell)''' : The shrine bell, originally installed in {{ill|Enpō 8 (1680)|ja|延宝8th year （1680th year ）|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, was reconstructed after the war in 1965 as a "peace bell." === Sub-Shrines === * '''Shōnan Awashima Shrine''' — Dedicated to {{ill|Sukunahikona|ja|少彦名命|1=Sukunabikona|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and {{ill|Awashima-no-Kami|ja|淡島神|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. * '''Tsurumine Inari Shrine''' — Dedicated to {{ill|Ukanomitama|ja|宇迦之御魂|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. == Access == Take the JR Tōkaidō Main Line to {{ill|Chigasaki Station|ja|茅ケ崎駅|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. From the north exit, take a bus and alight at "Tsurumine Elementary School," then walk 1 minute. == References == {{Cite source help}} {{Reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|Minamoto no Yoriyoshi|ja|源頼義|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Iwashimizu Hachimangū|ja|石清水八幡宮|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Hamaori Festival|ja|浜降祭|qq=draft|qq=draft}} {{draft categories| }} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Tsurumine Hachiman Shrine]] [[Category:Shrines in Kanagawa]] [[Category:Former Village Shrines in Japan]] [[Category:Former District Shrines in Japan]] [[Category:Hachiman Shrines]] [[Category:Chigasaki City History]] }} [[sn:Tsurumine Hachiman Shrine]] [[ja:鶴嶺八幡宮]] [[sn:surumine Hachiman Shrine]] [[en:Tsurumine Hachiman Shrine]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Tsurumine Hachiman Shrine}}
