revision.comment: 154 revisions imported: Bot: import full history from ja:鶴嶺八幡宮 up to rev 101890838
revision.text:
{{出典の明記|date=2018年6月28日 (木) 11:11 (UTC)}} {{神社 | 名称=鶴嶺八幡宮 | 画像=[[File:GrfKanagawakenChigasakishi-Turuminehachimangu-01.jpg|270px|茅ヶ崎市鶴嶺八幡宮　拝殿]]
拝殿
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=270|coord={{coord|35|20|18.3|N|139|23|28.3|E}}}} | 所在地=〒 253-0086
[[神奈川県]][[茅ヶ崎市]]浜之郷462 | ISO = JP-14 | 緯度度 = 35|緯度分 = 20|緯度秒 = 18.38 | 経度度 =139|経度分 = 23|経度秒 = 28.30 | 祭神=[[応神天皇]]
[[仁徳天皇]]
[[三島・大山祇信仰|佐塚大神]]
[[菅原道真]] | 社格=旧[[村社]]、旧[[郷社]]| | 創建=[[長元]]3年（[[1030年]]）| | 本殿=[[流造]] | 別名=鶴嶺八幡社 | 例祭=[[9月15日]] | 神事=[[浜降祭]]（[[7月海の日]]） | 地図= Japan Kanagawa }} '''鶴嶺八幡宮'''（つるみね はちまんぐう）は、[[神奈川県]][[茅ヶ崎市]]浜之郷にある[[神社]]。別名は鶴嶺八幡社。 相模国茅ヶ崎の総社として往古より八幡信仰の本地として名高い神奈川県神社庁による。また鶴嶺八幡宮によると、[[源氏]]が[[関東]]へ進出する際、創建した最初の[[氏社]]という。 == 由緒 == [[長元]]3年（[[1030年]]）[[9月 (旧暦)|9月]]に[[河内国]]（[[大阪府]][[羽曳野市]]）を本拠とする[[源頼信]]の子[[源頼義]]が[[下総の乱]]鎮定の折り、京都にある[[石清水八幡宮]]を'''懐嶋八幡宮'''として勧請した（[[宇佐神宮]]を勧請したとも）のが始まりであるという。後に源氏が現在の場所に分祀した。 [[天喜]]3年（[[1055年]]）に[[前九年の役]]の際に源頼義が祈願し勝利を収めると、[[康平]]6年（[[1063年]]）、戦勝に報いるため'''懐嶋八幡宮'''より分祀して鎌倉由比郷に鶴岡八幡宮の前身である'''元八幡を'''建立したという。その後、[[応徳]]2年（[[1085年]]）、[[源頼義]]の嫡男である[[源義家|八幡太郎義家]]が領地を寄進し、懐嶋郷の隣の浜之郷に'''鶴嶺八幡宮'''を創建した。この時期に元八幡の旧社であることから懐嶋八幡宮は'''本社宮'''と改称したという。 将軍[[源頼朝]]が[[治承]]年間に領地を寄進し、また[[建久]]年間にも頼朝は[[肥後国]]有為庄に鶴嶺八幡宮に700貫を寄進し、懐嶋権守[[平景能]]に修復を命じた。 [[建久]]2年（[[1191年]]）、[[大庭御厨]]の領主[[大庭景義]]が大仏殿や三重塔等を建て、'''鶴嶺八幡宮'''を再興した。同年、西隣りに[[大山祇神社|伊予の三島の神]]を祀る佐塚明神社が建立される。 古くは[[別当寺]]の常光院に十二坊の[[宿坊]]が存在したが、各地に散らばった後に廃止となる。[[江戸時代]]の[[正保]]年間、常光院住職の[[朝恵]]が地頭・[[山岡景信]]と相計りで荒れ果てた社殿を修復した。またこのとき参道を整備したという。 [[慶安]]2年（[[1649年]]）：3代将軍[[徳川家光]]が本村に7石の朱印地を与えた。江戸幕府崩壊後、明治時代になると神仏分離令により常光院が復飾し、祠掌となる。 その後、西側に隣接した'''佐塚大明神'''を合祀し、佐塚大明神の祭礼の[[浜降祭]]を主宰するようになった。下記の「年表」の1906年の項参照。 == 祭神 == * [[応神天皇]] * [[仁徳天皇]] * 佐塚大神（伊予の三島神（[[大山祇神社]]）と同義とされる） 合祀 * [[菅原道真]]（鶴嶺天満宮） == 年表 == * [[長元]]3年（[[1030年]]）[[9月 (旧暦)|9月]]：[[源頼義]]は[[下総の乱]]を鎮定のため懐嶋郷（鶴嶺八幡宮のある場所）矢畑村に源氏の守護神[[石清水八幡宮]]を勧請して戦勝祈願をし懐嶋八幡宮を創建。（別に[[宇佐八幡宮]]勧請説もある）。 * [[康平]]6年（[[1063年]]）：[[鎌倉市|鎌倉]]に元八幡に遷したが、懐嶋八幡宮は本社八幡と称した。 * [[治承]]年間（[[1177年]] - [[1184年]]）：[[源頼朝]]が懐嶋郷の領地を寄進した。 * [[建久]]年間（[[1190年]] - [[1198年]]）：[[肥後国]]有為庄に700貫を寄進。 * 建久2年（[[1191年]]）：大仏殿や三重塔等を建て、[[大庭景義]]が'''鶴嶺八幡宮'''を再興する。 * 建久2年（1191年）：鶴嶺[[八幡大菩薩]]の隣りに[[伊予国|伊予]]の[[大山祇神社|三島の神]]を祀る[[佐塚明神社]]が建立される。 * [[弘安]]4年（[[1281年]]）：蒙古退散の祈祷をし、[[6月30日 (旧暦)|閏6月30日]]の晦日に勝利したのを記念して「晦日祭」を始め、これが八幡宮と佐塚明神の例祭となった、と現在の'''鶴嶺八幡宮'''は説明している [http://members.jcom.home.ne.jp/tsurumine1030/top.html 鶴嶺八幡宮 御由緒]{{リンク切れ|date=2018年6月}}。。しかし、[[1876年]]（明治9年）以前には浜でのみそぎの神事（後の[[浜降祭]]）は[[6月29日]]に行われていた。1841年・1876年参照。 * [[永禄]]・[[元亀]]年間（[[1558年]] - [[1573年]]）：戦火に見舞われ、仏堂が焼失する。 * [[天正]]18年（[[1590年]]）：[[小田原城]]落城とともに社領を失い、荒廃する。 * [[正保]]年間（[[1644年]] - [[1648年]]）：[[別当寺]]・常光院住職の朝恵、社殿を修復・再興する。 * [[慶安]]2年（[[1649年]]）：3代将軍[[徳川家光]]、本村に七石の朱印地を寄進。朝恵はこれを記念して南大門馬場420間（約764メートル）の左右に松を植えた。 * [[天保]]年間（[[1830年]] - [[1843年]]）：本社宮を南に移す。 * 天保12年（[[1841年]]）：この年に幕府の[[昌平坂学問所|昌平黌]]地理局の編纂した『[[新編相模国風土記稿]]』に佐塚明神が[[6月29日 (旧暦)|6月29日]]に浜で[[禊]]（みそぎ）を行っているという記述がある{{sfn|新編相模国風土記稿 濱之郷村 佐塚明神社}}。 * [[明治]]元年（[[1868年]]）：[[太政官達]]（官庁に対する訓令）は権現、天王、八幡大菩薩などの仏教的神号を禁じた。「佐塚明神」は「佐塚大神」（さづかのおおかみ）、「鶴嶺八幡大菩薩」は「鶴嶺八幡」となる。また[[神仏分離]]により西側にあった[[別当寺]]の常光院を廃寺にし、持僧は神職に転身する。再興の租の朝恵上人の墓も廃す（戦後に復興）。 * 明治4年（[[1871年]]）：[[太政官布告]]「官社以下定額・神官職制等規則」が制定された。 * [[1873年]]（明治6年）：前々年の[[太政官布告]]によって[[村社]]となった'''鶴嶺八幡宮'''は、[[国幣中社]]となった[[寒川神社]]の[[摂社]]となる。 * [[1876年]]（明治9年）：旧暦の6月29日に行われていた「みそぎ」の神事を新暦の[[7月15日]]とし、名称も「[[浜降祭]]」へと変更。[[県令]]（現在の県知事の役）[[野村靖]]宛の申請書には農繁期を避ける旨が記されている。この年まで[[寒川神社]]は[[6月30日]]、'''鶴嶺八幡宮'''（佐塚大神）は[[6月29日]]に[[浜降祭]]を行っていた。 * [[1877年]]（明治10年）：'''鶴嶺八幡宮'''は[[寒川神社]]の[[摂社]]から解放され、[[浜降祭]]を単独で行うようになる。（場所は同一か不明） * [[1878年]]（明治11年）：[[浜降祭]]参加記録2社。[[寒川神社]]と'''鶴嶺八幡宮'''のみか不明。これ以前の参加社数記録無し。 * [[1906年]]（明治39年）：12月、[[内務省 (日本)|内務省]]は[[神社合祀令]]によって[[一村一社運動]]を起こし、明治末から大正初期にかけていわゆる[[神社整理]]を推進する。少なくともこの時期までに佐塚大神（旧、佐塚大明神）は'''鶴嶺八幡宮'''（旧、鶴嶺八幡大菩薩）に合祀され、旧佐塚大明神の浜降りの行事は鶴嶺八幡の主催となる。その時期は1873年（前記当該年参照）かそれ以前である可能性もある。 * [[1923年]]（[[大正]]12年）：'''鶴嶺八幡宮'''は改めて[[浜降祭]]に再参加するようになる。 * [[1927年]]（[[昭和]]2年）：本社宮を現在地へ戻す。 * [[1934年]]（昭和9年）[[9月15日]]：[[社格]][[制度]]制定で[[郷社]]となる。 * [[1969年]]（昭和44年）[[8月15日]]：参道の松並木が茅ヶ崎市の史跡・天然記念物に指定される。 == 祭事 == * [[浜降祭]]（第3月曜日 - [[海の日]]） * [[例大祭|晦日祭]]（[[6月30日]]） * [[放生会]]（[[9月15日]]） == 境内 == ; '''大銀杏''' : 社殿脇にある大銀杏は[[源義家]]が、戦勝を祈願して自ら植えたという大銀杏で、樹齢約950年。[[1962年]]（[[昭和]]37年）[[10月2日]]、県指定[[天然記念物]]。[[1984年]]（昭和59年）12月、かながわ名木百選に指定されている。 ; '''宮鐘''' : [[神仏習合]]の名残を留める宮鐘。[[延宝]]8年（[[1680年]]）に最初の鐘が納められ、戦時中、鐘は兵器製造のため供出によって消失するが、戦後の[[1965年]]（昭和40年）、「平和の鐘」として、再び鐘が奉納される。 === 境内社 === ; '''湘南淡嶋神社'''（ちがさき あわしまじんじゃ） : 祭神：[[少彦名命]]・[[淡島神]] ; '''鉾宮神社'''（ほこのみやじんじゃ） : 祭神：鉾宮大神・古刀比羅命・[[須佐之男命]] : 祭事：[[例祭|弥生祭]]（4月第一日曜日） ; '''鶴嶺稲荷神社'''（つるみね いなりじんじゃ） : 祭神：[[ウカノミタマ|宇迦之御魂大神]] : 祭事：[[初午祭]]（2月[[初午]]） == 境外摂社 == ; '''本社宮'''（ほんしゃぐう） : 祭神：[[応神天皇]] : もとは懐嶋八幡宮と呼ばれた。[[康平]]6年（[[1063年]]）、[[源頼義]]が懐嶋八幡宮を[[鎌倉]]に勧請し、'''鶴岡若宮'''（後の[[鶴岡八幡宮]]）を創建すると、その旧社にあたることから懐嶋八幡宮は'''本社宮'''と呼ばれた。また懐嶋郷矢畑村の鎮守となった。[[天保]]年間に現在地の南に移され、[[1927年]]（[[昭和]]2年）に現在地へ戻された。　 ; その他:松尾神明神社、柳島八幡宮、柳島海岸嚴島神社、円蔵神明大神、松尾神明神社、赤羽根神明大神、平太夫新田八幡宮、下町屋神明神社、三島大神、西久保日吉神社など == 関連史跡 == ; '''参道''' : 大鳥居から社殿までを八丁参道といい。鶴嶺八幡宮から[[国道1号|国道一号線]]（[[東海道]]）沿いを八丁松並木という。[[慶安]]2年（[[1649年]]）、[[江戸幕府]]の先例によって、七石の領地を与えられたのを機会に鶴嶺八幡宮の参道左右と馬場に松並木を植えられた。[[1969年]]（昭和44年）[[8月15日]]、市指定[[史跡]]、県指定[[天然記念物]]となる。 ; '''弁慶塚''' : [[源義経]]に仕えた[[僧兵]]の[[武蔵坊弁慶]]を祭った塚で、鶴嶺八幡宮参道脇にある。義経一族の霊を慰めるために造ったと伝わる。現存する塚は元の場所から[[1982年]]（昭和57年）に場所を少し移して作り直された。現在は私邸である。 ; '''南湖の左富士''' : [[江戸時代]]の[[浮世絵師]]・[[歌川広重]]が『[[東海道五十三次]]』の「南湖の松原左富士」に描いた。[[国道1号|国道1号線]]・鳥井戸橋付近に'''南湖の左富士'''の記念碑がある。[[天保]]3年（[[1832年]]）、[[東海道]]の旅路、広重は鳥井戸橋を渡り、下町屋の軒並みから見える[[富士山]]を描いた。 == 交通 == : [[東日本旅客鉄道|JR]][[東海道本線]][[茅ケ崎駅]]北口から[[バス (交通機関)|バス]]乗車、「[[鶴嶺小学校]]前」下車、徒歩1分。 == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} == 参考文献 == * {{cite book|和書|title=大日本地誌大系|volume=第38巻[[新編相模国風土記稿]]3巻之61村里部高座郡巻之3|chapter=大庭庄 濱之郷村 鶴嶺八幡社|id={{NDLJP|1179219/148}}|ref={{sfnref|新編相模国風土記稿 濱之郷村 鶴嶺八幡社}}|publisher=雄山閣|date=1932-8}} * {{cite book|和書|title=大日本地誌大系|volume=第38巻新編相模国風土記稿3巻之61村里部高座郡巻之3|chapter=大庭庄 濱之郷村 佐塚明神社|id={{NDLJP|1179219/149}}|ref={{sfnref|新編相模国風土記稿 濱之郷村 佐塚明神社}}|publisher=雄山閣|date=1932-8}} == 関連項目 == * [[源頼義]] * [[石清水八幡宮]] * [[源義家]] * [[鶴岡八幡宮]] * [[大庭景義]] * [[歌川広重]] * [[旧相模川橋脚]] * [[鶴嶺村]] == 外部リンク == * [https://www.tsuruminehachimangu.com 鶴嶺八幡宮] {{神道 横}} {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:つるみねはちまんくう}} [[Category:神奈川県の神社]] [[Category:神奈川県の旧村社]] [[Category:神奈川県の旧郷社]] [[Category:八幡宮]] [[Category:茅ヶ崎市の歴史]] [[Category:茅ヶ崎市の建築物]] [[Category:菅原道真]] [[Category:源頼義]]

revision.text:
{{Cite source|date=June 28, 2018}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name=Tsuruoka Hachimangū | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image=[[File:GrfKanagawakenChigasakishi-Turuminehachimangu-01.jpg|270px|Tsuruoka Hachimangū Haiden, Chigasaki City]]
Haiden
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=270|coord={{coord|35|20|18.3|N|139|23|28.3|E}}}} | location=〒 253-0086
{{ill|Kanagawa Prefecture|ja|神奈川県|qq=draft|qq=draft}}{{ill|Chigasaki|ja|茅ヶ崎市|qq=draft|qq=draft}} Hamano-go 462 | ISO=JP-14 | Latitude degrees = 35|Latitude minutes = 20|Latitude seconds = 18.38 | Longitude degrees=139|Longitude minutes=23|Longitude seconds=28.30 | deity={{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|応神天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft}}
{{ill|Emperor Nintoku|ja|仁徳天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft}}
{{ill|Sazuka Daimyōjin|ja|佐塚大神|qq=draft|qq=draft}}
{{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|Sugawara no Michizane|qq=draft|qq=draft}} | Shrine Rank=Former {{ill|Village shrine|ja|村社|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, Former {{ill|District shrine|ja|郷社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} | Foundation={{ill|Chōgen 3|ja|長元3rd year|qq=draft|qq=draft}} ({{ill|1030|ja|1030th year|qq=draft|qq=draft}}) | Main Hall=Nagare-zukuri | Alternate name=Tsuruoka Hachiman Shrine | festival={{ill|September 15|ja|9月15日|qq=draft|qq=draft}} | Rituals={{ill|Hamaori Festival|ja|浜降祭|qq=draft|qq=draft}} ({{ill|Marine Day|ja|海の日|qq=draft|qq=draft}}) | Map=Japan Kanagawa }} '''Tsuruoka Hachimangū''' (鶴嶺八幡宮) is a {{ill|shrine|ja|神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} located in {{ill|Chigasaki|ja|茅ヶ崎市|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Kanagawa Prefecture|ja|神奈川県|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. It is also known as Tsuruoka Hachiman Shrine. Renowned as the center of Hachiman worship in {{ill|Sagami Province|ja|相模国|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, the shrine has been revered since ancient times as a significant site of {{ill|Hachiman faith|ja|八幡信仰|qq=draft|qq=draft}}.Cited by the Kanagawa Prefecture Shrine Office According to the shrine, it was the first {{ill|clan shrine|ja|氏社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} established by the {{ill|Genji|ja|源氏|qq=draft|qq=draft}} when they expanded into the Kanto region. == History == The shrine was founded in {{ill|Chōgen 3 (1030)|ja|長元3rd year （1030th year ）|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, during the {{ill|Subjugation of Shimōsa|ja|下総の乱|qq=draft|qq=draft}} by {{ill|Minamoto no Yoriyoshi|ja|源頼義|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, who hailed from {{ill|Kawachi Province|ja|河内国|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (modern-day {{ill|Habikino|ja|羽曳野市|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府|qq=draft|qq=draft}}). Yoriyoshi enshrined {{ill|Iwashimizu Hachimangū|ja|石清水八幡宮|qq=draft|qq=draft}} at the site under the name "Kaijima Hachimangū" (another theory suggests it was {{ill|Usa Hachimangū|ja|宇佐八幡宮|qq=draft|qq=draft}}). Later, the Genji moved it to its current location. In {{ill|Tenki 3 (1055)|ja|天喜3rd year （1055th year ）|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, during the {{ill|Earlier Nine Years' War|ja|前九年の役|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, Yoriyoshi prayed at the shrine and achieved victory. To honor this, he established the {{ill|Tsurugaoka Hachimangū|lt=Original Hachiman Shrine|ja|元八幡|qq=draft|qq=draft}} in {{ill|Yuigahama|ja|由比ヶ浜|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, Kamakura, in {{ill|Kōhei 6 (1063)|ja|康平6年（1063rd year ）|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. In {{ill|Ōtoku 2 (1085)|ja|応徳2nd year （1085th year ）|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, Minamoto no Yoshiie, Yoriyoshi's heir, dedicated the shrine in Hamano-go as "Tsuruoka Hachimangū." Subsequently, Tsuruoka Hachimangū gained prominence, receiving land and monetary support from {{ill|Minamoto no Yoritomo|ja|源頼朝|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and others. Over time, it became the center for the famous {{ill|Hamaori Festival|ja|浜降祭|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and absorbed nearby shrines such as Sazuka Daimyōjin. == Deities == * {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|応神天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Emperor Nintoku|ja|仁徳天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * Sazuka Daimyōjin (associated with {{ill|Ōyamazumi Shrine|ja|大山祇神社|1=Ōyamazumi Shrine|qq=draft|qq=draft}}) * {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|Sugawara no Michizane|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (from Tsurumine Tenmangū) == Annual Events == * {{ill|Hamaori Festival|ja|浜降祭|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (Marine Day) * {{ill|Misorogi Ritual|ja|禊の神事|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (June 29, formerly held on July 15) * {{ill|Reitaisai|ja|例大祭|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (September 15) == Grounds == ; '''Great Ginkgo Tree''' : A 950-year-old tree said to have been planted by Minamoto no Yoshiie to pray for victory. ; '''Miyagane (Shrine Bell)''' : The shrine bell, originally installed in {{ill|Enpō 8 (1680)|ja|延宝8th year （1680th year ）|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, was reconstructed after the war in 1965 as a "peace bell." === Sub-Shrines === * '''Shōnan Awashima Shrine''' — Dedicated to {{ill|Sukunahikona|ja|少彦名命|1=Sukunabikona|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and {{ill|Awashima-no-Kami|ja|淡島神|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. * '''Tsurumine Inari Shrine''' — Dedicated to {{ill|Ukanomitama|ja|宇迦之御魂|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. == Access == Take the JR Tōkaidō Main Line to {{ill|Chigasaki Station|ja|茅ケ崎駅|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. From the north exit, take a bus and alight at "Tsurumine Elementary School," then walk 1 minute. == References == {{Cite source help}} {{Reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|Minamoto no Yoriyoshi|ja|源頼義|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Iwashimizu Hachimangū|ja|石清水八幡宮|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Hamaori Festival|ja|浜降祭|qq=draft|qq=draft}} {{draft categories| }} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Tsurumine Hachiman Shrine]] [[Category:Shrines in Kanagawa]] [[Category:Former Village Shrines in Japan]] [[Category:Former District Shrines in Japan]] [[Category:Hachiman Shrines]] [[Category:Chigasaki City History]] }} [[sn:Tsurumine Hachiman Shrine]] [[ja:鶴嶺八幡宮]] [[sn:surumine Hachiman Shrine]] [[en:Tsurumine Hachiman Shrine]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Tsurumine Hachiman Shrine}} [[Category:Ja import oversized]] [[Category:jawiki oversized to be reviewed]]

Yoriyoshi enshrined {{ill|Iwashimizu Hachimangū|ja|石清水八幡宮|qq=draft|qq=draft}} at the site under the name "Kaijima Hachimangū" (another theory suggests it was {{ill|Usa Hachimangū|ja|宇佐八幡宮|qq=draft|qq=draft}}). Later, the Genji moved it to its current location. In {{ill|Tenki 3 (1055)|ja|天喜3rd year （1055th year ）|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, during the {{ill|Earlier Nine Years' War|ja|前九年の役|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, Yoriyoshi prayed at the shrine and achieved victory. To honor this, he established the {{ill|Tsurugaoka Hachimangū|lt=Original Hachiman Shrine|ja|元八幡|qq=draft|qq=draft}} in {{ill|Yuigahama|ja|由比ヶ浜|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, Kamakura, in {{ill|Kōhei 6 (1063)|ja|康平6年（1063rd year ）|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. In {{ill|Ōtoku 2 (1085)|ja|応徳2nd year （1085th year ）|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, Minamoto no Yoshiie, Yoriyoshi's heir, dedicated the shrine in Hamano-go as "Tsuruoka Hachimangū." Subsequently, Tsuruoka Hachimangū gained prominence, receiving land and monetary support from {{ill|Minamoto no Yoritomo|ja|源頼朝|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and others. Over time, it became the center for the famous {{ill|Hamaori Festival|ja|浜降祭|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and absorbed nearby shrines such as Sazuka Daimyōjin. == Deities == * {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|応神天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Emperor Nintoku|ja|仁徳天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * Sazuka Daimyōjin (associated with {{ill|Ōyamazumi Shrine|ja|大山祇神社|1=Ōyamazumi Shrine|qq=draft|qq=draft}}) * {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|Sugawara no Michizane|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (from Tsurumine Tenmangū) == Annual Events == * {{ill|Hamaori Festival|ja|浜降祭|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (Marine Day) * {{ill|Misorogi Ritual|ja|禊の神事|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (June 29, formerly held on July 15) * {{ill|Reitaisai|ja|例大祭|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (September 15) == Grounds == ; '''Great Ginkgo Tree''' : A 950-year-old tree said to have been planted by Minamoto no Yoshiie to pray for victory. ; '''Miyagane (Shrine Bell)''' : The shrine bell, originally installed in {{ill|Enpō 8 (1680)|ja|延宝8th year （1680th year ）|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, was reconstructed after the war in 1965 as a "peace bell." === Sub-Shrines === * '''Shōnan Awashima Shrine''' — Dedicated to {{ill|Sukunahikona|ja|少彦名命|1=Sukunabikona|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and {{ill|Awashima-no-Kami|ja|淡島神|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. * '''Tsurumine Inari Shrine''' — Dedicated to {{ill|Ukanomitama|ja|宇迦之御魂|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. == Access == Take the JR Tōkaidō Main Line to {{ill|Chigasaki Station|ja|茅ケ崎駅|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. From the north exit, take a bus and alight at "Tsurumine Elementary School," then walk 1 minute. == References == {{Cite source help}} {{Reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|Minamoto no Yoriyoshi|ja|源頼義|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Iwashimizu Hachimangū|ja|石清水八幡宮|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Hamaori Festival|ja|浜降祭|qq=draft|qq=draft}} {{draft categories| }} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Tsurumine Hachiman Shrine]] [[Category:Shrines in Kanagawa]] [[Category:Former Village Shrines in Japan]] [[Category:Former District Shrines in Japan]] [[Category:Hachiman Shrines]] [[Category:Chigasaki City History]] }} [[sn:Tsurumine Hachiman Shrine]] [[ja:鶴嶺八幡宮]] [[sn:surumine Hachiman Shrine]] [[en:Tsurumine Hachiman Shrine]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Tsurumine Hachiman Shrine}} [[Category:Ja import oversized]]

revision.text:
{{Cite source|date=June 28, 2018}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name=Tsuruoka Hachimangū | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image=[[File:GrfKanagawakenChigasakishi-Turuminehachimangu-01.jpg|270px|Tsuruoka Hachimangū Haiden, Chigasaki City]]
Haiden
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=270|coord={{coord|35|20|18.3|N|139|23|28.3|E}}}} | location=〒 253-0086
{{ill|Kanagawa Prefecture|ja|神奈川県|qq=draft|qq=draft}}{{ill|Chigasaki|ja|茅ヶ崎市|qq=draft|qq=draft}} Hamano-go 462 | ISO=JP-14 | Latitude degrees = 35|Latitude minutes = 20|Latitude seconds = 18.38 | Longitude degrees=139|Longitude minutes=23|Longitude seconds=28.30 | deity={{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|応神天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft}}
{{ill|Emperor Nintoku|ja|仁徳天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft}}
{{ill|Sazuka Daimyōjin|ja|佐塚大神|qq=draft|qq=draft}}
{{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|Sugawara no Michizane|qq=draft|qq=draft}} | Shrine Rank=Former {{ill|Village shrine|ja|村社|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, Former {{ill|District shrine|ja|郷社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} | Foundation={{ill|Chōgen 3|ja|長元3rd year|qq=draft|qq=draft}} ({{ill|1030|ja|1030th year|qq=draft|qq=draft}}) | Main Hall=Nagare-zukuri | Alternate name=Tsuruoka Hachiman Shrine | festival={{ill|September 15|ja|9月15日|qq=draft|qq=draft}} | Rituals={{ill|Hamaori Festival|ja|浜降祭|qq=draft|qq=draft}} ({{ill|Marine Day|ja|海の日|qq=draft|qq=draft}}) | Map=Japan Kanagawa }} '''Tsuruoka Hachimangū''' (鶴嶺八幡宮) is a {{ill|shrine|ja|神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} located in {{ill|Chigasaki|ja|茅ヶ崎市|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Kanagawa Prefecture|ja|神奈川県|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. It is also known as Tsuruoka Hachiman Shrine. Renowned as the center of Hachiman worship in {{ill|Sagami Province|ja|相模国|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, the shrine has been revered since ancient times as a significant site of {{ill|Hachiman faith|ja|八幡信仰|qq=draft|qq=draft}}.Cited by the Kanagawa Prefecture Shrine Office According to the shrine, it was the first {{ill|clan shrine|ja|氏社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} established by the {{ill|Genji|ja|源氏|qq=draft|qq=draft}} when they expanded into the Kanto region. == History == The shrine was founded in {{ill|Chōgen 3 (1030)|ja|長元3rd year （1030th year ）|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, during the {{ill|Subjugation of Shimōsa|ja|下総の乱|qq=draft|qq=draft}} by {{ill|Minamoto no Yoriyoshi|ja|源頼義|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, who hailed from {{ill|Kawachi Province|ja|河内国|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (modern-day {{ill|Habikino|ja|羽曳野市|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府|qq=draft|qq=draft}}). Yoriyoshi enshrined {{ill|Iwashimizu Hachimangū|ja|石清水八幡宮|qq=draft|qq=draft}} at the site under the name "Kaijima Hachimangū" (another theory suggests it was {{ill|Usa Hachimangū|ja|宇佐八幡宮|qq=draft|qq=draft}}). Later, the Genji moved it to its current location. In {{ill|Tenki 3 (1055)|ja|天喜3rd year （1055th year ）|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, during the {{ill|Earlier Nine Years' War|ja|前九年の役|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, Yoriyoshi prayed at the shrine and achieved victory. To honor this, he established the {{ill|Tsurugaoka Hachimangū|lt=Original Hachiman Shrine|ja|元八幡|qq=draft|qq=draft}} in {{ill|Yuigahama|ja|由比ヶ浜|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, Kamakura, in {{ill|Kōhei 6 (1063)|ja|康平6年（1063rd year ）|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. In {{ill|Ōtoku 2 (1085)|ja|応徳2nd year （1085th year ）|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, Minamoto no Yoshiie, Yoriyoshi's heir, dedicated the shrine in Hamano-go as "Tsuruoka Hachimangū." Subsequently, Tsuruoka Hachimangū gained prominence, receiving land and monetary support from {{ill|Minamoto no Yoritomo|ja|源頼朝|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and others. Over time, it became the center for the famous {{ill|Hamaori Festival|ja|浜降祭|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and absorbed nearby shrines such as Sazuka Daimyōjin. == Deities == * {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|応神天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Emperor Nintoku|ja|仁徳天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * Sazuka Daimyōjin (associated with {{ill|Ōyamazumi Shrine|ja|大山祇神社|1=Ōyamazumi Shrine|qq=draft|qq=draft}}) * {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|Sugawara no Michizane|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (from Tsurumine Tenmangū) == Annual Events == * {{ill|Hamaori Festival|ja|浜降祭|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (Marine Day) * {{ill|Misorogi Ritual|ja|禊の神事|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (June 29, formerly held on July 15) * {{ill|Reitaisai|ja|例大祭|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (September 15) == Grounds == ; '''Great Ginkgo Tree''' : A 950-year-old tree said to have been planted by Minamoto no Yoshiie to pray for victory. ; '''Miyagane (Shrine Bell)''' : The shrine bell, originally installed in {{ill|Enpō 8 (1680)|ja|延宝8th year （1680th year ）|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, was reconstructed after the war in 1965 as a "peace bell." === Sub-Shrines === * '''Shōnan Awashima Shrine''' — Dedicated to {{ill|Sukunahikona|ja|少彦名命|1=Sukunabikona|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and {{ill|Awashima-no-Kami|ja|淡島神|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. * '''Tsurumine Inari Shrine''' — Dedicated to {{ill|Ukanomitama|ja|宇迦之御魂|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. == Access == Take the JR Tōkaidō Main Line to {{ill|Chigasaki Station|ja|茅ケ崎駅|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. From the north exit, take a bus and alight at "Tsurumine Elementary School," then walk 1 minute. == References == {{Cite source help}} {{Reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|Minamoto no Yoriyoshi|ja|源頼義|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Iwashimizu Hachimangū|ja|石清水八幡宮|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Hamaori Festival|ja|浜降祭|qq=draft|qq=draft}} {{draft categories| }} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Tsurumine Hachiman Shrine]] [[Category:Shrines in Kanagawa]] [[Category:Former Village Shrines in Japan]] [[Category:Former District Shrines in Japan]] [[Category:Hachiman Shrines]] [[Category:Chigasaki City History]] }} [[sn:Tsurumine Hachiman Shrine]] [[ja:鶴嶺八幡宮]] [[sn:surumine Hachiman Shrine]] [[en:Tsurumine Hachiman Shrine]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Tsurumine Hachiman Shrine}}

revision.text:
{{出典の明記|date=2018年6月28日 (木) 11:11 (UTC)}} {{神社 | 名称=鶴嶺八幡宮 | 画像=[[File:GrfKanagawakenChigasakishi-Turuminehachimangu-01.jpg|270px|茅ヶ崎市鶴嶺八幡宮　拝殿]]
拝殿
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=270|coord={{coord|35|20|18.3|N|139|23|28.3|E}}}} | 所在地=〒 253-0086
[[神奈川県]][[茅ヶ崎市]]浜之郷462 | ISO = JP-14 | 緯度度 = 35|緯度分 = 20|緯度秒 = 18.38 | 経度度 =139|経度分 = 23|経度秒 = 28.30 | 祭神=[[応神天皇]]
[[仁徳天皇]]
[[三島・大山祇信仰|佐塚大神]]
[[菅原道真]] | 社格=旧[[村社]]、旧[[郷社]]| | 創建=[[長元]]3年（[[1030年]]）| | 本殿=[[流造]] | 別名=鶴嶺八幡社 | 例祭=[[9月15日]] | 神事=[[浜降祭]]（[[7月海の日]]） | 地図= Japan Kanagawa }} '''鶴嶺八幡宮'''（つるみね はちまんぐう）は、[[神奈川県]][[茅ヶ崎市]]浜之郷にある[[神社]]。別名は鶴嶺八幡社。 相模国茅ヶ崎の総社として往古より八幡信仰の本地として名高い神奈川県神社庁による。また鶴嶺八幡宮によると、[[源氏]]が[[関東]]へ進出する際、創建した最初の[[氏社]]という。 == 由緒 == [[長元]]3年（[[1030年]]）[[9月 (旧暦)|9月]]に[[河内国]]（[[大阪府]][[羽曳野市]]）を本拠とする[[源頼信]]の子[[源頼義]]が[[下総の乱]]鎮定の折り、京都にある[[石清水八幡宮]]を'''懐嶋八幡宮'''として勧請した（[[宇佐神宮]]を勧請したとも）のが始まりであるという。後に源氏が現在の場所に分祀した。 [[天喜]]3年（[[1055年]]）に[[前九年の役]]の際に源頼義が祈願し勝利を収めると、[[康平]]6年（[[1063年]]）、戦勝に報いるため'''懐嶋八幡宮'''より分祀して鎌倉由比郷に鶴岡八幡宮の前身である'''元八幡を'''建立したという。その後、[[応徳]]2年（[[1085年]]）、[[源頼義]]の嫡男である[[源義家|八幡太郎義家]]が領地を寄進し、懐嶋郷の隣の浜之郷に'''鶴嶺八幡宮'''を創建した。この時期に元八幡の旧社であることから懐嶋八幡宮は'''本社宮'''と改称したという。 将軍[[源頼朝]]が[[治承]]年間に領地を寄進し、また[[建久]]年間にも頼朝は[[肥後国]]有為庄に鶴嶺八幡宮に700貫を寄進し、懐嶋権守[[平景能]]に修復を命じた。 [[建久]]2年（[[1191年]]）、[[大庭御厨]]の領主[[大庭景義]]が大仏殿や三重塔等を建て、'''鶴嶺八幡宮'''を再興した。同年、西隣りに[[大山祇神社|伊予の三島の神]]を祀る佐塚明神社が建立される。 古くは[[別当寺]]の常光院に十二坊の[[宿坊]]が存在したが、各地に散らばった後に廃止となる。[[江戸時代]]の[[正保]]年間、常光院住職の[[朝恵]]が地頭・[[山岡景信]]と相計りで荒れ果てた社殿を修復した。またこのとき参道を整備したという。 [[慶安]]2年（[[1649年]]）：3代将軍[[徳川家光]]が本村に7石の朱印地を与えた。江戸幕府崩壊後、明治時代になると神仏分離令により常光院が復飾し、祠掌となる。 その後、西側に隣接した'''佐塚大明神'''を合祀し、佐塚大明神の祭礼の[[浜降祭]]を主宰するようになった。下記の「年表」の1906年の項参照。 == 祭神 == * [[応神天皇]] * [[仁徳天皇]] * 佐塚大神（伊予の三島神（[[大山祇神社]]）と同義とされる） 合祀 * [[菅原道真]]（鶴嶺天満宮） == 年表 == * [[長元]]3年（[[1030年]]）[[9月 (旧暦)|9月]]：[[源頼義]]は[[下総の乱]]を鎮定のため懐嶋郷（鶴嶺八幡宮のある場所）矢畑村に源氏の守護神[[石清水八幡宮]]を勧請して戦勝祈願をし懐嶋八幡宮を創建。（別に[[宇佐八幡宮]]勧請説もある）。 * [[康平]]6年（[[1063年]]）：[[鎌倉市|鎌倉]]に元八幡に遷したが、懐嶋八幡宮は本社八幡と称した。 * [[治承]]年間（[[1177年]] - [[1184年]]）：[[源頼朝]]が懐嶋郷の領地を寄進した。 * [[建久]]年間（[[1190年]] - [[1198年]]）：[[肥後国]]有為庄に700貫を寄進。 * 建久2年（[[1191年]]）：大仏殿や三重塔等を建て、[[大庭景義]]が'''鶴嶺八幡宮'''を再興する。 * 建久2年（1191年）：鶴嶺[[八幡大菩薩]]の隣りに[[伊予国|伊予]]の[[大山祇神社|三島の神]]を祀る[[佐塚明神社]]が建立される。 * [[弘安]]4年（[[1281年]]）：蒙古退散の祈祷をし、[[6月30日 (旧暦)|閏6月30日]]の晦日に勝利したのを記念して「晦日祭」を始め、これが八幡宮と佐塚明神の例祭となった、と現在の'''鶴嶺八幡宮'''は説明している [http://members.jcom.home.ne.jp/tsurumine1030/top.html 鶴嶺八幡宮 御由緒]{{リンク切れ|date=2018年6月}}。。しかし、[[1876年]]（明治9年）以前には浜でのみそぎの神事（後の[[浜降祭]]）は[[6月29日]]に行われていた。1841年・1876年参照。 * [[永禄]]・[[元亀]]年間（[[1558年]] - [[1573年]]）：戦火に見舞われ、仏堂が焼失する。 * [[天正]]18年（[[1590年]]）：[[小田原城]]落城とともに社領を失い、荒廃する。 * [[正保]]年間（[[1644年]] - [[1648年]]）：[[別当寺]]・常光院住職の朝恵、社殿を修復・再興する。 * [[慶安]]2年（[[1649年]]）：3代将軍[[徳川家光]]、本村に七石の朱印地を寄進。朝恵はこれを記念して南大門馬場420間（約764メートル）の左右に松を植えた。 * [[天保]]年間（[[1830年]] - [[1843年]]）：本社宮を南に移す。 * 天保12年（[[1841年]]）：この年に幕府の[[昌平坂学問所|昌平黌]]地理局の編纂した『[[新編相模国風土記稿]]』に佐塚明神が[[6月29日 (旧暦)|6月29日]]に浜で[[禊]]（みそぎ）を行っているという記述がある{{sfn|新編相模国風土記稿 濱之郷村 佐塚明神社}}。 * [[明治]]元年（[[1868年]]）：[[太政官達]]（官庁に対する訓令）は権現、天王、八幡大菩薩などの仏教的神号を禁じた。「佐塚明神」は「佐塚大神」（さづかのおおかみ）、「鶴嶺八幡大菩薩」は「鶴嶺八幡」となる。また[[神仏分離]]により西側にあった[[別当寺]]の常光院を廃寺にし、持僧は神職に転身する。再興の租の朝恵上人の墓も廃す（戦後に復興）。 * 明治4年（[[1871年]]）：[[太政官布告]]「官社以下定額・神官職制等規則」が制定された。 * [[1873年]]（明治6年）：前々年の[[太政官布告]]によって[[村社]]となった'''鶴嶺八幡宮'''は、[[国幣中社]]となった[[寒川神社]]の[[摂社]]となる。 * [[1876年]]（明治9年）：旧暦の6月29日に行われていた「みそぎ」の神事を新暦の[[7月15日]]とし、名称も「[[浜降祭]]」へと変更。[[県令]]（現在の県知事の役）[[野村靖]]宛の申請書には農繁期を避ける旨が記されている。この年まで[[寒川神社]]は[[6月30日]]、'''鶴嶺八幡宮'''（佐塚大神）は[[6月29日]]に[[浜降祭]]を行っていた。 * [[1877年]]（明治10年）：'''鶴嶺八幡宮'''は[[寒川神社]]の[[摂社]]から解放され、[[浜降祭]]を単独で行うようになる。（場所は同一か不明） * [[1878年]]（明治11年）：[[浜降祭]]参加記録2社。[[寒川神社]]と'''鶴嶺八幡宮'''のみか不明。これ以前の参加社数記録無し。 * [[1906年]]（明治39年）：12月、[[内務省 (日本)|内務省]]は[[神社合祀令]]によって[[一村一社運動]]を起こし、明治末から大正初期にかけていわゆる[[神社整理]]を推進する。少なくともこの時期までに佐塚大神（旧、佐塚大明神）は'''鶴嶺八幡宮'''（旧、鶴嶺八幡大菩薩）に合祀され、旧佐塚大明神の浜降りの行事は鶴嶺八幡の主催となる。その時期は1873年（前記当該年参照）かそれ以前である可能性もある。 * [[1923年]]（[[大正]]12年）：'''鶴嶺八幡宮'''は改めて[[浜降祭]]に再参加するようになる。 * [[1927年]]（[[昭和]]2年）：本社宮を現在地へ戻す。 * [[1934年]]（昭和9年）[[9月15日]]：[[社格]][[制度]]制定で[[郷社]]となる。 * [[1969年]]（昭和44年）[[8月15日]]：参道の松並木が茅ヶ崎市の史跡・天然記念物に指定される。 == 祭事 == * [[浜降祭]]（第3月曜日 - [[海の日]]） * [[例大祭|晦日祭]]（[[6月30日]]） * [[放生会]]（[[9月15日]]） == 境内 == ; '''大銀杏''' : 社殿脇にある大銀杏は[[源義家]]が、戦勝を祈願して自ら植えたという大銀杏で、樹齢約950年。[[1962年]]（[[昭和]]37年）[[10月2日]]、県指定[[天然記念物]]。[[1984年]]（昭和59年）12月、かながわ名木百選に指定されている。 ; '''宮鐘''' : [[神仏習合]]の名残を留める宮鐘。[[延宝]]8年（[[1680年]]）に最初の鐘が納められ、戦時中、鐘は兵器製造のため供出によって消失するが、戦後の[[1965年]]（昭和40年）、「平和の鐘」として、再び鐘が奉納される。 === 境内社 === ; '''湘南淡嶋神社'''（ちがさき あわしまじんじゃ） : 祭神：[[少彦名命]]・[[淡島神]] ; '''鉾宮神社'''（ほこのみやじんじゃ） : 祭神：鉾宮大神・古刀比羅命・[[須佐之男命]] : 祭事：[[例祭|弥生祭]]（4月第一日曜日） ; '''鶴嶺稲荷神社'''（つるみね いなりじんじゃ） : 祭神：[[ウカノミタマ|宇迦之御魂大神]] : 祭事：[[初午祭]]（2月[[初午]]） == 境外摂社 == ; '''本社宮'''（ほんしゃぐう） : 祭神：[[応神天皇]] : もとは懐嶋八幡宮と呼ばれた。[[康平]]6年（[[1063年]]）、[[源頼義]]が懐嶋八幡宮を[[鎌倉]]に勧請し、'''鶴岡若宮'''（後の[[鶴岡八幡宮]]）を創建すると、その旧社にあたることから懐嶋八幡宮は'''本社宮'''と呼ばれた。また懐嶋郷矢畑村の鎮守となった。[[天保]]年間に現在地の南に移され、[[1927年]]（[[昭和]]2年）に現在地へ戻された。　 ; その他:松尾神明神社、柳島八幡宮、柳島海岸嚴島神社、円蔵神明大神、松尾神明神社、赤羽根神明大神、平太夫新田八幡宮、下町屋神明神社、三島大神、西久保日吉神社など == 関連史跡 == ; '''参道''' : 大鳥居から社殿までを八丁参道といい。鶴嶺八幡宮から[[国道1号|国道一号線]]（[[東海道]]）沿いを八丁松並木という。[[慶安]]2年（[[1649年]]）、[[江戸幕府]]の先例によって、七石の領地を与えられたのを機会に鶴嶺八幡宮の参道左右と馬場に松並木を植えられた。[[1969年]]（昭和44年）[[8月15日]]、市指定[[史跡]]、県指定[[天然記念物]]となる。 ; '''弁慶塚''' : [[源義経]]に仕えた[[僧兵]]の[[武蔵坊弁慶]]を祭った塚で、鶴嶺八幡宮参道脇にある。義経一族の霊を慰めるために造ったと伝わる。現存する塚は元の場所から[[1982年]]（昭和57年）に場所を少し移して作り直された。現在は私邸である。 ; '''南湖の左富士''' : [[江戸時代]]の[[浮世絵師]]・[[歌川広重]]が『[[東海道五十三次]]』の「南湖の松原左富士」に描いた。[[国道1号|国道1号線]]・鳥井戸橋付近に'''南湖の左富士'''の記念碑がある。[[天保]]3年（[[1832年]]）、[[東海道]]の旅路、広重は鳥井戸橋を渡り、下町屋の軒並みから見える[[富士山]]を描いた。 == 交通 == : [[東日本旅客鉄道|JR]][[東海道本線]][[茅ケ崎駅]]北口から[[バス (交通機関)|バス]]乗車、「[[鶴嶺小学校]]前」下車、徒歩1分。 == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} == 参考文献 == * {{cite book|和書|title=大日本地誌大系|volume=第38巻[[新編相模国風土記稿]]3巻之61村里部高座郡巻之3|chapter=大庭庄 濱之郷村 鶴嶺八幡社|id={{NDLJP|1179219/148}}|ref={{sfnref|新編相模国風土記稿 濱之郷村 鶴嶺八幡社}}|publisher=雄山閣|date=1932-8}} * {{cite book|和書|title=大日本地誌大系|volume=第38巻新編相模国風土記稿3巻之61村里部高座郡巻之3|chapter=大庭庄 濱之郷村 佐塚明神社|id={{NDLJP|1179219/149}}|ref={{sfnref|新編相模国風土記稿 濱之郷村 佐塚明神社}}|publisher=雄山閣|date=1932-8}} == 関連項目 == * [[源頼義]] * [[石清水八幡宮]] * [[源義家]] * [[鶴岡八幡宮]] * [[大庭景義]] * [[歌川広重]] * [[旧相模川橋脚]] * [[鶴嶺村]] == 外部リンク == * [https://www.tsuruminehachimangu.com 鶴嶺八幡宮] {{神道 横}} {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:つるみねはちまんくう}} [[Category:神奈川県の神社]] [[Category:神奈川県の旧村社]] [[Category:神奈川県の旧郷社]] [[Category:八幡宮]] {{Cite source|date=June 28, 2018}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name=Tsuruoka Hachimangū | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image=[[File:GrfKanagawakenChigasakishi-Turuminehachimangu-01.jpg|270px|Tsuruoka Hachimangū Haiden, Chigasaki City]]
Haiden
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=270|coord={{coord|35|20|18.3|N|139|23|28.3|E}}}} | location=〒 253-0086
{{ill|Kanagawa Prefecture|ja|神奈川県|qq=draft|qq=draft}}{{ill|Chigasaki|ja|茅ヶ崎市|qq=draft|qq=draft}} Hamano-go 462 | ISO=JP-14 | Latitude degrees = 35|Latitude minutes = 20|Latitude seconds = 18.38 | Longitude degrees=139|Longitude minutes=23|Longitude seconds=28.30 | deity={{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|応神天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft}}
{{ill|Emperor Nintoku|ja|仁徳天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft}}
{{ill|Sazuka Daimyōjin|ja|佐塚大神|qq=draft|qq=draft}}
{{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|Sugawara no Michizane|qq=draft|qq=draft}} | Shrine Rank=Former {{ill|Village shrine|ja|村社|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, Former {{ill|District shrine|ja|郷社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} | Foundation={{ill|Chōgen 3|ja|長元3rd year|qq=draft|qq=draft}} ({{ill|1030|ja|1030th year|qq=draft|qq=draft}}) | Main Hall=Nagare-zukuri | Alternate name=Tsuruoka Hachiman Shrine | festival={{ill|September 15|ja|9月15日|qq=draft|qq=draft}} | Rituals={{ill|Hamaori Festival|ja|浜降祭|qq=draft|qq=draft}} ({{ill|Marine Day|ja|海の日|qq=draft|qq=draft}}) | Map=Japan Kanagawa }} '''Tsuruoka Hachimangū''' (鶴嶺八幡宮) is a {{ill|shrine|ja|神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} located in {{ill|Chigasaki|ja|茅ヶ崎市|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Kanagawa Prefecture|ja|神奈川県|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. It is also known as Tsuruoka Hachiman Shrine. Renowned as the center of Hachiman worship in {{ill|Sagami Province|ja|相模国|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, the shrine has been revered since ancient times as a significant site of {{ill|Hachiman faith|ja|八幡信仰|qq=draft|qq=draft}}.Cited by the Kanagawa Prefecture Shrine Office According to the shrine, it was the first {{ill|clan shrine|ja|氏社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} established by the {{ill|Genji|ja|源氏|qq=draft|qq=draft}} when they expanded into the Kanto region. == History == The shrine was founded in {{ill|Chōgen 3 (1030)|ja|長元3rd year （1030th year ）|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, during the {{ill|Subjugation of Shimōsa|ja|下総の乱|qq=draft|qq=draft}} by {{ill|Minamoto no Yoriyoshi|ja|源頼義|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, who hailed from {{ill|Kawachi Province|ja|河内国|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (modern-day {{ill|Habikino|ja|羽曳野市|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府|qq=draft|qq=draft}}). Yoriyoshi enshrined {{ill|Iwashimizu Hachimangū|ja|石清水八幡宮|qq=draft|qq=draft}} at the site under the name "Kaijima Hachimangū" (another theory suggests it was {{ill|Usa Hachimangū|ja|宇佐八幡宮|qq=draft|qq=draft}}). Later, the Genji moved it to its current location. In {{ill|Tenki 3 (1055)|ja|天喜3rd year （1055th year ）|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, during the {{ill|Earlier Nine Years' War|ja|前九年の役|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, Yoriyoshi prayed at the shrine and achieved victory. To honor this, he established the {{ill|Tsurugaoka Hachimangū|lt=Original Hachiman Shrine|ja|元八幡|qq=draft|qq=draft}} in {{ill|Yuigahama|ja|由比ヶ浜|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, Kamakura, in {{ill|Kōhei 6 (1063)|ja|康平6年（1063rd year ）|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. In {{ill|Ōtoku 2 (1085)|ja|応徳2nd year （1085th year ）|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, Minamoto no Yoshiie, Yoriyoshi's heir, dedicated the shrine in Hamano-go as "Tsuruoka Hachimangū." Subsequently, Tsuruoka Hachimangū gained prominence, receiving land and monetary support from {{ill|Minamoto no Yoritomo|ja|源頼朝|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and others. Over time, it became the center for the famous {{ill|Hamaori Festival|ja|浜降祭|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and absorbed nearby shrines such as Sazuka Daimyōjin. == Deities == * {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|応神天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Emperor Nintoku|ja|仁徳天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * Sazuka Daimyōjin (associated with {{ill|Ōyamazumi Shrine|ja|大山祇神社|1=Ōyamazumi Shrine|qq=draft|qq=draft}}) * {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|Sugawara no Michizane|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (from Tsurumine Tenmangū) == Annual Events == * {{ill|Hamaori Festival|ja|浜降祭|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (Marine Day) * {{ill|Misorogi Ritual|ja|禊の神事|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (June 29, formerly held on July 15) * {{ill|Reitaisai|ja|例大祭|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (September 15) == Grounds == ; '''Great Ginkgo Tree''' : A 950-year-old tree said to have been planted by Minamoto no Yoshiie to pray for victory. ; '''Miyagane (Shrine Bell)''' : The shrine bell, originally installed in {{ill|Enpō 8 (1680)|ja|延宝8th year （1680th year ）|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, was reconstructed after the war in 1965 as a "peace bell." === Sub-Shrines === * '''Shōnan Awashima Shrine''' — Dedicated to {{ill|Sukunahikona|ja|少彦名命|1=Sukunabikona|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and {{ill|Awashima-no-Kami|ja|淡島神|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. * '''Tsurumine Inari Shrine''' — Dedicated to {{ill|Ukanomitama|ja|宇迦之御魂|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. == Access == Take the JR Tōkaidō Main Line to {{ill|Chigasaki Station|ja|茅ケ崎駅|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. From the north exit, take a bus and alight at "Tsurumine Elementary School," then walk 1 minute. == References == {{Cite source help}} {{Reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|Minamoto no Yoriyoshi|ja|源頼義|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Iwashimizu Hachimangū|ja|石清水八幡宮|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Hamaori Festival|ja|浜降祭|qq=draft|qq=draft}} {{draft categories| }} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Tsurumine Hachiman Shrine]] [[Category:Shrines in Kanagawa]] [[Category:Former Village Shrines in Japan]] [[Category:Former District Shrines in Japan]] [[Category:Hachiman Shrines]] [[Category:Chigasaki City History]] }} [[sn:Tsurumine Hachiman Shrine]] [[ja:鶴嶺八幡宮]] [[sn:surumine Hachiman Shrine]] [[en:Tsurumine Hachiman Shrine]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Tsurumine Hachiman Shrine}} {{translated page|ja|鶴嶺八幡宮|version=101890838|comment=Imported full ja history}}

It is also known as Tsuruoka Hachiman Shrine. Renowned as the center of Hachiman worship in {{ill|Sagami Province|ja|相模国|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, the shrine has been revered since ancient times as a significant site of {{ill|Hachiman faith|ja|八幡信仰|qq=draft|qq=draft}}.Cited by the Kanagawa Prefecture Shrine Office According to the shrine, it was the first {{ill|clan shrine|ja|氏社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} established by the {{ill|Genji|ja|源氏|qq=draft|qq=draft}} when they expanded into the Kanto region. == History == The shrine was founded in {{ill|Chōgen 3 (1030)|ja|長元3rd year （1030th year ）|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, during the {{ill|Subjugation of Shimōsa|ja|下総の乱|qq=draft|qq=draft}} by {{ill|Minamoto no Yoriyoshi|ja|源頼義|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, who hailed from {{ill|Kawachi Province|ja|河内国|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (modern-day {{ill|Habikino|ja|羽曳野市|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府|qq=draft|qq=draft}}). Yoriyoshi enshrined {{ill|Iwashimizu Hachimangū|ja|石清水八幡宮|qq=draft|qq=draft}} at the site under the name "Kaijima Hachimangū" (another theory suggests it was {{ill|Usa Hachimangū|ja|宇佐八幡宮|qq=draft|qq=draft}}). Later, the Genji moved it to its current location. In {{ill|Tenki 3 (1055)|ja|天喜3rd year （1055th year ）|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, during the {{ill|Earlier Nine Years' War|ja|前九年の役|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, Yoriyoshi prayed at the shrine and achieved victory. To honor this, he established the {{ill|Tsurugaoka Hachimangū|lt=Original Hachiman Shrine|ja|元八幡|qq=draft|qq=draft}} in {{ill|Yuigahama|ja|由比ヶ浜|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, Kamakura, in {{ill|Kōhei 6 (1063)|ja|康平6年（1063rd year ）|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. In {{ill|Ōtoku 2 (1085)|ja|応徳2nd year （1085th year ）|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, Minamoto no Yoshiie, Yoriyoshi's heir, dedicated the shrine in Hamano-go as "Tsuruoka Hachimangū." Subsequently, Tsuruoka Hachimangū gained prominence, receiving land and monetary support from {{ill|Minamoto no Yoritomo|ja|源頼朝|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and others. Over time, it became the center for the famous {{ill|Hamaori Festival|ja|浜降祭|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and absorbed nearby shrines such as Sazuka Daimyōjin. == Deities == * {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|応神天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Emperor Nintoku|ja|仁徳天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * Sazuka Daimyōjin (associated with {{ill|Ōyamazumi Shrine|ja|大山祇神社|1=Ōyamazumi Shrine|qq=draft|qq=draft}}) * {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|Sugawara no Michizane|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (from Tsurumine Tenmangū) == Annual Events == * {{ill|Hamaori Festival|ja|浜降祭|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (Marine Day) * {{ill|Misorogi Ritual|ja|禊の神事|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (June 29, formerly held on July 15) * {{ill|Reitaisai|ja|例大祭|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (September 15) == Grounds == ; '''Great Ginkgo Tree''' : A 950-year-old tree said to have been planted by Minamoto no Yoshiie to pray for victory. ; '''Miyagane (Shrine Bell)''' : The shrine bell, originally installed in {{ill|Enpō 8 (1680)|ja|延宝8th year （1680th year ）|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, was reconstructed after the war in 1965 as a "peace bell." === Sub-Shrines === * '''Shōnan Awashima Shrine''' — Dedicated to {{ill|Sukunahikona|ja|少彦名命|1=Sukunabikona|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and {{ill|Awashima-no-Kami|ja|淡島神|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. * '''Tsurumine Inari Shrine''' — Dedicated to {{ill|Ukanomitama|ja|宇迦之御魂|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. == Access == Take the JR Tōkaidō Main Line to {{ill|Chigasaki Station|ja|茅ケ崎駅|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. From the north exit, take a bus and alight at "Tsurumine Elementary School," then walk 1 minute. == References == {{Cite source help}} {{Reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|Minamoto no Yoriyoshi|ja|源頼義|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Iwashimizu Hachimangū|ja|石清水八幡宮|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Hamaori Festival|ja|浜降祭|qq=draft|qq=draft}} {{translated page|ja|鶴嶺八幡宮|version=101890838|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Tsurumine Hachiman Shrine]] [[Category:Shrines in Kanagawa]] [[Category:Former Village Shrines in Japan]] [[Category:Former District Shrines in Japan]] [[Category:Hachiman Shrines]] [[Category:Chigasaki City History]] }} [[sn:Tsurumine Hachiman Shrine]] [[ja:鶴嶺八幡宮]] [[sn:surumine Hachiman Shrine]] [[en:Tsurumine Hachiman Shrine]]

Over time, it became the center for the famous {{ill|Hamaori Festival|ja|浜降祭|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and absorbed nearby shrines such as Sazuka Daimyōjin. == Deities == * {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|応神天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Emperor Nintoku|ja|仁徳天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * Sazuka Daimyōjin (associated with {{ill|Ōyamazumi Shrine|ja|大山祇神社|1=Ōyamazumi Shrine|qq=draft|qq=draft}}) * {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|Sugawara no Michizane|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (from Tsurumine Tenmangū) == Annual Events == * {{ill|Hamaori Festival|ja|浜降祭|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (Marine Day) * {{ill|Misorogi Ritual|ja|禊の神事|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (June 29, formerly held on July 15) * {{ill|Reitaisai|ja|例大祭|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (September 15) == Grounds == ; '''Great Ginkgo Tree''' : A 950-year-old tree said to have been planted by Minamoto no Yoshiie to pray for victory. ; '''Miyagane (Shrine Bell)''' : The shrine bell, originally installed in {{ill|Enpō 8 (1680)|ja|延宝8th year （1680th year ）|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, was reconstructed after the war in 1965 as a "peace bell." === Sub-Shrines === * '''Shōnan Awashima Shrine''' — Dedicated to {{ill|Sukunahikona|ja|少彦名命|1=Sukunabikona|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and {{ill|Awashima-no-Kami|ja|淡島神|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. * '''Tsurumine Inari Shrine''' — Dedicated to {{ill|Ukanomitama|ja|宇迦之御魂|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. == Access == Take the JR Tōkaidō Main Line to {{ill|Chigasaki Station|ja|茅ケ崎駅|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. From the north exit, take a bus and alight at "Tsurumine Elementary School," then walk 1 minute. == References == {{Cite source help}} {{Reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|Minamoto no Yoriyoshi|ja|源頼義|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Iwashimizu Hachimangū|ja|石清水八幡宮|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Hamaori Festival|ja|浜降祭|qq=draft|qq=draft}} {{translated page|ja|鶴嶺八幡宮|version=101890838|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Tsurumine Hachiman Shrine]] [[Category:Shrines in Kanagawa]] [[Category:Former Village Shrines in Japan]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Japan]] [[Category:Hachiman Shrines]] [[Category:Chigasaki City History]] }} [[sn:Tsurumine Hachiman Shrine]] [[ja:鶴嶺八幡宮]] [[sn:surumine Hachiman Shrine]] [[en:Tsurumine Hachiman Shrine]]

{{Cite source|date=June 28, 2018}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name=Tsuruoka Hachimangū | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image=[[File:GrfKanagawakenChigasakishi-Turuminehachimangu-01.jpg|270px|Tsuruoka Hachimangū Haiden, Chigasaki City]]
Haiden
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=270|coord={{coord|35|20|18.3|N|139|23|28.3|E}}}} | location=〒 253-0086
{{ill|Kanagawa Prefecture|ja|神奈川県|qq=draft|qq=draft}}{{ill|Chigasaki|ja|茅ヶ崎市|qq=draft|qq=draft}} Hamano-go 462 | ISO=JP-14 | Latitude degrees = 35|Latitude minutes = 20|Latitude seconds = 18.38 | Longitude degrees=139|Longitude minutes=23|Longitude seconds=28.30 | deity={{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|応神天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft}}
{{ill|Emperor Nintoku|ja|仁徳天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft}}
{{ill|Sazuka Daimyōjin|ja|佐塚大神|qq=draft|qq=draft}}
{{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|Sugawara no Michizane|qq=draft|qq=draft}} | Shrine Rank=Former {{ill|Village shrine|ja|村社|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, Former {{ill|District shrine|ja|郷社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} | Foundation={{ill|Chōgen 3|ja|長元3rd year|qq=draft|qq=draft}} ({{ill|1030|ja|1030th year|qq=draft|qq=draft}}) | Main Hall=Nagare-zukuri | Alternate name=Tsuruoka Hachiman Shrine | festival={{ill|September 15|ja|9月15日|qq=draft|qq=draft}} | Rituals={{ill|Hamaori Festival|ja|浜降祭|qq=draft|qq=draft}} ({{ill|Marine Day|ja|海の日|qq=draft|qq=draft}}) | Map=Japan Kanagawa }} '''Tsuruoka Hachimangū''' (鶴嶺八幡宮) is a {{ill|shrine|ja|神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} located in {{ill|Chigasaki|ja|茅ヶ崎市|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Kanagawa Prefecture|ja|神奈川県|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. It is also known as Tsuruoka Hachiman Shrine. Renowned as the center of Hachiman worship in {{ill|Sagami Province|ja|相模国|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, the shrine has been revered since ancient times as a significant site of {{ill|Hachiman faith|ja|八幡信仰|qq=draft|qq=draft}}.Cited by the Kanagawa Prefecture Shrine Office According to the shrine, it was the first {{ill|clan shrine|ja|氏社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} established by the {{ill|Genji|ja|源氏|qq=draft|qq=draft}} when they expanded into the Kanto region. == History == The shrine was founded in {{ill|Chōgen 3 (1030)|ja|長元3rd year （1030th year ）|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, during the {{ill|Subjugation of Shimōsa|ja|下総の乱|qq=draft|qq=draft}} by {{ill|Minamoto no Yoriyoshi|ja|源頼義|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, who hailed from {{ill|Kawachi Province|ja|河内国|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (modern-day {{ill|Habikino|ja|羽曳野市|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府|qq=draft|qq=draft}}). Yoriyoshi enshrined {{ill|Iwashimizu Hachimangū|ja|石清水八幡宮|qq=draft|qq=draft}} at the site under the name "Kaijima Hachimangū" (another theory suggests it was {{ill|Usa Hachimangū|ja|宇佐八幡宮|qq=draft|qq=draft}}). Later, the Genji moved it to its current location. In {{ill|Tenki 3 (1055)|ja|天喜3rd year （1055th year ）|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, during the {{ill|Earlier Nine Years' War|ja|前九年の役|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, Yoriyoshi prayed at the shrine and achieved victory. To honor this, he established the {{ill|Tsurugaoka Hachimangū|lt=Original Hachiman Shrine|ja|元八幡|qq=draft|qq=draft}} in {{ill|Yuigahama|ja|由比ヶ浜|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, Kamakura, in {{ill|Kōhei 6 (1063)|ja|康平6年（1063rd year ）|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. In {{ill|Ōtoku 2 (1085)|ja|応徳2nd year （1085th year ）|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, Minamoto no Yoshiie, Yoriyoshi's heir, dedicated the shrine in Hamano-go as "Tsuruoka Hachimangū." Subsequently, Tsuruoka Hachimangū gained prominence, receiving land and monetary support from {{ill|Minamoto no Yoritomo|ja|源頼朝|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and others. Over time, it became the center for the famous {{ill|Hamaori Festival|ja|浜降祭|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and absorbed nearby shrines such as Sazuka Daimyōjin. == Deities == * {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|応神天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Emperor Nintoku|ja|仁徳天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * Sazuka Daimyōjin (associated with {{ill|Ōyamazumi Shrine|ja|大山祇神社|1=Ōyamazumi Shrine|qq=draft|qq=draft}}) * {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|Sugawara no Michizane|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (from Tsurumine Tenmangū) == Annual Events == * {{ill|Hamaori Festival|ja|浜降祭|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (Marine Day) * {{ill|Misorogi Ritual|ja|禊の神事|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (June 29, formerly held on July 15) * {{ill|Reitaisai|ja|例大祭|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (September 15) == Grounds == ; '''Great Ginkgo Tree''' : A 950-year-old tree said to have been planted by Minamoto no Yoshiie to pray for victory. ; '''Miyagane (Shrine Bell)''' : The shrine bell, originally installed in {{ill|Enpō 8 (1680)|ja|延宝8th year （1680th year ）|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, was reconstructed after the war in 1965 as a "peace bell." === Sub-Shrines === * '''Shōnan Awashima Shrine''' — Dedicated to {{ill|Sukunahikona|ja|少彦名命|1=Sukunabikona|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and {{ill|Awashima-no-Kami|ja|淡島神|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. * '''Tsurumine Inari Shrine''' — Dedicated to {{ill|Ukanomitama|ja|宇迦之御魂|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. == Access == Take the JR Tōkaidō Main Line to {{ill|Chigasaki Station|ja|茅ケ崎駅|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. From the north exit, take a bus and alight at "Tsurumine Elementary School," then walk 1 minute. == References == {{Cite source help}} {{Reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|Minamoto no Yoriyoshi|ja|源頼義|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Iwashimizu Hachimangū|ja|石清水八幡宮|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Hamaori Festival|ja|浜降祭|qq=draft|qq=draft}} {{translated page|ja|鶴嶺八幡宮|version=101890838|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Tsurumine Hachiman Shrine]] [[Category:Shrines in Kanagawa]] [[Category:Son-sha in Japan]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Japan]] [[Category:Hachiman Shrines]] [[Category:Chigasaki City History]] }} [[sn:Tsurumine Hachiman Shrine]] [[ja:鶴嶺八幡宮]] [[sn:surumine Hachiman Shrine]] [[en:Tsurumine Hachiman Shrine]]

{{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|Sugawara no Michizane|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugawara no Michizane|qq=}} | Shrine Rank=Former {{ill|Village shrine|ja|村社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Village shrine|qq=}}, Former {{ill|District shrine|ja|郷社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/District shrine|qq=}} | Foundation={{ill|Chōgen 3|ja|長元3rd year|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chōgen 3|qq=}} ({{ill|1030|ja|1030th year|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1030|qq=}}) | Main Hall=Nagare-zukuri | Alternate name=Tsuruoka Hachiman Shrine | festival={{ill|September 15|ja|9月15日|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/September 15|qq=}} | Rituals={{ill|Hamaori Festival|ja|浜降祭|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hamaori Festival|qq=}} ({{ill|Marine Day|ja|海の日|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Marine Day|qq=}}) | Map=Japan Kanagawa }} '''Tsuruoka Hachimangū''' (鶴嶺八幡宮) is a {{ill|shrine|ja|神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/shrine|qq=}} located in {{ill|Chigasaki|ja|茅ヶ崎市|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chigasaki|qq=}}, {{ill|Kanagawa Prefecture|ja|神奈川県|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanagawa Prefecture|qq=}}. It is also known as Tsuruoka Hachiman Shrine. Renowned as the center of Hachiman worship in {{ill|Sagami Province|ja|相模国|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sagami Province|qq=}}, the shrine has been revered since ancient times as a significant site of {{ill|Hachiman faith|ja|八幡信仰|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hachiman faith|qq=}}.Cited by the Kanagawa Prefecture Shrine Office According to the shrine, it was the first {{ill|clan shrine|ja|氏社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/clan shrine|qq=}} established by the {{ill|Genji|ja|源氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Genji|qq=}} when they expanded into the Kanto region. == History == The shrine was founded in {{ill|Chōgen 3 (1030)|ja|長元3rd year （1030th year ）|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chōgen 3 (1030)|qq=}}, during the {{ill|Subjugation of Shimōsa|ja|下総の乱|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Subjugation of Shimōsa|qq=}} by {{ill|Minamoto no Yoriyoshi|ja|源頼義|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Minamoto no Yoriyoshi|qq=}}, who hailed from {{ill|Kawachi Province|ja|河内国|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kawachi Province|qq=}} (modern-day {{ill|Habikino|ja|羽曳野市|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Habikino|qq=}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Osaka Prefecture|qq=}}). Yoriyoshi enshrined {{ill|Iwashimizu Hachimangū|ja|石清水八幡宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iwashimizu Hachimangū|qq=}} at the site under the name "Kaijima Hachimangū" (another theory suggests it was {{ill|Usa Hachimangū|ja|宇佐八幡宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Usa Hachimangū|qq=}}). Later, the Genji moved it to its current location. In {{ill|Tenki 3 (1055)|ja|天喜3rd year （1055th year ）|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenki 3 (1055)|qq=}}, during the {{ill|Earlier Nine Years' War|ja|前九年の役|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Earlier Nine Years' War|qq=}}, Yoriyoshi prayed at the shrine and achieved victory. To honor this, he established the {{ill|Tsurugaoka Hachimangū|lt=Original Hachiman Shrine|ja|元八幡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsurugaoka Hachimangū|qq=}} in {{ill|Yuigahama|ja|由比ヶ浜|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yuigahama|qq=}}, Kamakura, in {{ill|Kōhei 6 (1063)|ja|康平6年（1063rd year ）|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōhei 6 (1063)|qq=}}. In {{ill|Ōtoku 2 (1085)|ja|応徳2nd year （1085th year ）|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtoku 2 (1085)|qq=}}, Minamoto no Yoshiie, Yoriyoshi's heir, dedicated the shrine in Hamano-go as "Tsuruoka Hachimangū." Subsequently, Tsuruoka Hachimangū gained prominence, receiving land and monetary support from {{ill|Minamoto no Yoritomo|ja|源頼朝|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Minamoto no Yoritomo|qq=}} and others. Over time, it became the center for the famous {{ill|Hamaori Festival|ja|浜降祭|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hamaori Festival|qq=}} and absorbed nearby shrines such as Sazuka Daimyōjin. == Deities == * {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|応神天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Ōjin|qq=}} * {{ill|Emperor Nintoku|ja|仁徳天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Nintoku|qq=}} * Sazuka Daimyōjin (associated with {{ill|Ōyamazumi Shrine|ja|大山祇神社|1=Ōyamazumi Shrine|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamazumi Shrine|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamazumi Shrine|qq=}}) * {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|Sugawara no Michizane|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugawara no Michizane|qq=}} (from Tsurumine Tenmangū) == Annual Events == * {{ill|Hamaori Festival|ja|浜降祭|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hamaori Festival|qq=}} (Marine Day) * {{ill|Misorogi Ritual|ja|禊の神事|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Misorogi Ritual|qq=}} (June 29, formerly held on July 15) * {{ill|Reitaisai|ja|例大祭|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Reitaisai|qq=}} (September 15) == Grounds == ; '''Great Ginkgo Tree''' : A 950-year-old tree said to have been planted by Minamoto no Yoshiie to pray for victory. ; '''Miyagane (Shrine Bell)''' : The shrine bell, originally installed in {{ill|Enpō 8 (1680)|ja|延宝8th year （1680th year ）|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Enpō 8 (1680)|qq=}}, was reconstructed after the war in 1965 as a "peace bell." === Sub-Shrines === * '''Shōnan Awashima Shrine''' — Dedicated to {{ill|Sukunahikona|ja|少彦名命|1=Sukunabikona|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sukunahikona|13=User:Immanuelle/Sukunabikona|qq=}} and {{ill|Awashima-no-Kami|ja|淡島神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Awashima-no-Kami|qq=}}. * '''Tsurumine Inari Shrine''' — Dedicated to {{ill|Ukanomitama|ja|宇迦之御魂|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ukanomitama|qq=}}. == Access == Take the JR Tōkaidō Main Line to {{ill|Chigasaki Station|ja|茅ケ崎駅|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chigasaki Station|qq=}}. From the north exit, take a bus and alight at "Tsurumine Elementary School," then walk 1 minute. == References == {{Unreferenced help}} {{Reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|Minamoto no Yoriyoshi|ja|源頼義|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Minamoto no Yoriyoshi|qq=}} * {{ill|Iwashimizu Hachimangū|ja|石清水八幡宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iwashimizu Hachimangū|qq=}} * {{ill|Hamaori Festival|ja|浜降祭|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hamaori Festival|qq=}} {{translated page|ja|鶴嶺八幡宮|version=101890838|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Tsurumine Hachiman Shrine]] [[Category:Shrines in Kanagawa]] [[Category:Son-sha in Japan]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Japan]] [[Category:Hachiman Shrines]] [[Category:Chigasaki City History]] }} [[sn:Tsurumine Hachiman Shrine]] [[ja:鶴嶺八幡宮]] [[sn:surumine Hachiman Shrine]] [[en:Tsurumine Hachiman Shrine]]

Yoriyoshi enshrined {{ill|Iwashimizu Hachimangū|ja|石清水八幡宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iwashimizu Hachimangū|qq=}} at the site under the name "Kaijima Hachimangū" (another theory suggests it was {{ill|Usa Hachimangū|ja|宇佐八幡宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Usa Hachimangū|qq=}}). Later, the Genji moved it to its current location. In {{ill|Tenki 3 (1055)|ja|天喜3rd year （1055th year ）|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenki 3 (1055)|qq=}}, during the {{ill|Earlier Nine Years' War|ja|前九年の役|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Earlier Nine Years' War|qq=}}, Yoriyoshi prayed at the shrine and achieved victory. To honor this, he established the {{ill|Tsurugaoka Hachimangū|lt=Original Hachiman Shrine|ja|元八幡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsurugaoka Hachimangū|qq=}} in {{ill|Yuigahama|ja|由比ヶ浜|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yuigahama|qq=}}, Kamakura, in {{ill|Kōhei 6 (1063)|ja|康平6年（1063rd year ）|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōhei 6 (1063)|qq=}}. In {{ill|Ōtoku 2 (1085)|ja|応徳2nd year （1085th year ）|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtoku 2 (1085)|qq=}}, Minamoto no Yoshiie, Yoriyoshi's heir, dedicated the shrine in Hamano-go as "Tsuruoka Hachimangū." Subsequently, Tsuruoka Hachimangū gained prominence, receiving land and monetary support from {{ill|Minamoto no Yoritomo|ja|源頼朝|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Minamoto no Yoritomo|qq=}} and others. Over time, it became the center for the famous {{ill|Hamaori Festival|ja|浜降祭|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hamaori Festival|qq=}} and absorbed nearby shrines such as Sazuka Daimyōjin. == Deities == * {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|応神天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Ōjin|qq=}} * {{ill|Emperor Nintoku|ja|仁徳天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Nintoku|qq=}} * Sazuka Daimyōjin (associated with {{ill|Ōyamazumi Shrine|ja|大山祇神社|1=Ōyamazumi Shrine|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamazumi Shrine|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamazumi Shrine|qq=}}) * {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|Sugawara no Michizane|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugawara no Michizane|qq=}} (from Tsurumine Tenmangū) == Annual Events == * {{ill|Hamaori Festival|ja|浜降祭|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hamaori Festival|qq=}} (Marine Day) * {{ill|Misorogi Ritual|ja|禊の神事|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Misorogi Ritual|qq=}} (June 29, formerly held on July 15) * {{ill|Reitaisai|ja|例大祭|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Reitaisai|qq=}} (September 15) == Grounds == ; '''Great Ginkgo Tree''' : A 950-year-old tree said to have been planted by Minamoto no Yoshiie to pray for victory. ; '''Miyagane (Shrine Bell)''' : The shrine bell, originally installed in {{ill|Enpō 8 (1680)|ja|延宝8th year （1680th year ）|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Enpō 8 (1680)|qq=}}, was reconstructed after the war in 1965 as a "peace bell." === Sub-Shrines === * '''Shōnan Awashima Shrine''' — Dedicated to {{ill|Sukunahikona|ja|少彦名命|1=Sukunabikona|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sukunahikona|13=User:Immanuelle/Sukunabikona|qq=}} and {{ill|Awashima-no-Kami|ja|淡島神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Awashima-no-Kami|qq=}}. * '''Tsurumine Inari Shrine''' — Dedicated to {{ill|Ukanomitama|ja|宇迦之御魂|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ukanomitama|qq=}}. == Access == Take the JR Tōkaidō Main Line to {{ill|Chigasaki Station|ja|茅ケ崎駅|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chigasaki Station|qq=}}. From the north exit, take a bus and alight at "Tsurumine Elementary School," then walk 1 minute. == References == {{Unreferenced help}} {{Reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|Minamoto no Yoriyoshi|ja|源頼義|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Minamoto no Yoriyoshi|qq=}} * {{ill|Iwashimizu Hachimangū|ja|石清水八幡宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iwashimizu Hachimangū|qq=}} * {{ill|Hamaori Festival|ja|浜降祭|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hamaori Festival|qq=}} {{translated page|ja|鶴嶺八幡宮|version=101890838|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Shrines in Kanagawa]] [[Category:Son-sha in Japan]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Japan]] [[Category:Hachiman Shrines]] }} [[sn:Tsurumine Hachiman Shrine]] [[ja:鶴嶺八幡宮]] [[sn:surumine Hachiman Shrine]] [[en:Tsurumine Hachiman Shrine]]

Over time, it became the center for the famous {{ill|Hamaori Festival|ja|浜降祭|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hamaori Festival|qq=}} and absorbed nearby shrines such as Sazuka Daimyōjin. == Deities == * {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|応神天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Ōjin|qq=}} * {{ill|Emperor Nintoku|ja|仁徳天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Nintoku|qq=}} * Sazuka Daimyōjin (associated with {{ill|Ōyamazumi Shrine|ja|大山祇神社|1=Ōyamazumi Shrine|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamazumi Shrine|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamazumi Shrine|qq=}}) * {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|Sugawara no Michizane|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugawara no Michizane|qq=}} (from Tsurumine Tenmangū) == Annual Events == * {{ill|Hamaori Festival|ja|浜降祭|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hamaori Festival|qq=}} (Marine Day) * {{ill|Misorogi Ritual|ja|禊の神事|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Misorogi Ritual|qq=}} (June 29, formerly held on July 15) * {{ill|Reisai|lt=Reisai|lt=Reitaisai|ja|例大祭|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Reitaisai|qq=}} (September 15) == Grounds == ; '''Great Ginkgo Tree''' : A 950-year-old tree said to have been planted by Minamoto no Yoshiie to pray for victory. ; '''Miyagane (Shrine Bell)''' : The shrine bell, originally installed in {{ill|Enpō 8 (1680)|ja|延宝8th year （1680th year ）|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Enpō 8 (1680)|qq=}}, was reconstructed after the war in 1965 as a "peace bell." === Sub-Shrines === * '''Shōnan Awashima Shrine''' — Dedicated to {{ill|Sukunahikona|ja|少彦名命|1=Sukunabikona|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sukunahikona|13=User:Immanuelle/Sukunabikona|qq=}} and {{ill|Awashima-no-Kami|ja|淡島神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Awashima-no-Kami|qq=}}. * '''Tsurumine Inari Shrine''' — Dedicated to {{ill|Ukanomitama|ja|宇迦之御魂|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ukanomitama|qq=}}. == Access == Take the JR Tōkaidō Main Line to {{ill|Chigasaki Station|ja|茅ケ崎駅|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chigasaki Station|qq=}}. From the north exit, take a bus and alight at "Tsurumine Elementary School," then walk 1 minute. == References == {{Unreferenced help}} {{Reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|Minamoto no Yoriyoshi|ja|源頼義|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Minamoto no Yoriyoshi|qq=}} * {{ill|Iwashimizu Hachimangū|ja|石清水八幡宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iwashimizu Hachimangū|qq=}} * {{ill|Hamaori Festival|ja|浜降祭|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hamaori Festival|qq=}} {{translated page|ja|鶴嶺八幡宮|version=101890838|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Shrines in Kanagawa]] [[Category:Son-sha in Japan]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Japan]] [[Category:Hachiman Shrines]] }} [[sn:Tsurumine Hachiman Shrine]] [[ja:鶴嶺八幡宮]] [[sn:surumine Hachiman Shrine]] [[en:Tsurumine Hachiman Shrine]]

{{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|Sugawara no Michizane|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugawara no Michizane|qq=}} | Shrine Rank=Former {{ill|Village shrine|ja|村社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Village shrine|qq=}}, Former {{ill|District shrine|ja|郷社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/District shrine|qq=}} | Foundation={{ill|Chōgen 3|ja|長元3年|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chōgen 3|qq=}} ({{ill|1030|ja|1030年|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1030|qq=}}) | Main Hall=Nagare-zukuri | Alternate name=Tsuruoka Hachiman Shrine | festival={{ill|September 15|ja|9月15日|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/September 15|qq=}} | Rituals={{ill|Hamaori Festival|ja|浜降祭|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hamaori Festival|qq=}} ({{ill|Marine Day|ja|海の日|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Marine Day|qq=}}) | Map=Japan Kanagawa }} '''Tsuruoka Hachimangū''' (鶴嶺八幡宮) is a {{ill|shrine|ja|神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/shrine|qq=}} located in {{ill|Chigasaki|ja|茅ヶ崎市|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chigasaki|qq=}}, {{ill|Kanagawa Prefecture|ja|神奈川県|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanagawa Prefecture|qq=}}. It is also known as Tsuruoka Hachiman Shrine. Renowned as the center of Hachiman worship in {{ill|Sagami Province|ja|相模国|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sagami Province|qq=}}, the shrine has been revered since ancient times as a significant site of {{ill|Hachiman faith|ja|八幡信仰|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hachiman faith|qq=}}.Cited by the Kanagawa Prefecture Shrine Office According to the shrine, it was the first {{ill|clan shrine|ja|氏社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/clan shrine|qq=}} established by the {{ill|Genji|ja|源氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Genji|qq=}} when they expanded into the Kanto region. == History == The shrine was founded in {{ill|Chōgen 3 (1030)|ja|長元3rd year （1030th year ）|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chōgen 3 (1030)|qq=}}, during the {{ill|Subjugation of Shimōsa|ja|下総の乱|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Subjugation of Shimōsa|qq=}} by {{ill|Minamoto no Yoriyoshi|ja|源頼義|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Minamoto no Yoriyoshi|qq=}}, who hailed from {{ill|Kawachi Province|ja|河内国|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kawachi Province|qq=}} (modern-day {{ill|Habikino|ja|羽曳野市|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Habikino|qq=}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Osaka Prefecture|qq=}}). Yoriyoshi enshrined {{ill|Iwashimizu Hachimangū|ja|石清水八幡宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iwashimizu Hachimangū|qq=}} at the site under the name "Kaijima Hachimangū" (another theory suggests it was {{ill|Usa Hachimangū|ja|宇佐八幡宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Usa Hachimangū|qq=}}). Later, the Genji moved it to its current location. In {{ill|Tenki 3 (1055)|ja|天喜3rd year （1055th year ）|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenki 3 (1055)|qq=}}, during the {{ill|Earlier Nine Years' War|ja|前九年の役|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Earlier Nine Years' War|qq=}}, Yoriyoshi prayed at the shrine and achieved victory. To honor this, he established the {{ill|Tsurugaoka Hachimangū|lt=Original Hachiman Shrine|ja|元八幡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsurugaoka Hachimangū|qq=}} in {{ill|Yuigahama|ja|由比ヶ浜|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yuigahama|qq=}}, Kamakura, in {{ill|Kōhei 6 (1063)|ja|康平6年（1063rd year ）|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōhei 6 (1063)|qq=}}. In {{ill|Ōtoku 2 (1085)|ja|応徳2nd year （1085th year ）|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtoku 2 (1085)|qq=}}, Minamoto no Yoshiie, Yoriyoshi's heir, dedicated the shrine in Hamano-go as "Tsuruoka Hachimangū." Subsequently, Tsuruoka Hachimangū gained prominence, receiving land and monetary support from {{ill|Minamoto no Yoritomo|ja|源頼朝|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Minamoto no Yoritomo|qq=}} and others. Over time, it became the center for the famous {{ill|Hamaori Festival|ja|浜降祭|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hamaori Festival|qq=}} and absorbed nearby shrines such as Sazuka Daimyōjin. == Deities == * {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|応神天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Ōjin|qq=}} * {{ill|Emperor Nintoku|ja|仁徳天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Nintoku|qq=}} * Sazuka Daimyōjin (associated with {{ill|Ōyamazumi Shrine|ja|大山祇神社|1=Ōyamazumi Shrine|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamazumi Shrine|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamazumi Shrine|qq=}}) * {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|Sugawara no Michizane|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugawara no Michizane|qq=}} (from Tsurumine Tenmangū) == Annual Events == * {{ill|Hamaori Festival|ja|浜降祭|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hamaori Festival|qq=}} (Marine Day) * {{ill|Misorogi Ritual|ja|禊の神事|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Misorogi Ritual|qq=}} (June 29, formerly held on July 15) * {{ill|Reisai|lt=Reisai|lt=Reitaisai|ja|例大祭|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Reitaisai|qq=}} (September 15) == Grounds == ; '''Great Ginkgo Tree''' : A 950-year-old tree said to have been planted by Minamoto no Yoshiie to pray for victory. ; '''Miyagane (Shrine Bell)''' : The shrine bell, originally installed in {{ill|Enpō 8 (1680)|ja|延宝8th year （1680th year ）|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Enpō 8 (1680)|qq=}}, was reconstructed after the war in 1965 as a "peace bell." === Sub-Shrines === * '''Shōnan Awashima Shrine''' — Dedicated to {{ill|Sukunahikona|ja|少彦名命|1=Sukunabikona|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sukunahikona|13=User:Immanuelle/Sukunabikona|qq=}} and {{ill|Awashima-no-Kami|ja|淡島神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Awashima-no-Kami|qq=}}. * '''Tsurumine Inari Shrine''' — Dedicated to {{ill|Ukanomitama|ja|宇迦之御魂|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ukanomitama|qq=}}. == Access == Take the JR Tōkaidō Main Line to {{ill|Chigasaki Station|ja|茅ケ崎駅|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chigasaki Station|qq=}}. From the north exit, take a bus and alight at "Tsurumine Elementary School," then walk 1 minute. == References == {{Unreferenced help}} {{Reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|Minamoto no Yoriyoshi|ja|源頼義|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Minamoto no Yoriyoshi|qq=}} * {{ill|Iwashimizu Hachimangū|ja|石清水八幡宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iwashimizu Hachimangū|qq=}} * {{ill|Hamaori Festival|ja|浜降祭|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hamaori Festival|qq=}} {{translated page|ja|鶴嶺八幡宮|version=101890838|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Shrines in Kanagawa]] [[Category:Son-sha in Japan]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Japan]] [[Category:Hachiman Shrines]] }} [[sn:Tsurumine Hachiman Shrine]] [[ja:鶴嶺八幡宮]] [[sn:surumine Hachiman Shrine]] [[en:Tsurumine Hachiman Shrine]]

{{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|Sugawara no Michizane|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugawara no Michizane|qq=}} | Shrine Rank=Former {{ill|Village shrine|ja|村社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Village shrine|qq=}}, Former {{ill|District shrine|ja|郷社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/District shrine|qq=}} | Foundation={{ill|Chōgen 3|ja|長元3年|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chōgen 3|qq=}} ([[:en:1030|1030]]) | Main Hall=Nagare-zukuri | Alternate name=Tsuruoka Hachiman Shrine | festival=[[:en:September 15|September 15]] | Rituals={{ill|Hamaori Festival|ja|浜降祭|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hamaori Festival|qq=}} ([[:en:Marine Day|Marine Day]]) | Map=Japan Kanagawa }} '''Tsuruoka Hachimangū''' (鶴嶺八幡宮) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|shrine]] located in [[:en:Chigasaki, Kanagawa|Chigasaki]], [[:en:Kanagawa Prefecture|Kanagawa Prefecture]]. It is also known as Tsuruoka Hachiman Shrine. Renowned as the center of Hachiman worship in [[:en:Sagami Province|Sagami Province]], the shrine has been revered since ancient times as a significant site of {{ill|Hachiman faith|ja|八幡信仰|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hachiman faith|qq=}}.Cited by the Kanagawa Prefecture Shrine Office According to the shrine, it was the first {{ill|clan shrine|ja|氏社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/clan shrine|qq=}} established by the [[:en:Minamoto clan|Genji]] when they expanded into the Kanto region. == History == The shrine was founded in {{ill|Chōgen 3 (1030)|ja|長元3rd year （1030th year ）|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chōgen 3 (1030)|qq=}}, during the {{ill|Subjugation of Shimōsa|ja|下総の乱|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Subjugation of Shimōsa|qq=}} by [[:en:Minamoto no Yoriyoshi|Minamoto no Yoriyoshi]], who hailed from [[:en:Kawachi Province|Kawachi Province]] (modern-day [[:en:Habikino|Habikino]], [[:en:Osaka Prefecture|Osaka Prefecture]]). Yoriyoshi enshrined [[:en:Iwashimizu Hachimangū|Iwashimizu Hachimangū]] at the site under the name "Kaijima Hachimangū" (another theory suggests it was {{ill|Usa Hachimangū|ja|宇佐八幡宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Usa Hachimangū|qq=}}). Later, the Genji moved it to its current location. In {{ill|Tenki 3 (1055)|ja|天喜3rd year （1055th year ）|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenki 3 (1055)|qq=}}, during the [[:en:Former Nine Years' War|Earlier Nine Years' War]], Yoriyoshi prayed at the shrine and achieved victory. To honor this, he established the {{ill|Tsurugaoka Hachimangū|lt=Original Hachiman Shrine|ja|元八幡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsurugaoka Hachimangū|qq=}} in [[:en:Yuigahama|Yuigahama]], Kamakura, in {{ill|Kōhei 6 (1063)|ja|康平6年（1063rd year ）|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōhei 6 (1063)|qq=}}. In {{ill|Ōtoku 2 (1085)|ja|応徳2nd year （1085th year ）|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtoku 2 (1085)|qq=}}, Minamoto no Yoshiie, Yoriyoshi's heir, dedicated the shrine in Hamano-go as "Tsuruoka Hachimangū." Subsequently, Tsuruoka Hachimangū gained prominence, receiving land and monetary support from [[:en:Minamoto no Yoritomo|Minamoto no Yoritomo]] and others. Over time, it became the center for the famous {{ill|Hamaori Festival|ja|浜降祭|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hamaori Festival|qq=}} and absorbed nearby shrines such as Sazuka Daimyōjin. == Deities == * [[:en:Emperor Ōjin|Emperor Ōjin]] * [[:en:Emperor Nintoku|Emperor Nintoku]] * Sazuka Daimyōjin (associated with [[:en:Ōyamazumi Shrine|Ōyamazumi Shrine]]) * {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|Sugawara no Michizane|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugawara no Michizane|qq=}} (from Tsurumine Tenmangū) == Annual Events == * {{ill|Hamaori Festival|ja|浜降祭|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hamaori Festival|qq=}} (Marine Day) * {{ill|Misorogi Ritual|ja|禊の神事|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Misorogi Ritual|qq=}} (June 29, formerly held on July 15) * {{ill|Reisai|lt=Reisai|lt=Reitaisai|ja|例大祭|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Reitaisai|qq=}} (September 15) == Grounds == ; '''Great Ginkgo Tree''' : A 950-year-old tree said to have been planted by Minamoto no Yoshiie to pray for victory. ; '''Miyagane (Shrine Bell)''' : The shrine bell, originally installed in {{ill|Enpō 8 (1680)|ja|延宝8th year （1680th year ）|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Enpō 8 (1680)|qq=}}, was reconstructed after the war in 1965 as a "peace bell." === Sub-Shrines === * '''Shōnan Awashima Shrine''' — Dedicated to {{ill|Sukunahikona|ja|少彦名命|1=Sukunabikona|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sukunahikona|13=User:Immanuelle/Sukunabikona|qq=}} and {{ill|Awashima-no-Kami|ja|淡島神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Awashima-no-Kami|qq=}}. * '''Tsurumine Inari Shrine''' — Dedicated to {{ill|Ukanomitama|ja|宇迦之御魂|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ukanomitama|qq=}}. == Access == Take the JR Tōkaidō Main Line to [[:en:Chigasaki Station|Chigasaki Station]]. From the north exit, take a bus and alight at "Tsurumine Elementary School," then walk 1 minute. == References == {{Unreferenced help}} {{Reflist}} == See Also == * [[:en:Minamoto no Yoriyoshi|Minamoto no Yoriyoshi]] * [[:en:Iwashimizu Hachimangū|Iwashimizu Hachimangū]] * {{ill|Hamaori Festival|ja|浜降祭|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hamaori Festival|qq=}} {{translated page|ja|鶴嶺八幡宮|version=101890838|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Shrines in Kanagawa]] [[Category:Son-sha in Japan]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Japan]] [[Category:Hachiman Shrines]] }} [[sn:Tsurumine Hachiman Shrine]] [[ja:鶴嶺八幡宮]] [[sn:surumine Hachiman Shrine]] [[en:Tsurumine Hachiman Shrine]]

{{ill|Sazuka Daimyōjin|ja|佐塚大神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sazuka Daimyōjin|qq=}}
{{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|Sugawara no Michizane|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugawara no Michizane|qq=}} | Shrine Rank=Former {{ill|Village shrine|ja|村社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Village shrine|qq=}}, Former {{ill|District shrine|ja|郷社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/District shrine|qq=}} | Foundation={{ill|Chōgen 3|ja|長元3年|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chōgen 3|qq=}} ([[:en:1030|1030]]) | Main Hall=Nagare-zukuri | Alternate name=Tsuruoka Hachiman Shrine | festival=[[:en:September 15|September 15]] | Rituals={{ill|Hamaori Festival|ja|浜降祭|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hamaori Festival|qq=}} ([[:en:Marine Day|Marine Day]]) | Map=Japan Kanagawa }} '''Tsuruoka Hachimangū''' (鶴嶺八幡宮) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|shrine]] located in [[:en:Chigasaki, Kanagawa|Chigasaki]], [[:en:Kanagawa Prefecture|Kanagawa Prefecture]]. It is also known as Tsuruoka Hachiman Shrine. Renowned as the center of Hachiman worship in [[:en:Sagami Province|Sagami Province]], the shrine has been revered since ancient times as a significant site of {{ill|Hachiman faith|ja|八幡信仰|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hachiman faith|qq=}}.Cited by the Kanagawa Prefecture Shrine Office According to the shrine, it was the first {{ill|clan shrine|ja|氏社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/clan shrine|qq=}} established by the [[:en:Minamoto clan|Genji]] when they expanded into the Kanto region. == History == The shrine was founded in {{ill|Chōgen 3 (1030)|ja|長元3rd year （1030th year ）|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chōgen 3 (1030)|qq=}}, during the {{ill|Subjugation of Shimōsa|ja|下総の乱|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Subjugation of Shimōsa|qq=}} by [[:en:Minamoto no Yoriyoshi|Minamoto no Yoriyoshi]], who hailed from [[:en:Kawachi Province|Kawachi Province]] (modern-day [[:en:Habikino|Habikino]], [[:en:Osaka Prefecture|Osaka Prefecture]]). Yoriyoshi enshrined [[:en:Iwashimizu Hachimangū|Iwashimizu Hachimangū]] at the site under the name "Kaijima Hachimangū" (another theory suggests it was {{ill|Usa Hachimangū|ja|宇佐八幡宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Usa Hachimangū|qq=}}). Later, the Genji moved it to its current location. In {{ill|Tenki 3 (1055)|ja|天喜3rd year （1055th year ）|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenki 3 (1055)|qq=}}, during the [[:en:Former Nine Years' War|Earlier Nine Years' War]], Yoriyoshi prayed at the shrine and achieved victory. To honor this, he established the {{ill|Tsurugaoka Hachimangū|lt=Original Hachiman Shrine|ja|元八幡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsurugaoka Hachimangū|qq=}} in [[:en:Yuigahama|Yuigahama]], Kamakura, in {{ill|Kōhei 6 (1063)|ja|康平6年（1063rd year ）|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōhei 6 (1063)|qq=}}. In {{ill|Ōtoku 2 (1085)|ja|応徳2nd year （1085th year ）|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtoku 2 (1085)|qq=}}, Minamoto no Yoshiie, Yoriyoshi's heir, dedicated the shrine in Hamano-go as "Tsuruoka Hachimangū." Subsequently, Tsuruoka Hachimangū gained prominence, receiving land and monetary support from [[:en:Minamoto no Yoritomo|Minamoto no Yoritomo]] and others. Over time, it became the center for the famous {{ill|Hamaori Festival|ja|浜降祭|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hamaori Festival|qq=}} and absorbed nearby shrines such as Sazuka Daimyōjin. == Deities == * [[:en:Emperor Ōjin|Emperor Ōjin]] * [[:en:Emperor Nintoku|Emperor Nintoku]] * Sazuka Daimyōjin (associated with [[:en:Ōyamazumi Shrine|Ōyamazumi Shrine]]) * {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|Sugawara no Michizane|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugawara no Michizane|qq=}} (from Tsurumine Tenmangū) == Annual Events == * {{ill|Hamaori Festival|ja|浜降祭|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hamaori Festival|qq=}} (Marine Day) * {{ill|Misorogi Ritual|ja|禊の神事|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Misorogi Ritual|qq=}} (June 29, formerly held on July 15) * {{ill|Reisai|lt=Reisai|lt=Reitaisai|ja|例大祭|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Reitaisai|qq=}} (September 15) == Grounds == ; '''Great Ginkgo Tree''' : A 950-year-old tree said to have been planted by Minamoto no Yoshiie to pray for victory. ; '''Miyagane (Shrine Bell)''' : The shrine bell, originally installed in {{ill|Enpō 8 (1680)|ja|延宝8th year （1680th year ）|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Enpō 8 (1680)|qq=}}, was reconstructed after the war in 1965 as a "peace bell." === Sub-Shrines === * '''Shōnan Awashima Shrine''' — Dedicated to {{ill|Sukunahikona|ja|少彦名命|1=Sukunabikona|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sukunahikona|13=User:Immanuelle/Sukunabikona|qq=}} and {{ill|Awashima-no-Kami|ja|淡島神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Awashima-no-Kami|qq=}}. * '''Tsurumine Inari Shrine''' — Dedicated to {{ill|Ukanomitama|ja|宇迦之御魂|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ukanomitama|qq=}}. == Access == Take the JR Tōkaidō Main Line to [[:en:Chigasaki Station|Chigasaki Station]]. From the north exit, take a bus and alight at "Tsurumine Elementary School," then walk 1 minute. == References == {{Unreferenced help}} {{Reflist}} == See Also == * [[:en:Minamoto no Yoriyoshi|Minamoto no Yoriyoshi]] * [[:en:Iwashimizu Hachimangū|Iwashimizu Hachimangū]] * {{ill|Hamaori Festival|ja|浜降祭|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hamaori Festival|qq=}} {{translated page|ja|鶴嶺八幡宮|version=101890838|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Shrines in Kanagawa]] [[Category:Son-sha in Japan]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Japan]] [[Category:Hachiman Shrines]] }} [[sn:Tsurumine Hachiman Shrine]] [[ja:鶴嶺八幡宮]] [[sn:surumine Hachiman Shrine]] [[en:Tsurumine Hachiman Shrine]] [[Category:Hachiman shrines]]
