In {{ill|Ōtoku 2 (1085)|ja|応徳2nd year （1085th year ）|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtoku 2 (1085)|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, Minamoto no Yoshiie, Yoriyoshi's heir, dedicated the shrine in Hamano-go as "Tsuruoka Hachimangū." Subsequently, Tsuruoka Hachimangū gained prominence, receiving land and monetary support from [[:en:Minamoto no Yoritomo|Minamoto no Yoritomo]] and others. Over time, it became the center for the famous {{ill|Hamaori Festival|ja|浜降祭|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hamaori Festival|qq=}} and absorbed nearby shrines such as Sazuka Daimyōjin. == Deities == * [[:en:Emperor Ōjin|Emperor Ōjin]] * [[:en:Emperor Nintoku|Emperor Nintoku]] * Sazuka Daimyōjin (associated with [[:en:Ōyamazumi Shrine|Ōyamazumi Shrine]]) * {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|Sugawara no Michizane|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugawara no Michizane|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (from Tsurumine Tenmangū) == Annual Events == * {{ill|Hamaori Festival|ja|浜降祭|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hamaori Festival|qq=}} (Marine Day) * {{ill|Misorogi Ritual|ja|禊の神事|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Misorogi Ritual|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (June 29, formerly held on July 15) * {{ill|Reisai|lt=Reisai|lt=Reitaisai|ja|例大祭|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Reitaisai|qq=|ja_comment=fixed the redirect|1=Reisai|2=ja|3=例祭|4=fr|5=Reisai|lt=Reitaisai}} (September 15) == Grounds == ; '''Great Ginkgo Tree''' : A 950-year-old tree said to have been planted by Minamoto no Yoshiie to pray for victory. ; '''Miyagane (Shrine Bell)''' : The shrine bell, originally installed in {{ill|Enpō 8 (1680)|ja|延宝8th year （1680th year ）|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Enpō 8 (1680)|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, was reconstructed after the war in 1965 as a "peace bell." === Sub-Shrines === * '''Shōnan Awashima Shrine''' — Dedicated to {{ill|Sukunahikona|ja|少彦名命|1=Sukunabikona|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sukunahikona|13=User:Immanuelle/Sukunabikona|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スクナビコナ}} and {{ill|Awashima-no-Kami|ja|淡島神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Awashima-no-Kami|qq=}}. * '''Tsurumine Inari Shrine''' — Dedicated to {{ill|Ukanomitama|ja|宇迦之御魂|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ukanomitama|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ウカノミタマ}}. == Access == Take the JR Tōkaidō Main Line to [[:en:Chigasaki Station|Chigasaki Station]]. From the north exit, take a bus and alight at "Tsurumine Elementary School," then walk 1 minute. == References == {{Unreferenced help}} {{Reflist}} == See Also == * [[:en:Minamoto no Yoriyoshi|Minamoto no Yoriyoshi]] * [[:en:Iwashimizu Hachimangū|Iwashimizu Hachimangū]] * {{ill|Hamaori Festival|ja|浜降祭|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hamaori Festival|qq=}} {{translated page|ja|鶴嶺八幡宮|version=101890838|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 2]] }} [[sn:Tsurumine Hachiman Shrine]] [[ja:鶴嶺八幡宮]] [[sn:surumine Hachiman Shrine]] [[Category:Hachiman shrines]] [[Category:Minamoto no Yoriyoshi]] [[Category:Son-sha in Kanagawa Prefecture]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Kanagawa Prefecture]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kanagawa Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings in Chigasaki, Kanagawa]] [[Category:Municipal History of Chigasaki]] [[Category:Sugawara no Michizane]] [[Category:pages without english wikidata name]] [[Category:qq]][[en:Tsurumine Hachiman Shrine]][[Category:qq]]

{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q199699]] Wikidata name: [[Category:Add wikidata]] [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:10, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Unreferenced|date=June 28, 2018}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name=Tsuruoka Hachimangū | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image=File:GrfKanagawakenChigasakishi-Turuminehachimangu-01.jpg | location=〒 253-0086
[[:en:Kanagawa Prefecture|Kanagawa Prefecture]][[:en:Chigasaki, Kanagawa|Chigasaki]] Hamano-go 462 | ISO=JP-14 | Latitude degrees = 35|Latitude minutes = 20|Latitude seconds = 18.38 | Longitude degrees=139|Longitude minutes=23|Longitude seconds=28.30 | deity={{ill|Ōjin|qid=Q317997}} | Shrine Rank=Former {{ill|Village shrine|ja|村社|lt=Son-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Village shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}, Former {{ill|District shrine|ja|郷社|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/District shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} | Foundation={{ill|Chōgen 3|ja|長元3年|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chōgen 3|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} ([[:en:1030|1030]]) | Main Hall=Nagare-zukuri | Alternate name=Tsuruoka Hachiman Shrine | festival=[[:en:September 15|September 15]] | Rituals={{ill|Hamaori Festival|ja|浜降祭|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hamaori Festival|qq=}} ([[:en:Marine Day|Marine Day]]) | Map=Japan Kanagawa | prefecture={{ill|Chigasaki|qid=Q633911}} | coordinates={{Coord|35.33843888888889|N|139.39119444444444|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | architecture_style={{ill|nagare-zukuri|qid=Q210684}} }} '''Tsuruoka Hachimangū''' (鶴嶺八幡宮) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|shrine]] located in [[:en:Chigasaki, Kanagawa|Chigasaki]], [[:en:Kanagawa Prefecture|Kanagawa Prefecture]]. It is also known as Tsuruoka Hachiman Shrine. Renowned as the center of Hachiman worship in [[:en:Sagami Province|Sagami Province]], the shrine has been revered since ancient times as a significant site of {{ill|Hachiman faith|ja|八幡信仰|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hachiman faith|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 八幡神|1=Hachiman|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q261637}}.Cited by the Kanagawa Prefecture Shrine Office According to the shrine, it was the first {{ill|clan shrine|ja|氏社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/clan shrine|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} established by the [[:en:Minamoto clan|Genji]] when they expanded into the Kanto region. == History == The shrine was founded in {{ill|Chōgen 3 (1030)|ja|長元3rd year （1030th year ）|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chōgen 3 (1030)|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, during the {{ill|Subjugation of Shimōsa|ja|下総の乱|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Subjugation of Shimōsa|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} by [[:en:Minamoto no Yoriyoshi|Minamoto no Yoriyoshi]], who hailed from [[:en:Kawachi Province|Kawachi Province]] (modern-day [[:en:Habikino|Habikino]], [[:en:Osaka Prefecture|Osaka Prefecture]]). Yoriyoshi enshrined [[:en:Iwashimizu Hachimangū|Iwashimizu Hachimangū]] at the site under the name "Kaijima Hachimangū" (another theory suggests it was {{ill|Usa Hachimangū|ja|宇佐八幡宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Usa Hachimangū|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 宇佐神宮|1=Usa Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q715632}}). Later, the Genji moved it to its current location. In {{ill|Tenki 3 (1055)|ja|天喜3rd year （1055th year ）|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenki 3 (1055)|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, during the [[:en:Former Nine Years' War|Earlier Nine Years' War]], Yoriyoshi prayed at the shrine and achieved victory. To honor this, he established the {{ill|Tsurugaoka Hachimangū|lt=Original Hachiman Shrine|ja|元八幡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsurugaoka Hachimangū|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 鶴岡八幡宮}} in [[:en:Yuigahama|Yuigahama]], Kamakura, in {{ill|Kōhei 6 (1063)|ja|康平6年（1063rd year ）|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōhei 6 (1063)|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. In {{ill|Ōtoku 2 (1085)|ja|応徳2nd year （1085th year ）|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtoku 2 (1085)|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, Minamoto no Yoshiie, Yoriyoshi's heir, dedicated the shrine in Hamano-go as "Tsuruoka Hachimangū." Subsequently, Tsuruoka Hachimangū gained prominence, receiving land and monetary support from [[:en:Minamoto no Yoritomo|Minamoto no Yoritomo]] and others. Over time, it became the center for the famous {{ill|Hamaori Festival|ja|浜降祭|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hamaori Festival|qq=}} and absorbed nearby shrines such as Sazuka Daimyōjin. == Deities == * [[:en:Emperor Ōjin|Emperor Ōjin]] * [[:en:Emperor Nintoku|Emperor Nintoku]] * Sazuka Daimyōjin (associated with [[:en:Ōyamazumi Shrine|Ōyamazumi Shrine]]) * {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|Sugawara no Michizane|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugawara no Michizane|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (from Tsurumine Tenmangū) == Annual Events == * {{ill|Hamaori Festival|ja|浜降祭|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hamaori Festival|qq=}} (Marine Day) * {{ill|Misorogi Ritual|ja|禊の神事|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Misorogi Ritual|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (June 29, formerly held on July 15) * {{ill|Reisai|lt=Reisai|lt=Reitaisai|ja|例大祭|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Reitaisai|qq=|ja_comment=fixed the redirect|1=Reisai|2=ja|3=例祭|4=fr|5=Reisai|lt=Reitaisai}} (September 15) == Grounds == ; '''Great Ginkgo Tree''' : A 950-year-old tree said to have been planted by Minamoto no Yoshiie to pray for victory. ; '''Miyagane (Shrine Bell)''' : The shrine bell, originally installed in {{ill|Enpō 8 (1680)|ja|延宝8th year （1680th year ）|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Enpō 8 (1680)|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, was reconstructed after the war in 1965 as a "peace bell." === Sub-Shrines === * '''Shōnan Awashima Shrine''' — Dedicated to {{ill|Sukunahikona|ja|少彦名命|1=Sukunabikona|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sukunahikona|13=User:Immanuelle/Sukunabikona|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スクナビコナ}} and {{ill|Awashima-no-Kami|ja|淡島神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Awashima-no-Kami|qq=}}. * '''Tsurumine Inari Shrine''' — Dedicated to {{ill|Ukanomitama|ja|宇迦之御魂|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ukanomitama|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ウカノミタマ}}. == Access == Take the JR Tōkaidō Main Line to [[:en:Chigasaki Station|Chigasaki Station]]. From the north exit, take a bus and alight at "Tsurumine Elementary School," then walk 1 minute. == References == {{Unreferenced help}} {{Reflist}} == See Also == * [[:en:Minamoto no Yoriyoshi|Minamoto no Yoriyoshi]] * [[:en:Iwashimizu Hachimangū|Iwashimizu Hachimangū]] * {{ill|Hamaori Festival|ja|浜降祭|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hamaori Festival|qq=}} {{translated page|ja|鶴嶺八幡宮|version=101890838|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 2]] }} [[sn:Tsurumine Hachiman Shrine]] [[ja:鶴嶺八幡宮]] [[sn:surumine Hachiman Shrine]] [[Category:Hachiman shrines]] [[Category:Minamoto no Yoriyoshi]] [[Category:Son-sha in Kanagawa Prefecture]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Kanagawa Prefecture]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kanagawa Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings in Chigasaki, Kanagawa]] [[Category:Municipal History of Chigasaki]] [[Category:Sugawara no Michizane]] [[Category:pages without english wikidata name]] [[Category:qq]][[en:Tsurumine Hachiman Shrine]][[Category:qq]]

[[:en:Kanagawa Prefecture|Kanagawa Prefecture]][[:en:Chigasaki, Kanagawa|Chigasaki]] Hamano-go 462 | ISO=JP-14 | Latitude degrees = 35|Latitude minutes = 20|Latitude seconds = 18.38 | Longitude degrees=139|Longitude minutes=23|Longitude seconds=28.30 | deity={{ill|Ōjin|qid=Q317997}} | Shrine Rank=Former {{ill|Village shrine|ja|村社|lt=Son-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Village shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}, Former {{ill|District shrine|ja|郷社|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/District shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} | Foundation={{ill|Chōgen 3|ja|長元3年|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chōgen 3|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} ([[:en:1030|1030]]) | Main Hall=Nagare-zukuri | Alternate name=Tsuruoka Hachiman Shrine | festival=[[:en:September 15|September 15]] | Rituals={{ill|Hamaori Festival|ja|浜降祭|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hamaori Festival|qq=}} ([[:en:Marine Day|Marine Day]]) | Map=Japan Kanagawa | prefecture={{ill|Chigasaki|qid=Q633911}} | coordinates={{Coord|35.33843888888889|N|139.39119444444444|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | architecture_style={{ill|nagare-zukuri|qid=Q210684}} }} '''Tsuruoka Hachimangū''' (鶴嶺八幡宮) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|shrine]] located in [[:en:Chigasaki, Kanagawa|Chigasaki]], [[:en:Kanagawa Prefecture|Kanagawa Prefecture]]. It is also known as Tsuruoka Hachiman Shrine. Renowned as the center of Hachiman worship in [[:en:Sagami Province|Sagami Province]], the shrine has been revered since ancient times as a significant site of {{ill|Hachiman faith|ja|八幡信仰|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hachiman faith|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 八幡神|1=Hachiman|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q261637}}.Cited by the Kanagawa Prefecture Shrine Office According to the shrine, it was the first {{ill|clan shrine|ja|氏社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/clan shrine|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} established by the [[:en:Minamoto clan|Genji]] when they expanded into the Kanto region. == History == The shrine was founded in {{ill|Chōgen 3 (1030)|ja|長元3rd year （1030th year ）|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chōgen 3 (1030)|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, during the {{ill|Subjugation of Shimōsa|ja|下総の乱|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Subjugation of Shimōsa|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} by [[:en:Minamoto no Yoriyoshi|Minamoto no Yoriyoshi]], who hailed from [[:en:Kawachi Province|Kawachi Province]] (modern-day [[:en:Habikino|Habikino]], [[:en:Osaka Prefecture|Osaka Prefecture]]). Yoriyoshi enshrined [[:en:Iwashimizu Hachimangū|Iwashimizu Hachimangū]] at the site under the name "Kaijima Hachimangū" (another theory suggests it was {{ill|Usa Hachimangū|ja|宇佐八幡宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Usa Hachimangū|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 宇佐神宮|1=Usa Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q715632}}). Later, the Genji moved it to its current location. In {{ill|Tenki 3 (1055)|ja|天喜3rd year （1055th year ）|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenki 3 (1055)|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, during the [[:en:Former Nine Years' War|Earlier Nine Years' War]], Yoriyoshi prayed at the shrine and achieved victory. To honor this, he established the {{ill|Tsurugaoka Hachimangū|lt=Original Hachiman Shrine|ja|元八幡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsurugaoka Hachimangū|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 鶴岡八幡宮}} in [[:en:Yuigahama|Yuigahama]], Kamakura, in {{ill|Kōhei 6 (1063)|ja|康平6年（1063rd year ）|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōhei 6 (1063)|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. In {{ill|Ōtoku 2 (1085)|ja|応徳2nd year （1085th year ）|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtoku 2 (1085)|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, Minamoto no Yoshiie, Yoriyoshi's heir, dedicated the shrine in Hamano-go as "Tsuruoka Hachimangū." Subsequently, Tsuruoka Hachimangū gained prominence, receiving land and monetary support from [[:en:Minamoto no Yoritomo|Minamoto no Yoritomo]] and others. Over time, it became the center for the famous {{ill|Hamaori Festival|ja|浜降祭|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hamaori Festival|qq=}} and absorbed nearby shrines such as Sazuka Daimyōjin. == Deities == * [[:en:Emperor Ōjin|Emperor Ōjin]] * [[:en:Emperor Nintoku|Emperor Nintoku]] * Sazuka Daimyōjin (associated with [[:en:Ōyamazumi Shrine|Ōyamazumi Shrine]]) * {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|Sugawara no Michizane|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugawara no Michizane|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (from Tsurumine Tenmangū) == Annual Events == * {{ill|Hamaori Festival|ja|浜降祭|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hamaori Festival|qq=}} (Marine Day) * {{ill|Misorogi Ritual|ja|禊の神事|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Misorogi Ritual|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (June 29, formerly held on July 15) * {{ill|Reisai|lt=Reisai|lt=Reitaisai|ja|例大祭|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Reitaisai|qq=|ja_comment=fixed the redirect|1=Reisai|2=ja|3=例祭|4=fr|5=Reisai|lt=Reitaisai}} (September 15) == Grounds == ; '''Great Ginkgo Tree''' : A 950-year-old tree said to have been planted by Minamoto no Yoshiie to pray for victory. ; '''Miyagane (Shrine Bell)''' : The shrine bell, originally installed in {{ill|Enpō 8 (1680)|ja|延宝8th year （1680th year ）|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Enpō 8 (1680)|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, was reconstructed after the war in 1965 as a "peace bell." === Sub-Shrines === * '''Shōnan Awashima Shrine''' — Dedicated to {{ill|Sukunahikona|ja|少彦名命|1=Sukunabikona|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sukunahikona|13=User:Immanuelle/Sukunabikona|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スクナビコナ}} and {{ill|Awashima-no-Kami|ja|淡島神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Awashima-no-Kami|qq=}}. * '''Tsurumine Inari Shrine''' — Dedicated to {{ill|Ukanomitama|ja|宇迦之御魂|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ukanomitama|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ウカノミタマ}}. == Access == Take the JR Tōkaidō Main Line to [[:en:Chigasaki Station|Chigasaki Station]]. From the north exit, take a bus and alight at "Tsurumine Elementary School," then walk 1 minute. == References == {{Unreferenced help}} {{Reflist}} == See Also == * [[:en:Minamoto no Yoriyoshi|Minamoto no Yoriyoshi]] * [[:en:Iwashimizu Hachimangū|Iwashimizu Hachimangū]] * {{ill|Hamaori Festival|ja|浜降祭|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hamaori Festival|qq=}} {{translated page|ja|鶴嶺八幡宮|version=101890838|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 2]] }} [[sn:Tsurumine Hachiman Shrine]] [[ja:鶴嶺八幡宮]] [[sn:surumine Hachiman Shrine]] [[Category:Hachiman shrines]] [[Category:Minamoto no Yoriyoshi]] [[Category:Son-sha in Kanagawa Prefecture]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Kanagawa Prefecture]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kanagawa Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings in Chigasaki, Kanagawa]] [[Category:Municipal History of Chigasaki]] [[Category:Sugawara no Michizane]] [[Category:pages without english wikidata name]] [[Category:qq]][[en:Tsurumine Hachiman Shrine]][[Category:qq]]

In {{ill|Tenki 3 (1055)|ja|天喜3rd year （1055th year ）|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenki 3 (1055)|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, during the [[:en:Former Nine Years' War|Earlier Nine Years' War]], Yoriyoshi prayed at the shrine and achieved victory. To honor this, he established the {{ill|Tsurugaoka Hachimangū|lt=Original Hachiman Shrine|ja|元八幡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsurugaoka Hachimangū|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 鶴岡八幡宮}} in [[:en:Yuigahama|Yuigahama]], Kamakura, in {{ill|Kōhei 6 (1063)|ja|康平6年（1063rd year ）|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōhei 6 (1063)|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. In {{ill|Ōtoku 2 (1085)|ja|応徳2nd year （1085th year ）|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtoku 2 (1085)|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, Minamoto no Yoshiie, Yoriyoshi's heir, dedicated the shrine in Hamano-go as "Tsuruoka Hachimangū." Subsequently, Tsuruoka Hachimangū gained prominence, receiving land and monetary support from [[:en:Minamoto no Yoritomo|Minamoto no Yoritomo]] and others. Over time, it became the center for the famous {{ill|Hamaori Festival|ja|浜降祭|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hamaori Festival|qq=}} and absorbed nearby shrines such as Sazuka Daimyōjin. == Deities == * [[:en:Emperor Ōjin|Emperor Ōjin]] * [[:en:Emperor Nintoku|Emperor Nintoku]] * Sazuka Daimyōjin (associated with [[:en:Ōyamazumi Shrine|Ōyamazumi Shrine]]) * {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|Sugawara no Michizane|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugawara no Michizane|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (from Tsurumine Tenmangū) == Annual Events == * {{ill|Hamaori Festival|ja|浜降祭|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hamaori Festival|qq=}} (Marine Day) * {{ill|Misorogi Ritual|ja|禊の神事|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Misorogi Ritual|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (June 29, formerly held on July 15) * {{ill|Reisai|lt=Reisai|lt=Reitaisai|ja|例大祭|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Reitaisai|qq=|ja_comment=fixed the redirect|1=Reisai|2=ja|3=例祭|4=fr|5=Reisai|lt=Reitaisai}} (September 15) == Grounds == ; '''Great Ginkgo Tree''' : A 950-year-old tree said to have been planted by Minamoto no Yoshiie to pray for victory. ; '''Miyagane (Shrine Bell)''' : The shrine bell, originally installed in {{ill|Enpō 8 (1680)|ja|延宝8th year （1680th year ）|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Enpō 8 (1680)|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, was reconstructed after the war in 1965 as a "peace bell." === Sub-Shrines === * '''Shōnan Awashima Shrine''' — Dedicated to {{ill|Sukunahikona|ja|少彦名命|1=Sukunabikona|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sukunahikona|13=User:Immanuelle/Sukunabikona|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スクナビコナ}} and {{ill|Awashima-no-Kami|ja|淡島神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Awashima-no-Kami|qq=}}. * '''Tsurumine Inari Shrine''' — Dedicated to {{ill|Ukanomitama|ja|宇迦之御魂|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ukanomitama|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ウカノミタマ}}. == Access == Take the JR Tōkaidō Main Line to [[:en:Chigasaki Station|Chigasaki Station]]. From the north exit, take a bus and alight at "Tsurumine Elementary School," then walk 1 minute. == References == {{Unreferenced help}} {{Reflist}} == See Also == * [[:en:Minamoto no Yoriyoshi|Minamoto no Yoriyoshi]] * [[:en:Iwashimizu Hachimangū|Iwashimizu Hachimangū]] * {{ill|Hamaori Festival|ja|浜降祭|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hamaori Festival|qq=}} {{translated page|ja|鶴嶺八幡宮|version=101890838|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 2]] }} [[sn:Tsurumine Hachiman Shrine]] [[ja:鶴嶺八幡宮]] [[sn:surumine Hachiman Shrine]] [[Category:Hachiman shrines]] [[Category:Minamoto no Yoriyoshi]] [[Category:Son-sha in Kanagawa Prefecture]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Kanagawa Prefecture]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kanagawa Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings in Chigasaki, Kanagawa]] [[Category:Municipal History of Chigasaki]] [[Category:Sugawara no Michizane]] [[Category:pages without english wikidata name]] [[en:Tsurumine Hachiman Shrine]]

{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[Category:Has wikidata]][[d:Q199699]] Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:10, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Unreferenced|date=June 28, 2018}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name=Tsuruoka Hachimangū | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image=File:GrfKanagawakenChigasakishi-Turuminehachimangu-01.jpg | location=〒 253-0086
[[:en:Kanagawa Prefecture|Kanagawa Prefecture]][[:en:Chigasaki, Kanagawa|Chigasaki]] Hamano-go 462 | ISO=JP-14 | Latitude degrees = 35|Latitude minutes = 20|Latitude seconds = 18.38 | Longitude degrees=139|Longitude minutes=23|Longitude seconds=28.30 | deity={{ill|Ōjin|qid=Q317997}} | Shrine Rank=Former {{ill|Village shrine|ja|村社|lt=Son-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Village shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}, Former {{ill|District shrine|ja|郷社|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/District shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} | Foundation={{ill|Chōgen 3|ja|長元3年|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chōgen 3|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} ([[:en:1030|1030]]) | Main Hall=Nagare-zukuri | Alternate name=Tsuruoka Hachiman Shrine | festival=[[:en:September 15|September 15]] | Rituals={{ill|Hamaori Festival|ja|浜降祭|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hamaori Festival|qq=}} ([[:en:Marine Day|Marine Day]]) | Map=Japan Kanagawa | prefecture={{ill|Chigasaki|qid=Q633911}} | coordinates={{Coord|35.33843888888889|N|139.39119444444444|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | architecture_style={{ill|nagare-zukuri|qid=Q210684}} }} '''Tsuruoka Hachimangū''' (鶴嶺八幡宮) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|shrine]] located in [[:en:Chigasaki, Kanagawa|Chigasaki]], [[:en:Kanagawa Prefecture|Kanagawa Prefecture]]. It is also known as Tsuruoka Hachiman Shrine. Renowned as the center of Hachiman worship in [[:en:Sagami Province|Sagami Province]], the shrine has been revered since ancient times as a significant site of {{ill|Hachiman faith|ja|八幡信仰|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hachiman faith|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 八幡神|1=Hachiman|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q261637}}.Cited by the Kanagawa Prefecture Shrine Office According to the shrine, it was the first {{ill|clan shrine|ja|氏社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/clan shrine|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} established by the [[:en:Minamoto clan|Genji]] when they expanded into the Kanto region. == History == The shrine was founded in {{ill|Chōgen 3 (1030)|ja|長元3rd year （1030th year ）|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chōgen 3 (1030)|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, during the {{ill|Subjugation of Shimōsa|ja|下総の乱|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Subjugation of Shimōsa|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} by [[:en:Minamoto no Yoriyoshi|Minamoto no Yoriyoshi]], who hailed from [[:en:Kawachi Province|Kawachi Province]] (modern-day [[:en:Habikino|Habikino]], [[:en:Osaka Prefecture|Osaka Prefecture]]). Yoriyoshi enshrined [[:en:Iwashimizu Hachimangū|Iwashimizu Hachimangū]] at the site under the name "Kaijima Hachimangū" (another theory suggests it was {{ill|Usa Hachimangū|ja|宇佐八幡宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Usa Hachimangū|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 宇佐神宮|1=Usa Jingū|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q715632}}). Later, the Genji moved it to its current location. In {{ill|Tenki 3 (1055)|ja|天喜3rd year （1055th year ）|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenki 3 (1055)|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, during the [[:en:Former Nine Years' War|Earlier Nine Years' War]], Yoriyoshi prayed at the shrine and achieved victory. To honor this, he established the {{ill|Tsurugaoka Hachimangū|lt=Original Hachiman Shrine|ja|元八幡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsurugaoka Hachimangū|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 鶴岡八幡宮}} in [[:en:Yuigahama|Yuigahama]], Kamakura, in {{ill|Kōhei 6 (1063)|ja|康平6年（1063rd year ）|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōhei 6 (1063)|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. In {{ill|Ōtoku 2 (1085)|ja|応徳2nd year （1085th year ）|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtoku 2 (1085)|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, Minamoto no Yoshiie, Yoriyoshi's heir, dedicated the shrine in Hamano-go as "Tsuruoka Hachimangū." Subsequently, Tsuruoka Hachimangū gained prominence, receiving land and monetary support from [[:en:Minamoto no Yoritomo|Minamoto no Yoritomo]] and others. Over time, it became the center for the famous {{ill|Hamaori Festival|ja|浜降祭|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hamaori Festival|qq=}} and absorbed nearby shrines such as Sazuka Daimyōjin. == Deities == * [[:en:Emperor Ōjin|Emperor Ōjin]] * [[:en:Emperor Nintoku|Emperor Nintoku]] * Sazuka Daimyōjin (associated with [[:en:Ōyamazumi Shrine|Ōyamazumi Shrine]]) * {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|ja|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugawara no Michizane|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (from Tsurumine Tenmangū) == Annual Events == * {{ill|Hamaori Festival|ja|浜降祭|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hamaori Festival|qq=}} (Marine Day) * {{ill|Misorogi Ritual|ja|禊の神事|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Misorogi Ritual|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (June 29, formerly held on July 15) * {{ill|Reisai|lt=Reisai|lt=Reitaisai|ja|例大祭|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Reitaisai|qq=|ja_comment=fixed the redirect|1=Reisai|2=ja|3=例祭|4=fr|5=Reisai|lt=Reitaisai}} (September 15) == Grounds == ; '''Great Ginkgo Tree''' : A 950-year-old tree said to have been planted by Minamoto no Yoshiie to pray for victory. ; '''Miyagane (Shrine Bell)''' : The shrine bell, originally installed in {{ill|Enpō 8 (1680)|ja|延宝8th year （1680th year ）|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Enpō 8 (1680)|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, was reconstructed after the war in 1965 as a "peace bell." === Sub-Shrines === * '''Shōnan Awashima Shrine''' — Dedicated to {{ill|Sukunahikona|ja|少彦名命|1=Sukunabikona|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sukunahikona|13=User:Immanuelle/Sukunabikona|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スクナビコナ}} and {{ill|Awashima-no-Kami|ja|淡島神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Awashima-no-Kami|qq=}}. * '''Tsurumine Inari Shrine''' — Dedicated to {{ill|Ukanomitama|ja|宇迦之御魂|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ukanomitama|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ウカノミタマ}}. == Access == Take the JR Tōkaidō Main Line to [[:en:Chigasaki Station|Chigasaki Station]]. From the north exit, take a bus and alight at "Tsurumine Elementary School," then walk 1 minute. == References == {{Unreferenced help}} {{Reflist}} == See Also == * [[:en:Minamoto no Yoriyoshi|Minamoto no Yoriyoshi]] * [[:en:Iwashimizu Hachimangū|Iwashimizu Hachimangū]] * {{ill|Hamaori Festival|ja|浜降祭|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hamaori Festival|qq=}} {{translated page|ja|鶴嶺八幡宮|version=101890838|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 2]] }} [[sn:Tsurumine Hachiman Shrine]] [[ja:鶴嶺八幡宮]] [[sn:surumine Hachiman Shrine]] [[Category:Hachiman shrines]] [[Category:Minamoto no Yoriyoshi]] [[Category:Son-sha in Kanagawa Prefecture]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Kanagawa Prefecture]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kanagawa Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings in Chigasaki, Kanagawa]] [[Category:Municipal History of Chigasaki]] [[Category:Sugawara no Michizane]] [[Category:pages without english wikidata name]] [[en:Tsurumine Hachiman Shrine]]

{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[Category:Has wikidata]][[d:Q199699]] Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:10, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Unreferenced|date=June 28, 2018}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name=Tsuruoka Hachimangū | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image=File:GrfKanagawakenChigasakishi-Turuminehachimangu-01.jpg | location=〒 253-0086
[[:en:Kanagawa Prefecture|Kanagawa Prefecture]][[:en:Chigasaki, Kanagawa|Chigasaki]] Hamano-go 462 | ISO=JP-14 | Latitude degrees = 35|Latitude minutes = 20|Latitude seconds = 18.38 | Longitude degrees=139|Longitude minutes=23|Longitude seconds=28.30 | deity={{ill|Ōjin|qid=Q317997}} | Shrine Rank=Former {{ill|Village shrine|ja|村社|lt=Son-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Village shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}, Former {{ill|District shrine|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|qid=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/District shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} | Foundation={{ill|Chōgen 3|ja|長元3年|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chōgen 3|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} ([[:en:1030|1030]]) | Main Hall=Nagare-zukuri | Alternate name=Tsuruoka Hachiman Shrine | festival=[[:en:September 15|September 15]] | Rituals={{ill|Hamaori Festival|ja|浜降祭|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hamaori Festival|qq=}} ([[:en:Marine Day|Marine Day]]) | Map=Japan Kanagawa | prefecture={{ill|Chigasaki|qid=Q633911}} | coordinates={{Coord|35.33843888888889|N|139.39119444444444|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | architecture_style={{ill|nagare-zukuri|qid=Q210684}} }} '''Tsuruoka Hachimangū''' (鶴嶺八幡宮) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|shrine]] located in [[:en:Chigasaki, Kanagawa|Chigasaki]], [[:en:Kanagawa Prefecture|Kanagawa Prefecture]]. It is also known as Tsuruoka Hachiman Shrine. Renowned as the center of Hachiman worship in [[:en:Sagami Province|Sagami Province]], the shrine has been revered since ancient times as a significant site of {{ill|Hachiman faith|ja|八幡信仰|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hachiman faith|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 八幡神|1=Hachiman|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q261637}}.Cited by the Kanagawa Prefecture Shrine Office According to the shrine, it was the first {{ill|clan shrine|ja|氏社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/clan shrine|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} established by the [[:en:Minamoto clan|Genji]] when they expanded into the Kanto region. == History == The shrine was founded in {{ill|Chōgen 3 (1030)|ja|長元3rd year （1030th year ）|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chōgen 3 (1030)|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, during the {{ill|Subjugation of Shimōsa|ja|下総の乱|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Subjugation of Shimōsa|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} by [[:en:Minamoto no Yoriyoshi|Minamoto no Yoriyoshi]], who hailed from [[:en:Kawachi Province|Kawachi Province]] (modern-day [[:en:Habikino|Habikino]], [[:en:Osaka Prefecture|Osaka Prefecture]]). Yoriyoshi enshrined [[:en:Iwashimizu Hachimangū|Iwashimizu Hachimangū]] at the site under the name "Kaijima Hachimangū" (another theory suggests it was {{ill|Usa Hachimangū|ja|宇佐八幡宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Usa Hachimangū|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 宇佐神宮|1=Usa Jingū|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q715632}}). Later, the Genji moved it to its current location. In {{ill|Tenki 3 (1055)|ja|天喜3rd year （1055th year ）|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenki 3 (1055)|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, during the [[:en:Former Nine Years' War|Earlier Nine Years' War]], Yoriyoshi prayed at the shrine and achieved victory. To honor this, he established the {{ill|Tsurugaoka Hachimangū|lt=Original Hachiman Shrine|ja|元八幡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsurugaoka Hachimangū|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 鶴岡八幡宮}} in [[:en:Yuigahama|Yuigahama]], Kamakura, in {{ill|Kōhei 6 (1063)|ja|康平6年（1063rd year ）|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōhei 6 (1063)|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. In {{ill|Ōtoku 2 (1085)|ja|応徳2nd year （1085th year ）|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtoku 2 (1085)|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, Minamoto no Yoshiie, Yoriyoshi's heir, dedicated the shrine in Hamano-go as "Tsuruoka Hachimangū." Subsequently, Tsuruoka Hachimangū gained prominence, receiving land and monetary support from [[:en:Minamoto no Yoritomo|Minamoto no Yoritomo]] and others. Over time, it became the center for the famous {{ill|Hamaori Festival|ja|浜降祭|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hamaori Festival|qq=}} and absorbed nearby shrines such as Sazuka Daimyōjin. == Deities == * [[:en:Emperor Ōjin|Emperor Ōjin]] * [[:en:Emperor Nintoku|Emperor Nintoku]] * Sazuka Daimyōjin (associated with [[:en:Ōyamazumi Shrine|Ōyamazumi Shrine]]) * {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|ja|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugawara no Michizane|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q382005}} (from Tsurumine Tenmangū) == Annual Events == * {{ill|Hamaori Festival|ja|浜降祭|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hamaori Festival|qq=}} (Marine Day) * {{ill|Misorogi Ritual|ja|禊の神事|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Misorogi Ritual|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (June 29, formerly held on July 15) * {{ill|Reisai|lt=Reisai|lt=Reitaisai|ja|例大祭|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Reitaisai|qq=|ja_comment=fixed the redirect|1=Reisai|2=ja|3=例祭|4=fr|5=Reisai|lt=Reitaisai}} (September 15) == Grounds == ; '''Great Ginkgo Tree''' : A 950-year-old tree said to have been planted by Minamoto no Yoshiie to pray for victory. ; '''Miyagane (Shrine Bell)''' : The shrine bell, originally installed in {{ill|Enpō 8 (1680)|ja|延宝8th year （1680th year ）|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Enpō 8 (1680)|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, was reconstructed after the war in 1965 as a "peace bell." === Sub-Shrines === * '''Shōnan Awashima Shrine''' — Dedicated to {{ill|Sukunahikona|ja|少彦名命|1=Sukunabikona|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sukunahikona|13=User:Immanuelle/Sukunabikona|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スクナビコナ}} and {{ill|Awashima-no-Kami|ja|淡島神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Awashima-no-Kami|qq=}}. * '''Tsurumine Inari Shrine''' — Dedicated to {{ill|Ukanomitama|ja|宇迦之御魂|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ukanomitama|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ウカノミタマ}}. == Access == Take the JR Tōkaidō Main Line to [[:en:Chigasaki Station|Chigasaki Station]]. From the north exit, take a bus and alight at "Tsurumine Elementary School," then walk 1 minute. == References == {{Unreferenced help}} {{Reflist}} == See Also == * [[:en:Minamoto no Yoriyoshi|Minamoto no Yoriyoshi]] * [[:en:Iwashimizu Hachimangū|Iwashimizu Hachimangū]] * {{ill|Hamaori Festival|ja|浜降祭|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hamaori Festival|qq=}} {{translated page|ja|鶴嶺八幡宮|version=101890838|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 2]] }} [[sn:Tsurumine Hachiman Shrine]] [[ja:鶴嶺八幡宮]] [[sn:surumine Hachiman Shrine]] [[Category:Hachiman shrines]] [[Category:Minamoto no Yoriyoshi]] [[Category:Son-sha in Kanagawa Prefecture]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Kanagawa Prefecture]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kanagawa Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings in Chigasaki, Kanagawa]] [[Category:Municipal History of Chigasaki]] [[Category:Sugawara no Michizane]] [[Category:pages without english wikidata name]] [[en:Tsurumine Hachiman Shrine]]

It is also known as Tsuruoka Hachiman Shrine. Renowned as the center of Hachiman worship in [[:en:Sagami Province|Sagami Province]], the shrine has been revered since ancient times as a significant site of {{ill|Hachiman faith|ja|八幡信仰|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hachiman faith|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 八幡神|1=Hachiman|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q261637}}.Cited by the Kanagawa Prefecture Shrine Office According to the shrine, it was the first {{ill|clan shrine|ja|氏社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/clan shrine|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} established by the [[:en:Minamoto clan|Genji]] when they expanded into the Kanto region. == History == The shrine was founded in {{ill|Chōgen 3 (1030)|ja|長元3rd year （1030th year ）|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chōgen 3 (1030)|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, during the {{ill|Subjugation of Shimōsa|ja|下総の乱|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Subjugation of Shimōsa|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} by [[:en:Minamoto no Yoriyoshi|Minamoto no Yoriyoshi]], who hailed from [[:en:Kawachi Province|Kawachi Province]] (modern-day [[:en:Habikino|Habikino]], [[:en:Osaka Prefecture|Osaka Prefecture]]). Yoriyoshi enshrined [[:en:Iwashimizu Hachimangū|Iwashimizu Hachimangū]] at the site under the name "Kaijima Hachimangū" (another theory suggests it was {{ill|Usa Hachimangū|ja|宇佐八幡宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Usa Hachimangū|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 宇佐神宮|1=Usa Jingū|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q715632}}). Later, the Genji moved it to its current location. In {{ill|Tenki 3 (1055)|ja|天喜3rd year （1055th year ）|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenki 3 (1055)|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, during the [[:en:Former Nine Years' War|Earlier Nine Years' War]], Yoriyoshi prayed at the shrine and achieved victory. In {{ill|Ōtoku 2 (1085)|ja|応徳2nd year （1085th year ）|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtoku 2 (1085)|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, Minamoto no Yoshiie, Yoriyoshi's heir, dedicated the shrine in Hamano-go as "Tsuruoka Hachimangū." Subsequently, Tsuruoka Hachimangū gained prominence, receiving land and monetary support from [[:en:Minamoto no Yoritomo|Minamoto no Yoritomo]] and others. Over time, it became the center for the famous {{ill|Hamaori Festival|ja|浜降祭|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hamaori Festival|qq=}} and absorbed nearby shrines such as Sazuka Daimyōjin. == Deities == * [[:en:Emperor Ōjin|Emperor Ōjin]] * [[:en:Emperor Nintoku|Emperor Nintoku]] * Sazuka Daimyōjin (associated with [[:en:Ōyamazumi Shrine|Ōyamazumi Shrine]]) * {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|ja|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugawara no Michizane|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q382005}} (from Tsurumine Tenmangū) == Annual Events == * {{ill|Hamaori Festival|ja|浜降祭|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hamaori Festival|qq=}} (Marine Day) * {{ill|Misorogi Ritual|ja|禊の神事|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Misorogi Ritual|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (June 29, formerly held on July 15) * {{ill|Reisai|lt=Reisai|lt=Reitaisai|ja|例大祭|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Reitaisai|qq=|ja_comment=fixed the redirect|1=Reisai|2=ja|3=例祭|4=fr|5=Reisai|lt=Reitaisai}} (September 15) == Grounds == ; '''Great Ginkgo Tree''' : A 950-year-old tree said to have been planted by Minamoto no Yoshiie to pray for victory. ; '''Miyagane (Shrine Bell)''' : The shrine bell, originally installed in {{ill|Enpō 8 (1680)|ja|延宝8th year （1680th year ）|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Enpō 8 (1680)|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, was reconstructed after the war in 1965 as a "peace bell." === Sub-Shrines === * '''Shōnan Awashima Shrine''' — Dedicated to {{ill|Sukunahikona|ja|少彦名命|1=Sukunabikona|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sukunahikona|13=User:Immanuelle/Sukunabikona|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スクナビコナ}} and {{ill|Awashima-no-Kami|ja|淡島神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Awashima-no-Kami|qq=}}. * '''Tsurumine Inari Shrine''' — Dedicated to {{ill|Ukanomitama|ja|宇迦之御魂|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ukanomitama|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ウカノミタマ}}. == Access == Take the JR Tōkaidō Main Line to [[:en:Chigasaki Station|Chigasaki Station]]. From the north exit, take a bus and alight at "Tsurumine Elementary School," then walk 1 minute. == References == {{Unreferenced help}} {{Reflist}} == See Also == * [[:en:Minamoto no Yoriyoshi|Minamoto no Yoriyoshi]] * [[:en:Iwashimizu Hachimangū|Iwashimizu Hachimangū]] * {{ill|Hamaori Festival|ja|浜降祭|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hamaori Festival|qq=}} {{translated page|ja|鶴嶺八幡宮|version=101890838|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 2]] }} [[sn:Tsurumine Hachiman Shrine]] [[ja:鶴嶺八幡宮]] [[sn:surumine Hachiman Shrine]] [[Category:Hachiman shrines]] [[Category:Minamoto no Yoriyoshi]] [[Category:Son-sha in Kanagawa Prefecture]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Kanagawa Prefecture]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kanagawa Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings in Chigasaki, Kanagawa]] [[Category:Municipal History of Chigasaki]] [[Category:Sugawara no Michizane]] [[Category:pages without english wikidata name]] [[en:Tsurumine Hachiman Shrine]]

In {{ill|Tenki 3 (1055)|ja|天喜3rd year （1055th year ）|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenki 3 (1055)|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, during the [[:en:Former Nine Years' War|Earlier Nine Years' War]], Yoriyoshi prayed at the shrine and achieved victory. To honor this, he established the {{ill|Tsurugaoka Hachimangū|lt=Original Hachiman Shrine|ja|元八幡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsurugaoka Hachimangū|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 鶴岡八幡宮}} in [[:en:Yuigahama|Yuigahama]], Kamakura, in {{ill|Kōhei 6 (1063)|ja|康平6年（1063rd year ）|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōhei 6 (1063)|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. In {{ill|Ōtoku 2 (1085)|ja|応徳2nd year （1085th year ）|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtoku 2 (1085)|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, Minamoto no Yoshiie, Yoriyoshi's heir, dedicated the shrine in Hamano-go as "Tsuruoka Hachimangū." Subsequently, Tsuruoka Hachimangū gained prominence, receiving land and monetary support from [[:en:Minamoto no Yoritomo|Minamoto no Yoritomo]] and others. Over time, it became the center for the famous {{ill|Hamaori Festival|ja|浜降祭|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hamaori Festival|qq=}} and absorbed nearby shrines such as Sazuka Daimyōjin. == Deities == * [[:en:Emperor Ōjin|Emperor Ōjin]] * [[:en:Emperor Nintoku|Emperor Nintoku]] * Sazuka Daimyōjin (associated with [[:en:Ōyamazumi Shrine|Ōyamazumi Shrine]]) * {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|ja|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugawara no Michizane|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q382005}} (from Tsurumine Tenmangū) == Annual Events == * {{ill|Hamaori Festival|ja|浜降祭|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hamaori Festival|qq=}} (Marine Day) * {{ill|Misorogi Ritual|ja|禊の神事|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Misorogi Ritual|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (June 29, formerly held on July 15) * {{ill|Reisai|lt=Reisai|lt=Reitaisai|ja|例大祭|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Reitaisai|qq=|ja_comment=fixed the redirect|1=Reisai|2=ja|3=例祭|4=fr|5=Reisai|lt=Reitaisai}} (September 15) == Grounds == ; '''Great Ginkgo Tree''' : A 950-year-old tree said to have been planted by Minamoto no Yoshiie to pray for victory. ; '''Miyagane (Shrine Bell)''' : The shrine bell, originally installed in {{ill|Enpō 8 (1680)|ja|延宝8th year （1680th year ）|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Enpō 8 (1680)|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, was reconstructed after the war in 1965 as a "peace bell." === Sub-Shrines === * '''Shōnan Awashima Shrine''' — Dedicated to {{ill|Sukunahikona|ja|少彦名命|1=Sukunabikona|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sukunahikona|13=User:Immanuelle/Sukunabikona|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スクナビコナ}} and {{ill|Awashima-no-Kami|ja|淡島神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Awashima-no-Kami|qq=}}. * '''Tsurumine Inari Shrine''' — Dedicated to {{ill|Ukanomitama|ja|宇迦之御魂|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ukanomitama|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ウカノミタマ}}. == Access == Take the JR Tōkaidō Main Line to [[:en:Chigasaki Station|Chigasaki Station]]. From the north exit, take a bus and alight at "Tsurumine Elementary School," then walk 1 minute. == References == {{Unreferenced help}} {{Reflist}} == See Also == * [[:en:Minamoto no Yoriyoshi|Minamoto no Yoriyoshi]] * [[:en:Iwashimizu Hachimangū|Iwashimizu Hachimangū]] * {{ill|Hamaori Festival|ja|浜降祭|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hamaori Festival|qq=}} {{translated page|ja|鶴嶺八幡宮|version=101890838|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 2]] }} [[sn:Tsurumine Hachiman Shrine]] [[ja:鶴嶺八幡宮]] [[sn:surumine Hachiman Shrine]] [[Category:Hachiman shrines]] [[Category:Minamoto no Yoriyoshi]] [[Category:Son-sha in Kanagawa Prefecture]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Kanagawa Prefecture]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kanagawa Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings in Chigasaki, Kanagawa]] [[Category:Municipal History of Chigasaki]] [[Category:Sugawara no Michizane]] [[Category:pages without english wikidata name]] [[en:Tsurumine Hachiman Shrine]] {{wikidata link|Q199699}}

{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[Category:Has wikidata]][[d:Q199699]] Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:10, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Unreferenced|date=June 28, 2018}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name=Tsuruoka Hachimangū | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image=File:GrfKanagawakenChigasakishi-Turuminehachimangu-01.jpg | location=〒 253-0086
[[:en:Kanagawa Prefecture|Kanagawa Prefecture]][[:en:Chigasaki, Kanagawa|Chigasaki]] Hamano-go 462 | ISO=JP-14 | Latitude degrees = 35|Latitude minutes = 20|Latitude seconds = 18.38 | Longitude degrees=139|Longitude minutes=23|Longitude seconds=28.30 | deity={{ill|Ōjin|qid=Q317997}} | Shrine Rank=Former {{ill|Village shrine|ja|村社|1=Son-sha|qid=Q134917284}}{{efn|Former shrine rank|lt=Son-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Village shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}, Former {{ill|District shrine|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|qid=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/District shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} | Foundation={{ill|Chōgen 3|ja|長元3年|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chōgen 3|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} ([[:en:1030|1030]]) | Main Hall=Nagare-zukuri | Alternate name=Tsuruoka Hachiman Shrine | festival=[[:en:September 15|September 15]] | Rituals={{ill|Hamaori Festival|ja|浜降祭|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hamaori Festival|qq=}} ([[:en:Marine Day|Marine Day]]) | Map=Japan Kanagawa | prefecture={{ill|Chigasaki|qid=Q633911}} | coordinates={{Coord|35.33843888888889|N|139.39119444444444|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | architecture_style={{ill|nagare-zukuri|qid=Q210684}} }} '''Tsuruoka Hachimangū''' (鶴嶺八幡宮) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|shrine]] located in [[:en:Chigasaki, Kanagawa|Chigasaki]], [[:en:Kanagawa Prefecture|Kanagawa Prefecture]]. It is also known as Tsuruoka Hachiman Shrine. Renowned as the center of Hachiman worship in [[:en:Sagami Province|Sagami Province]], the shrine has been revered since ancient times as a significant site of {{ill|Hachiman faith|ja|八幡信仰|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hachiman faith|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 八幡神|1=Hachiman|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q261637}}.Cited by the Kanagawa Prefecture Shrine Office According to the shrine, it was the first {{ill|clan shrine|ja|氏社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/clan shrine|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} established by the [[:en:Minamoto clan|Genji]] when they expanded into the Kanto region. == History == The shrine was founded in {{ill|Chōgen 3 (1030)|ja|長元3rd year （1030th year ）|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chōgen 3 (1030)|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, during the {{ill|Subjugation of Shimōsa|ja|下総の乱|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Subjugation of Shimōsa|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} by [[:en:Minamoto no Yoriyoshi|Minamoto no Yoriyoshi]], who hailed from [[:en:Kawachi Province|Kawachi Province]] (modern-day [[:en:Habikino|Habikino]], [[:en:Osaka Prefecture|Osaka Prefecture]]). Yoriyoshi enshrined [[:en:Iwashimizu Hachimangū|Iwashimizu Hachimangū]] at the site under the name "Kaijima Hachimangū" (another theory suggests it was {{ill|Usa Hachimangū|ja|宇佐八幡宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Usa Hachimangū|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 宇佐神宮|1=Usa Jingū|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q715632}}). Later, the Genji moved it to its current location. In {{ill|Tenki 3 (1055)|ja|天喜3rd year （1055th year ）|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenki 3 (1055)|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, during the [[:en:Former Nine Years' War|Earlier Nine Years' War]], Yoriyoshi prayed at the shrine and achieved victory. To honor this, he established the {{ill|Tsurugaoka Hachimangū|lt=Original Hachiman Shrine|ja|元八幡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsurugaoka Hachimangū|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 鶴岡八幡宮}} in [[:en:Yuigahama|Yuigahama]], Kamakura, in {{ill|Kōhei 6 (1063)|ja|康平6年（1063rd year ）|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōhei 6 (1063)|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. In {{ill|Ōtoku 2 (1085)|ja|応徳2nd year （1085th year ）|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtoku 2 (1085)|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, Minamoto no Yoshiie, Yoriyoshi's heir, dedicated the shrine in Hamano-go as "Tsuruoka Hachimangū." Subsequently, Tsuruoka Hachimangū gained prominence, receiving land and monetary support from [[:en:Minamoto no Yoritomo|Minamoto no Yoritomo]] and others. Over time, it became the center for the famous {{ill|Hamaori Festival|ja|浜降祭|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hamaori Festival|qq=}} and absorbed nearby shrines such as Sazuka Daimyōjin. == Deities == * [[:en:Emperor Ōjin|Emperor Ōjin]] * [[:en:Emperor Nintoku|Emperor Nintoku]] * Sazuka Daimyōjin (associated with [[:en:Ōyamazumi Shrine|Ōyamazumi Shrine]]) * {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|ja|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugawara no Michizane|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q382005}} (from Tsurumine Tenmangū) == Annual Events == * {{ill|Hamaori Festival|ja|浜降祭|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hamaori Festival|qq=}} (Marine Day) * {{ill|Misorogi Ritual|ja|禊の神事|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Misorogi Ritual|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (June 29, formerly held on July 15) * {{ill|Reisai|lt=Reisai|lt=Reitaisai|ja|例大祭|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Reitaisai|qq=|ja_comment=fixed the redirect|1=Reisai|2=ja|3=例祭|4=fr|5=Reisai|lt=Reitaisai}} (September 15) == Grounds == ; '''Great Ginkgo Tree''' : A 950-year-old tree said to have been planted by Minamoto no Yoshiie to pray for victory. ; '''Miyagane (Shrine Bell)''' : The shrine bell, originally installed in {{ill|Enpō 8 (1680)|ja|延宝8th year （1680th year ）|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Enpō 8 (1680)|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, was reconstructed after the war in 1965 as a "peace bell." === Sub-Shrines === * '''Shōnan Awashima Shrine''' — Dedicated to {{ill|Sukunahikona|ja|少彦名命|1=Sukunabikona|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sukunahikona|13=User:Immanuelle/Sukunabikona|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スクナビコナ}} and {{ill|Awashima-no-Kami|ja|淡島神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Awashima-no-Kami|qq=}}. * '''Tsurumine Inari Shrine''' — Dedicated to {{ill|Ukanomitama|ja|宇迦之御魂|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ukanomitama|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ウカノミタマ}}. == Access == Take the JR Tōkaidō Main Line to [[:en:Chigasaki Station|Chigasaki Station]]. From the north exit, take a bus and alight at "Tsurumine Elementary School," then walk 1 minute. == References == {{Unreferenced help}} {{Reflist}} == See Also == * [[:en:Minamoto no Yoriyoshi|Minamoto no Yoriyoshi]] * [[:en:Iwashimizu Hachimangū|Iwashimizu Hachimangū]] * {{ill|Hamaori Festival|ja|浜降祭|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hamaori Festival|qq=}} {{translated page|ja|鶴嶺八幡宮|version=101890838|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 2]] }} [[sn:Tsurumine Hachiman Shrine]] [[ja:鶴嶺八幡宮]] [[sn:surumine Hachiman Shrine]] [[Category:Hachiman shrines]] [[Category:Minamoto no Yoriyoshi]] [[Category:Son-sha in Kanagawa Prefecture]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Kanagawa Prefecture]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kanagawa Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings in Chigasaki, Kanagawa]] [[Category:Municipal History of Chigasaki]] [[Category:Sugawara no Michizane]] [[Category:pages without english wikidata name]] {{wikidata link|Q199699}}

'''Great Ginkgo Tree''' : A 950-year-old tree said to have been planted by Minamoto no Yoshiie to pray for victory. ; '''Miyagane (Shrine Bell)''' : The shrine bell, originally installed in {{ill|Enpō 8 (1680)|ja|延宝8th year （1680th year ）|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Enpō 8 (1680)|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, was reconstructed after the war in 1965 as a "peace bell." === Sub-Shrines === * '''Shōnan Awashima Shrine''' — Dedicated to {{ill|Sukunahikona|ja|少彦名命|1=Sukunabikona|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sukunahikona|13=User:Immanuelle/Sukunabikona|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スクナビコナ}} and {{ill|Awashima-no-Kami|ja|淡島神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Awashima-no-Kami|qq=}}. * '''Tsurumine Inari Shrine''' — Dedicated to {{ill|Ukanomitama|ja|宇迦之御魂|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ukanomitama|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ウカノミタマ}}. == Access == Take the JR Tōkaidō Main Line to [[:en:Chigasaki Station|Chigasaki Station]]. From the north exit, take a bus and alight at "Tsurumine Elementary School," then walk 1 minute. == References == {{Unreferenced help}} {{Reflist}} == See Also == * [[:en:Minamoto no Yoriyoshi|Minamoto no Yoriyoshi]] * [[:en:Iwashimizu Hachimangū|Iwashimizu Hachimangū]] * {{ill|Hamaori Festival|ja|浜降祭|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hamaori Festival|qq=}} {{translated page|ja|鶴嶺八幡宮|version=101890838|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 2]] }} [[ja:鶴嶺八幡宮]] [[Category:Hachiman shrines]] [[Category:Minamoto no Yoriyoshi]] [[Category:Son-sha in Kanagawa Prefecture]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Kanagawa Prefecture]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kanagawa Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings in Chigasaki, Kanagawa]] [[Category:Municipal History of Chigasaki]] [[Category:Sugawara no Michizane]] [[Category:pages without english wikidata name]] {{wikidata link|Q199699}}

{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[Category:Has wikidata]][[d:Q199699]] Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:10, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Unreferenced|date=June 28, 2018}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name=Tsuruoka Hachimangū | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image=File:GrfKanagawakenChigasakishi-Turuminehachimangu-01.jpg | location=〒 253-0086
[[:en:Kanagawa Prefecture|Kanagawa Prefecture]][[:en:Chigasaki, Kanagawa|Chigasaki]] Hamano-go 462 | ISO=JP-14 | Latitude degrees = 35|Latitude minutes = 20|Latitude seconds = 18.38 | Longitude degrees=139|Longitude minutes=23|Longitude seconds=28.30 | deity={{ill|Ōjin|qid=Q317997}} | Shrine Rank=Former {{ill|Village shrine|ja|村社|1=Son-sha|qid=Q134917284}}{{efn|Former shrine rank|lt=Son-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Village shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}, Former {{ill|District shrine|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|qid=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/District shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} | Foundation={{ill|Chōgen 3|ja|長元3年|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chōgen 3|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} ([[:en:1030|1030]]) | Main Hall=Nagare-zukuri | Alternate name=Tsuruoka Hachiman Shrine | festival=[[:en:September 15|September 15]] | Rituals={{ill|Hamaori Festival|ja|浜降祭|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hamaori Festival|qq=}} ([[:en:Marine Day|Marine Day]]) | Map=Japan Kanagawa | prefecture={{ill|Chigasaki|qid=Q633911}} | coordinates={{Coord|35.33843888888889|N|139.39119444444444|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | architecture_style={{ill|nagare-zukuri|qid=Q210684}} }} '''Tsuruoka Hachimangū''' (鶴嶺八幡宮) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|shrine]] located in [[:en:Chigasaki, Kanagawa|Chigasaki]], [[:en:Kanagawa Prefecture|Kanagawa Prefecture]]. It is also known as Tsuruoka Hachiman Shrine. Renowned as the center of Hachiman worship in [[:en:Sagami Province|Sagami Province]], the shrine has been revered since ancient times as a significant site of {{ill|Hachiman faith|ja|八幡信仰|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hachiman faith|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 八幡神|1=Hachiman|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q261637}}.Cited by the Kanagawa Prefecture Shrine Office According to the shrine, it was the first {{ill|clan shrine|ja|氏社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/clan shrine|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} established by the [[:en:Minamoto clan|Genji]] when they expanded into the Kanto region. == History == The shrine was founded in {{ill|Chōgen 3 (1030)|ja|長元3rd year （1030th year ）|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chōgen 3 (1030)|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, during the {{ill|Subjugation of Shimōsa|ja|下総の乱|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Subjugation of Shimōsa|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} by [[:en:Minamoto no Yoriyoshi|Minamoto no Yoriyoshi]], who hailed from [[:en:Kawachi Province|Kawachi Province]] (modern-day [[:en:Habikino|Habikino]], [[:en:Osaka Prefecture|Osaka Prefecture]]). Yoriyoshi enshrined [[:en:Iwashimizu Hachimangū|Iwashimizu Hachimangū]] at the site under the name "Kaijima Hachimangū" (another theory suggests it was {{ill|Usa Hachimangū|ja|宇佐八幡宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Usa Hachimangū|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 宇佐神宮|1=Usa Jingū|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q715632}}). Later, the Genji moved it to its current location. In {{ill|Tenki 3 (1055)|ja|天喜3rd year （1055th year ）|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenki 3 (1055)|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, during the [[:en:Former Nine Years' War|Earlier Nine Years' War]], Yoriyoshi prayed at the shrine and achieved victory. To honor this, he established the {{ill|Tsurugaoka Hachimangū|lt=Original Hachiman Shrine|ja|元八幡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsurugaoka Hachimangū|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 鶴岡八幡宮}} in [[:en:Yuigahama|Yuigahama]], Kamakura, in {{ill|Kōhei 6 (1063)|ja|康平6年（1063rd year ）|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōhei 6 (1063)|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. In {{ill|Ōtoku 2 (1085)|ja|応徳2nd year （1085th year ）|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtoku 2 (1085)|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, Minamoto no Yoshiie, Yoriyoshi's heir, dedicated the shrine in Hamano-go as "Tsuruoka Hachimangū." Subsequently, Tsuruoka Hachimangū gained prominence, receiving land and monetary support from [[:en:Minamoto no Yoritomo|Minamoto no Yoritomo]] and others. Over time, it became the center for the famous {{ill|Hamaori Festival|ja|浜降祭|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hamaori Festival|qq=}} and absorbed nearby shrines such as Sazuka Daimyōjin. == Deities == * [[:en:Emperor Ōjin|Emperor Ōjin]] * [[:en:Emperor Nintoku|Emperor Nintoku]] * Sazuka Daimyōjin (associated with [[:en:Ōyamazumi Shrine|Ōyamazumi Shrine]]) * {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|ja|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugawara no Michizane|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q382005}} (from Tsurumine Tenmangū) == Annual Events == * {{ill|Hamaori Festival|ja|浜降祭|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hamaori Festival|qq=}} (Marine Day) * {{ill|Misorogi Ritual|ja|禊の神事|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Misorogi Ritual|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (June 29, formerly held on July 15) * {{ill|Reisai|lt=Reisai|lt=Reitaisai|ja|例大祭|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Reitaisai|qq=|ja_comment=fixed the redirect|1=Reisai|2=ja|3=例祭|4=fr|5=Reisai|lt=Reitaisai}} (September 15) == Grounds == ; '''Great Ginkgo Tree''' : A 950-year-old tree said to have been planted by Minamoto no Yoshiie to pray for victory. ; '''Miyagane (Shrine Bell)''' : The shrine bell, originally installed in {{ill|Enpō 8 (1680)|ja|延宝8th year （1680th year ）|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Enpō 8 (1680)|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, was reconstructed after the war in 1965 as a "peace bell." === Sub-Shrines === * '''Shōnan Awashima Shrine''' — Dedicated to {{ill|Sukunahikona|ja|少彦名命|1=Sukunabikona|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sukunahikona|13=User:Immanuelle/Sukunabikona|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スクナビコナ}} and {{ill|Awashima-no-Kami|ja|淡島神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Awashima-no-Kami|qq=}}. * '''Tsurumine Inari Shrine''' — Dedicated to {{ill|Ukanomitama|ja|宇迦之御魂|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ukanomitama|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ウカノミタマ}}. == Access == Take the JR Tōkaidō Main Line to [[:en:Chigasaki Station|Chigasaki Station]]. From the north exit, take a bus and alight at "Tsurumine Elementary School," then walk 1 minute. == References == {{Unreferenced help}} {{Reflist}} == See Also == * [[:en:Minamoto no Yoriyoshi|Minamoto no Yoriyoshi]] * [[:en:Iwashimizu Hachimangū|Iwashimizu Hachimangū]] * {{ill|Hamaori Festival|ja|浜降祭|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hamaori Festival|qq=}} {{translated page|ja|鶴嶺八幡宮|version=101890838|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 2]] }} [[ja:鶴嶺八幡宮]] [[Category:Hachiman shrines]] [[Category:Minamoto no Yoriyoshi]] [[Category:Son-sha in Kanagawa Prefecture]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Kanagawa Prefecture]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kanagawa Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings in Chigasaki, Kanagawa]] [[Category:Municipal History of Chigasaki]] [[Category:Sugawara no Michizane]] [[Category:pages without english wikidata name]] [[Category:Qq 4]] {{wikidata link|Q199699}}

It is also known as Tsuruoka Hachiman Shrine. Renowned as the center of Hachiman worship in [[:en:Sagami Province|Sagami Province]], the shrine has been revered since ancient times as a significant site of {{ill|Hachiman faith|ja|八幡信仰|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hachiman faith|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 八幡神|1=Hachiman|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q261637}}.Cited by the Kanagawa Prefecture Shrine Office According to the shrine, it was the first {{ill|clan shrine|ja|氏社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/clan shrine|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} established by the [[:en:Minamoto clan|Genji]] when they expanded into the Kanto region. == History == The shrine was founded in {{ill|Chōgen 3 (1030)|ja|長元3rd year （1030th year ）|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chōgen 3 (1030)|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, during the {{ill|Subjugation of Shimōsa|ja|下総の乱|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Subjugation of Shimōsa|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} by [[:en:Minamoto no Yoriyoshi|Minamoto no Yoriyoshi]], who hailed from [[:en:Kawachi Province|Kawachi Province]] (modern-day [[:en:Habikino|Habikino]], [[:en:Osaka Prefecture|Osaka Prefecture]]). Yoriyoshi enshrined [[:en:Iwashimizu Hachimangū|Iwashimizu Hachimangū]] at the site under the name "Kaijima Hachimangū" (another theory suggests it was {{ill|Usa Hachimangū|ja|宇佐八幡宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Usa Hachimangū|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 宇佐神宮|1=Usa Jingū|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q715632}}). Later, the Genji moved it to its current location. In {{ill|Tenki 3 (1055)|ja|天喜3rd year （1055th year ）|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenki 3 (1055)|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, during the [[:en:Former Nine Years' War|Earlier Nine Years' War]], Yoriyoshi prayed at the shrine and achieved victory. To honor this, he established the {{ill|Tsurugaoka Hachimangū|lt=Original Hachiman Shrine|ja|元八幡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsurugaoka Hachimangū|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 鶴岡八幡宮}} in [[:en:Yuigahama|Yuigahama]], Kamakura, in {{ill|Kōhei 6 (1063)|ja|康平6年（1063rd year ）|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōhei 6 (1063)|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. In {{ill|Ōtoku 2 (1085)|ja|応徳2nd year （1085th year ）|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtoku 2 (1085)|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, Minamoto no Yoshiie, Yoriyoshi's heir, dedicated the shrine in Hamano-go as "Tsuruoka Hachimangū." Subsequently, Tsuruoka Hachimangū gained prominence, receiving land and monetary support from [[:en:Minamoto no Yoritomo|Minamoto no Yoritomo]] and others. Over time, it became the center for the famous {{ill|Hamaori Festival|ja|浜降祭|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hamaori Festival|qq=}} and absorbed nearby shrines such as Sazuka Daimyōjin. == Deities == * [[:en:Emperor Ōjin|Emperor Ōjin]] * [[:en:Emperor Nintoku|Emperor Nintoku]] * Sazuka Daimyōjin (associated with [[:en:Ōyamazumi Shrine|Ōyamazumi Shrine]]) * {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|ja|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugawara no Michizane|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q382005}} (from Tsurumine Tenmangū) == Annual Events == * {{ill|Hamaori Festival|ja|浜降祭|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hamaori Festival|qq=}} (Marine Day) * {{ill|Misorogi Ritual|ja|禊の神事|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Misorogi Ritual|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (June 29, formerly held on July 15) * {{ill|Reisai|lt=Reisai|lt=Reitaisai|ja|例大祭|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Reitaisai|qq=|ja_comment=fixed the redirect|1=Reisai|2=ja|3=例祭|4=fr|5=Reisai|lt=Reitaisai}} (September 15) == Grounds == ; '''Great Ginkgo Tree''' : A 950-year-old tree said to have been planted by Minamoto no Yoshiie to pray for victory. ; '''Miyagane (Shrine Bell)''' : The shrine bell, originally installed in {{ill|Enpō 8 (1680)|ja|延宝8th year （1680th year ）|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Enpō 8 (1680)|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, was reconstructed after the war in 1965 as a "peace bell." === Sub-Shrines === * '''Shōnan Awashima Shrine''' — Dedicated to {{ill|Sukunahikona|ja|少彦名命|1=Sukunabikona|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sukunahikona|13=User:Immanuelle/Sukunabikona|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スクナビコナ}} and {{ill|Awashima-no-Kami|ja|淡島神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Awashima-no-Kami|qq=}}. * '''Tsurumine Inari Shrine''' — Dedicated to {{ill|Ukanomitama|ja|宇迦之御魂|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ukanomitama|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ウカノミタマ}}. == Access == Take the JR Tōkaidō Main Line to [[:en:Chigasaki Station|Chigasaki Station]]. From the north exit, take a bus and alight at "Tsurumine Elementary School," then walk 1 minute. == References == {{Unreferenced help}} {{Reflist}} == See Also == * [[:en:Minamoto no Yoriyoshi|Minamoto no Yoriyoshi]] * [[:en:Iwashimizu Hachimangū|Iwashimizu Hachimangū]] * {{ill|Hamaori Festival|ja|浜降祭|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hamaori Festival|qq=}} {{translated page|ja|鶴嶺八幡宮|version=101890838|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 2]] }} [[ja:鶴嶺八幡宮]] [[Category:Hachiman shrines]] [[Category:Minamoto no Yoriyoshi]] [[Category:Son-sha in Kanagawa Prefecture]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Kanagawa Prefecture]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kanagawa Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings in Chigasaki, Kanagawa]] [[Category:Municipal History of Chigasaki]] [[Category:Sugawara no Michizane]] {{wikidata link|Q199699}}

Subsequently, Tsuruoka Hachimangū gained prominence, receiving land and monetary support from [[:en:Minamoto no Yoritomo|Minamoto no Yoritomo]] and others. Over time, it became the center for the famous {{ill|Hamaori Festival|ja|浜降祭|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hamaori Festival|qq=}} and absorbed nearby shrines such as Sazuka Daimyōjin. == Deities == * [[:en:Emperor Ōjin|Emperor Ōjin]] * [[:en:Emperor Nintoku|Emperor Nintoku]] * Sazuka Daimyōjin (associated with [[:en:Ōyamazumi Shrine|Ōyamazumi Shrine]]) * {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|ja|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugawara no Michizane|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q382005}} (from Tsurumine Tenmangū) == Annual Events == * {{ill|Hamaori Festival|ja|浜降祭|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hamaori Festival|qq=}} (Marine Day) * {{ill|Misorogi Ritual|ja|禊の神事|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Misorogi Ritual|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (June 29, formerly held on July 15) * {{ill|Reisai|lt=Reisai|lt=Reitaisai|ja|例大祭|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Reitaisai|qq=|ja_comment=fixed the redirect|1=Reisai|2=ja|3=例祭|4=fr|5=Reisai|lt=Reitaisai}} (September 15) == Grounds == ; '''Great Ginkgo Tree''' : A 950-year-old tree said to have been planted by Minamoto no Yoshiie to pray for victory. ; '''Miyagane (Shrine Bell)''' : The shrine bell, originally installed in {{ill|Enpō 8 (1680)|ja|延宝8th year （1680th year ）|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Enpō 8 (1680)|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, was reconstructed after the war in 1965 as a "peace bell." === Sub-Shrines === * '''Shōnan Awashima Shrine''' — Dedicated to {{ill|Sukunahikona|ja|少彦名命|1=Sukunabikona|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sukunahikona|13=User:Immanuelle/Sukunabikona|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スクナビコナ}} and {{ill|Awashima-no-Kami|ja|淡島神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Awashima-no-Kami|qq=}}. * '''Tsurumine Inari Shrine''' — Dedicated to {{ill|Ukanomitama|ja|宇迦之御魂|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ukanomitama|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ウカノミタマ}}. == Access == Take the JR Tōkaidō Main Line to [[:en:Chigasaki Station|Chigasaki Station]]. From the north exit, take a bus and alight at "Tsurumine Elementary School," then walk 1 minute. == References == {{Unreferenced help}} {{Reflist}} == See Also == * [[:en:Minamoto no Yoriyoshi|Minamoto no Yoriyoshi]] * [[:en:Iwashimizu Hachimangū|Iwashimizu Hachimangū]] * {{ill|Hamaori Festival|ja|浜降祭|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hamaori Festival|qq=}} {{translated page|ja|鶴嶺八幡宮|version=101890838|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 2]] }} [[ja:鶴嶺八幡宮]] [[Category:Hachiman shrines]] [[Category:Minamoto no Yoriyoshi]] [[Category:Son-sha in Kanagawa Prefecture]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Kanagawa Prefecture]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kanagawa Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings in Chigasaki, Kanagawa]] [[Category:Municipal History of Chigasaki]] [[Category:Sugawara no Michizane]] {{wikidata link|Q199699}}
