[[:en:Kanagawa Prefecture|Kanagawa Prefecture]][[:en:Chigasaki, Kanagawa|Chigasaki]] Hamano-go 462 | ISO=JP-14 | Latitude degrees = 35|Latitude minutes = 20|Latitude seconds = 18.38 | Longitude degrees=139|Longitude minutes=23|Longitude seconds=28.30 | deity={{ill|Ōjin|WD=Q317997}} | Shrine Rank=Former {{ill|Village shrine|ja|村社|1=Son-sha|WD=Q134917284}}{{efn|Former shrine rank|lt=Son-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Village shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}, Former {{ill|District shrine|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|WD=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/District shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} | Foundation={{ill|Chōgen 3|ja|長元3年|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chōgen 3|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} ([[:en:1030|1030]]) | Main Hall=Nagare-zukuri | Alternate name=Tsuruoka Hachiman Shrine | festival=[[:en:September 15|September 15]] | Rituals={{ill|Hamaori Festival|ja|浜降祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hamaori Festival|qq=}} ([[:en:Marine Day|Marine Day]]) | Map=Japan Kanagawa | prefecture={{ill|Chigasaki|WD=Q633911}} | coordinates={{Coord|35.33843888888889|N|139.39119444444444|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | architecture_style={{ill|nagare-zukuri|WD=Q210684}} }} '''Tsuruoka Hachimangū''' (鶴嶺八幡宮) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|shrine]] located in [[:en:Chigasaki, Kanagawa|Chigasaki]], [[:en:Kanagawa Prefecture|Kanagawa Prefecture]]. It is also known as Tsuruoka Hachiman Shrine. Renowned as the center of Hachiman worship in [[:en:Sagami Province|Sagami Province]], the shrine has been revered since ancient times as a significant site of {{ill|Hachiman faith|ja|八幡信仰|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hachiman faith|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 八幡神|1=Hachiman|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q261637}}.Cited by the Kanagawa Prefecture Shrine Office According to the shrine, it was the first {{ill|clan shrine|ja|氏社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/clan shrine|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} established by the [[:en:Minamoto clan|Genji]] when they expanded into the Kanto region. == History == The shrine was founded in {{ill|Chōgen 3 (1030)|ja|長元3rd year （1030th year ）|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chōgen 3 (1030)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, during the {{ill|Subjugation of Shimōsa|ja|下総の乱|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Subjugation of Shimōsa|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} by [[:en:Minamoto no Yoriyoshi|Minamoto no Yoriyoshi]], who hailed from [[:en:Kawachi Province|Kawachi Province]] (modern-day [[:en:Habikino|Habikino]], [[:en:Osaka Prefecture|Osaka Prefecture]]). Yoriyoshi enshrined [[:en:Iwashimizu Hachimangū|Iwashimizu Hachimangū]] at the site under the name "Kaijima Hachimangū" (another theory suggests it was {{ill|Usa Hachimangū|ja|宇佐八幡宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Usa Hachimangū|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 宇佐神宮|1=Usa Jingū|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q715632}}). Later, the Genji moved it to its current location. In {{ill|Tenki 3 (1055)|ja|天喜3rd year （1055th year ）|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenki 3 (1055)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, during the [[:en:Former Nine Years' War|Earlier Nine Years' War]], Yoriyoshi prayed at the shrine and achieved victory. To honor this, he established the {{ill|Tsurugaoka Hachimangū|lt=Original Hachiman Shrine|ja|元八幡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsurugaoka Hachimangū|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 鶴岡八幡宮}} in [[:en:Yuigahama|Yuigahama]], Kamakura, in {{ill|Kōhei 6 (1063)|ja|康平6年（1063rd year ）|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōhei 6 (1063)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. In {{ill|Ōtoku 2 (1085)|ja|応徳2nd year （1085th year ）|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtoku 2 (1085)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, Minamoto no Yoshiie, Yoriyoshi's heir, dedicated the shrine in Hamano-go as "Tsuruoka Hachimangū." Subsequently, Tsuruoka Hachimangū gained prominence, receiving land and monetary support from [[:en:Minamoto no Yoritomo|Minamoto no Yoritomo]] and others. Over time, it became the center for the famous {{ill|Hamaori Festival|ja|浜降祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hamaori Festival|qq=}} and absorbed nearby shrines such as Sazuka Daimyōjin. == Deities == * [[:en:Emperor Ōjin|Emperor Ōjin]] * [[:en:Emperor Nintoku|Emperor Nintoku]] * Sazuka Daimyōjin (associated with [[:en:Ōyamazumi Shrine|Ōyamazumi Shrine]]) * {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|ja|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugawara no Michizane|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q382005}} (from Tsurumine Tenmangū) == Annual Events == * {{ill|Hamaori Festival|ja|浜降祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hamaori Festival|qq=}} (Marine Day) * {{ill|Misorogi Ritual|ja|禊の神事|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Misorogi Ritual|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (June 29, formerly held on July 15) * {{ill|Reisai|lt=Reisai|lt=Reitaisai|ja|例大祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Reitaisai|ja_comment=fixed the redirect|1=Reisai|2=ja|3=例祭|4=fr|5=Reisai|lt=Reitaisai}} (September 15) == Grounds == ; '''Great Ginkgo Tree''' : A 950-year-old tree said to have been planted by Minamoto no Yoshiie to pray for victory. ; '''Miyagane (Shrine Bell)''' : The shrine bell, originally installed in {{ill|Enpō 8 (1680)|ja|延宝8th year （1680th year ）|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Enpō 8 (1680)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, was reconstructed after the war in 1965 as a "peace bell." === Sub-Shrines === * '''Shōnan Awashima Shrine''' — Dedicated to {{ill|Sukunahikona|ja|少彦名命|1=Sukunabikona|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sukunahikona|13=User:Immanuelle/Sukunabikona|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スクナビコナ}} and {{ill|Awashima-no-Kami|ja|淡島神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Awashima-no-Kami|qq=}}. * '''Tsurumine Inari Shrine''' — Dedicated to {{ill|Ukanomitama|ja|宇迦之御魂|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ukanomitama|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ウカノミタマ}}. == Access == Take the JR Tōkaidō Main Line to [[:en:Chigasaki Station|Chigasaki Station]]. From the north exit, take a bus and alight at "Tsurumine Elementary School," then walk 1 minute. == References == {{Unreferenced help}} {{Reflist}} == See Also == * [[:en:Minamoto no Yoriyoshi|Minamoto no Yoriyoshi]] * [[:en:Iwashimizu Hachimangū|Iwashimizu Hachimangū]] * {{ill|Hamaori Festival|ja|浜降祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hamaori Festival|qq=}} {{translated page|ja|鶴嶺八幡宮|version=101890838|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:鶴嶺八幡宮]] {{wikidata link|Q199699}} [[Category:Buildings in Chigasaki, Kanagawa]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Kanagawa Prefecture]] [[Category:Hachiman shrines]] [[Category:Has wikidata]] [[Category:Minamoto no Yoriyoshi]] [[Category:Municipal History of Chigasaki]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kanagawa Prefecture]] [[Category:Son-sha in Kanagawa Prefecture]] [[Category:Sugawara no Michizane]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]]

In {{ill|Ōtoku 2 (1085)|ja|応徳2nd year （1085th year ）|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtoku 2 (1085)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, Minamoto no Yoshiie, Yoriyoshi's heir, dedicated the shrine in Hamano-go as "Tsuruoka Hachimangū." Subsequently, Tsuruoka Hachimangū gained prominence, receiving land and monetary support from {{ill|Minamoto no Yoritomo|en|Minamoto no Yoritomo|WD=Q242800|lt=Minamoto no Yoritomo}} and others. Over time, it became the center for the famous {{ill|Hamaori Festival|ja|浜降祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hamaori Festival|qq=}} and absorbed nearby shrines such as Sazuka Daimyōjin. == Deities == * {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|en|Emperor Ōjin|WD=Q317997|lt=Emperor Ōjin}} * {{ill|Emperor Nintoku|en|Emperor Nintoku|WD=Q313119|lt=Emperor Nintoku}} * Sazuka Daimyōjin (associated with {{ill|Ōyamazumi Shrine|en|Ōyamazumi Shrine|WD=Q703904|lt=Ōyamazumi Shrine}}) * {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|ja|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugawara no Michizane|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q382005}} (from Tsurumine Tenmangū) == Annual Events == * {{ill|Hamaori Festival|ja|浜降祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hamaori Festival|qq=}} (Marine Day) * {{ill|Misorogi Ritual|ja|禊の神事|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Misorogi Ritual|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (June 29, formerly held on July 15) * {{ill|Reisai|lt=Reisai|lt=Reitaisai|ja|例大祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Reitaisai|ja_comment=fixed the redirect|1=Reisai|2=ja|3=例祭|4=fr|5=Reisai|lt=Reitaisai}} (September 15) == Grounds == ; '''Great Ginkgo Tree''' : A 950-year-old tree said to have been planted by Minamoto no Yoshiie to pray for victory. ; '''Miyagane (Shrine Bell)''' : The shrine bell, originally installed in {{ill|Enpō 8 (1680)|ja|延宝8th year （1680th year ）|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Enpō 8 (1680)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, was reconstructed after the war in 1965 as a "peace bell." === Sub-Shrines === * '''Shōnan Awashima Shrine''' — Dedicated to {{ill|Sukunahikona|ja|少彦名命|1=Sukunabikona|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sukunahikona|13=User:Immanuelle/Sukunabikona|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スクナビコナ}} and {{ill|Awashima-no-Kami|ja|淡島神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Awashima-no-Kami|qq=}}. * '''Tsurumine Inari Shrine''' — Dedicated to {{ill|Ukanomitama|ja|宇迦之御魂|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ukanomitama|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ウカノミタマ}}. == Access == Take the JR Tōkaidō Main Line to {{ill|Chigasaki Station|en|Chigasaki Station|WD=Q800622|lt=Chigasaki Station}}. From the north exit, take a bus and alight at "Tsurumine Elementary School," then walk 1 minute. == References == {{Unreferenced help}} {{Reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|Minamoto no Yoriyoshi|en|Minamoto no Yoriyoshi|WD=Q3082851|lt=Minamoto no Yoriyoshi}} * {{ill|Iwashimizu Hachimangū|en|Iwashimizu Hachimangū|WD=Q710098|lt=Iwashimizu Hachimangū}} * {{ill|Hamaori Festival|ja|浜降祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hamaori Festival|qq=}} {{translated page|ja|鶴嶺八幡宮|version=101890838|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:鶴嶺八幡宮]] {{wikidata link|Q199699}} [[Category:Buildings in Chigasaki, Kanagawa]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Kanagawa Prefecture]] [[Category:Hachiman shrines]] [[Category:Minamoto no Yoriyoshi]] [[Category:Municipal History of Chigasaki]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kanagawa Prefecture]] [[Category:Son-sha in Kanagawa Prefecture]] [[Category:Sugawara no Michizane]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]]

[[ja:鶴嶺八幡宮]] [[ja:鶴嶺八幡宮]] [[ja:鶴嶺八幡宮]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q199699]] Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:10, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Unreferenced|date=June 28, 2018}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name=Tsuruoka Hachimangū | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image=File:GrfKanagawakenChigasakishi-Turuminehachimangu-01.jpg | location=〒 253-0086
{{ill|Kanagawa Prefecture|en|Kanagawa Prefecture|WD=Q127513|lt=Kanagawa Prefecture}}[[:en:Chigasaki, Kanagawa|Chigasaki]] Hamano-go 462 | ISO=JP-14 | Latitude degrees = 35|Latitude minutes = 20|Latitude seconds = 18.38 | Longitude degrees=139|Longitude minutes=23|Longitude seconds=28.30 | deity={{ill|Ōjin|WD=Q317997}} | Shrine Rank=Former {{ill|Village shrine|ja|村社|1=Son-sha|WD=Q134917284}}{{efn|Former shrine rank|lt=Son-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Village shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}, Former {{ill|District shrine|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|WD=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/District shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} | Foundation={{ill|Chōgen 3|ja|長元3年|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chōgen 3|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} ({{ill|1030|en|1030|WD=Q19301|lt=1030}}) | Main Hall=Nagare-zukuri | Alternate name=Tsuruoka Hachiman Shrine | festival={{ill|September 15|en|September 15|WD=Q2846|lt=September 15}} | Rituals={{ill|Hamaori Festival|ja|浜降祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hamaori Festival|qq=}} ({{ill|Marine Day|en|Marine Day|WD=Q1355833|lt=Marine Day}}) | Map=Japan Kanagawa | prefecture={{ill|Chigasaki|WD=Q633911}} | coordinates={{Coord|35.33843888888889|N|139.39119444444444|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | architecture_style={{ill|nagare-zukuri|WD=Q210684}} }} '''Tsuruoka Hachimangū''' (鶴嶺八幡宮) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=shrine}} located in [[:en:Chigasaki, Kanagawa|Chigasaki]], {{ill|Kanagawa Prefecture|en|Kanagawa Prefecture|WD=Q127513|lt=Kanagawa Prefecture}}. It is also known as Tsuruoka Hachiman Shrine. Renowned as the center of Hachiman worship in {{ill|Sagami Province|en|Sagami Province|WD=Q1053732|lt=Sagami Province}}, the shrine has been revered since ancient times as a significant site of {{ill|Hachiman faith|ja|八幡信仰|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hachiman faith|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 八幡神|1=Hachiman|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q261637}}.Cited by the Kanagawa Prefecture Shrine Office According to the shrine, it was the first {{ill|clan shrine|ja|氏社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/clan shrine|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} established by the {{ill|Minamoto clan|en|Minamoto clan|WD=Q599881|lt=Genji}} when they expanded into the Kanto region. == History == The shrine was founded in {{ill|Chōgen 3 (1030)|ja|長元3rd year （1030th year ）|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chōgen 3 (1030)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, during the {{ill|Subjugation of Shimōsa|ja|下総の乱|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Subjugation of Shimōsa|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} by {{ill|Minamoto no Yoriyoshi|en|Minamoto no Yoriyoshi|WD=Q3082851|lt=Minamoto no Yoriyoshi}}, who hailed from {{ill|Kawachi Province|en|Kawachi Province|WD=Q1047150|lt=Kawachi Province}} (modern-day {{ill|Habikino|en|Habikino|WD=Q694308|lt=Habikino}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|en|Osaka Prefecture|WD=Q122723|lt=Osaka Prefecture}}). Yoriyoshi enshrined {{ill|Iwashimizu Hachimangū|en|Iwashimizu Hachimangū|WD=Q710098|lt=Iwashimizu Hachimangū}} at the site under the name "Kaijima Hachimangū" (another theory suggests it was {{ill|Usa Hachimangū|ja|宇佐八幡宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Usa Hachimangū|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 宇佐神宮|1=Usa Jingū|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q715632}}). Later, the Genji moved it to its current location. In {{ill|Tenki 3 (1055)|ja|天喜3rd year （1055th year ）|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenki 3 (1055)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, during the {{ill|Former Nine Years' War|en|Former Nine Years' War|WD=Q1187448|lt=Earlier Nine Years' War}}, Yoriyoshi prayed at the shrine and achieved victory. To honor this, he established the {{ill|Tsurugaoka Hachimangū|lt=Original Hachiman Shrine|ja|元八幡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsurugaoka Hachimangū|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 鶴岡八幡宮}} in {{ill|Yuigahama|en|Yuigahama|WD=Q8060490|lt=Yuigahama}}, Kamakura, in {{ill|Kōhei 6 (1063)|ja|康平6年（1063rd year ）|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōhei 6 (1063)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. In {{ill|Ōtoku 2 (1085)|ja|応徳2nd year （1085th year ）|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtoku 2 (1085)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, Minamoto no Yoshiie, Yoriyoshi's heir, dedicated the shrine in Hamano-go as "Tsuruoka Hachimangū." Subsequently, Tsuruoka Hachimangū gained prominence, receiving land and monetary support from {{ill|Minamoto no Yoritomo|en|Minamoto no Yoritomo|WD=Q242800|lt=Minamoto no Yoritomo}} and others. Over time, it became the center for the famous {{ill|Hamaori Festival|ja|浜降祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hamaori Festival|qq=}} and absorbed nearby shrines such as Sazuka Daimyōjin. == Deities == * {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|en|Emperor Ōjin|WD=Q317997|lt=Emperor Ōjin}} * {{ill|Emperor Nintoku|en|Emperor Nintoku|WD=Q313119|lt=Emperor Nintoku}} * Sazuka Daimyōjin (associated with {{ill|Ōyamazumi Shrine|en|Ōyamazumi Shrine|WD=Q703904|lt=Ōyamazumi Shrine}}) * {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|ja|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugawara no Michizane|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q382005}} (from Tsurumine Tenmangū) == Annual Events == * {{ill|Hamaori Festival|ja|浜降祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hamaori Festival|qq=}} (Marine Day) * {{ill|Misorogi Ritual|ja|禊の神事|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Misorogi Ritual|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (June 29, formerly held on July 15) * {{ill|Reisai|lt=Reisai|lt=Reitaisai|ja|例大祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Reitaisai|ja_comment=fixed the redirect|1=Reisai|2=ja|3=例祭|4=fr|5=Reisai|lt=Reitaisai}} (September 15) == Grounds == ; '''Great Ginkgo Tree''' : A 950-year-old tree said to have been planted by Minamoto no Yoshiie to pray for victory. ; '''Miyagane (Shrine Bell)''' : The shrine bell, originally installed in {{ill|Enpō 8 (1680)|ja|延宝8th year （1680th year ）|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Enpō 8 (1680)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, was reconstructed after the war in 1965 as a "peace bell." === Sub-Shrines === * '''Shōnan Awashima Shrine''' — Dedicated to {{ill|Sukunahikona|ja|少彦名命|1=Sukunabikona|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sukunahikona|13=User:Immanuelle/Sukunabikona|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スクナビコナ}} and {{ill|Awashima-no-Kami|ja|淡島神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Awashima-no-Kami|qq=}}. * '''Tsurumine Inari Shrine''' — Dedicated to {{ill|Ukanomitama|ja|宇迦之御魂|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ukanomitama|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ウカノミタマ}}. == Access == Take the JR Tōkaidō Main Line to {{ill|Chigasaki Station|en|Chigasaki Station|WD=Q800622|lt=Chigasaki Station}}. From the north exit, take a bus and alight at "Tsurumine Elementary School," then walk 1 minute. == References == {{Unreferenced help}} {{Reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|Minamoto no Yoriyoshi|en|Minamoto no Yoriyoshi|WD=Q3082851|lt=Minamoto no Yoriyoshi}} * {{ill|Iwashimizu Hachimangū|en|Iwashimizu Hachimangū|WD=Q710098|lt=Iwashimizu Hachimangū}} * {{ill|Hamaori Festival|ja|浜降祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hamaori Festival|qq=}} {{translated page|ja|鶴嶺八幡宮|version=101890838|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:鶴嶺八幡宮]] {{wikidata link|Q199699}} [[Category:Buildings in Chigasaki, Kanagawa]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Kanagawa Prefecture]] [[Category:Hachiman shrines]] [[Category:Minamoto no Yoriyoshi]] [[Category:Municipal History of Chigasaki]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kanagawa Prefecture]] [[Category:Son-sha in Kanagawa Prefecture]] [[Category:Sugawara no Michizane]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]]

Over time, it became the center for the famous {{ill|Hamaori Festival|ja|浜降祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hamaori Festival|qq=}} and absorbed nearby shrines such as Sazuka Daimyōjin. == Deities == * {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|en|Emperor Ōjin|WD=Q317997|lt=Emperor Ōjin}} * {{ill|Emperor Nintoku|en|Emperor Nintoku|WD=Q313119|lt=Emperor Nintoku}} * Sazuka Daimyōjin (associated with {{ill|Ōyamazumi Shrine|en|Ōyamazumi Shrine|WD=Q703904|lt=Ōyamazumi Shrine}}) * {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|ja|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugawara no Michizane|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q382005}} (from Tsurumine Tenmangū) == Annual Events == * {{ill|Hamaori Festival|ja|浜降祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hamaori Festival|qq=}} (Marine Day) * {{ill|Misorogi Ritual|ja|禊の神事|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Misorogi Ritual|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (June 29, formerly held on July 15) * {{ill|Reisai|lt=Reisai|lt=Reitaisai|ja|例大祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Reitaisai|ja_comment=fixed the redirect|1=Reisai|2=ja|3=例祭|4=fr|5=Reisai|lt=Reitaisai}} (September 15) == Grounds == ; revision.text:
[[ja:鶴嶺八幡宮]] [[ja:鶴嶺八幡宮]] [[ja:鶴嶺八幡宮]] {{Unreferenced|date=June 28, 2018}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name=Tsuruoka Hachimangū | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image=File:GrfKanagawakenChigasakishi-Turuminehachimangu-01.jpg | location=〒 253-0086
{{ill|Kanagawa Prefecture|en|Kanagawa Prefecture|WD=Q127513|lt=Kanagawa Prefecture}}[[:en:Chigasaki, Kanagawa|Chigasaki]] Hamano-go 462 | ISO=JP-14 | Latitude degrees = 35|Latitude minutes = 20|Latitude seconds = 18.38 | Longitude degrees=139|Longitude minutes=23|Longitude seconds=28.30 | deity={{ill|Ōjin|WD=Q317997}} | Shrine Rank=Former {{ill|Village shrine|ja|村社|1=Son-sha|WD=Q134917284}}{{efn|Former shrine rank|lt=Son-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Village shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}, Former {{ill|District shrine|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|WD=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/District shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} | Foundation={{ill|Chōgen 3|ja|長元3年|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chōgen 3|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} ({{ill|1030|en|1030|WD=Q19301|lt=1030}}) | Main Hall=Nagare-zukuri | Alternate name=Tsuruoka Hachiman Shrine | festival={{ill|September 15|en|September 15|WD=Q2846|lt=September 15}} | Rituals={{ill|Hamaori Festival|ja|浜降祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hamaori Festival|qq=}} ({{ill|Marine Day|en|Marine Day|WD=Q1355833|lt=Marine Day}}) | Map=Japan Kanagawa | prefecture={{ill|Chigasaki|WD=Q633911}} | coordinates={{Coord|35.33843888888889|N|139.39119444444444|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | architecture_style={{ill|nagare-zukuri|WD=Q210684}} }} '''Tsuruoka Hachimangū''' (鶴嶺八幡宮) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=shrine}} located in [[:en:Chigasaki, Kanagawa|Chigasaki]], {{ill|Kanagawa Prefecture|en|Kanagawa Prefecture|WD=Q127513|lt=Kanagawa Prefecture}}. It is also known as Tsuruoka Hachiman Shrine. Renowned as the center of Hachiman worship in {{ill|Sagami Province|en|Sagami Province|WD=Q1053732|lt=Sagami Province}}, the shrine has been revered since ancient times as a significant site of {{ill|Hachiman faith|ja|八幡信仰|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hachiman faith|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 八幡神|1=Hachiman|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q261637}}.Cited by the Kanagawa Prefecture Shrine Office According to the shrine, it was the first {{ill|clan shrine|ja|氏社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/clan shrine|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} established by the {{ill|Minamoto clan|en|Minamoto clan|WD=Q599881|lt=Genji}} when they expanded into the Kanto region. == History == The shrine was founded in {{ill|Chōgen 3 (1030)|ja|長元3rd year （1030th year ）|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chōgen 3 (1030)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, during the {{ill|Subjugation of Shimōsa|ja|下総の乱|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Subjugation of Shimōsa|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} by {{ill|Minamoto no Yoriyoshi|en|Minamoto no Yoriyoshi|WD=Q3082851|lt=Minamoto no Yoriyoshi}}, who hailed from {{ill|Kawachi Province|en|Kawachi Province|WD=Q1047150|lt=Kawachi Province}} (modern-day {{ill|Habikino|en|Habikino|WD=Q694308|lt=Habikino}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|en|Osaka Prefecture|WD=Q122723|lt=Osaka Prefecture}}). Yoriyoshi enshrined {{ill|Iwashimizu Hachimangū|en|Iwashimizu Hachimangū|WD=Q710098|lt=Iwashimizu Hachimangū}} at the site under the name "Kaijima Hachimangū" (another theory suggests it was {{ill|Usa Hachimangū|ja|宇佐八幡宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Usa Hachimangū|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 宇佐神宮|1=Usa Jingū|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q715632}}). Later, the Genji moved it to its current location. In {{ill|Tenki 3 (1055)|ja|天喜3rd year （1055th year ）|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenki 3 (1055)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, during the {{ill|Former Nine Years' War|en|Former Nine Years' War|WD=Q1187448|lt=Earlier Nine Years' War}}, Yoriyoshi prayed at the shrine and achieved victory. To honor this, he established the {{ill|Tsurugaoka Hachimangū|lt=Original Hachiman Shrine|ja|元八幡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsurugaoka Hachimangū|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 鶴岡八幡宮}} in {{ill|Yuigahama|en|Yuigahama|WD=Q8060490|lt=Yuigahama}}, Kamakura, in {{ill|Kōhei 6 (1063)|ja|康平6年（1063rd year ）|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōhei 6 (1063)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. In {{ill|Ōtoku 2 (1085)|ja|応徳2nd year （1085th year ）|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtoku 2 (1085)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, Minamoto no Yoshiie, Yoriyoshi's heir, dedicated the shrine in Hamano-go as "Tsuruoka Hachimangū." Subsequently, Tsuruoka Hachimangū gained prominence, receiving land and monetary support from {{ill|Minamoto no Yoritomo|en|Minamoto no Yoritomo|WD=Q242800|lt=Minamoto no Yoritomo}} and others. Over time, it became the center for the famous {{ill|Hamaori Festival|ja|浜降祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hamaori Festival|qq=}} and absorbed nearby shrines such as Sazuka Daimyōjin. == Deities == * {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|en|Emperor Ōjin|WD=Q317997|lt=Emperor Ōjin}} * {{ill|Emperor Nintoku|en|Emperor Nintoku|WD=Q313119|lt=Emperor Nintoku}} * Sazuka Daimyōjin (associated with {{ill|Ōyamazumi Shrine|en|Ōyamazumi Shrine|WD=Q703904|lt=Ōyamazumi Shrine}}) * {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|ja|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugawara no Michizane|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q382005}} (from Tsurumine Tenmangū) == Annual Events == * {{ill|Hamaori Festival|ja|浜降祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hamaori Festival|qq=}} (Marine Day) * {{ill|Misorogi Ritual|ja|禊の神事|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Misorogi Ritual|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (June 29, formerly held on July 15) * {{ill|Reisai|lt=Reisai|lt=Reitaisai|ja|例大祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Reitaisai|ja_comment=fixed the redirect|1=Reisai|2=ja|3=例祭|4=fr|5=Reisai|lt=Reitaisai}} (September 15) == Grounds == ; '''Great Ginkgo Tree''' : A 950-year-old tree said to have been planted by Minamoto no Yoshiie to pray for victory. ; '''Miyagane (Shrine Bell)''' : The shrine bell, originally installed in {{ill|Enpō 8 (1680)|ja|延宝8th year （1680th year ）|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Enpō 8 (1680)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, was reconstructed after the war in 1965 as a "peace bell." === Sub-Shrines === * '''Shōnan Awashima Shrine''' — Dedicated to {{ill|Sukunahikona|ja|少彦名命|1=Sukunabikona|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sukunahikona|13=User:Immanuelle/Sukunabikona|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スクナビコナ}} and {{ill|Awashima-no-Kami|ja|淡島神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Awashima-no-Kami|qq=}}. * '''Tsurumine Inari Shrine''' — Dedicated to {{ill|Ukanomitama|ja|宇迦之御魂|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ukanomitama|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ウカノミタマ}}. == Access == Take the JR Tōkaidō Main Line to {{ill|Chigasaki Station|en|Chigasaki Station|WD=Q800622|lt=Chigasaki Station}}. From the north exit, take a bus and alight at "Tsurumine Elementary School," then walk 1 minute. == References == {{Unreferenced help}} {{Reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|Minamoto no Yoriyoshi|en|Minamoto no Yoriyoshi|WD=Q3082851|lt=Minamoto no Yoriyoshi}} * {{ill|Iwashimizu Hachimangū|en|Iwashimizu Hachimangū|WD=Q710098|lt=Iwashimizu Hachimangū}} * {{ill|Hamaori Festival|ja|浜降祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hamaori Festival|qq=}} {{translated page|ja|鶴嶺八幡宮|version=101890838|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:鶴嶺八幡宮]] {{wikidata link|Q199699}} [[Category:Buildings in Chigasaki, Kanagawa]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Kanagawa Prefecture]] [[Category:Hachiman shrines]] [[Category:Minamoto no Yoriyoshi]] [[Category:Municipal History of Chigasaki]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kanagawa Prefecture]] [[Category:Son-sha in Kanagawa Prefecture]] [[Category:Sugawara no Michizane]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]]

Later, the Genji moved it to its current location. In {{ill|Tenki 3 (1055)|ja|天喜3rd year （1055th year ）|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenki 3 (1055)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, during the {{ill|Former Nine Years' War|en|Former Nine Years' War|WD=Q1187448|lt=Earlier Nine Years' War}}, Yoriyoshi prayed at the shrine and achieved victory. To honor this, he established the {{ill|Tsurugaoka Hachimangū|lt=Original Hachiman Shrine|ja|元八幡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsurugaoka Hachimangū|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 鶴岡八幡宮}} in {{ill|Yuigahama|en|Yuigahama|WD=Q8060490|lt=Yuigahama}}, Kamakura, in {{ill|Kōhei 6 (1063)|ja|康平6年（1063rd year ）|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōhei 6 (1063)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. In {{ill|Ōtoku 2 (1085)|ja|応徳2nd year （1085th year ）|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtoku 2 (1085)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, Minamoto no Yoshiie, Yoriyoshi's heir, dedicated the shrine in Hamano-go as "Tsuruoka Hachimangū." Subsequently, Tsuruoka Hachimangū gained prominence, receiving land and monetary support from {{ill|Minamoto no Yoritomo|en|Minamoto no Yoritomo|WD=Q242800|lt=Minamoto no Yoritomo}} and others. Over time, it became the center for the famous {{ill|Hamaori Festival|ja|浜降祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hamaori Festival|qq=}} and absorbed nearby shrines such as Sazuka Daimyōjin. == Deities == * {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|en|Emperor Ōjin|WD=Q317997|lt=Emperor Ōjin}} * {{ill|Emperor Nintoku|en|Emperor Nintoku|WD=Q313119|lt=Emperor Nintoku}} * Sazuka Daimyōjin (associated with {{ill|Ōyamazumi Shrine|en|Ōyamazumi Shrine|WD=Q703904|lt=Ōyamazumi Shrine}}) * {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|ja|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugawara no Michizane|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q382005}} (from Tsurumine Tenmangū) == Annual Events == * {{ill|Hamaori Festival|ja|浜降祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hamaori Festival|qq=}} (Marine Day) * {{ill|Misorogi Ritual|ja|禊の神事|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Misorogi Ritual|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (June 29, formerly held on July 15) * {{ill|Reisai|lt=Reisai|lt=Reitaisai|ja|例大祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Reitaisai|ja_comment=fixed the redirect|1=Reisai|2=ja|3=例祭|4=fr|5=Reisai|lt=Reitaisai}} (September 15) == Grounds == ; '''Great Ginkgo Tree''' : A 950-year-old tree said to have been planted by Minamoto no Yoshiie to pray for victory. ; '''Miyagane (Shrine Bell)''' : The shrine bell, originally installed in {{ill|Enpō 8 (1680)|ja|延宝8th year （1680th year ）|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Enpō 8 (1680)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, was reconstructed after the war in 1965 as a "peace bell." === Sub-Shrines === * '''Shōnan Awashima Shrine''' — Dedicated to {{ill|Sukunahikona|ja|少彦名命|1=Sukunabikona|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sukunahikona|13=User:Immanuelle/Sukunabikona|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スクナビコナ}} and {{ill|Awashima-no-Kami|ja|淡島神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Awashima-no-Kami|qq=}}. * '''Tsurumine Inari Shrine''' — Dedicated to {{ill|Ukanomitama|ja|宇迦之御魂|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ukanomitama|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ウカノミタマ}}. == Access == Take the JR Tōkaidō Main Line to {{ill|Chigasaki Station|en|Chigasaki Station|WD=Q800622|lt=Chigasaki Station}}. From the north exit, take a bus and alight at "Tsurumine Elementary School," then walk 1 minute. == References == {{Unreferenced help}} {{Reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|Minamoto no Yoriyoshi|en|Minamoto no Yoriyoshi|WD=Q3082851|lt=Minamoto no Yoriyoshi}} * {{ill|Iwashimizu Hachimangū|en|Iwashimizu Hachimangū|WD=Q710098|lt=Iwashimizu Hachimangū}} * {{ill|Hamaori Festival|ja|浜降祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hamaori Festival|qq=}} {{translated page|ja|鶴嶺八幡宮|version=101890838|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:鶴嶺八幡宮]] {{wikidata link|Q199699}} [[Category:Buildings in Chigasaki, Kanagawa]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Kanagawa Prefecture]] [[Category:Hachiman shrines]] [[Category:Minamoto no Yoriyoshi]] [[Category:Municipal History of Chigasaki]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kanagawa Prefecture]] [[Category:Son-sha in Kanagawa Prefecture]] [[Category:Sugawara no Michizane]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:pages]]

[[ja:鶴嶺八幡宮]] [[ja:鶴嶺八幡宮]] [[ja:鶴嶺八幡宮]] {{Unreferenced|date=June 28, 2018}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name=Tsuruoka Hachimangū | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image=File:GrfKanagawakenChigasakishi-Turuminehachimangu-01.jpg | location=〒 253-0086
{{ill|Kanagawa Prefecture|en|Kanagawa Prefecture|WD=Q127513|lt=Kanagawa Prefecture|1=Kanagawa Prefecture}}[[:en:Chigasaki, Kanagawa|Chigasaki]] Hamano-go 462 | ISO=JP-14 | Latitude degrees = 35|Latitude minutes = 20|Latitude seconds = 18.38 | Longitude degrees=139|Longitude minutes=23|Longitude seconds=28.30 | deity={{ill|Ōjin|WD=Q317997|1=Emperor Ōjin}} | Shrine Rank=Former {{ill|Village shrine|ja|村社|1=Son-sha|WD=Q134917284}}{{efn|Former shrine rank|lt=Son-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Village shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}, Former {{ill|District shrine|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|WD=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|WD=Q90139226|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/District shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} | Foundation={{ill|Chōgen 3|ja|長元3年|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chōgen 3|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} ({{ill|1030|en|1030|WD=Q19301|lt=1030|1=1030}}) | Main Hall=Nagare-zukuri | Alternate name=Tsuruoka Hachiman Shrine | festival={{ill|September 15|en|September 15|WD=Q2846|lt=September 15|1=September 15}} | Rituals={{ill|Hamaori Festival|ja|浜降祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hamaori Festival|qq=}} ({{ill|Marine Day|en|Marine Day|WD=Q1355833|lt=Marine Day|1=Marine Day}}) | Map=Japan Kanagawa | prefecture={{ill|Chigasaki|WD=Q633911|1=Chigasaki}} | coordinates={{Coord|35.33843888888889|N|139.39119444444444|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | architecture_style={{ill|nagare-zukuri|WD=Q210684|1=Nagare-zukuri}} }} '''Tsuruoka Hachimangū''' (鶴嶺八幡宮) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=shrine|1=Shinto shrine}} located in [[:en:Chigasaki, Kanagawa|Chigasaki]], {{ill|Kanagawa Prefecture|en|Kanagawa Prefecture|WD=Q127513|lt=Kanagawa Prefecture|1=Kanagawa Prefecture}}. It is also known as Tsuruoka Hachiman Shrine. Renowned as the center of Hachiman worship in {{ill|Sagami Province|en|Sagami Province|WD=Q1053732|lt=Sagami Province|1=Sagami Province}}, the shrine has been revered since ancient times as a significant site of {{ill|Hachiman faith|ja|八幡信仰|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hachiman faith|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 八幡神|1=Hachiman|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q261637}}.Cited by the Kanagawa Prefecture Shrine Office According to the shrine, it was the first {{ill|clan shrine|ja|氏社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/clan shrine|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} established by the {{ill|Minamoto clan|en|Minamoto clan|WD=Q599881|lt=Genji|1=Minamoto clan}} when they expanded into the Kanto region. == History == The shrine was founded in {{ill|Chōgen 3 (1030)|ja|長元3rd year （1030th year ）|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chōgen 3 (1030)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, during the {{ill|Subjugation of Shimōsa|ja|下総の乱|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Subjugation of Shimōsa|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} by {{ill|Minamoto no Yoriyoshi|en|Minamoto no Yoriyoshi|WD=Q3082851|lt=Minamoto no Yoriyoshi|1=Minamoto no Yoriyoshi}}, who hailed from {{ill|Kawachi Province|en|Kawachi Province|WD=Q1047150|lt=Kawachi Province|1=Kawachi Province}} (modern-day {{ill|Habikino|en|Habikino|WD=Q694308|lt=Habikino|1=Habikino}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|en|Osaka Prefecture|WD=Q122723|lt=Osaka Prefecture|1=Osaka Prefecture}}). Yoriyoshi enshrined {{ill|Iwashimizu Hachimangū|en|Iwashimizu Hachimangū|WD=Q710098|lt=Iwashimizu Hachimangū|1=Iwashimizu Hachimangū}} at the site under the name "Kaijima Hachimangū" (another theory suggests it was {{ill|Usa Hachimangū|ja|宇佐八幡宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Usa Hachimangū|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 宇佐神宮|1=Usa Jingū|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q715632}}). Later, the Genji moved it to its current location. In {{ill|Tenki 3 (1055)|ja|天喜3rd year （1055th year ）|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenki 3 (1055)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, during the {{ill|Former Nine Years' War|en|Former Nine Years' War|WD=Q1187448|lt=Earlier Nine Years' War|1=Former Nine Years' War}}, Yoriyoshi prayed at the shrine and achieved victory. To honor this, he established the {{ill|Tsurugaoka Hachimangū|lt=Original Hachiman Shrine|ja|元八幡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsurugaoka Hachimangū|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 鶴岡八幡宮}} in {{ill|Yuigahama|en|Yuigahama|WD=Q8060490|lt=Yuigahama|1=Yuigahama}}, Kamakura, in {{ill|Kōhei 6 (1063)|ja|康平6年（1063rd year ）|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōhei 6 (1063)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. In {{ill|Ōtoku 2 (1085)|ja|応徳2nd year （1085th year ）|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtoku 2 (1085)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, Minamoto no Yoshiie, Yoriyoshi's heir, dedicated the shrine in Hamano-go as "Tsuruoka Hachimangū." Subsequently, Tsuruoka Hachimangū gained prominence, receiving land and monetary support from {{ill|Minamoto no Yoritomo|en|Minamoto no Yoritomo|WD=Q242800|lt=Minamoto no Yoritomo|1=Minamoto no Yoritomo}} and others. Over time, it became the center for the famous {{ill|Hamaori Festival|ja|浜降祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hamaori Festival|qq=}} and absorbed nearby shrines such as Sazuka Daimyōjin. == Deities == * {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|en|Emperor Ōjin|WD=Q317997|lt=Emperor Ōjin|1=Emperor Ōjin}} * {{ill|Emperor Nintoku|en|Emperor Nintoku|WD=Q313119|lt=Emperor Nintoku|1=Emperor Nintoku}} * Sazuka Daimyōjin (associated with {{ill|Ōyamazumi Shrine|en|Ōyamazumi Shrine|WD=Q703904|lt=Ōyamazumi Shrine|1=Ōyamazumi Shrine}}) * {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|ja|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugawara no Michizane|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q382005}} (from Tsurumine Tenmangū) == Annual Events == * {{ill|Hamaori Festival|ja|浜降祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hamaori Festival|qq=}} (Marine Day) * {{ill|Misorogi Ritual|ja|禊の神事|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Misorogi Ritual|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (June 29, formerly held on July 15) * {{ill|Reisai|lt=Reisai|lt=Reitaisai|ja|例大祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Reitaisai|ja_comment=fixed the redirect|1=Reisai|2=ja|3=例祭|4=fr|5=Reisai|lt=Reitaisai}} (September 15) == Grounds == ; '''Great Ginkgo Tree''' : A 950-year-old tree said to have been planted by Minamoto no Yoshiie to pray for victory. ; '''Miyagane (Shrine Bell)''' : The shrine bell, originally installed in {{ill|Enpō 8 (1680)|ja|延宝8th year （1680th year ）|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Enpō 8 (1680)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, was reconstructed after the war in 1965 as a "peace bell." === Sub-Shrines === * '''Shōnan Awashima Shrine''' — Dedicated to {{ill|Sukunahikona|ja|少彦名命|1=Sukunabikona|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sukunahikona|13=User:Immanuelle/Sukunabikona|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スクナビコナ}} and {{ill|Awashima-no-Kami|ja|淡島神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Awashima-no-Kami|qq=}}. * '''Tsurumine Inari Shrine''' — Dedicated to {{ill|Ukanomitama|ja|宇迦之御魂|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ukanomitama|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ウカノミタマ}}. == Access == Take the JR Tōkaidō Main Line to {{ill|Chigasaki Station|en|Chigasaki Station|WD=Q800622|lt=Chigasaki Station|1=Chigasaki Station}}. From the north exit, take a bus and alight at "Tsurumine Elementary School," then walk 1 minute. == References == {{Unreferenced help}} {{Reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|Minamoto no Yoriyoshi|en|Minamoto no Yoriyoshi|WD=Q3082851|lt=Minamoto no Yoriyoshi|1=Minamoto no Yoriyoshi}} * {{ill|Iwashimizu Hachimangū|en|Iwashimizu Hachimangū|WD=Q710098|lt=Iwashimizu Hachimangū|1=Iwashimizu Hachimangū}} * {{ill|Hamaori Festival|ja|浜降祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hamaori Festival|qq=}} {{translated page|ja|鶴嶺八幡宮|version=101890838|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:鶴嶺八幡宮]] {{wikidata link|Q199699}} [[Category:Buildings in Chigasaki, Kanagawa]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Kanagawa Prefecture]] [[Category:Hachiman shrines]] [[Category:Minamoto no Yoriyoshi]] [[Category:Municipal History of Chigasaki]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kanagawa Prefecture]] [[Category:Son-sha in Kanagawa Prefecture]] [[Category:Sugawara no Michizane]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:pages]]

Yoriyoshi enshrined {{ill|Iwashimizu Hachimangū|en|Iwashimizu Hachimangū|WD=Q710098|lt=Iwashimizu Hachimangū|1=Iwashimizu Hachimangū}} at the site under the name "Kaijima Hachimangū" (another theory suggests it was {{ill|Usa Hachimangū|ja|宇佐八幡宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Usa Hachimangū|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 宇佐神宮|1=Usa Jingū|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q715632}}). Later, the Genji moved it to its current location. In {{ill|Tenki 3 (1055)|ja|天喜3rd year （1055th year ）|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenki 3 (1055)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, during the {{ill|Former Nine Years' War|en|Former Nine Years' War|WD=Q1187448|lt=Earlier Nine Years' War|1=Former Nine Years' War}}, Yoriyoshi prayed at the shrine and achieved victory. To honor this, he established the {{ill|Tsurugaoka Hachimangū|lt=Original Hachiman Shrine|ja|元八幡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsurugaoka Hachimangū|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 鶴岡八幡宮}} in {{ill|Yuigahama|en|Yuigahama|WD=Q8060490|lt=Yuigahama|1=Yuigahama}}, Kamakura, in {{ill|Kōhei 6 (1063)|ja|康平6年（1063rd year ）|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōhei 6 (1063)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. In {{ill|Ōtoku 2 (1085)|ja|応徳2nd year （1085th year ）|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtoku 2 (1085)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, Minamoto no Yoshiie, Yoriyoshi's heir, dedicated the shrine in Hamano-go as "Tsuruoka Hachimangū." Subsequently, Tsuruoka Hachimangū gained prominence, receiving land and monetary support from {{ill|Minamoto no Yoritomo|en|Minamoto no Yoritomo|WD=Q242800|lt=Minamoto no Yoritomo|1=Minamoto no Yoritomo}} and others. Over time, it became the center for the famous {{ill|Hamaori Festival|ja|浜降祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hamaori Festival|qq=}} and absorbed nearby shrines such as Sazuka Daimyōjin. == Deities == * {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|en|Emperor Ōjin|WD=Q317997|lt=Emperor Ōjin|1=Emperor Ōjin}} * {{ill|Emperor Nintoku|en|Emperor Nintoku|WD=Q313119|lt=Emperor Nintoku|1=Emperor Nintoku}} * Sazuka Daimyōjin (associated with {{ill|Ōyamazumi Shrine|en|Ōyamazumi Shrine|WD=Q703904|lt=Ōyamazumi Shrine|1=Ōyamazumi Shrine}}) * {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|ja|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugawara no Michizane|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q382005}} (from Tsurumine Tenmangū) == Annual Events == * {{ill|Hamaori Festival|ja|浜降祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hamaori Festival|qq=}} (Marine Day) * {{ill|Misorogi Ritual|ja|禊の神事|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Misorogi Ritual|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (June 29, formerly held on July 15) * {{ill|Reisai|lt=Reisai|lt=Reitaisai|ja|例大祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Reitaisai|ja_comment=fixed the redirect|1=Reisai|2=ja|3=例祭|4=fr|5=Reisai|lt=Reitaisai}} (September 15) == Grounds == ; '''Great Ginkgo Tree''' : A 950-year-old tree said to have been planted by Minamoto no Yoshiie to pray for victory. ; '''Miyagane (Shrine Bell)''' : The shrine bell, originally installed in {{ill|Enpō 8 (1680)|ja|延宝8th year （1680th year ）|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Enpō 8 (1680)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, was reconstructed after the war in 1965 as a "peace bell." === Sub-Shrines === * '''Shōnan Awashima Shrine''' — Dedicated to {{ill|Sukunahikona|ja|少彦名命|1=Sukunabikona|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sukunahikona|13=User:Immanuelle/Sukunabikona|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スクナビコナ}} and {{ill|Awashima-no-Kami|ja|淡島神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Awashima-no-Kami|qq=}}. * '''Tsurumine Inari Shrine''' — Dedicated to {{ill|Ukanomitama|ja|宇迦之御魂|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ukanomitama|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ウカノミタマ}}. == Access == Take the JR Tōkaidō Main Line to {{ill|Chigasaki Station|en|Chigasaki Station|WD=Q800622|lt=Chigasaki Station|1=Chigasaki Station}}. From the north exit, take a bus and alight at "Tsurumine Elementary School," then walk 1 minute. == References == {{Unreferenced help}} {{Reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|Minamoto no Yoriyoshi|en|Minamoto no Yoriyoshi|WD=Q3082851|lt=Minamoto no Yoriyoshi|1=Minamoto no Yoriyoshi}} * {{ill|Iwashimizu Hachimangū|en|Iwashimizu Hachimangū|WD=Q710098|lt=Iwashimizu Hachimangū|1=Iwashimizu Hachimangū}} * {{ill|Hamaori Festival|ja|浜降祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hamaori Festival|qq=}} {{translated page|ja|鶴嶺八幡宮|version=101890838|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:鶴嶺八幡宮]] {{wikidata link|Q199699}} [[Category:Buildings in Chigasaki, Kanagawa]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Kanagawa Prefecture]] [[Category:Hachiman shrines]] [[Category:Minamoto no Yoriyoshi]] [[Category:Municipal History of Chigasaki]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kanagawa Prefecture]] [[Category:Son-sha in Kanagawa Prefecture]] [[Category:Sugawara no Michizane]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:pages]]

[[ja:鶴嶺八幡宮]] [[ja:鶴嶺八幡宮]] [[ja:鶴嶺八幡宮]] {{Unreferenced|date=June 28, 2018}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name=Tsuruoka Hachimangū | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image=File:GrfKanagawakenChigasakishi-Turuminehachimangu-01.jpg | location=〒 253-0086
{{ill|Kanagawa Prefecture|en|Kanagawa Prefecture|WD=Q127513|lt=Kanagawa Prefecture|1=Kanagawa Prefecture}}[[:en:Chigasaki, Kanagawa|Chigasaki]] Hamano-go 462 | ISO=JP-14 | Latitude degrees = 35|Latitude minutes = 20|Latitude seconds = 18.38 | Longitude degrees=139|Longitude minutes=23|Longitude seconds=28.30 | deity={{ill|Ōjin|WD=Q317997|1=Emperor Ōjin}} | Shrine Rank=Former {{ill|Village shrine|ja|村社|1=Son-sha|WD=Q134917284}}{{efn|Former shrine rank|lt=Son-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Village shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}, Former {{ill|District shrine|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|WD=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|WD=Q90139226|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/District shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} | Foundation={{ill|Chōgen 3|ja|長元3年|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chōgen 3|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} ({{ill|1030|en|1030|WD=Q19301|lt=1030|1=1030}}) | Main Hall=Nagare-zukuri | Alternate name=Tsuruoka Hachiman Shrine | festival={{ill|September 15|en|September 15|WD=Q2846|lt=September 15|1=September 15}} | Rituals={{ill|Hamaori Festival|ja|浜降祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hamaori Festival|qq=}} ({{ill|Marine Day|en|Marine Day|WD=Q1355833|lt=Marine Day|1=Marine Day}}) | Map=Japan Kanagawa | prefecture={{ill|Chigasaki|WD=Q633911|1=Chigasaki}} | coordinates={{Coord|35.33843888888889|N|139.39119444444444|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | architecture_style={{ill|nagare-zukuri|WD=Q210684|1=Nagare-zukuri}} }} '''Tsuruoka Hachimangū''' (鶴嶺八幡宮) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=shrine|1=Shinto shrine}} located in [[:en:Chigasaki, Kanagawa|Chigasaki]], {{ill|Kanagawa Prefecture|en|Kanagawa Prefecture|WD=Q127513|lt=Kanagawa Prefecture|1=Kanagawa Prefecture}}. It is also known as Tsuruoka Hachiman Shrine. Renowned as the center of Hachiman worship in {{ill|Sagami Province|en|Sagami Province|WD=Q1053732|lt=Sagami Province|1=Sagami Province}}, the shrine has been revered since ancient times as a significant site of {{ill|Hachiman faith|ja|八幡信仰|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hachiman faith|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 八幡神|1=Hachiman|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q261637}}.Cited by the Kanagawa Prefecture Shrine Office According to the shrine, it was the first {{ill|clan shrine|ja|氏社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/clan shrine|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} established by the {{ill|Minamoto clan|en|Minamoto clan|WD=Q599881|lt=Genji|1=Minamoto clan}} when they expanded into the Kanto region. == History == The shrine was founded in {{ill|Chōgen 3 (1030)|ja|長元3rd year （1030th year ）|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chōgen 3 (1030)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, during the {{ill|Subjugation of Shimōsa|ja|下総の乱|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Subjugation of Shimōsa|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} by {{ill|Minamoto no Yoriyoshi|en|Minamoto no Yoriyoshi|WD=Q3082851|lt=Minamoto no Yoriyoshi|1=Minamoto no Yoriyoshi}}, who hailed from {{ill|Kawachi Province|en|Kawachi Province|WD=Q1047150|lt=Kawachi Province|1=Kawachi Province}} (modern-day {{ill|Habikino|en|Habikino|WD=Q694308|lt=Habikino|1=Habikino}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|en|Osaka Prefecture|WD=Q122723|lt=Osaka Prefecture|1=Osaka Prefecture}}). Yoriyoshi enshrined {{ill|Iwashimizu Hachimangū|en|Iwashimizu Hachimangū|WD=Q710098|lt=Iwashimizu Hachimangū|1=Iwashimizu Hachimangū}} at the site under the name "Kaijima Hachimangū" (another theory suggests it was {{ill|Usa Hachimangū|ja|宇佐八幡宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Usa Hachimangū|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 宇佐神宮|1=Usa Jingū|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q715632}}). Later, the Genji moved it to its current location. In {{ill|Tenki 3 (1055)|ja|天喜3rd year （1055th year ）|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenki 3 (1055)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, during the {{ill|Former Nine Years' War|en|Former Nine Years' War|WD=Q1187448|lt=Earlier Nine Years' War|1=Former Nine Years' War}}, Yoriyoshi prayed at the shrine and achieved victory. To honor this, he established the {{ill|Tsurugaoka Hachimangū|lt=Original Hachiman Shrine|ja|元八幡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsurugaoka Hachimangū|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 鶴岡八幡宮}} in {{ill|Yuigahama|en|Yuigahama|WD=Q8060490|lt=Yuigahama|1=Yuigahama}}, Kamakura, in {{ill|Kōhei 6 (1063)|ja|康平6年（1063rd year ）|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōhei 6 (1063)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. In {{ill|Ōtoku 2 (1085)|ja|応徳2nd year （1085th year ）|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtoku 2 (1085)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, Minamoto no Yoshiie, Yoriyoshi's heir, dedicated the shrine in Hamano-go as "Tsuruoka Hachimangū." Subsequently, Tsuruoka Hachimangū gained prominence, receiving land and monetary support from {{ill|Minamoto no Yoritomo|en|Minamoto no Yoritomo|WD=Q242800|lt=Minamoto no Yoritomo|1=Minamoto no Yoritomo}} and others. Over time, it became the center for the famous {{ill|Hamaori Festival|ja|浜降祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hamaori Festival|qq=}} and absorbed nearby shrines such as Sazuka Daimyōjin. == Deities == * {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|en|Emperor Ōjin|WD=Q317997|lt=Emperor Ōjin|1=Emperor Ōjin}} * {{ill|Emperor Nintoku|en|Emperor Nintoku|WD=Q313119|lt=Emperor Nintoku|1=Emperor Nintoku}} * Sazuka Daimyōjin (associated with {{ill|Ōyamazumi Shrine|en|Ōyamazumi Shrine|WD=Q703904|lt=Ōyamazumi Shrine|1=Ōyamazumi Shrine}}) * {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|ja|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugawara no Michizane|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q382005}} (from Tsurumine Tenmangū) == Annual Events == * {{ill|Hamaori Festival|ja|浜降祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hamaori Festival|qq=}} (Marine Day) * {{ill|Misorogi Ritual|ja|禊の神事|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Misorogi Ritual|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (June 29, formerly held on July 15) * {{ill|Reisai|lt=Reisai|lt=Reitaisai|ja|例大祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Reitaisai|ja_comment=fixed the redirect|1=Reisai|2=ja|3=例祭|4=fr|5=Reisai|lt=Reitaisai}} (September 15) == Grounds == ; '''Great Ginkgo Tree''' : A 950-year-old tree said to have been planted by Minamoto no Yoshiie to pray for victory. ; '''Miyagane (Shrine Bell)''' : The shrine bell, originally installed in {{ill|Enpō 8 (1680)|ja|延宝8th year （1680th year ）|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Enpō 8 (1680)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, was reconstructed after the war in 1965 as a "peace bell." === Sub-Shrines === * '''Shōnan Awashima Shrine''' — Dedicated to {{ill|Sukunahikona|ja|少彦名命|1=Sukunabikona|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sukunahikona|13=User:Immanuelle/Sukunabikona|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スクナビコナ}} and {{ill|Awashima-no-Kami|ja|淡島神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Awashima-no-Kami|qq=}}. * '''Tsurumine Inari Shrine''' — Dedicated to {{ill|Ukanomitama|ja|宇迦之御魂|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ukanomitama|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ウカノミタマ}}. == Access == Take the JR Tōkaidō Main Line to {{ill|Chigasaki Station|en|Chigasaki Station|WD=Q800622|lt=Chigasaki Station|1=Chigasaki Station}}. From the north exit, take a bus and alight at "Tsurumine Elementary School," then walk 1 minute. == References == {{Unreferenced help}} {{Reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|Minamoto no Yoriyoshi|en|Minamoto no Yoriyoshi|WD=Q3082851|lt=Minamoto no Yoriyoshi|1=Minamoto no Yoriyoshi}} * {{ill|Iwashimizu Hachimangū|en|Iwashimizu Hachimangū|WD=Q710098|lt=Iwashimizu Hachimangū|1=Iwashimizu Hachimangū}} * {{ill|Hamaori Festival|ja|浜降祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hamaori Festival|qq=}} {{translated page|ja|鶴嶺八幡宮|version=101890838|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:鶴嶺八幡宮]] {{wikidata link|Q199699}} [[Category:Buildings in Chigasaki, Kanagawa]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Kanagawa Prefecture]] [[Category:Hachiman shrines]] [[Category:Minamoto no Yoriyoshi]] [[Category:Municipal History of Chigasaki]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kanagawa Prefecture]] [[Category:Son-sha in Kanagawa Prefecture]] [[Category:Sugawara no Michizane]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]]

Later, the Genji moved it to its current location. Over time, it became the center for the famous {{ill|Hamaori Festival|ja|浜降祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hamaori Festival|qq=}} and absorbed nearby shrines such as Sazuka Daimyōjin. == Deities == * {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|en|Emperor Ōjin|WD=Q317997|lt=Emperor Ōjin|1=Emperor Ōjin}} * {{ill|Emperor Nintoku|en|Emperor Nintoku|WD=Q313119|lt=Emperor Nintoku|1=Emperor Nintoku}} * Sazuka Daimyōjin (associated with {{ill|Ōyamazumi Shrine|en|Ōyamazumi Shrine|WD=Q703904|lt=Ōyamazumi Shrine|1=Ōyamazumi Shrine}}) * {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|ja|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugawara no Michizane|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q382005}} (from Tsurumine Tenmangū) == Annual Events == * {{ill|Hamaori Festival|ja|浜降祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hamaori Festival|qq=}} (Marine Day) * {{ill|Misorogi Ritual|ja|禊の神事|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Misorogi Ritual|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (June 29, formerly held on July 15) * {{ill|Reisai|lt=Reisai|lt=Reitaisai|ja|例大祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Reitaisai|ja_comment=fixed the redirect|1=Reisai|2=ja|3=例祭|4=fr|5=Reisai|lt=Reitaisai}} (September 15) == Grounds == ; '''Great Ginkgo Tree''' : A 950-year-old tree said to have been planted by Minamoto no Yoshiie to pray for victory. ; '''Miyagane (Shrine Bell)''' : The shrine bell, originally installed in {{ill|Enpō 8 (1680)|ja|延宝8th year （1680th year ）|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Enpō 8 (1680)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, was reconstructed after the war in 1965 as a "peace bell." === Sub-Shrines === * '''Shōnan Awashima Shrine''' — Dedicated to {{ill|Sukunahikona|ja|少彦名命|1=Sukunabikona|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sukunahikona|13=User:Immanuelle/Sukunabikona|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スクナビコナ}} and {{ill|Awashima-no-Kami|ja|淡島神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Awashima-no-Kami|qq=}}. * '''Tsurumine Inari Shrine''' — Dedicated to {{ill|Ukanomitama|ja|宇迦之御魂|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ukanomitama|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ウカノミタマ}}. == Access == Take the JR Tōkaidō Main Line to {{ill|Chigasaki Station|en|Chigasaki Station|WD=Q800622|lt=Chigasaki Station|1=Chigasaki Station}}. From the north exit, take a bus and alight at "Tsurumine Elementary School," then walk 1 minute. == References == {{Unreferenced help}} {{Reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|Minamoto no Yoriyoshi|en|Minamoto no Yoriyoshi|WD=Q3082851|lt=Minamoto no Yoriyoshi|1=Minamoto no Yoriyoshi}} * {{ill|Iwashimizu Hachimangū|en|Iwashimizu Hachimangū|WD=Q710098|lt=Iwashimizu Hachimangū|1=Iwashimizu Hachimangū}} * {{ill|Hamaori Festival|ja|浜降祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hamaori Festival|qq=}} {{translated page|ja|鶴嶺八幡宮|version=101890838|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:鶴嶺八幡宮]] {{wikidata link|Q199699}} [[Category:Buildings and structures in Chigasaki, Kanagawa]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Kanagawa Prefecture]] [[Category:Hachiman shrines]] [[Category:Minamoto no Yoriyoshi]] [[Category:Municipal History of Chigasaki]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kanagawa Prefecture]] [[Category:Son-sha in Kanagawa Prefecture]] [[Category:Sugawara no Michizane]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]]

revision.text:
{{Unreferenced|date=June 28, 2018}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name=Tsuruoka Hachimangū | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image=File:GrfKanagawakenChigasakishi-Turuminehachimangu-01.jpg | location=〒 253-0086
{{ill|Kanagawa Prefecture|en|Kanagawa Prefecture|WD=Q127513|lt=Kanagawa Prefecture|1=Kanagawa Prefecture}}[[:en:Chigasaki, Kanagawa|Chigasaki]] Hamano-go 462 | ISO=JP-14 | Latitude degrees = 35|Latitude minutes = 20|Latitude seconds = 18.38 | Longitude degrees=139|Longitude minutes=23|Longitude seconds=28.30 | deity={{ill|Ōjin|WD=Q317997|1=Emperor Ōjin}} | Shrine Rank=Former {{ill|Village shrine|ja|村社|1=Son-sha|WD=Q134917284}}{{efn|Former shrine rank|lt=Son-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Village shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}, Former {{ill|District shrine|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|WD=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|WD=Q90139226|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/District shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} | Foundation={{ill|Chōgen 3|ja|長元3年|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chōgen 3|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} ({{ill|1030|en|1030|WD=Q19301|lt=1030|1=1030}}) | Main Hall=Nagare-zukuri | Alternate name=Tsuruoka Hachiman Shrine | festival={{ill|September 15|en|September 15|WD=Q2846|lt=September 15|1=September 15}} | Rituals={{ill|Hamaori Festival|ja|浜降祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hamaori Festival|qq=}} ({{ill|Marine Day|en|Marine Day|WD=Q1355833|lt=Marine Day|1=Marine Day}}) | Map=Japan Kanagawa | prefecture={{ill|Chigasaki|WD=Q633911|1=Chigasaki}} | coordinates={{Coord|35.33843888888889|N|139.39119444444444|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | architecture_style={{ill|nagare-zukuri|WD=Q210684|1=Nagare-zukuri}} }} '''Tsuruoka Hachimangū''' (鶴嶺八幡宮) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=shrine|1=Shinto shrine}} located in [[:en:Chigasaki, Kanagawa|Chigasaki]], {{ill|Kanagawa Prefecture|en|Kanagawa Prefecture|WD=Q127513|lt=Kanagawa Prefecture|1=Kanagawa Prefecture}}. It is also known as Tsuruoka Hachiman Shrine. Renowned as the center of Hachiman worship in {{ill|Sagami Province|en|Sagami Province|WD=Q1053732|lt=Sagami Province|1=Sagami Province}}, the shrine has been revered since ancient times as a significant site of {{ill|Hachiman faith|ja|八幡信仰|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hachiman faith|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 八幡神|1=Hachiman|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q261637}}.Cited by the Kanagawa Prefecture Shrine Office According to the shrine, it was the first {{ill|clan shrine|ja|氏社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/clan shrine|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} established by the {{ill|Minamoto clan|en|Minamoto clan|WD=Q599881|lt=Genji|1=Minamoto clan}} when they expanded into the Kanto region. == History == The shrine was founded in {{ill|Chōgen 3 (1030)|ja|長元3rd year （1030th year ）|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chōgen 3 (1030)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, during the {{ill|Subjugation of Shimōsa|ja|下総の乱|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Subjugation of Shimōsa|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} by {{ill|Minamoto no Yoriyoshi|en|Minamoto no Yoriyoshi|WD=Q3082851|lt=Minamoto no Yoriyoshi|1=Minamoto no Yoriyoshi}}, who hailed from {{ill|Kawachi Province|en|Kawachi Province|WD=Q1047150|lt=Kawachi Province|1=Kawachi Province}} (modern-day {{ill|Habikino|en|Habikino|WD=Q694308|lt=Habikino|1=Habikino}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|en|Osaka Prefecture|WD=Q122723|lt=Osaka Prefecture|1=Osaka Prefecture}}). Yoriyoshi enshrined {{ill|Iwashimizu Hachimangū|en|Iwashimizu Hachimangū|WD=Q710098|lt=Iwashimizu Hachimangū|1=Iwashimizu Hachimangū}} at the site under the name "Kaijima Hachimangū" (another theory suggests it was {{ill|Usa Hachimangū|ja|宇佐八幡宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Usa Hachimangū|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 宇佐神宮|1=Usa Jingū|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q715632}}). Later, the Genji moved it to its current location. In {{ill|Tenki 3 (1055)|ja|天喜3rd year （1055th year ）|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenki 3 (1055)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, during the {{ill|Former Nine Years' War|en|Former Nine Years' War|WD=Q1187448|lt=Earlier Nine Years' War|1=Former Nine Years' War}}, Yoriyoshi prayed at the shrine and achieved victory. To honor this, he established the {{ill|Tsurugaoka Hachimangū|lt=Original Hachiman Shrine|ja|元八幡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsurugaoka Hachimangū|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 鶴岡八幡宮}} in {{ill|Yuigahama|en|Yuigahama|WD=Q8060490|lt=Yuigahama|1=Yuigahama}}, Kamakura, in {{ill|Kōhei 6 (1063)|ja|康平6年（1063rd year ）|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōhei 6 (1063)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. In {{ill|Ōtoku 2 (1085)|ja|応徳2nd year （1085th year ）|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtoku 2 (1085)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, Minamoto no Yoshiie, Yoriyoshi's heir, dedicated the shrine in Hamano-go as "Tsuruoka Hachimangū." Subsequently, Tsuruoka Hachimangū gained prominence, receiving land and monetary support from {{ill|Minamoto no Yoritomo|en|Minamoto no Yoritomo|WD=Q242800|lt=Minamoto no Yoritomo|1=Minamoto no Yoritomo}} and others. Over time, it became the center for the famous {{ill|Hamaori Festival|ja|浜降祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hamaori Festival|qq=}} and absorbed nearby shrines such as Sazuka Daimyōjin. == Deities == * {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|en|Emperor Ōjin|WD=Q317997|lt=Emperor Ōjin|1=Emperor Ōjin}} * {{ill|Emperor Nintoku|en|Emperor Nintoku|WD=Q313119|lt=Emperor Nintoku|1=Emperor Nintoku}} * Sazuka Daimyōjin (associated with {{ill|Ōyamazumi Shrine|en|Ōyamazumi Shrine|WD=Q703904|lt=Ōyamazumi Shrine|1=Ōyamazumi Shrine}}) * {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|ja|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugawara no Michizane|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q382005}} (from Tsurumine Tenmangū) == Annual Events == * {{ill|Hamaori Festival|ja|浜降祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hamaori Festival|qq=}} (Marine Day) * {{ill|Misorogi Ritual|ja|禊の神事|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Misorogi Ritual|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (June 29, formerly held on July 15) * {{ill|Reisai|lt=Reisai|lt=Reitaisai|ja|例大祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Reitaisai|ja_comment=fixed the redirect|1=Reisai|2=ja|3=例祭|4=fr|5=Reisai|lt=Reitaisai}} (September 15) == Grounds == ; '''Great Ginkgo Tree''' : A 950-year-old tree said to have been planted by Minamoto no Yoshiie to pray for victory. ; '''Miyagane (Shrine Bell)''' : The shrine bell, originally installed in {{ill|Enpō 8 (1680)|ja|延宝8th year （1680th year ）|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Enpō 8 (1680)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, was reconstructed after the war in 1965 as a "peace bell." === Sub-Shrines === * '''Shōnan Awashima Shrine''' — Dedicated to {{ill|Sukunahikona|ja|少彦名命|1=Sukunabikona|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sukunahikona|13=User:Immanuelle/Sukunabikona|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スクナビコナ}} and {{ill|Awashima-no-Kami|ja|淡島神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Awashima-no-Kami|qq=}}. * '''Tsurumine Inari Shrine''' — Dedicated to {{ill|Ukanomitama|ja|宇迦之御魂|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ukanomitama|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ウカノミタマ}}. == Access == Take the JR Tōkaidō Main Line to {{ill|Chigasaki Station|en|Chigasaki Station|WD=Q800622|lt=Chigasaki Station|1=Chigasaki Station}}. From the north exit, take a bus and alight at "Tsurumine Elementary School," then walk 1 minute. == References == {{Unreferenced help}} {{Reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|Minamoto no Yoriyoshi|en|Minamoto no Yoriyoshi|WD=Q3082851|lt=Minamoto no Yoriyoshi|1=Minamoto no Yoriyoshi}} * {{ill|Iwashimizu Hachimangū|en|Iwashimizu Hachimangū|WD=Q710098|lt=Iwashimizu Hachimangū|1=Iwashimizu Hachimangū}} * {{ill|Hamaori Festival|ja|浜降祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hamaori Festival|qq=}} {{translated page|ja|鶴嶺八幡宮|version=101890838|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{wikidata link|Q199699|ja|鶴嶺八幡宮}} [[Category:Buildings and structures in Chigasaki, Kanagawa]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Kanagawa Prefecture]] [[Category:Hachiman shrines]] [[Category:Minamoto no Yoriyoshi]] [[Category:Municipal History of Chigasaki]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kanagawa Prefecture]] [[Category:Son-sha in Kanagawa Prefecture]] [[Category:Sugawara no Michizane]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]]
